title: Venus flytrap-inspired PVDF-based sensitive strain sensor with modulus-tuned design for anisotropic bending recognition
abstract: Based on the modulus difference and stress concentration mechanism, we designed PVDF-based nanofibers via coaxial electrospinning and applied them to piezoelectric sensors.

title: The fate of nanoparticle surface chemistry during reductive electrosynthesis in aprotic media
abstract: Reductive electrochemical coupling of carbon dioxide with organic molecules in aprotic media is affected significantly by the surface chemistry of the electocatalyst.

title: The key approach to fabricate ultra-high strength dual-phase titanium alloys and its practice
abstract: Based on a strengthening model, this study identifies key yield strength-influencing factors, proposes a universal design approach for ultra-high-strength duplex titanium alloys, and experimentally fabricates one with a 1615 MPa yield strength.

title: A theoretical study on doping Pd-like superatoms into defective graphene quantum dots: an efficient strategy to design single superatom catalysts for the Suzuki reaction
abstract: A new kind of single superatom catalysts (SSCs) has been designed via doping palladium-like superatoms into a defective graphene quantum dot (GQD) model with a double-vacancy site, which paves the way to develop novel superatom-based catalysts.

title: Boosting Cu ion capture in high-salinity environments with amino-functionalized millispheres
abstract: This study demonstrates the development of amino-functionalized chloromethylated polystyrene (EDA@CMPS) millispheres that exhibit enhanced Cu ion adsorption in saline environments through salt-promoted protonation of amino groups.

title: High-performance van der Waals stacked transistors based on ultrathin GaPS 4 dielectrics
abstract: The layered wide bandgap semiconductor gallium thiophosphate can be applied as a gate dielectric to achieve high-performance Van der Waals field-effect transistors.

title: Toward flexible and omnidirectional self-powered near-ultraviolet photodetection by constructing a mixed-dimensional nanobelt/nanosheet heterojunction of CdS/PbI 2
abstract: A mixed-dimensional nanobelt/nanosheet heterojunction of CdS/PbI 2 has been constructed successfully for self-powered flexible and omnidirectional near-ultraviolet photodetection.

title: Exclusive production of acetone using a copper nanoparticle anchored LSCO perovskite electrocatalyst: cell design and metal–support interaction governed electrocatalysis
abstract: This study explores the selective acetone production via CO 2 electroreduction using a Cu nanoparticle decorated La 0.8 Sr 0.2 CoO 3 (LSCO) perovskite catalyst. Catalyst image created using VESTA 3 software (https://jp-minerals.org/vesta/en/).

title: Selenium-modified In 2 O 3 photoanode: oxygen vacancy-mediated “defect capture-interface transport” and extended light absorption for efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: Se steals O to form new O V and In–Se bonds in In 2 O 3− x @In 2 Se 3 .

title: Mechanistic insight into RCNs-induced conductivity enhancement in sustainable CWPU/PEDOT:PSS nanocomposites for flexible and biocompatible electronics
abstract: Mechanistic model illustrating the formation of PEDOT-rich domains in CWPU/RCNs/PEDOT:PSS films through solvent evaporation, hydrophobic aggregation, π–π stacking, and hydrogen bonding–driven phase separation during film formation.

title: Formation of 3D Cr 2 C through solid state reaction-mediated Al extraction within Cr 2 AlC/Cu thin films
abstract: Metastable Cr 2 C was formed upon Al extraction from Cr 2 AlC MAX phase thin films using Cu as a getter material.

title: Hollow Au nanoparticles for single-molecule Raman spectroscopy via a synergistic electromagnetic and chemical enhancement strategy
abstract: The integration of a 2D WS 2 monolayer into a plasmonic nanogap leads to a synergistic enhancement, achieving an unprecedented Raman enhancement factor of 10 14 –10 15 and ultrasensitive single-molecule SERS detection (10 −16 M).

title: Correction: Hollow Au nanoparticles for single-molecule Raman spectroscopy via a synergistic electromagnetic and chemical enhancement strategy
abstract: Correction for ‘Hollow Au nanoparticles for single-molecule Raman spectroscopy via a synergistic electromagnetic and chemical enhancement strategy’ by Zihan Gao et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4nr05311g.

title: Correction: Heterostructure interface-engineered 3D/2D CoMn 2 O 4 /CoFe 2 O 4 /NF core/shell Bi-functional electrocatalytic nanomaterials for efficient overall water splitting application in alkaline media
abstract: Correction for ‘Heterostructure interface-engineered 3D/2D CoMn 2 O 4 /CoFe 2 O 4 /NF core/shell Bi-functional electrocatalytic nanomaterials for efficient overall water splitting application in alkaline media’ by Moorthy Krishnamachari et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 18190–18201, https://doi.org/10…

title: Non-volatile resistive switching characteristics in Cu 2− x S-based memristor
abstract: A Cu 2− x S based memristor fabricated at room temperature shows stable non-filamentary switching via Cu 2 S–CuS phase change, with low-voltage operation, high ON/OFF ratio, and potential for neuromorphic computing.

title: Anticooperative self-assembly of perylene diimide dyes in water unveiled by advanced molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: The anti-cooperative self-assembly of bulky substituted PDIs in water is studied via classical MD and enhanced sampling, revealing dimers as primary blocks and an etropically driven aggregation mechanism, modulated by solvation dynamics.

title: Ultra-low gadolinium doping in multi-core iron oxide enables efficient dual-mode MRI and magnetic hyperthermia: a structure–function study
abstract: Engineered nanoflowers of gadolinium-doped maghemite (γ-Fe 2 O 3 ) offer a novel theranostic platform. Ultra-low Gd 3+ doping optimizes them for both magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and magnetic hyperthermia.

title: High-sensitivity broadband terahertz detection enabled by synergistic effects in an antenna-integrated TaCo 2 Te 2 /graphene heterostructure
abstract: A highly sensitive and broadband THz detector is achieved through the unidirectional movement of photoexcited charge carriers driven either by electric fields or temperature gradients in an antenna-integrated TaCo 2 Te 2 /graphene heterostructure.

title: Transforming docetaxel delivery: lung-targeted polyphosphazene nanoparticles with enhanced safety profile
abstract: Polytaxel™, a polyphosphazene-based nanocarrier for docetaxel, outperforms Taxotere in A549 xenograft models by enhancing tumor suppression, prolonging survival, and reducing systemic toxicity through targeted low-dose delivery. Created in BioRender. REJINOLD, S. (2025) https://BioRender.com/rvzmjtw…

title: Construction of a circularly polarized luminescence sensor based on self-assembly of carbon dots and G-quartet chiral nanofibers
abstract: This study develops a highly sensitive chiroptical sensor for detecting Hg 2+ and I − ions through the co-assembly of G-quartet nanofibers and N–S-CDs.

title: Dextran stabilised hematite: a sustainable anode in aqueous electrolytes
abstract: Hybrid anode electrodes comprising hematite particles as the active material and dextran cross-linked via hydrazone bonds as the binder, exhibited high cyclability and promising electrochemical performance for multivalent aqueous batteries.

title: Impact of processing atmosphere on nanoscale properties of highly efficient Cs 0.05 MA 0.05 FA 0.9 PbI 3 perovskite solar cells
abstract: The fabrication process of triple-cation-halide organic inorganic perovskites in different annealing atmospheres change the amount of PbI 2 on the surface, which impacts the optoelectronic properties of the absorbers and of the solar cell devices.

title: Sustainable and energy-saving hydrogen production via binder-free and in situ electrodeposited Ni–Mn–S nanowires on Ni–Cu 3-D substrates
abstract: A bi-functional electrocatalyst based on Ni–Mn–S nanowires@Ni–Cu micro–nano dendrites was designed for the HER and UOR, which demonstrated favorable catalytic activity and stability for energy-saving hydrogen production.

title: Transparent and flexible photonic crystal films with enhanced optical performance for multi-level anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: Solvent-assisted fabrication of transparent and flexible photonic crystal films is designed to enhance the optical performance, and the local area modification enables the demonstration of multi-level visual effects.

title: A novel spherical Mg/Sn co-doped alluaudite-type Na 2+2 x Fe 2− x (SO 4 ) 3 cathode material for durable low-temperature sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel carbon-coated and Mg/Sn co-doped Na 2+2 x Fe 2− x (SO 4 ) 3 cathode material was successfully obtained for durable low-temperature SIBs. The robust CEI and fast ionic transport kinetics of NFMS/C-Sn0.03 enable practical SIBs in frigid environments.

title: Robust fluorine-rich YF 3 artificial interfacial layer for providing uniform Zn 2+ flux and enhancing cycling stability of Zn anodes
abstract: A robust fluorine-rich YF 3 artificial interfacial layer is employed to facilitate Zn 2+ migration and ensure a uniform Zn 2+ flux, thereby achieving uniform Zn deposition and enhancing the cycling stability of the Zn anode.

title: Experimental and first principle DFT comprehensions of metal and bimetal modified bismuth titanate for wastewater treatment and CO 2 hydrogenation
abstract: p–n heterojunction of bismuth titanate with CuO and Ag (electron sink) enhances electron transport, increases binding energy (BE), and improves catalytic activity over pristine bismuth titanate.

title: A sustainable and economical approach for effective adsorption of antibiotics and heavy metal ions using Neolamarckia cadamba -mediated MgO–rGO nanocomposites
abstract: This study investigates the adsorption behavior of antibiotics and heavy metals ions on MgO–rGO NCs and their transport in aqueous systems.

title: Graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets as scatterers for random laser action
abstract: We demonstrate random laser (RL) emission from rhodamine 610 using graphitic carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) nanosheets synthesized using a thermal polymerization method as scatterers in a liquid suspension to provide feedback for the RL emission.

title: Data-driven optimization of nanoparticle size using the prediction reliability enhancing parameter (PREP)
abstract: A data-driven method (PREP) enables precise control of nanoparticle size using minimal experimental iterations, offering a new pathway for rapid nanomaterial optimization.

title: Comparative analysis of steady-state creep in SUS-ZrO 2 and Al-ZrO 2 functionally graded pressurized cylinders and its implications
abstract: This paper focuses on secondary creep analysis in functionally graded (FG) rotating cylinders subjected to internal and external pressure.

title: Two-terminal analog memory comprising self-assembled monolayers of edge-fused porphyrin oligomers
abstract: Here we measure the electrical properties of monolayers of porphyrin oligomers. We demonstrate hysteresis in current–voltage responses that becomes more prominent with increasing molecular length and show voltage-controlled conductance tuneability.

title: Uniform spherical chiral polyrotaxanes with steerable circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: Here, this work demonstrates an example of a CPL-active spherical CPR materials and provides insight into macrocyclic size-dependent CPL generation through a mechanically interlocked strategy and covalent reticular chemistry.

title: Tuning the dimensionality of semiconducting nanostructures by self-assembled tetraphenylethylene substituted corroles
abstract: TPE substituents at the meso position of corroles determine the planarity of the structure, resulting in self-assembled 1D nanotubes, 2D sheets and 0D spheres with remarkable electrical conductivity and potential for organic electronics.

title: First-principles methods for unraveling the structure–catalytic activity relationship and mechanism of δ-MnO 2 -supported metal cluster catalysts in ambient temperature benzene to CO 2 degradation
abstract: δ-MnO 2 based Ag 4 facilitated benzene degradation to CO 2 and H 2 O at room temperature.

title: Application of functional coatings in water electrolyzers and fuel cells
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in functional coatings in water electrolyzers and fuel cells, covering both compositional design and mechanism understanding of coating materials.

title: Controlling plasmonic charge carrier flow at a nanoparticle–molecule interface using ligand chemistry
abstract: We demonstate wavelength specific modulation of plasmonic catalysis using selected ligands.

title: Direct band-gap monolayer phosphorene via pentagon–octagon–pentagon defects: highly anisotropic carrier transport and efficient photocatalytic activity
abstract: Computational design of direct band-gap monolayer phosphorene via pentagon–octagon–pentagon defects exhibiting highly anisotropic carrier transport and efficient photocatalytic activity.

title: Environment-responsive polydopamine nanoparticle cross-linked phenylboronic-grafted hyaluronic acid with enzyme-like properties for tumor synergistic therapy
abstract: Nattokinase-mediated tumor microenvironment to enhance the infiltration of drugs and immune cells into solid tumor tissues.

title: Enhanced supercapacitor performance of NiCr–LDH nanosheets supported on exfoliated nano-graphite flakes by a one-step cathode glow discharge electrolysis strategy
abstract: A flower-like NiCr–LDH/graphite nanocomposite is fabricated by CGDE strategy. The nanocomposite exhibits 2577.3 F g −1 specific capacity. The NiCr–LDH/graphite//AC shows 44.37 Wh kg −1 energy density and 91.2% capacity retention after 10 000 cycles.

title: Colloidal organometallic synthesis of solution-processable barium titanate nanoparticles for nanoelectronic applications
abstract: Colloidal BaTiO 3 nanoparticles were synthesized, ligand exchanged with oxalic acid to make an ink, and their properties explored for piezoelectrics.

title: Ionic liquid-directed synthesis of Au–AgBr Janus nanoparticles via digestive ripening and solvated metal atom dispersion
abstract: Solvated metal atom dispersion followed by digestive ripening of Au and Ag nanoparticles results in the formation of different heterostructures including Janus Au–AgBr NPs which is mediated by ionic liquid.

title: Lorentz force-assisted growth of romanesco-like Ni–Fe nano-cone arrays for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction at high current densities
abstract: A novel Lorentz-force-assisted electrodeposition method is introduced to fabricate Romanesco-like Ni–Fe nano-cone arrays with enhanced stability and efficient bubble release for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) under high current densities.

title: Unlocking efficiency: experimental and theoretical insights into biomass-derived carbon nanofluids with enhanced thermal conductivity
abstract: Heat transfer in biomass-based carbon nanospheres based nanofluid for automobile application.

title: Leveraging ensemble machine learning models (XGBoost and random forest) and genetic algorithms to predict factors contributing to the liposomal entrapment of therapeutics
abstract: Analyses revealed high accuracy of the model utilized for the prediction of entrapment efficiency in liposomal carriers in the current study. Water solubility was the main variable affecting the drug entrapment.

title: Pt atomic cluster-decorated Fe 2 (MoO 4 ) 3 hollow microspheres for detecting sub-parts-per-million xylene prepared by the atomic layer deposition method
abstract: This study employed the atomic layer deposition method to fabricate Pt atomic cluster-decorated Fe 2 (MoO 4 ) 3 hollow microspheres for detecting sub-parts-per-million xylene.

title: Unveiling of free carrier transport and ion migration in a 2D–3D perovskite mixture for stable optoelectronic devices
abstract: Understanding charge transport behaviors and ion migration in multiphase 2D–3D mixed perovskites at the micro and nanoscale.

title: Nanomaterial-based synergistic strategies for combating dental caries: progress and perspectives
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive insight into the latest research advancements in developing novel nanoparticle-based synergistic strategies mainly for remineralization of dental hard tissues and inhibition of cariogenic biofilms.

title: Controllable syntheses of two structurally new {AsW 9 O 33 }-mediated silver alkynyl clusters
abstract: Two atomically-precise {AsW 9 O 33 }-mediated silver alkynyl clusters have been synthesized by adjusting mixed reaction solvents, showing efficient photothermal performance and catalytic activities towards the detoxification of 4-nitrophenol.

title: Layer-number-dependent photoswitchability in 2D MoS 2 -diarylethene hybrids
abstract: Optoelectronic properties of atomically-thin MoS 2 flakes are modulated using photochromic diarylethene molecules. The layer-number-dependent charge transfer behaviors at the hybrid heterointerface are elucidated.

title: Bimetallic Fe(OH) x @Co 0.8 Fe 0.2 -MOF/NF composites as effective electrocatalysts for the production of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid from 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
abstract: Electrochemical conversion of biobased HMF to FDCA with bimetallic Fe (OH) x @Co 0.8 Fe 0.2 metal–organic framework/nickel foam composites, coupled with hydrogen production.

title: Molecular mechanisms behind the anti corona virus activity of small metal oxide nanoparticles
abstract: The activity of small non-photoactive titania nanoparticles against corona viruses emerges due most probably to direct membrane disruption via complexation with phospholipid molecules and their subsequent removal.

title: Nanozyme-driven remodeling of the immune microenvironment: from catalytic therapy to precise immune regulation
abstract: By mimicking enzymatic activities, nanozymes emerge as versatile platforms to reprogram immune responses, paving the way for next-generation immunotherapy.

title: Nanophotonic structures energized short-wave infrared quantum dot photodetectors and their advancements in imaging and large-scale fabrication techniques
abstract: The schematic includes nanophotonic structures energized short-wave infrared quantum dot photodetectors and their advancements in imaging and large-scale fabrication techniques.

title: Metal-based micro/nanomaterials for hydrogen therapy and their biomedical applications
abstract: This review comprehensively explores the biological effects of hydrogen, hydrogen release mechanisms, synthesis methods, and biomedical applications of hydrogen-releasing metal-based micro/nanomaterials.

title: Epoxy-based vitrimeric semi-interpenetrating network/MXene nanocomposites for hydrogen gas barrier applications
abstract: The H 2 gas-barrier performance of a nylon 6 substrate is improved by an MXene-reinforced semi-interpenetrating network comprised of a poly(ethylene- co -vinyl alcohol)/epoxy vitrimer nanocomposite with the added feature of self-healing characteristics.

title: Impact of Bi 3+ and Cu 2+ doping on the optical and electronic properties of CsPbBr 3 for photocatalytic toluene oxidation
abstract: Doping of metal halide perovskites (MHPs) offers an opportunity to introduce trap states that are reported to be beneficial for the charge carrier dynamics.

title: CVD synthesis and optical study of bandgap-tunable Sn-doped monolayer molybdenum disulfide
abstract: SnCl 2 as an efficient dopant to grow Sn-doped monolayer MoS 2 and it dramatically imposes the growth behavior and the band structure of MoS 2 due to the lattice mismatch between SnS (SnS 2 ) and MoS 2 .

title: Cutting-edge synthetic strategies for designing multifunctional electrocatalysts with exceptional activity towards efficient hydrogen production and green metrics assessment
abstract: Using a simple auto-combustion strategy, we prepared Ni-Fe-doped manganese oxide that integrates green synthesis with advanced features, yielding enhanced multifunctional catalytic activity.

title: Unravelling the molecular armor, cellular dynamics and nuclear trafficking of ultra-stable oligopeptylated-AuNPs: PEG-rivalling nanocargos
abstract: Ultra-stable oligopeptylated-AuNPs exhibit PEG-comparable stability, low toxicity, and efficient endocytosis, uniquely achieving nuclear import, positioning them as next-generation biocompatible, stealth nanocargos for theragnostics.

title: CoFe-NaYF 4 :Yb/Er@SMPC derived carbon film: integrated precise stimuli-responsive NIR photothermal sensing and shape-memory performance
abstract: Utilizing CoFe alloy LSPR and biomass carbon films, the CoFe-NaYF 4 :Yb/Er@SMPC employs NaYF 4 :Yb/Er as a multifunctional filler for precise NIR-responsive shape memory and thermal sensing.

title: Constructing sponge-like structured molecularly imprinted composite membranes for acteoside separation
abstract: Hollow mesoporous carbon spheres engineer sponge-like molecularly imprinted membranes with abundant binding sites and interconnected channels, achieving superior acteoside adsorption capacity and permeation selectivity.

title: Helical magnetism in poly(aniline- co -ferrocene): structure and magnetism
abstract: This study reports the synthesis and characterisation of a ferrocene-based conjugated polymer and a chiral composite.

title: Ultrafast laser-assisted hybrid fabrication of biomimetic superhydrophobic surfaces: strategies, mechanisms, and applications
abstract: Ultrafast laser-assisted hybrid strategies enable the fabrication of robust superhydrophobic metal surfaces with hierarchical structures, exhibiting multifunctionality in anti-corrosion, anti-icing, droplet control, and oil–water separation.

title: Isomeric effects of Al 13 Keggin clusters as catalyst support on oxidation reactions
abstract: Isomeric aluminum oxo-hydroxide clusters as supports determine catalytic performance of POM-based ionic crystals in sulfide oxidation.

title: Recent advances in flexible multifunctional electrochromic devices
abstract: This review comprehensively summarizes the recent development of flexible multifunctional electrochromic devices, including energy harvesting, energy storage, multicolor displays, and smart windows.

title: Dispersion of carbon nanotubes triggered by the helical self-assembly of poly(methyl methacrylate)
abstract: Theta-solvent-driven self-assembly of poly(methyl methacrylate) enables selective and stable dispersion of single-walled carbon nanotubes.

title: Graphene or MoS 2 nanopores: pore adhesion and protein linearization
abstract: Graphene nanopores in a GdmCl solution can linearize proteins and provide better-resolved signals, while MoS 2 nanopores in pure KCl offer slower translocation dynamics and less effective protein linearization.

title: Elastomer-assisted graphene transfer technique for enhanced resonance characteristics in chemisorption-based graphene resonant mass sensors
abstract: We develop a graphene-based resonant sensor to enhance the resonant properties by applied strain. An aptamer-modified sensor exhibited a frequency shift in response to inactivated SARS-CoV-2 virus, demonstrating a mass sensitivity of 0.99 zg Hz −1 .

title: Microwave-assisted synthesis of ZnO/BiNbO 4 heterojunctions for enhanced hydrogen production
abstract: Heterojunction based on ZnO and BiNbO 4 boosts photocurrents on OER and enhances H 2 production offering a fast, energy-efficient solution for photo(electro)catalytic applications.

title: Bismuth oxycarbonates loaded on nitrogen-doped carbon: an efficient nanocomposite catalyst for electrochemical reduction of CO 2 to formate
abstract: Flake-structured Bi 2 O 2 CO 3 -NC composite nanomaterials can efficiently and stably electrocatalyze the greenhouse gas CO 2 reduction to formate.

title: Effect of γ-irradiation on hexagonal boron nitride membranes and printed films
abstract: Printed films and membranes made with water-based hBN inks are able to retain their structure without degrading upon γ-ray exposure, making them very attractive for aerospace and nuclear applications.

title: Comprehensive determination of highly symmetric transition metal dichalcogenide multilayers
abstract: We extend JAM to four TMDC types (1H, 1T, Janus 1H, and Janus 1T). Using MoSSe bilayers, we analyze stability, band gaps, and interlayer interactions, advancing 2D materials research.

title: Thiolated molybdenum diselenide quantum dots as a bifunctional catalyst towards the synthesis of benzimidazoles
abstract: The effect of surface chemistry modulation on the catalytic output of heterogeneous platforms has been demonstrated with 3-mercaptopropionic acid functionalized MoSe 2 QDs as a bifunctional catalyst for the synthesis of several benzimidazoles.

title: Catalyst-assisted growth of CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanowires
abstract: The formation of CsPbBr 3 NW via the solution–solid–solid. At elevated temperatures, the ME catalyst undergoes a phase transition, facilitating the diffusion of reactants and the subsequent precipitation of solid CsPbBr 3 nanowires.

title: Two-dimensional single-crystal periodic macroporous ZnO sheets for extraordinary photocatalytic performance for antibiotics
abstract: 2D single-crystal periodic macroporous structure of ZnO effectively accelerates carrier migration.

title: Efficient green emission of circularly polarized luminescence from chiral Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites stabilized in mesoporous silica with alloyed spacer cations
abstract: The combined mesoporous confinement and dual-cation engineering establish a universal strategy to develop CPL-active perovskites with tunable emission, near-unity PLQY, high FM, and excellent stability.

title: Programming a multiplex lanthanide nanoparticle for customized cancer treatment with real-time efficiency feedback
abstract: Customized cancer therapy relies on timely therapeutic effect evaluation to provide prescription adjustment for individual cases.

title: Near-infrared imaging of intraperitoneal ovarian cancer enhanced by BrCy112 in a dual mode (MR and NIR), dual-Gd nanoparticle
abstract: Dual-Mode-Dual-Gd (DMDG) nanoparticles incorporating BrCy112 or ICG.

title: Granular memristors with tunable stochasticity
abstract: This study explores dynamics of silver-based memristors under steadybias, revealing current fluctuations with power-law distribution. This is analyzed using Marcus-Tang theory. This has implications for encryption and computing.

title: TMN (TM = V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co) monolayers – a new class of non-van der Waals 2D magnets
abstract: We have systematically examined various parameters of t- (tetragonal) and h- (hexagonal) lattices of transition metal nitride (TMN) monolayers through first-principles calculations, emphasising their structural and magnetic properties.

title: Porous organic cages for pollution removal and analysis
abstract: This review highlights the design and modification of porous organic cages to meet the requirements of pollution removal and analysis and their applications in the adsorption, separation and sensing of contaminants. Images reproduced with permission, full details in article.

title: Inter-helical excitonic coupling dye assemblies templated with anti-parallel and parallel DNA motifs
abstract: Interhelical exciton coupling between cyanine dye molecules was achieved by DNA motifs such as DNA antiparallel, parallel, and paranemic crossover. Photonic networks for constructing artificial light harvesting systems can be imagined in the future.

title: Phase-dependent electronic structure modulation of nickel selenides by Fe doping for enhanced bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: Different phases of nickel selenides are synthesized from a single coordination polymer precursor by tuning the electronic structure for bifunctional electrocatalytic OER and ORR in alkaline media.

title: Mid-level data fusion of pleural effusion SERS spectra and serum CEA levels using machine learning algorithms for precise lung cancer detection
abstract: We have developed an innovative mid-level data fusion technology that combines pleural effusion SERS spectra and serum CEA levels with machine learning algorithms to achieve accurate lung cancer detection.

title: Optimized plasmonic enhancement and deformation reduction in MoS 2 monolayers using Au-nanowire-embedded polymers
abstract: Embedding Au nanowires in polymers enhances photoluminescence and suppresses strain in MoS 2 , enabling clear decoupling of mechanical and plasmonic effects in MoS 2 -metal nanostructures.

title: Enhancing chemical signal transformation in lateral flow assays using aptamer-architectured plasmonic nanozymes and para-phenylenediamine
abstract: Enhancing chemical signals in lateral flow assays using aptamer-architectured plasmonic gold nanoparticles and para-phenylenediamine enables digital, quantitative detection of COVID-19 spike protein with improved sensitivity and specificity.

title: Multifunctional and stimuli-responsive ionic liquid-polymeric hydrogel: a promising platform for co-drug delivery in cancer treatment
abstract: Designing intelligent drug delivery systems that offer high drug loading, site-specific release, and improved therapeutic outcomes is crucial to overcome the limitations of conventional cancer treatments.

title: Stepwise kinetics of the early-stage nucleation in chiral perovskites via ab initio molecular dynamics and free-energy calculations
abstract: This study examines the early-stage nucleation of chiral perovskites, uncovering a stepwise kinetic mechanism across timescales from picoseconds to microseconds.

title: Cutting edge(s): towards realistic modelling of MXene flakes
abstract: Understanding the properties of realistically shaped Ti 2 C MXene flakes, constructed via the Wulff construction method to reflect experimental morphologies, using all electron DFT-based calculations.

title: Photogenerated charge carrier dynamics on Pt-loaded SrTiO 3 nanoparticles studied via transient-absorption spectroscopy
abstract: Charge carrier recombination on a Pt cocatalyst was demonstrated using pico–nano and nano–micro second transient absorption spectroscopy.

title: An injectable hydrogel with photothermal and chemodynamic therapies for targeted promotion of ferroptosis in oral squamous cell carcinoma
abstract: Ferroptosis-driven tumor ablation strategies based on nanotechnology could be achieved by increasing hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) concentrations or decreasing glutathione (GSH) levels.

title: Porous carbon-supported PtPdCu alloy heterostructures with a three-dimensional spatial network for efficient ethanol electro-oxidation
abstract: Porous carbon-supported PtPdCu ternary alloy heterostructures derived from MOFs exhibit robust EOR performance.

title: Non-conjugated alkyl chain engineering to tune condensed state photophysical and supramolecular assembly properties
abstract: A simple yet promising synthetic tweak in alkyl chain engineering has been unveiled to fine-tune the supramolecular self-assembly and condensed state photophysical properties of planar naphthalimide cores.

title: NiFeMo layered triple hydroxide and MXene heterostructure for boosted oxygen evolution reaction in anion exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: A heterointerface between NiFeMo-LTH and Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene created to achieve superior OER activity showing outstanding performance in AEMWE system with energy efficiency of 60.5% alongside great stability in alkaline seawater.

title: Synergistic enhancement in energy storage in lithium/sodium ion batteries using engineered composites of MXenes and carbon nanohorns
abstract: A schematic procedure for the synthesis of Sf-MXene/CNH composites and their energy storage performance are discussed.

title: Nano-spherical tip-based smoothing with minimal damage for 2D van der Waals heterostructures
abstract: Two-dimensional materials and their heterostructures have significant potential for future developments in materials science and optoelectronics due to their unique properties.

title: Expression of concern: Anti-aggregation effect of carbon quantum dots on diabetogenic and beta-cell cytotoxic amylin and beta amyloid heterocomplexes
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Anti-aggregation effect of carbon quantum dots on diabetogenic and beta-cell cytotoxic amylin and beta amyloid heterocomplexes’ by Anna Voronova et al. , Nanoscale , 2022, 14 , 14683–14694, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2NR03173F.

title: Molecular coordination inheritance of single Co atom catalysts for two-electron oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: We present the molecular coordination inheritance strategy for synthesizing 2e-ORR SACs with well-defined coordination environments and reveal the formation mechanism of the active sites. The Co–N 2 O 2 catalyst shows the highest 2e-ORR selectivity.

title: Emergence of material-driven two-dimensional electron gas by thermodynamically robust layers in Al 2 O 3 /In 2 O 3 /Al 2 O 3 nanolaminate structures
abstract: 2D electron gas (2DEG) was formed at Al 2 O 3 (AO)/In 2 O 3 (IO) and IO/AO interfaces. Conventional reductive-precursor-driven 2DEG was generated at AO/IO, while material-driven 2DEG was developed at IO/AO, unaffected by fabrication process.

title: Material characterization methods for investigating charge storage processes in 2D and layered materials-based batteries and supercapacitors
abstract: An overview of the characterization techniques for probing charge storage mechanisms of 2D and thin-layered materials as electrodes in electrochemical energy storage systems.

title: Small-angle neutron scattering differentiates molecular-level structural models of nanoparticle interfaces
abstract: Small-angle neutron scattering can be utilized to distinguish structural models of nanoparticles with molecular- and Å-scale differences.

title: Rhodium–silver superatomic nanoclusters stabilized by diselenophosphate ligands
abstract: Selenium passivated coinage metal rich superatoms are significantly rare. Herein the first series of Rh Ag bimetallic NCs, [RhH x Ag 21− x {Se 2 P(O i Pr) 2 } 12 ] ( x = 0–2), prepared via ligand replacement are fully investigated.

title: Unravelling the nanoarchitectonics of –C–N– linkages in covalent organic frameworks for CO 2 capture—a mini-review
abstract: Overview of design strategies and adsorption interactions in stable covalent organic frameworks (COFs) featuring functionalized 3D Schiff base C–N linkages, emphasizing their effectiveness in CO 2 capture applications.

title: Combined effects of electrode morphology and electrolyte composition on single H 2 gas bubble detachment during hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Depending on the direction of the Marangoni effects, gas bubble locations and consequently the force balance determining gas bubble detachment can vary significantly.

title: Bulk and surface defect manipulation of the ZnO ETL for all-inorganic CsPbBr 3 perovskite solar cells
abstract: Alkali metal chloride KCl is introduced into sol–gel ZnO films through surface modification and doping. Finally, a champion efficiency of 9.22% is obtained, which is the highest PCE of a (FTO/ITO)/ZnO/CsPbBr 3 /carbon structure to date.

title: A metal–organic framework-derived α-MnS/MWCNT composite as a promising pseudocapacitive material for a flexible quasi-solid-state asymmetric supercapacitor device
abstract: A metal-organic framework-derived α-MnS/MWCNT composite possesses a higher specific surface area and a favorable pore size distribution which results in a higher specific capacitance.

title: Advances in Ga 2 O 3 -based memristor devices, modeling, properties, and applications for low power neuromorphic computing
abstract: Ga 2 O 3 -based memristor with low-power operation, fast switching via conductive filaments, and potential integration with neuromorphic systems mimicking the human visual pathway.

title: Effect of Cs vacancy on thermal conductivity in CsPbBr 3 perovskites unveiled by deep potential molecular dynamics
abstract: We utilize DP-GEN to train a complete DP model of CsPbBr 3 , aiming to investigate its thermal conductivity and explore the effect of Cs vacancy.

title: Advances and applications of dynamic surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) for single molecule studies
abstract: Dynamic SERS is a promising tool to capture in situ molecular fingerprints in complex chemical and biological systems, with continuously evolving sensitivity and temporal resolution.

title: Sodiophilic Au-diamane polypropylene separator enabled dendrite-free sodium metal batteries
abstract: An Au-diamane/PP separator is developed to enhance Na + ion diffusion and suppress dendrite growth for high performance sodium metal anodes, enabling over 2100 h of cylcing at 2 mA cm −2 with 1 mA h cm −2 .

title: Sustainable N/S co-doped porous carbon from waste lemon peels for high-performance zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Lemon peel-derived N/S co-doped porous carbon delivers high energy density and ultra-stable zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Boosting carrier separation over ultrathin g-C 3 N 4 by P doping for enhanced photocatalytic performance
abstract: Schematic illustration of the preparation of P-doped ultrathin g-C 3 N 4 .

title: Enhancing the pseudocapacitance of Ti 3 C 2 T x with its own hydroxyl-rich quantum dots for increased redox sites and fast ion transport
abstract: Hydroxyl-rich quantum dots enhance the Ti 3 C 2 T x pseudocapacitance by providing abundant redox-active sites and enabling rapid ion transport.

title: Multifunctional DNA nanomaterials: a new frontier in rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis and treatment
abstract: DNA nanomaterials offer promising advancements in addressing the challenges of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) through targeted drug delivery, biomarker detection, and therapeutic innovation.

title: Computational screening of M 2 N 4 -C-type dual-atom-catalysts for electrochemical ammonia synthesis by the first-principles DFT and machine learning
abstract: Electrochemical ammonia synthesis is expected to complement the conventional Haber–Bosch method due to its low carbon emissions and stable operation under ambient conditions.

title: An oxygen-scavenging system without impact on DNA mechanical properties in single-molecule fluorescence experiments
abstract: Using magnetic tweezers, we discovered that commonly used PCD and POC oxygen scavenging systems (OSSs) can alter DNA mechanical properties while BOD OSS does not. BOD OSS also exhibited superior performance in enhancing dye stability.

title: Advancing supercapacitors with graphene-based 2D–2D heterostructures and heteroatom engineering
abstract: Graphene-based two-dimensional heterostructures are gaining attention as promising solutions for supercapacitor applications.

title: Pure red emission with spectral stability in full iodine-based quasi-2D perovskite films by controlling phase distribution
abstract: We synthesized full iodine-based quasi-2D perovskite films with pure red emission and a narrow peak width by manipulating the phase distribution of the films.

title: Germanium nanocrystal non-volatile memory devices: fabrication, charge storage mechanism and characterization
abstract: The charge storage mechanism of Ge nanocrystals is associated with the reduction, by hydrogen species, of Si–O–Ge bonds, which are located at the outer surface of the Ge nanocrystals where the Ge atoms form bonds with the silicon oxide matrix.

title: Ultrasensitive non-enzymatic electrochemical detection of paraoxon-ethyl in fruit samples using 2D Ti 3 C 2 T x /MWCNT-OH
abstract: A highly sensitive non-enzymatic electrochemical sensor using a Ti 3 C 2 T x /MWCNT-OH nanocomposite detects paraoxon-ethyl in fruit with high selectivity, a 0.1–100 μM range, and a 10 nM LOD, offering a sustainable alternative for pesticide monitoring.

title: Advances in simulating dilute alloy nanoparticles for catalysis
abstract: Dilute and single-atom alloy catalysts have attracted tremendous attention in heterogeneous catalysis. Herein, we discuss recent progress on simulating their stability and catalytic behavior.

title: Theoretical design of 2D Pca 2 1 SiNOX (X = H, F, and Cl) phases: a new family of flexible wide bandgap semiconductors
abstract: Three 2D Pca 2 1 SiNOX (X = H, F, and Cl) phases were rationally designed by theoretical exfoliation of bulk α-LiSiON and their electronic, mechanical, and thermal conductivity properties were investigated by first-principles calculations.

title: A physics-informed machine learning perspective to present the structures and properties of titanium matrixes and nanoclusters through atomic modeling
abstract: A machine learning potential using an artificial neural network model was developed to describe atomic interactions in titanium bulks and nanoclusters, using atomic simulations to present their structures and mechanical properties.

title: Hydrodynamics inside brush decorated nano-confinements: an all-atom molecular dynamics study
abstract: All-atom molecular dynamics of polymer grafting nano-confinements reveal interplay of nanoscale features in the resulting hydrodynamics leading to applications in drug delivery, DNA translocation, and energy generation.

title: Ultrathin and high-performance electromagnetic wave absorbers enabled by phase-engineered FeSiAl@1T/2H MoS 2 interfaces
abstract: These FeSiAl@MoS 2 heterointerfaces can efficiently control polarization loss, balance impedance matching in magnetic materials, and boost electromagnetic wave absorption.

title: Synergistic effects of bimetallic CuNi–ZrO 2 catalysts in catalytic transfer hydrogenation of furfural
abstract: The electronic interaction between Cu and Ni facilitates the formation of more Cu 0 /Cu + active sites in the Cu 2 Ni 1 –ZrO 2 catalyst, which enables the catalyst achieve 88.9% FAL conversion with 93.6% FOL selectivity at 170 °C for 12 h in the CTH process.

title: Interwoven MnO/Co-derived N-doped carbon nanotube composites as highly efficient and durable bifunctional oxygen catalysts for zinc–air batteries
abstract: MnO/Co-N-CNT composites with interwoven carbon nanotube structures were designed, offering superior bifunctional catalytic activity and exceptional stability, making them highly efficient for next-generation zinc–air battery applications.

title: Amino-ended hyperbranched polyamide-cross-linked conducting polymer hydrogels with enhanced performance for wearable electronics
abstract: Amino-ended hyperbranched polyamide (HBPN) cross-links PVA and PEDOT:PSS to form a tough, stretchable conducting polymer hydrogel for strain sensors, enabling stable and sensitive motion monitoring.

title: A non-enzymatic dual sensing approach for the detection of cholesterol in real samples using silk fiber functionalized phosphorene quantum dots
abstract: Reliable point-of-care (POC) detection of the specific biomarkers responsible for different diseases is crucial for health monitoring.

title: Boron-containing electron transport materials based on naphthalene diimide for organic solar cells: a theoretical study
abstract: This study proposed NDIN-based electron transport materials (ETMs) by replacing nitrogen with boron, offering insights into donor/acceptor/ETM interfaces and highlighting boron substitution as an effective way to tune organic solar cell properties.

title: Tailoring the photoluminescence of AIE-type gold nanoclusters via biomineralization-inspired polymorphism
abstract: By mimicking biomineralization, this study introduces a facile approach to incorporate Au 22 SG 18 nanoclusters within different polymorphs of CaCO 3 and reveal that variations in the nanocluster photoluminescence are linked to the polymorph stability.

title: Self-assembled colloidal glass with 100% lanthanide nanocrystal loading for high-resolution X-ray imaging
abstract: A new class of bright, transparent colloidal glasses with 100% nanoparticle loading was developed via self-assembly, addressing the longstanding trade-offs between optical transparency and scintillation efficiency in X-ray imaging technology.

title: Quantum dot antennas for DTE-BODIPY dyads: efficient “on–off” photoluminescence switching with a broad excitation spectrum
abstract: The fluorescence of the BODIPY chromophore can be photomodulated by the thermally stable DTE photoswitch. The efficiency of this photomodulation is increased by the attachment of this dyad to a quantum dot through a cascade of FRET processes.

title: Room-temperature Pauli spin blockade and current rectification in 15-13-15 armchair graphene nanoribbon heterostructures
abstract: In this study, we investigate the electronic structures of 13-11-13 and 15-13-15 armchair graphene nanoribbon (AGNR) superlattices (SLs) using a tight-binding model.

title: A rapid technique for approximating the zeta potential of metal oxide nanoparticles based on pH measurement and machine learning nano-QSPR models
abstract: It is essential to adopt a systematic approach for assessing the risk associated with the deposition and dispersion of engineered nanomaterials (ENMs) in the environment.

title: Type-dependent effects of nanoplastics on microglial activation and CXCR2-mediated chemotactic migration
abstract: Nanoplastics from different polymers distinctly modulate microglial activation. PMMANPs, compared to PSNPs and PPNPs, most strongly enhance CXCL1/2–CXCR2 signaling, leading to the highest immune cell migration in the brain.

title: Dislocation-enabled plasticity in rutile TiO 2− x at room temperature
abstract: (a) Bright-field cross-section TEM micrograph showing the indentation-induced dislocations. (b) High-resolution TEM micrograph showing the (101) and (1̄01) stacking faults. (c) Weak-beam dark-field TEM micrograph showing dislocations.

title: Orientation dependence of current blockade in single amino acid translocation through a graphene nanopore
abstract: Molecular dynamics shows that single amino acids can be distinguished via translocation through a graphene nanopore. We probe the effect of their orientation on the current blockade.

title: Laser-initiated site-selective formation of fluorescing silver–iron oxide nanocomposites for electron detection
abstract: Laser-initiated silver–iron oxide emits unique red fluorescence whose intensity increases 10× upon electron interaction, enabling a rapid optical response to electron detection.

title: Sustainable interfacial solar steam generation with a biochar–alginate bilayer for RO brine treatment
abstract: Interfacial solar steam generation for brine treatment using advanced bi-layer biochar-alginate foam.

title: A molecular strategy for creating functional vesicles with balancing structural stability and stimuli-responsiveness
abstract: Amphiphiles with amide linkages formed stable vesicles, surviving against osmotic shock. When amphiphiles with azobenzene moiety were added, the vesicles exhibited large deformation. The finding contributes to the development of functional vesicles.

title: Low-dimensional AgTePh disordered aggregation versus directed coalescence for shortwave-IR to visible semiconductors
abstract: We report a rapid, one-pot synthesis of 2D AgTePh crystals from 1D nanowire intermediates. The route enables structural control and reveals how aggregation versus coalescence governs photophysics from the SWIR to visible region.

title: Hollow porous PtFe nanospheres prepared by a cyanogel-derived strategy for formic acid oxidation
abstract: We present a facile cyanogel-derived synthetic strategy to fabricate hollow porous PtFe nanospheres with enhanced dehydrogenation pathway and superior electrocatalytic performance towards formic acid oxidation reduction.

title: Cationic amino acid-engineered peptide hydrogels for sustained and potent antigen delivery enabling single-administration vaccination
abstract: Cationic peptide hydrogels have attracted considerable interest as vaccine delivery systems.

title: Small-molecule engineering of a PbS QD/ZnO nanowire interface and its impact on infrared PbS QD solar cell performance
abstract: Small molecules with different functional groups have been shown to modulate the interfacial properties of PbS quantum dot/ZnO nanowire heterojunctions, thereby influencing the performance of infrared solar cells.

title: Energetic MOF-derived Fe 3 C nanoparticles encased in N,S-codoped mesoporous pod-like carbon nanotubes for efficient oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: A unique hybrid electrocatalyst consisting of Fe 3 C NPs and N, S-codoped mesoporous carbon nanotubes was manufactured by pyrolysis of a new energetic metal-organic framework, demonstrating excellent activity for oxygen reduction reaction.

title: Homogenization enabled efficient regeneration of spent Ni-rich LiNi x Co y Mn 1− x − y O 2 cathodes
abstract: This work employs ball-milling for size homogenization to regenerate cracked Ni-rich NCM cathodes. Nano-sized NCM lowers the Li + diffusion barrier, enhancing relithiation efficiency over polycrystalline materials.

title: Defect-assisted surface modification of a g-C 3 N 4 @WC heterostructure for tetracycline degradation: DFT calculations, degradation pathways, and nematode-based ecological assessment
abstract: A carbon vacancy enriched gC 3 N 4 @WC heterostructure was prepared as a visible light photocatalyst for the effective removal of TC pollutants.

title: Invariant optical properties of dielectric nanofins in geometric phase metasurfaces
abstract: Elliptical and rectangular dielectric nanofins with identical cross-sectional area and aspect ratio exhibit the same optical response in geometric phase modulation.

title: Preparation of bovine serum albumin nanospheres via desolvation: a study of synthesis, characterization, and aging
abstract: Bovine serum albumin nanoparticles synthesized via a modified desolvation method exhibit superior long-term stability in aqueous dispersion. Glucose investigated as a “green” alternative crosslinker.

title: Nanoparticle-based drug delivery systems: opportunities and challenges in the treatment of esophageal squamous cell carcinoma (ESCC)
abstract: Nanoparticles boost ESCC treatment efficacy by enhancing drug targeting and overcoming biological barriers, despite biocompatibility and scalability challenges.

title: Titanium diboride-derived nanosheets enhance the mechanical properties of polyurethane: experiments and simulation
abstract: Enhancing the mechanical properties of polyurethane by adding titanium diboride-based nanosheets.

title: Unveiling surface dynamics: in situ oxidation of defective WS 2
abstract: Defects in WS 2 accelerate oxidation, affecting material properties. Oxidation of defective WS 2 is slow at low O 2 pressure but rapid at high O 2 pressure, as tracked by XPS. DFT shows O 2 dissociates at S vacancies, with a barrier dependent on W coordination.

title: Magnetoresistance effect based on spin-selective transport in nanodevices using chiral molecules
abstract: We have successfully observed the chirality-induced spin selectivity (CISS)-based magnetoresistance (MR) effect in Au/ N -(3 S )-3,7-dimethyloctyl[1]benzothieno[3,2- b ]benzothiophene-2-carboxyamide ( S -BTBT-CONHR)/Ni 78 Fe 22 nanodevices at room temperature.

title: Recent progress in gemcitabine-loaded nanoparticles for pancreatic cancer therapy: a review
abstract: Gemcitabine-based nanodelivery strategies for pancreatic cancer include efficient carriers, combination therapies, and prodrug systems to enhance stability, targeting, and efficacy.

title: Materials, mechanisms, and emerging applications of electrochromic systems
abstract: Low-dimensional electrochromic materials emerge as game-changers, revealing how strategic nanostructuring and composites influence optical switching for enhanced performance, functionality, and potential for scalable smart technologies.

title: A parametric study on CoFe-based ferrite and alloy nanoparticle synthesis
abstract: Exploring a wide range of synthetic parameters leads to the controlled chemical synthesis of CoFe-based ferrite and alloy nanostructures with varying compositions, morphologies and attractive properties.

title: Tough, durable and strongly bonded self-healing cartilage-mimicking noncovalently assembled hydrogel nanostructures: the interplay between experiment and theory
abstract: The PAM-Ag-BN hydrogel showed high strength, improved stability in SBF, perfect bioactivity and strong bonding, making it a prime implant for cartilage repair applications.

title: Landé g -factors of electrons and holes strongly confined in CsPbI 3 perovskite nanocrystals in glass
abstract: The Landé g -factor of charge carriers is a key parameter in spin physics controlling spin polarization and spin dynamics.

title: Cuticular proteins (crusticuls) affect 3D chitin bundle nanostructure
abstract: Crusticul proteins knockdown altered the 3D nanostructure of skeletal chitin fibers assembly into layered bundles, affecting cuticle hardness and elasticity.

title: Unveiling nanoparticle–immune interactions: how super-resolution imaging illuminates the invisible
abstract: This mini-review focuses on how super-resolution techniques uncover the detailed interactions between nanoparticles and immune cells.

title: Zr-MOF composites with zipped and unzipped carbon nanotubes for high-performance electrochemical supercapacitors
abstract: The composites of zirconium-based MOFs with graphene oxide nanoribbons and functionalized carbon nanotubes (FCNTs) were investigated and showed remarkable supercapacitance performance, offering a compelling approach for energy storage applications.

title: An atomic surface of an aluminum alloy induced by novel green chemical mechanical polishing using hybrid rare earth abrasives and mechanisms unraveled
abstract: A novel green chemical mechanical polishing was developed for an aluminum alloy, and the slurry consisted of hydrogen peroxide, tyrosine, sodium carbonate, and hybrid abrasives composed of silica, yttria, and ceria.

title: Two-dimensional ferroelectric synaptic devices enabled by diverse coupling mechanisms
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) van der Waals (vdW) ferroelectric (FE) materials have recently emerged as promising candidates for advanced synaptic devices in brain-inspired neuromorphic computing systems.

title: Optoelectronic MXene quantum dots: frontiers in sensor technology for next-generation diagnostics and environmental monitoring
abstract: MXene QDs are illustrated, showcasing their synthesis, properties, and emerging applications in next-generation diagnostics and environmental monitoring.

title: Exploring piezoelectric and piezophototronic properties of nanostructured LN-ZnSnS 3 for photoresponsive vibrational energy harvesting
abstract: Piezoelectric and piezophototronic properties are reported from solution-processed LN-type ZnSnS 3 nanocrystals for light-induced self-powered mechanical energy harvesting.

title: Plasmonic heating by indium tin oxide nanoparticles: spectrally enabling decoupled near-infrared theranostics
abstract: Indium tin oxide nanoparticles are effective near-infrared photothermal agents. Their spectral properties make them ideal moieties to build up optically decoupled theranostic systems together with state-of-the-art luminescent nanoparticles.

title: A linearly polarized AC-driven perovskite light emitting device with nanoscale metal contact
abstract: This paper reveals an AC-driven perovskite light-emitting device with a large emission area and strong linear polarization by integrating nanostructures within the device contact.

title: Blister test to measure the out-of-plane shear modulus of few-layer graphene
abstract: We measure the out-of-plane shear modulus of few-layer graphene (FLG) by a blister test.

title: Contactless health monitoring in autonomous self-reporting ceramic coatings
abstract: Proof of concept for contactless tracking of phase transitions and grain growth in self-reporting ceramic Cr–Al–B coatings by contactless resistivity measurements. The results are verified by state-of-the-art in situ and ex situ methods.

title: Optimized loading of Pt single atoms from platinic acid solution and the sweet spot for activity
abstract: pH-controlled speciation of H 2 PtCl 6 enables anchoring of Pt 2+ single atoms on TiO 2 , emphasizing the role of precursor hydrolysis and Pt–O–Ti bonding in photocatalytic H 2 generation.

title: Oxygen vacancies induced strong metal–support interactions of Ru@CoMoO 4 –O v for significantly enhanced oxygen evolution reaction performance in alkaline seawater electrolytes
abstract: Introducing oxygen vacancies can enhance the interaction between Ru and CoMoO 4 and improve the adsorption capacity of oxygen-containing intermediates, thereby boosting the OER performance of Ru@CoMoO 4 –O v /NF.

title: MoS 2 -nanoparticle-blended PAN composite membrane for enhanced removal of Pb–EDTA via H 2 O 2 activation: synergistic decomplexation and adsorption
abstract: This work develops a stable 1T-MoS 2 /PAN (TMP) membrane to construct a TMP/H 2 O 2 system that achieves efficient Pb–EDTA removal via synergistic decomplexation–adsorption, maintaining effluent Pb concentrations below 0.1 mg L −1 in dynamic filtration.

title: Electronic fingerprints of confined and adsorbed water in SWCNTs
abstract: Electronic fingerprints of water in SWCNT transistors reveal three adsorption sites—chemisorbed, physisorbed, and confined—with doping as the main charge transfer mechanism, independent of CNT metallicity.

title: Designing highly tunable nanostructured peptide hydrogels using differential thermal histories to achieve variable cellular responses
abstract: Exploring the pathway-dependent self-assembly of molecules to access diverse supramolecular nanostructures within a single gelator regime by simply varying the thermal histories, thereby invoking different cellular behaviours.

title: Zinc complex-based multifunctional binders for lithium sulfide-based lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A novel Zn complex/PEI binder enhances Li 2 S cathode stability and redox kinetics via polysulfide trapping and catalytic activation.

title: Self-powered, broadband Ta 2 NiSe 5 /p-GaAs van der Waals heterojunction photodetector with high polarization sensitivity
abstract: A self-powered Ta 2 NiSe 5 /p-GaAs van der Waals heterojunction photodetector demonstrates broadband detection up to 1310 nm, high responsivity, and strong polarization sensitivity.

title: Correction: Controllable synthesis of hollow mesoporous organosilica nanoparticles with pyridine-2,6-bis-imidazolium frameworks for CO 2 conversion
abstract: Correction for ‘Controllable synthesis of hollow mesoporous organosilica nanoparticles with pyridine-2,6-bis-imidazolium frameworks for CO 2 conversion’ by Ghazale Anvarian-Asl et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 16977–16989, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR02144D.

title: Advances in perovskite-based neuromorphic computing devices
abstract: This review summarizes the latest research progress on neural morphology computing devices based on perovskite materials with different structures and summarizes different application scenarios.

title: Introduction to nanomedicines for crossing biological barriers
abstract: Lucie Sancey, Ariane Boudier, Elise Lepeltier, Marie-Pierre Rols, Jeanne Leblond Chain and Nguyên T. K. Thanh introduce this Nanoscale themed collection on nanomedicines for crossing biological barriers which aligns with the themes of the French Society of Nanomedicine.

title: An evaluation of the size-dependent electrocatalytic activity of single IrO 2 nanoparticles via nano-impact electrochemistry
abstract: This study employs nano-impact electrochemistry (NIE) to investigate the size-dependent electrocatalytic activity of single IrO 2 nanoparticles, leveraging the technique's high mass transport and in situ analysis capabilities.

title: Nanoparticle-protein corona enhances accuracy of Ca-19.9-based pancreatic cancer classification
abstract: The integration of nanotechnology with traditional biomarker analysis offers a novel and promising pathway to enhance the accuracy and early detection of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

title: Gradient CNT/PMN-PT/PVDF piezoelectric composites for gait monitoring during weight-bearing walking
abstract: A gradient-structured CNT/PMN-PT/PVDF wearable sensor enables gait monitoring under load conditions. The dual-filler gradient design enhances piezoelectric output, achieving high sensitivity and stability for continuous biomechanical sensing.

title: A carbon nanotube wrapped Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 cathode with a dual cation electrolyte: enhancing high-voltage stability and cyclability in zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A carbon nanotube wrapped NVPF cathode, integrated with a highly concentrated dual cation electrolyte, enhances the electrochemical performance of aqueous rechargeable zinc-ion batteries by enabling a synergistic Na + and Zn 2+ co-insertion mechanism.

title: Ab initio calculations of vibrational fingerprints in the photoluminescence of graphene quantum dots
abstract: We used a computational method based on ab initio (constrained) density functional theory to obtain the photoluminescence spectrum of graphene quantum dots with up to 240 carbon atoms, including the effect of multiphonons.

title: Exploring dual-iron atomic catalysts for efficient nitrogen reduction: a comprehensive study on structural and electronic optimization
abstract: A dual-Fe catalyst embedded in B/N co-doped graphene (Fe 2 N 3 B@G) is theoretically designed, showing enhanced N 2 activation and stability for an efficient nitrogen reduction reaction.

title: Solvent stabilization mechanisms and deactivation pathways of the inert stereo -configuration in iridium carbonyl iodide complexes (Ir(CO) 2 I 2 − )
abstract: A novel stereo -Ir(CO) 2 I 2 − configuration accumulates via solvent-triggered stabilization, causing catalyst deactivation.

title: Atomically precise bismuth oxido nanoclusters: cerium doping for optical modification and supramolecular self-assembly on Au(111)
abstract: It is demonstrated how doping and chiral modification influence the optical properties and self-assembly of atomically precise metal oxido nanoclusters.

title: Multi-parameter magnetic resonance imaging based on NaGdF 4 nanoprobes for diagnosis of intraperitoneal tumors
abstract: A NaGdF 4 -based nanoparticle capable of simultaneously enabling sensitive T 1 - and T 2 -weighted imaging has been developed for tumor diagnosis. (Created in BioRender.Ma.Y. (2025) https://BioRendercom/1avlpyx)

title: Formamidinium-based Pb–Sn mixed-halide perovskite solar cells with near-optimal bandgap: FAPb 1− x Sn x (I 0.8 Br 0.2 ) 3
abstract: FA-based Pb–Sn binary perovskites with I–Br blend were developed via compositional engineering, demonstrating bandgaps close to the ideal range for single-junction solar cells.

title: Synergizing superwetting and architected electrodes for high-rate water splitting
abstract: Review of recent advancements in tackling bubble-related issues through the design and implementation of superwetting electrodes, including surface modification techniques and structural optimizations.

title: Catalytic surface controls hot-carriers in hybrid plasmonic nanostructures
abstract: Catalytic Pd/Pt surface layers on Al nanobricks precisely tune hot-carrier generation and transfer to adsorbed pyrazine, enabling controlled plasmon-driven photocatalysis at the nanoscale.

title: Non-precious metal high-entropy alloys for CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: A low-cost non-precious CuCoAlFeNi high-entropy alloy (HEA) for effective CO 2 reduction reaction was prepared using a simple solvothermal method. The CuCoAlFeNi HEA showed a Faraday efficiency of 36.56% towards CH 3 CH 2 OH in an H-type cell.

title: Boosting peroxidase-like activity of single-atom nanozymes via sulfur-doped Fe–N–C sites for rapid rhodamine B degradation at neutral pH
abstract: FeNC–SO x SAzyme, boosted by S-doping, generates ˙OH at pH 6.5 to degrade >96% RhB in 15 min. Operates in real waters for alkaline wastewater remediation.

title: Negative differential resistance in memristive systems: historical evolution, mechanisms and neuromorphic applications of niobium oxide devices
abstract: Elucidates diverse NDR mechanisms, modulation strategies, and historical evolution. Comprehensive review of NDR and its application in niobium oxide systems. Offers insights into NDR-memristor synergy for neuromorphic and low-power circuits.

title: Additive electronics manufacturing via droplet jetting technologies: materials, methods, applications, and opportunities
abstract: An overview of nanomaterials ink for precision droplet-based printed electronics highlights broad challenges and opportunities in emerging application areas.

title: MOF-derived CoO x /MgO dual modification boosting hematite photoelectrocatalysis
abstract: The MgO interlayer facilitates hole transport while enhancing visible-light absorption, and the MOF-derived CoO x simultaneously provides abundant catalytic sites and optimizes surface states.

title: Engineering hot carrier dynamics in Ti 2 CO 2 MXene via methane adsorption
abstract: Adsorption of CH 4 on Ti 2 CO 2 MXene induces local orbital hybridization that leads to red-shifted absorption spectra and modified hot-carrier distributions, with effects persisting from single-molecule to high coverage.

title: Rational synthesis of carbon-rich hollow carbon nitride spheres for photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production and Cr( vi ) reduction
abstract: Hollow carbon nitride spheres with a well-designed architecture and excellent optical properties serve as promising polymers for high-value chemical production and heavy metal treatment.

title: Stability and phase transition of polar topological defects in multiferroic superlattices
abstract: Phase-field simulations are performed to elucidate the stability phase diagrams of multiferroic BiFeO 3 /SrTiO 3 superlattices under varying thickness, strain, and temperature conditions.

title: Novel photocatalytic SrTiO 3 @TiO 2 /SrSO 4 heterojunction and its high performance in RhB degradation
abstract: A one-step melting method constructs porous SrTiO 3 @TiO 2 /SrSO 4 heterojunction. With enhanced light harvesting and charge separation, ZS-STO-550 achieves 98.90% RhB degradation in 24 min ( k = 0.292 min −1 ) and 92.53% stability after 5 cycles.

title: Tuning electronic and optical properties of 2D polymeric C 60 by stacking two layers
abstract: Our study provides strategies to tune electronic and optical properties of 2D polymeric C 60 via the introduction of stacking degrees of freedom.

title: Phonon thermal transport in Bi 2 Te 3 /Sb 2 Te 3 monolayer superlattices: a neural network potential study
abstract: The decrease of thermal conductivity in coherent short-period Bi 2 Te 3 /Sb 2 Te 3 monolayer superlattices is attributed to the variation of phonon scattering behaviors, rather than the decrease in phonon group velocity, which is contrary to expectations.

title: Rapid and sustained growth of carbon nanotube forests through coupled support design and catalyst particle formation dynamics
abstract: This study breaks the long-standing trade-off between catalyst lifetime and carbon nanotube growth rate by combining support design with control over the catalyst formation process.

title: A sustainable synthesis of mesoporous Mn 1− x Ni x Co 2 O 4 nanoparticles: exploring calcination effects on supercapacitor performance
abstract: Uncalcined Mn 0.9 Ni 0.1 Co 2 O 4 nanoparticles synthesized via a calcination-free route deliver high energy density, long cycle life, and ∼100% coulombic efficiency, offering a sustainable solution for next-generation supercapacitors.

title: Microwave-assisted growth of Ag nanoparticles on Ti 3 CNT x MXene for antibacterial food packaging
abstract: Graphical representation for the synthesis of Ag/Ti 3 CNT x and its antibacterial activity.

title: Densely distributed Co onto carbon-layer-coated flower-like Ni/Al 2 O 3 and its tailored integration into a stirrer for multiple catalytic degradation and solar-powered water evaporation
abstract: Multiple functional tailored materials have shown great potential for both pollutant degradation and freshwater recovery.

title: Challenges and strategies for first-principles simulations of two-dimensional magnetic phenomena
abstract: This review introduces key concepts and tools for modelling 2D magnets with DFT, covering magnetism basics, challenges like spin order, and methods to extract Hamiltonian parameters and critical temperatures for various anisotropies.

title: An efficient hydrogen evolution catalyst constructed using Pt-modified Ni 3 S 2 /MoS 2 with optimized kinetics across the full pH range
abstract: Pt-decorated Ni 3 S 2 /MoS 2 nanorod arrays boost HER activity by optimizing electronic structure, favoring proton adsorption, and enabling fast electron transport. This design lowers overpotential with excellent stability in acidic and alkaline media.

title: Enantiomorphic single component conducting nickel( ii ) and platinum( ii ) bis(diethyl-dddt) crystalline complexes
abstract: Enantiopure and racemic neutral Ni( ii ) and Pt( ii ) bis(dithiolene) complexes, based on the de-dddt ligand and prepared by electrocrystallization of the anionic precursors, crystallize in enantiomorphic space groups and are band gap semiconductors.

title: Structural and electronic transition of layered PtSe 2 into non-layered PtSe
abstract: Metallic PtSe is obtained from thermal conversion of PtSe 2 grown by chemical vapor deposition, highlighting the significance of sub-stoichiometric phases within transition metal dichalcogenide films.

title: Correction: Nano-spherical tip-based smoothing with minimal damage for 2D van der Waals heterostructures
abstract: Correction for ‘Nano-spherical tip-based smoothing with minimal damage for 2D van der Waals heterostructures’ by Xiaolei Ding et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 3095–3104, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR03583F.

title: Correction: Membrane-localized magnetic hyperthermia promotes intracellular delivery of cell-impermeant probes
abstract: Correction for ‘Membrane-localized magnetic hyperthermia promotes intracellular delivery of cell-impermeant probes’ by Javier Idiago-López et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 15176–15195, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR01955E.

title: In vivo vectorization and delivery systems for gene therapies and RNA-based therapeutics in oncology
abstract: Payload delivery systems for DNA- and RNA-based therapies are advancing with viral and non-viral vectors, including lipidic and polymeric nanoparticles. This work highlights their role in oncology, innovations, and use in clinical trials.

title: Strain and doping transfer between suspended and supported bilayer graphene
abstract: The effect of high pressure on suspended and supported bilayer graphene was probed via Raman spectroscopy, revealing highly efficient strain and doping transfer throughout the graphene.

title: Radiofrequency-triggered release of therapeutics from graphene oxide-loaded polysaccharide based core–shell microspheres
abstract: Graphene-loaded AcD/CMC core–shell microspheres exhibit RF-triggered drug release and release profiles showing enhanced payload delivery under RF stimulation.

title: Sulfur-modified charge-asymmetry FeNi nanoalloy catalysts anchored on N-doped carbon nanosheets for efficient electrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: The electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction reaction (CO 2 RR) plays a crucial role in mitigating global CO 2 emissions and achieving carbon neutrality.

title: Electron beam oxidation of silicon nitride membranes in liquid phase transmission electron microscopy
abstract: STEM-EELS investigation of silicon nitride oxidation in liquid-phase TEM. Exposure to the STEM electron beam in the presence of water induces oxidation of the silicon nitride membrane.

title: A ZIF-8-based dual-modal smart responsive nanoplatform for overcoming radiotherapy resistance in advanced tumors
abstract: ZIF-8-based dual-modality biomimetic smart nanoparticles loaded with ICG and rapamycin are used for the combination therapy of radiation sensitization and photothermal therapy of advanced tumors.

title: Enhanced detection of Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (BDNF) using a reduced graphene oxide field-effect transistor aptasensor
abstract: Neurodegenerative diseases, characterized by the progressive deterioration of neuronal function and structure, pose significant global public health and economic challenges.

title: Uniform coating of separated nanodiamonds via thin polymer-assisted electrostatic self-assembly for thermal sensing
abstract: A scalable method enables uniform coating of separated nanodiamonds, achieving low optical loss, reduced thermal spreading, and high precision for non-invasive thermal sensing.

title: Two dimensional-material-coated microcantilevers for enhanced mass sensing and material characterization
abstract: The integration of 2D material coatings on microcantilevers marks a transformative advancement in nanomechanical sensing. The graphical abstract was created with VESTA, data via Materials Project (Creative Commons CC BY 4.0 licence).

title: Graphene-based materials are not skin sensitizers: adoption of the in chemico / in vitro OECD test guidelines
abstract: Graphene-based materials skin sensitization property was evaluated through the adoptable OECD test guidelines: OECD TG 442D and E indicate that they are not skin sensitizers. Image created in BioRender. Pelin, M. (2025) https://BioRender.com/tsaolqr

title: Molecularly-anchored single PbS quantum dots as resonant tunnelling transistors
abstract: Ten negative differential resistances (NDRs) were demonstrated in the output characteristics of molecularly-anchored single PbS quantum dot resonant tunneling transistors, which utilized heteroepitaxial spherical Au/Pt nanogap electrodes.

title: Comparative study of the therapeutic potential of C 24 , C 32 , B 12 N 12 , and B 16 N 16 nanocages as drug delivery carriers for delivering an erlotinib derivative: DFT and QTAIM investigations
abstract: The use of nanostructures as drug delivery vehicles for various anticancer medications represents an innovative biomedical application, reducing severe side effects by enabling targeted delivery to tumor cells.

title: Exploring many-body phenomena: biexciton generation and auger recombination in Ag 2 S-based nanocrystals
abstract: Transient absorption spectroscopy of surface-passivated Ag 2 S nanocrystals reveals that while trapping and exciton recombination dominate at low exciton densities, biexcitons and Auger recombination become significant at higher exciton ones.

title: A Cu 0.76 Co 2.24 O 4 /γ-Cu 2 (OH) 3 Cl composite catalyst for efficient neutral nitrate reduction
abstract: High-valent Cu δ + and Co δ + in CCOC boost water dissociation and active hydrogen generation, enhancing the NO 3 − and intermediates adsorption for efficient, stable NH 3 production via nitrate electrocatalysis under neutral conditions.

title: The BeP 2 monolayer exhibits ultra-high and highly anisotropic carrier mobility and 29.3% photovoltaic efficiency
abstract: We predict that the hex-BeP 2 monolayer exhibits a high electron mobility on the order of 10 5 cm 2 V −1 s −1 , along with a remarkable photovoltaic efficiency of 29.3%.

title: Quantitative to qualitative transition: comparison of in vitro and in vivo behavior and glioma-targeted delivery of PEGylated lipid-based nanoparticles
abstract: Increasing the PEG ratio induces a transition from liposomes to nanodiscs to micelles. pV ligand-modified nanodiscs exhibit superior glioma targeting ability, showing advantages in multiple stages.

title: Chemically active colloidal superstructures
abstract: This review explores the self-propulsion mechanisms of chemically driven colloidal motors, the principles of their chemical communication, and strategies for the directed assembly of active colloidal superstructures.

title: Honeycomb-shaped microporous carbon-confined selenium as a Zn–Se battery cathode material
abstract: The Se@C material exhibits superior electrochemical performance.

title: Exfoliation of triazole-based C 3 N 4.8 , C 3 N 6 , and C 3 N 7 nanosheets for efficient photocatalytic ammonia production
abstract: Triazole-based C 3 N 7 nanosheets exhibit excellent photocatalytic ammonia production performance, attributed to their inherent properties of an optimised conduction band position and efficient charge separation.

title: Chirality of sub-nanometer nanowires/nanobelts
abstract: In this review, we summarize the origination, transfer and amplification of chirality in sub-nanometer nanowires/nanobelts (SNWs/SNBs).

title: A comprehensive study on Herba Epimedium -derived extracellular nanovesicles as a prospective therapy for alveolar bone regeneration in postmenopausal osteoporosis
abstract: EELNs were rich in metabolites and miRNAs with the therapeutic potential in osteoporosis. In vitro , EELNs enhanced osteogenesis targeting Pi3k/Akt/mTOR pathway. In vivo , EELN-loaded HA nano-whisker composite successfully repaired mandibular defect in osteoporosis rat.

title: Gadolinium oxide-decorated graphene oxide-based dual-stimuli-responsive smart fluids
abstract: Gadolinium oxide-based compounds are employed for the first time in the fabrication of dual-stimuli-responsive smart fluids that respond to both electric and magnetic fields.

title: Tuning of plasmonic surface lattice resonances: on the crucial impact of the excitation efficiency of grazing diffraction orders
abstract: Plasmonic nanoparticles exhibit remarkable optical properties through surface lattice resonances (SLR).

title: Optical temperature-sensitive hydrophobic membrane based on Eu( iii )-doped yttrium oxide nanosheets
abstract: An optical thermosensitive hydrophobic membrane modified with Eu( iii )-doped Y 2 O 3 nanosheets shows excellent thermal responsiveness (283–363 K) and high sensitivity (7.61% K −1 in air, 5.41% K −1 in water), ideal for flexible temperature monitoring.

title: Ultra-high absorption efficiency of InN nanowires with a wide bandwidth in the short-wave infrared range
abstract: The group III–V semiconductor InN is a highly promising candidate for photoelectric detectors in the short-wave infrared (SWIR) range but faces significant challenges in material quality.

title: Ferroelectric recovery of scaled-down Y-doped HfO 2 thin films on NiSi 2 after annealing
abstract: Ferroelectricity of Y-doped HfO 2 (YHO) thin films increases by low temperature annealing in Ar gas, highlighting the importance of controlling YHO/NiSi 2 interface properties and YHO crystallization.

title: Film thickness dependence of nanoscale arrangement of a chiral electron donor in its blends with an achiral electron acceptor
abstract: The active layer of solar cells in gradient films of a small molecule acceptor and a chiral donor were imaged using Mueller matrix polarimetry showing thickness dependence in supramolecular organization reflected in optoelectronic behaviour.

title: An infection microenvironment-responsive multifunctional nanocomposite for chronic wound treatment through redox homeostasis and macrophage regulation
abstract: The MXene/Cu 2- x O was constructed for wounds healing, which exhibited the capabilities of synergistic therapy of multiple treatment modalities and angiogenesis, and could eradicate bacteria by regulating the phenotype of macrophages.
