title: Dual-stimuli-responsive nanoparticles for the co-delivery of small molecules to promote neural differentiation of human iPSCs
abstract: The differentiation of human induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs) into neural progenitor cells (NPCs) is a promising approach for the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases and regenerative medicine.

title: Photothermoelectric effect driven self-powered broadband photodetection in 1T′-MoTe 2 with asymmetric electrodes
abstract: Asymmetric doping in the 1T’-MoTe 2 devices facilitated broadband fast photodetection, which is driven by the photothermoelectric effect dominating photoresponse under global illumination.

title: Efficient spatial self-phase modulation in the near-infrared and visible regimes of transition metal carbonitride Ti 3 CN
abstract: The hetero-MXene Ti 3 CN exhibits efficient spatial self-phase modulation in both near-infrared and visible regimes, with a large third-order nonlinear optical susceptibility (∼10 −9 e.s.u.).

title: Percolative phase transition in few-layered MoSe 2 field-effect transistors using Co and Cr contacts
abstract: The metal-to-insulator phase transition in 2D materials has revealed interesting electronic behavior and the need for a deeper understanding of electronic transport processes while attracting interest in developing next-generation electronic devices.

title: Recent advances in carbon nanotube composite films for electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Optimized CNT composite films deliver exceptional EMI shielding. By integrating conductivity and layered structures, these lightweight, flexible materials are promising for future electronics and aerospace solutions. Images reproduced with permission; full details in paper.

title: A flexible tactile sensor with decoupled multimodal sensing capacity based on melamine sponge-MXene@CsPbBr 3 aerogel
abstract: A flexible tactile sensor is assembled based on a melamine sponge-MXene@CsPbBr3 hybrid aerogel, realizing the multimodal response behaviors to pressure, temperature and ultraviolet without cross-coupling via different kinds of sensing mechanisms.

title: Harnessing light in tandem: advanced erbium-polyaniline QD composites for next-generation energy storage
abstract: Marked improvement in electrochemical performance of erbium ions with stable PANI quantum dots (8 nm).

title: Nanostructuring copper thin film electrodes for CO 2 electroreduction to C 2+ products
abstract: Anodization induced nanostructuring of copper surfaces facilitates CO 2 electroreduction to C 2+ products.

title: Silver nanoparticles with multimodal biological activities integrated into advanced material platforms for chronic wound management
abstract: This review comprehensively analyzes silver nanoparticles’ multimodal functions in wound healing, integrating antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and pro-angiogenic mechanisms with advanced material design for next-generation wound therapeutics.

title: Synergy between multi-components and Ir dopant in Ir-doped high-entropy alloy nanoparticles for efficient and robust ethylene glycol electro-oxidation at an industrial-level current
abstract: Ir-doped PdPtAuNiCu HEANs were rationally designed, synthesized, and fabricated in a flow cell fed by ethylene glycol. The cell delivered an industrial-level performance with >300 mA cm −2 under 0.724 V for >1200 hours.

title: Micro–nano scale motion control of light-driven morphing pillars for biointerfacing
abstract: This study presents a light-responsive platform based on azobenzene polymer micropillars that enables precise, reversible micro-nanoscale deformations via laser stimulation, demonstrating biocompatibility and potential for real-time cell modulation.

title: Recent research progress in polyoxometalate-based composite materials applied to electrochemical biosensors for biomolecule detection
abstract: This review outlines the working principles of POM-based composite material-integrated electrochemical biosensors and highlights significant recent advancements in the detection of biomolecules.

title: Self-biased silicon transistor with a piezoelectric gate for an efficient mechanical energy harvesting device
abstract: The piezo-potential gated self-biased silicon transistor, dedicated to converting mechanical energy into electrical energy, in DC form, with a conversion efficiency of ∼75%.

title: Reconfigurable optoelectronic functionality implemented in a Ta 2 NiSe 5 /WS 2 heterostructure toward multifunctional applications
abstract: A Ta 2 NiSe 5 /WS 2 heterostructure exhibits dual-modal functionality, integrating photodetection and neuromorphic visual simulation within a single device.

title: Atomically dispersed iron–zinc dual-metal sites to boost catalytic oxygen reduction activities for efficient zinc–air batteries
abstract: A novel catalyst with Fe and Zn diatomic sites atomically dispersed on nitrogen-doped hierarchical porous carbon (FeZn–NC-800) was synthesized, which exhibited excellent ORR activity, stability, and potential as Zn–air battery cathode.

title: Ultrathin MXene conductive films with percolation-driven electron transport and thickness-dependent microwave absorption/shielding dual functionality
abstract: By tuning nanoscale structure and electron dynamics, MXene films enable precision control over microwave transparency, absorption, and shielding.

title: Enhancement of the piezoelectric response of ZnO nanowires grown via PLI-MOCVD using post-deposition treatments through adjusted screening and surface effects
abstract: As a biocompatible and non-critical material, ZnO, specifically in its nanowire morphology, holds great promise for integration into highly efficient mechanical energy transducers.

title: Research progress of multifunctional inorganic nanomaterials for tumor photothermal therapy
abstract: This review summarizes the design and research progress of multifunctional inorganic photothermal nanomaterials and multi-mode synergistic therapy, providing guidance for novel diagnostic and therapeutic materials and insights into future PTT development.

title: Fabrication of a foldable transparent conductive electrode with suppressed silver migration by anchoring fluorinated alkyl passivated silver nanowires on a PET surface
abstract: Tethering silver nanowires (AgNWs) on optical polymeric membranes is a key strategy for constructing flexible transparent conductive electrodes (FTCEs) to meet flexible electronics demands.

title: Grain size engineering via a Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 seed layer for FeFET memory and synaptic devices
abstract: A ferroelectric thin film using a seed layer and a higher deposition temperature process brings about a reduction in grain size, leading to lower device-to-device variation and improved electrical characteristics including P – V and synaptic functions.

title: Chiral nanomaterials: theory, synthesis, applications and challenges
abstract: Nicholas A. Kotov, Jeanne Crassous, David B. Amabilino and Pengfei Duan introduce the themed collection on chiral nanomaterials.

title: Preparing nitrogen and sulfur-codoped carbon quantum dots to achieve labeling of bovine serum albumin
abstract: Blue, yellow and red emissive N,S-CQDs were synthesized via a one-step solvothermal method. The isothiocyanate functional group was attached to the red emissive CQDs during synthesis, which were applied to achieve the labeling of BSA.

title: Immobilization of horseradish peroxidase on UiO-66-NH 2 for colorimetric and fluorometric sensing of nitrite
abstract: Development of a colorimetric and fluorometric dual-mode sensor using HRP immobilized on UiO-66-NH 2 , leveraging the enzyme's catalytic efficiency and the fluorescence properties of the MOF.

title: Enhancing CO oxidation performance by controlling the interconnected pore structure in porous three-way catalyst particles
abstract: Precisely controlled interconnected pore structure, thin framework, and high macroporosity of three-way catalyst particles using the template-assisted spray method.

title: Computational evaluation of structural and chemical possibilities exposed by Ti 2 CT x nanoribbons and 2D-nanoparticles
abstract: MXenes are a rapidly expanding family of 2D materials known for their unique tunability, yet studies of their edges remain sparse compared to their better-characterized basal planes.

title: Improving colloidal stability and response performances using looped thermal-responsive brushes
abstract: Looped construction formed denser brushes in the inner layer and sparser chains in the outer layer, enhancing colloidal, size stability during heating–cooling cycles causing significant dye release at high temperatures.

title: A-site composition tuning in methylammonium-based metal halide perovskite colloidal nanocrystals
abstract: Methylammonium-based A-site composition tuning in lead halide perovskites via post-synthetic cross-ion exchange to generate phase-stable colloidal nanocrystals of FA x MA 1− x PbI 3 and Cs x FA y MA 1− x − y PbI 3 (TCPbI 3 ).

title: Phonon induced near-field effects on heat transfer across nanogap electrodes
abstract: Anomalous phonon-induced heat transfer has been observed across gold nanogaps with sub-nanoscale spacing. The distance dependence of thermal conductance suggests that mechanical interactions between the electrodes facilitate phonon propagation.

title: Significantly enhanced breakdown strength and energy density performances of methyl methacrylate- co -glycidyl methacrylate nanocomposites filled with BNNs@PDA-Ag hybrid structures
abstract: The energy density of MG films has been enhanced through the synergistic effect of the Coulomb-blockade and deep traps at the organic–inorganic interface.

title: Introduction to Superwetting nanoelectrodes for renewable energy
abstract: Zuankai Wang, Alex Bell, Alberto Vomiero and Xiaoming Sun introduce the Nanoscale themed collection on Superwetting nanoelectrodes for renewable energy.

title: An intramolecular FRET biosensor for the detection of SARS-CoV-2 in biological fluids
abstract: Using molecular modeling and fluorescence experiments, we designed and validated a FRET-based biosensor for SARS-CoV-2 Spike protein, advancing protein-based biosensor development.

title: Carbon dots derived from ginger for inhibiting inflammatory osteolysis
abstract: A new kind of carbon dots are synthesized from ginger, with excellent biocompatibility, potent reactive oxygen species scavenging ability, and significant anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used for treating inflammatory osteolytic diseases.

title: Fabrication of fluorescent supramolecular organic frameworks through host–guest interactions and their application in ratiometric detection of I −
abstract: Three-dimensional cube-like SOF structures were obtained through the co-assembly of a fluorescent amphiphilic molecule with γ-cyclodextrin. And we developed a ratiometric fluorescence platform for detection of I − using the SOFs with AuNCs.

title: Nanoscale engineering of electronic and magnetic modulations in gradient functional oxide heterostructures
abstract: Engineering of artificially disordered interfaces provides an alternative way to control the ground state of correlated oxide heterostructures, which enables the shaping of future electronic and magnetic nanodevices with enhanced performance.

title: Axial electrokinetic trapping of label-free nanoparticles using evanescent field scattering
abstract: Anti-Brownian electrokinetic trapping enables the confinement of individual nanoparticles in liquids by applying electric fields. Image created via Blender Foundation (www.blender.org).

title: High-throughput screening accelerated by machine learning for the morphology of silica nanoparticles with high cell permeability
abstract: High-throughput screening framework accelerated by machine learning for the morphology of silica nanoparticles. Our simulation results confirm that icosahedral nanoparticles, resembling viral morphology, have significantly high cell permeability.

title: Correction: Dual-targeting hybrid nanoparticles for the delivery of SN38 to Her2 and CD44 overexpressed human gastric cancer
abstract: Correction for ‘Dual-targeting hybrid nanoparticles for the delivery of SN38 to Her2 and CD44 overexpressed human gastric cancer’ by Zhe Yang et al. , Nanoscale , 2016, 8 , 11543–11558, https://doi.org/10.1039/C6NR01749E.

title: Correction: Recent advances in using severe plastic deformation for the processing of nanomaterials
abstract: Correction for ‘Recent advances in using severe plastic deformation for the processing of nanomaterials’ by Terence G. Langdon, Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 17417–17427, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR01886B.

title: Investigation of the phase transition to the Ruddlesden–Popper phase in La- or Nb-doped Sr 2 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ double perovskites and the impact of lanthanum or niobium doping
abstract: The influence of La- or Nb-doping was studied for the phase transition of Sr 2 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6−δ . Niobium does not have a positive impact on the stability and properties, while La enhances the electrical conductivity and stability of double perovskites.

title: Enhancing low-temperature H 2 S sensing performance of SnO 2 quantum wires via transition metal doping and oxygen vacancy engineering
abstract: Doped SnO 2 quantum wires enhance selectivity and detection limits for hydrogen sulfide (H 2 S) sensing, achieving sub-ppb sensitivity at low temperatures.

title: Magnetism of transition-metal-doped tetrel nanoclusters: multi-reference character and spin–orbit effects in Sn 12 TM (TM = Cr, Mn, Fe)
abstract: Magnetic beam deflection shows an enhanced g -factor for Sn 12 Fe. CASSCF calculations reveal the multi-reference character of Sn 12 Fe and charge transfer from Sn 5p to Fe 3d orbitals. Spin–orbit contributions of the Sn atoms enhance the g -factor.

title: Designing atomically precise gold nanocluster architectures with DNA-guided self-assembly and biofunctionalization approaches
abstract: Atomically precise gold nanoclusters (AuNCs) are nanomolecular species with unique optoelectronic properties, both at the individual and assembled levels.

title: A rice husk-derived SiO x /C composite for effective lithium-sulfur battery separator modification
abstract: A rice husk-derived SiO x /C composite for effective lithium-sulfur battery separator modification.

title: Dual-mode sensing by cobalt oxyhydroxide nanosheets for antibiotics
abstract: Here, we present a cobalt oxyhydroxide (CoOOH) nanosheet-driven dual-mode sensor for fluoroquinolone antibiotics detection using fluorescence and colorimetric strategies and a mobile device-assisted paper-based sensor for on-site monitoring.

title: Laser-induced optical and structural modification in AgI thin films loaded with silver nanoparticles
abstract: Light-induced colouration and crystallinity promotion in Ag–AgI films.

title: Multiple roles of MXene-based materials in sustainable pesticides
abstract: The roles of MXenes in constructing sustainable pesticides are summarized, including delivery systems, detection platforms and removal techniques.

title: Arrays of nano-light-mixers for enhanced broadband and omnidirectional absorption of solar radiation for solar energy technologies
abstract: Broadband and omnidirectional absorption is the first requirement for any solar energy harvesting technology.

title: The role of polymer coatings in lipid membrane penetration by graphene oxide dots
abstract: Coarse-grained simulations reveal the effect of polymer coating density and hydrophilic/hydrophobic balance on the spontaneity of graphene oxide penetration into lipid membranes, guiding safer nanosystem design.

title: Fluoride binding-modulated supramolecular chirality of urea-containing triarylamine and its photo-manifestation
abstract: The addition of F − led to amplification of the supramolecular chirality of the triarylamine derivative containing a urea unit, and the photoirradiation allowed the F − in the system to be visualized by the naked eye.

title: Lattice stability-induced enhanced performance of a quasi-single-crystal LiNi 0.75 Co 0.1 Mn 0.15 O 2 cathode for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The material preparation in this work is entirely based on a kilogram-scale basis. An energt-efficient process was achieved by adopting segmented sintering and shortening the high-temperature reaction time (900 °C) to 2 hours.

title: Photoinduced luminescence activation of hydrophilic ‘caged’ carbons dots
abstract: Photoactivatable carbon dots bearing photocleavable 2-nitrobenzyl quenchers enable tunable luminescence in solution and live cells, offering new opportunities for bioimaging.

title: Effects of ethylenediamine on the intrinsic photoresponse of PbS quantum dots
abstract: We examine the intrinsic recombination dynamics of PbS quantum dots (QDs) functionalized with ethylenediamine (EDA) and compare it to that of the same QDs functionalized with ethane-1,2-dithiol (EDT).

title: Mechanism of methane activation and graphene growth on oxide ceramics
abstract: Mechanisms of graphene growth via chemical vapor deposition on oxide ceramics such as Al 2 O 3 , MgO, CaO, and SiO 2 are discussed, with a focus on substrate-specific methane activation processes, toward versatile three-dimensional graphene synthesis.

title: Tuning the electronic properties of ZnO nanofilms via strain-induced structural phase transformations and quantum confinement
abstract: We show how the electronic structure of ZnO nanofilms varies with thickness, strain and phase transformations. Using Crystal Orbital Hamiltonian Population (COHP) analyses, we highlight the effects of quantum confinement and surface localisation.

title: Sequential control of catalyst alloying and oxygen-mediated nucleation for continuous synthesis of SnO nanowires floating in the gas phase
abstract: SnO nanowires are synthesised continuously via floating catalyst chemical vapour deposition (FCCVD). Fast growth (residence time of <10 s) and high selectivity are achieved through sequential injection of the catalyst aerosol and precursor gases.

title: Density functional theory study of the magnetic properties of 2D ferromagnetic semiconductor CrCl 3− x Br x ( x = 1–3) monolayers
abstract: By isovalent alloying with W atoms, the T C of intrinsic ferromagnetic semiconductor CrCl 3− x Br x monolayers can be remarkably enhanced.

title: A 3D-printed cassette coupled with a paper-based aptasensor for advanced diagnosis of chikungunya virus towards commercialization
abstract: A 3D printed cassette integrated paper aptasensor enables rapid and ultrasensitive electrochemical detection of chikungunya virus at the point of care. Graphical abstract images via Canva.

title: Promoting transcellular traversal of the blood–brain barrier by simultaneously improving cellular uptake and accelerating lysosomal escape
abstract: A pH-sensitive nanocarrier coated with a brain metastatic tumor cell membrane (CA-iRGD-CS@M) is designed to enhance the drug delivery across the BBB by simultaneously improving cellular uptake and accelerating lysosomal escape.

title: Unsymmetrical S -annulated perylene diester imide, stabilizing room temperature columnar phase as a dopant for greenish-yellow OLEDs with an outstanding EQE of 6.9%
abstract: Room-temperature liquid crystalline unsymmetrical S -annulated perylene diester imide as a dopant for greenish-yellow OLEDs with high EQE of 6.9%

title: Contrast-enhanced imaging of carbon fiber composites using hafnium oxide nanocrystals
abstract: HfO 2 nanocrystal-doped carbon fiber-reinforced polymers showed a drastic improvement in Computed Tomography contrast, allowing for segmentation of the carbon fiber tows and visualization of micrometer-scale cracks.

title: A ferroelectrically modulated ultrasensitive two-dimensional perovskite phototransistor with zero-gate-bias
abstract: A gate-bias-free perovskite phototransistor with ultrasensitive optoelectronic performances is achieved through a high-capacity nonvolatile ferroelectric-gating.

title: Stability matters: evaluating the long-term performance of AuNP–DNA conjugates in lateral flow assays through varied conjugation methods and storage buffers
abstract: In this work we compare the stability of different Au nanoparticle DNA conjugates in different commonly used buffers to assess how this affects their performance in lateral flow assays.

title: A magnetic hybrid sol–gel ionic network catalyst for direct alcohol esterification under solvent-free conditions
abstract: A cross-linked magnetic acidic poly(ionic liquid) network with a high ionic content as an efficient, water-tolerant, and recyclable catalyst for direct esterification.

title: Ultra-thin flexible solid-gated graphene field-effect transistors fabricated using laser lift-off
abstract: This work features wafer-scale fabrication via spin-coated polyimide & laser lift-off that creates ultra-thin flexible GFET arrays. They enable highly sensitive strain detection exceeding commercial options while staying stable when bent.

title: Carbon dots endow glucose oxidase with autocatalytic features and anti-poisoning ability
abstract: Carbon dots regulate glucose oxidase via H 2 O 2 decomposition and O 2 regeneration to build an autocatalytic cycle, enabling high-performance bioelectrocatalytic and immobilized enzyme systems.

title: A modified MXene composite sensor with sulphur impurities for electrochemical detection of lead in the aqueous system
abstract: Representation of low cost MXene based electrochemical sensor for lead detection.

title: Starvation induces diffusion hindrance at the nanoscale in mammalian cells
abstract: Prolonged starvation leads to intracellular diffusion hindrance, inducing a state of cellular dormancy with reduced energy consumption.

title: CNT/PVA-PVA double-layer hydrogel interfacial evaporators for stable solar desalination and water purification
abstract: The B-CPVA double-layer evaporator can enhance the overall water evaporation performance by leveraging the synergistic effects of the thermal and salt concentration gradients.

title: Superior photoconversion efficiency of nanocrystal sensitized solar cells based on all-inorganic CsPbX 3 (X = Br, I) perovskites
abstract: Nanocrystal-sensitized solar cells have emerged as potential alternatives to traditional photovoltaic technology due to their unique light absorption and emission characteristics and size-dependent bandgap.

title: Structural modification strategies for ferritin nanoparticles and their applications in biomedicine: a narrative review
abstract: Engineered ferritin nanocages exploit biocompatibility and TfR1 targeting. Chemical/genetic/mineralization strategies enable therapeutics delivery, imaging, and vaccines (in trials). Advancing smart precision nanomedicine.

title: Nanoengineered electrochemical biosensors: a next-gen technology in cancer biomarker detection
abstract: Recent advancements in electrochemical biosensors for cancer diagnostics, with a focus on the incorporation of nanomaterials and cutting-edge sensing strategies.

title: Enhanced MoS 2 heterojunctions by interface engineering with self-assembled monolayers
abstract: This work introduces molecular SAMs with different terminal groups but similar dipole moments at the metal-MoS 2 interface to enhance the electronic and optoelectronic properties of the MoS 2 heterojunctions.

title: A review of heat transport in solvated gold nanoparticles: Molecular dynamics modeling and experimental perspectives
abstract: Knowledge map of key research themes for solvated gold nanoparticles: interfacial heat transfer and chemistry, computational modeling, experiments, and impacts on biomedical applications like photothermal therapy and drug delivery.

title: Advanced flexible self-healing triboelectric nanogenerators for applications in complex environments
abstract: Advanced flexible self-healing triboelectric nanogenerators (F-TENGs) achieve reliable energy harvesting in harsh environments, enabling sustainable wearable electronics and sensors through dynamic material innovations.

title: Ultra-uniform high-quality plasmonic metasurfaces through electrostatic self-assembly of gold nanoparticles on chemically unmodified glass
abstract: Direct electrostatic self-assembly of cationic AuNPs on glass delivers ultra-uniform, high-density metasurfaces via solvent exchange and fast drying. When particle size is optimal, far-field coupling emerges, boosting plasmonic Q -factors up to ∼15.

title: From synthesis to application: a review of BaZrS 3 chalcogenide perovskites
abstract: This review covers BaZrS 3 thin-film synthesis methods, optical, optoelectronic, electronic, and defect properties, and highlights device applications.

title: Soft acid Bi 3+ doping in Li 2 ZrCl 6 to enhance ionic conductivity and electrochemical stability
abstract: Herein, a small amount of Bi 3+ was added to Li 2 ZrCl 6 to synthesize the Li 2.15 Zr 0.85 Bi 0.15 Cl 6 electrolyte, which exhibited a room-temperature lithium-ion conductivity of 0.49 mS cm −1 .

title: Construction of hollow urchin-like carbon frameworks with Co, P, and N doping and encapsulated Co/Co 2 P heterojunction as bifunctional electrocatalysts for rechargeable Zn–air batteries
abstract: A hollow urchin-like bifunctional oxygen electrocatalyst is developed for rechargeable Zn–air batteries by assembling P, N-doped carbon nanotubes with confined Co/Co 2 P heterojunctions on heteroatom-doped carbon hollow spheres.

title: QPHO-graphene: a two-dimensional hexagon-free carbon allotrope as a high-performance anode material for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: QPHO-graphene, a two-dimensional hexagon-free carbon allotrope, is a high-performance anode material for sodium-ion batteries.

title: Synergistic effects of europium doping on MOF-5: exploring its photoluminescent and non-linear optical behaviour for enhanced optical limiting
abstract: Controlled Eu-doping of MOF-5 nanomaterials exhibiting enhanced optical limiting behaviour, analyzed with Z -scan technique.

title: A corus line: nanophase diagrams of miscible bimetallic nanoparticles
abstract: Coring in nanoparticles, identified by size- and shape-dependent nanophase diagrams, may lead to metastable core–shell configurations that may survive deposition.

title: A 3D mixed ion-electron conducting framework for dendrite-free lithium metal anodes
abstract: A mixed ion-electron conducting framework, LLTO@CNT, was designed for lithium metal batteries with uniform Li deposition and long cycling stability.

title: Computational design of metal–organic frameworks with triangular adsorbaphores for highly selective adsorption of m -xylene
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) with triangular pores are designed for xylene separation.

title: Nanobody engineering for enhanced-sensitivity rapid COVID-19 tests
abstract: Short description: Nanobody-conjugated gold nanoparticles enabled a highly sensitive SARS-CoV-2 lateral flow assay within 15 minutes, outperforming antibody-based rapid tests and offering strong potential for surveillance and outbreak control.

title: Watching lanthanide nanoparticles one at a time: characterization of their photoluminescence dynamics at the single nanoparticle level
abstract: Photoluminescence burst analysis and fluorescence correlation spectroscopy were used to assess the emission brightness and photodynamics of single Sm- and Eu-based lanthanide nanoparticles (LnNPs) with single nanoparticle sensitivity.

title: ATP-responsive nanoparticles for improved chemodynamic therapy and dual starvation therapy
abstract: ZIF-based nanoparticles called Cu 2+ –SK–GOD@ZIF-90 are constructed for improved chemodynamic therapy and dual starvation therapy.

title: Engineering biocompatible hydrogen titanate nanocarriers with blood brain barrier (BBB) crossing potential for doxorubicin delivery to glioma cells
abstract: Schematic illustration of H 2 Ti 3 O 7 nanotube synthesis and Dox loading for targeted anti-glioma therapy applications. (Created in BioRender. Choudhury, S. (2025) https://BioRender.com/e3j13qm).

title: Adsorption and electron transfer of metal-reducing decaheme cytochrome protein MtrF on iron oxide nanoparticle surfaces
abstract: A simulation study reveals the adsorption behavior of Shewanella multiheme cytochrome protein MtrF on small-sized α-Fe 2 O 3 nanoparticles in water and its electron transfer across the adsorbed protein.

title: Size-, shape-, facet- and support-dependent selectivity of Cu nanoparticles in CO 2 reduction through multiparameter optimization
abstract: This study investigates the limited selectivity of the Cu111 surface for C–C bond formation during CO 2 reduction and explores the factors influencing selectivity using Cu nanoparticles smaller than 2 nm.

title: Production of synthetic natural gas: a study of nickel catalysts obtained by solid-state combustion of nickel complexes with ethylenediamine
abstract: Nickel-alumina catalyst was prepared by solid-state combustion from energy-rich complex obtained from ethylenediamine and nickel nitrate directly in the crucible. It catalyzed the methanation of CO 2 more efficiently than a commercial catalyst containing ∼2 times more nickel.

title: Machine learning-assisted detection of single-point mutations via DNA-templated gold nanoparticle growth
abstract: Detection of single-point mutations, such as PIK3CA, is vital for precision diagnostics. A DNA-templated gold nanoparticle biosensor, combined with machine learning, enables rapid, sensitive, and specific colorimetric detection.

title: A dual PO group additive and anti-solvent synergistic strategy enables efficient and stable quasi-2D red perovskite LEDs
abstract: Efficient and stable quasi-2D red PeLEDs were achieved by adopting an anti-solvent/dual PO additive synergistic strategy. The EQE max and T 50 of the optimized PeLED were 5.25 and 4.92 times those of the control device, respectively.

title: Tailoring graphene oxide nanosheets by alkyl amine grafting for enhanced adsorption of PFASs in drinking water: a combined theoretical and experimental study
abstract: This study examines PFAS adsorption on graphene oxide modified with N , N -dimethylethylenediamine (GO-DMEN), offering insigths into the chemical interactions crucial for designing materials for effective PFAS removal from water.

title: Core–sheath FeCo@vertically aligned carbon fiber arrays for integrated ultrahigh through-plane thermal conductivity and absorption-dominant electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Constructing integrated TC and absorption-dominant EMI shielding networks via in situ growth of FeCo nanoparticles on VACF arrays. A through-plane TC of 42.03 W (mK) −1 and an SE T of 41.6 dB were achieved, while the SE R was only 1.5 dB.

title: The true atomistic structure of a disordered crystal: a computational study on the photon upconverting material β-NaYF 4 and its Er 3+ -, Tm 3+ -, and Yb 3+ -doped derivates
abstract: True atomic positions were determined for β-NaYF 4 (:Ln 3+ ) using density functional theory. The arrangement of the F − ions closest to doping sites is strongly affected by the occupation of disordered sites reducing their effective point group to C 1 .

title: Gold nanorods as multidimensional optical nanomaterials: machine learning-enhanced quantitative fingerprinting of proteins for diagnostic applications
abstract: The rapid and precise quantification and identification of proteins as key diagnostic biomarkers hold significant promise in allergy testing, disease diagnosis, clinical treatment, and proteomics.

title: 2D nanoarchitectonics for exfoliated nanosheets: tailoring surface roughness of thin-film coatings
abstract: The thinner nanosheets with higher aspect ratio provide the flat surface exhibiting the structural color originating from the interference. The thicker nanosheets with lower aspect ratio form the rough surface exhibiting hydrophobicity.

title: Fabrication of MXene films through various techniques: a mini review
abstract: MXenes are 2D materials with high conductivity and tunable surfaces, ideal for energy storage and flexible electronics.

title: Lauryl-NrTP6 lipopeptide self-assembled nanorods for nuclear-targeted delivery of doxorubicin
authors: Ana Beatriz Caribé dos Santos Valle
abstract: Lipopeptide assemblies carrying doxorubicin in their hydrophilic regions target the nuclei of cancer cells and slowly deliver the encapsulated drug.

title: Continuous flow synthesis of plasmonic magnesium nanoparticles with tunable optical properties from grignard precursors
abstract: Continuous flow reduction of Grignard reagents produces plasmonic Mg NPs with tunable light absorption across the solar spectrum and beyond.

title: Exploring the insertion mechanism of pseudocapacitive perovskite oxide La–Ni–Co–O anode materials and the application to Li-ion capacitor and Li-based dual ion batteries
abstract: Designed a pseudocapacitive binary perovskite oxide LNCO(1 : 3)/LO anode material, deeply explored its insertion mechanism by electrochemical analysis and ex situ characterization techniques, constructed LICs and Li-DIBs devices.

title: Composite metamaterial of hyperbolic nanoridges and gold nanoparticles for biosensing
abstract: Hyperbolic metamaterials have gained considerable attention in the field of optical biosensing due to their ability to support highly sensitive plasmonic modes.

title: Magnetic nanoparticle-mediated non-invasive imaging and eradication of lymph nodes
abstract: This study aimed to develop a multi-functional citrate-coated magnetite nanoparticle that could specifically drain to and accumulate in lymph nodes for non-invasive imaging and eradication of lymph nodes.

title: Development of supramolecular ionic gels with self-healing capability and biodegradability using a bioderived ionic liquid and poly(vinyl alcohol)
abstract: The physical crosslinking of poly(vinyl alcohol) and a bioderived ionic liquid yields supramolecular ionic gels with self-healing capability, reconfigurability, and biodegradability for wearables, implants, and environmental sensing.

title: Enhanced interfacial thermal transport in diamond nanothread reinforced polymer nanocomposites: insights from atomistic simulations and density functional theory
abstract: Chiral DNTs with methyl groups in a nanocomposite, i.e. DNT_C-CH 3 , exhibit the highest interfacial thermal conductance of 0.115 GW m −2 K −1 , representing a 140% improvement over CNTs.

title: Recent progress in the synthesis of CuCr 2 O 4 nanomaterials and their composites for catalytic, energy, and biomedical applications: a state-of-the-art-review
abstract: Copper chromite nanoparticles (CuCr 2 O 4 NPs) are in the spotlight of modern nanoscience due to their versatile applications across various scientific and industrial domains. The graphical abstract was created, in part, with Canva.com.

title: Tailoring metal–ligand coordination in Prussian blue cathodes for long-cycling and high-rate sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This study modulates coordination by adjusting Na 3 -Cit and VC binding, activating LS iron in PBAs by reducing [Fe(CN) 6 ] 4− vacancies. FeMn-2VC shows high rate capability (105.98 mAh g −1 at 0.5 A g −1 ) and cycling stability (71.72% after 700 cycles).

title: Brønsted-Lewis acidic ionic liquid-derived ZnS quantum dots: synthesis, characterization, and multifunctional applications in pollutant degradation and iodine sorption
abstract: A pair of acidic chlorozincate salts of 2-alkyl-1,3-disulfoimidazolium cations was developed as a template for fabricating ZnS QDs. The prepared QDs were used as recyclable photocatalysts for degradation of organic pollutants and iodine sorption.

title: Nanomaterial-mediated ferroptosis as a promising strategy for cancer therapy
abstract: Conventional cancer therapies, such as chemotherapy and radiotherapy, often face limitations due to drug resistance, systemic toxicity, and tumor heterogeneity, which significantly limit the therapeutic effect.

title: Effect of Perovskite Electronic Structure Modulation on Catalytic Combustion of VOCs
abstract: The emission of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) is a serious threat to the environment and human health, and catalytic combustion technology has become an important direction for the treatment of...

title: Leveraging metal sites in doped metal halide perovskites for enhanced photocatalysis: fundamentals and applications
abstract: This review systematically reveals the in-depth relationship between doping strategies, property regulation, and functional applications as well as promotes more efficient applications of metal-doped MHP materials for photocatalysis.

title: Precursor engineering towards orange- and red-emissive carbon dots for LEDs with tunable emission colors
abstract: Two benzenoid acids are used as precursors to synthesize CDs with different emissions and electroluminescences. This work provides a typical example that reveals the precursor-dependent structural and optical/optoelectronic diversity of CDs.

title: Sulfide-mediated growth of NIR luminescent Pd/Ag atomically precise nanoclusters
abstract: Designing nanoclusters with tailored characteristics: utilizing sulfide to tune near-infrared emission in Ag-rich Pd alloys.

title: Mechanisms and effects of gas intercalation into ionic liquids confined within charged nanoscale volumes
abstract: A combined simulation and experimental study into mechanisms and effects of gas intercalation in ionic liquids confined to charged and neutral carbon nanotubes, providing insights for gas sensing and separation technologies.

title: Influence of branched ligand architectures on nanoparticle interactions with lipid bilayers
abstract: We utilize coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations and enhanced sampling methods to reveal the effect of ligand branching on the thermodynamics of nanoparticle adsorption to lipid bilayers.

title: Investigation of the role of in-plane stress behavior on ferroelectric properties of scaled-up hafnium zirconium oxide superlattices
abstract: La-doped HZO superlattices with different numbers of HZO layer repeats show preservation of in-plane tensile stress, leading to similar FE response.

title: Flexible piezo-responsive photodetector based on hierarchical zinc oxide nanowire branches on a silver nanowire backbone
abstract: A flexible piezo-responsive photodetector using hierarchical zinc oxide nanowires grown on silver nanowire backbones, enhanced by ultraviolet laser patterning and PDMS coating for improved durability and flexibility.

title: Effects of crystal orientations on oxidation of epitaxial TiN thin films
abstract: Epitaxial [001]-, [110]-, and [111]-oriented TiN thin films were grown by pulsed laser deposition under high vacuum. The oxidation behavior and electrical property of TiN thin films were investigated.

title: Controlled cerium doping in In 2 O 3 derived from an In-MOF to boost electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction for selective formate production
abstract: A Ce-doped In 2 O 3 catalyst derived from an In-MOF showed high selectivity for formate production with superior faradaic efficiency in the electrocatalytic CO 2 RR and this work provides design strategies for the development of novel electrocatalysts.

title: Recent progress in nickel oxide-based chemiresistive toxic-gas sensors
abstract: Continuous advancements in technology and industry have resulted in the discharge of toxic and hazardous gases that pose a threat to human health and the environment.

title: Interface-driven Pd–Ag–Cu/C nanocomposites with increased oxygen reduction reaction kinetics
abstract: Modification of the Pd lattice with Cu and Ag leads to improved catalytic activity and greater durability during the ORR in an alkaline medium.

title: Enhancing photocatalytic performance of F-doped TiO 2 through the integration of small amounts of a quinoline-based covalent triazine framework
abstract: We present a hybrid material formed by F-TiO 2 and quinoline-based covalent triazine framework ( Quin-CTF ) with improved photocatalytic performance towards pollutant degradation in water.

title: Structural modulation of tin nickelate nanostructures embedded in reduced graphene oxide for high-performance asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Designing of a new and cost-effective spinel SnNi 2 O 4 integrated graphene nanosheets through simple ultra-sonication technique as electrode material for the construction of effective energy storage device.

title: Tungsten oxide nanocrystals doped with interstitial methylammonium cations
abstract: The first colloidal synthesis of tungsten oxide nanocrystals doped with large organic cations is presented. The colloids show strong plasmonic properties in the near infrared.

title: Anodic expulsion of Cu nanoparticles from a polycrystalline Cu substrate: a novel corrosion and single entity study approach
abstract: Cu is the dominant heterogeneous metal catalyst for CO 2 reduction (CO 2 R) in combatting climate change, which often relies on Cu oxides (CuO or Cu 2 O). Created, in part, with Blender (www.blender.org).

title: Defective plane induced fractal TON zeolites for efficient hydroisomerization of long-chain hydrocarbons
abstract: The heteroatom substitution at the framework on the defective {021} facets triggered a self-similar structure that evolved into a snowflake-like TON fractal. The growth mode of the framework's crystal phase and its structural origin were disclosed.

title: Nanostructured systems to combat NO x air pollution through Vis-light activated nanoarchitectonics: how, where and why…?
abstract: This review highlights recent advancements in Vis-light activated photocatalysts for the degradation of nitrogen oxides in air, emphasizing key strategies to boost their performances for environmental remediation.

title: Supramolecular electrostatic functionalization of 1T-MoS 2 based on alkylammonium salts
abstract: The electrostatic functionalization between negative-charged 1T-MoS 2 and positive-charged alkylammonium salts allows high functionalization yields while preserving the material's favorable properties.

title: Effect of competition between swelling and dye adsorption on the performance and selectivity of graphene oxide membranes
abstract: The performance of graphene oxide (GO) based nanofiltration membranes is strongly influenced by their interlayer spacing, governed by two competing factors: GO swelling in the liquid phase and penetrant adsorption.

title: Pt nanoparticle–nanowire hybrids supported on single-walled carbon nanotubes for enhanced oxygen reduction reaction in polymer electrolyte fuel cells
abstract: The Pt nanoparticle–nanowire hybrid on SWCNTs combines high activity, efficient electron transport, and durability, achieving a 0.882 V half-wave potential, a 380 A g Pt −1 mass activity, and retaining performance after 60 000 cycles.

title: Nanoscale NiCu electrocatalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The controlled synthesis of a nanometric NiCu material bearing n -octyl silane ligands led to boosted electrocatalytic performance for the hydrogen evolution reaction with enhanced stability compared to pure Ni.

title: Insights into the structural features of crumpling graphene nanoribbons
abstract: Coarse-grained MD simulations reveal that the crumpling behavior of graphene nanoribbons depends strongly on width and aspect ratio, leading to distinct deformation modes, structural ordering, and mechanical responses.

title: NMR studies of PEG chain dynamics on mesoporous silica nanoparticles for minimizing non-specific binding
abstract: A suite of NMR techniques was used to study PEG dynamics and conformation on PEGylated MSNs, revealing how grafting density affects protein adsorption and guiding the design of protein-resistant nanomaterials for biomedical use.

title: Efficient and stable blue perovskite light-emitting diodes enabled by the synergistic incorporation of dual additives
abstract: The best blue perovskite light-emitting diode enabled by the synergistic incorporation of benzoic acid potassium (BAP) and guanidinium chloride (GACl) exhibits a stable spectrum at 476 nm and a maximum external quantum efficiency of 4.47%.

title: Role of nitrogen and oxygen in the nucleation and growth of silver nanoparticles in gas-phase synthesis
abstract: This study explores silver nanoparticle synthesis in gas phase, showing that ion-induced nucleation, initiated by trace oxygen (<0.5%), notably increases AgNPs surface density, offering insights into the role of reactive gases in nanoparticle growth.

title: Organic semiconductor bulk heterojunctions for solar-to-chemical conversion: recent advances and challenges
abstract: This minireview highlights recent progress in BHJ-based photoelectrochemical cells and photocatalysts for solar-to-chemical conversions.

title: Anion-driven supramolecular gel: naked-eye detection of picric acid, facilitating in situ formation of gel nanocomposites and enabling solid-state anion exchange
abstract: GUA-IND forms stimuli responsive anion-induced supramolecular gel (GI-G) which forms nanocomposites with metal salts that show antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. GUA-IND also exhibits anion exchange phenomena in the solid state.

title: Simulation of the crystallization process of Ge 2 Sb 2 Te 5 nanoconfined in superlattice geometries for phase change memories
abstract: The slab of recrystallized amorphous Ge 2 Sb 2 Te 5 (GST) confined by capping layers mimicking TiTe 2 in GST/TiTe 2 superlattices. The kinetics of crystallization was studied by molecular dynamics simulations with a machine learning interatomic potential.

title: Predicting biomolecule adsorption on nanomaterials: a hybrid framework of molecular simulations and machine learning
abstract: The adsorption of biomolecules on the surface of nanomaterials (NMs) is a critical determinant of their behavior, toxicity, and efficacy in biological systems.

title: The role of superlattice phases and interparticle distance in the magnetic behaviour of SPION thin films
abstract: Thin films composed of self-assembled SPIONs with controlled interparticle distance showed peculiar magnetic exchange characteristics correlated with the blocking temperature, which can be explained by the presence of internal superlattice phases.

title: Modulating thermal conductance at ligand/nanocrystal interfaces via oxygen-coordinated ligands
abstract: h lig–NC of oxygen-coordinated bridging bidentate > chelating bidentate > monodentate and decays exponentially with O–Cd separation.

title: Superior pseudocapacitive performance of a nanocomposite of graphdiyne nanoflakes decorated with poly(4-chloroaniline)
abstract: To achieve enhanced pseudocapacitive performance, a nanocomposite consisting of graphdiyne nanosheets adorned with poly(4-chloroaniline) is utilized.

title: 532- and 52-symmetric Au helicoids synthesized through controlled seed twinning and aspect ratio
abstract: Chiral plasmonic crystals with 5-fold symmetries were synthesized from Au icosahedra, decahedra, and pentatwinned nanorods, unraveling the effects of seed twinning and aspect ratio on chiral overgrowth directed by l -glutathione.

title: Scrutinizing the redox activity of MOF-derived Fe–Se–C and P@Fe–Se–C species to improve overall water electrolysis and alkaline zinc–air batteries
abstract: The robust structure of P-doped Fe–Se–C species for enhancing Zn–air batteries and overall water splitting is demonstrated in this work. Moreover, the intrinsic activities of Fe, Se, C, and P atoms are shown by density functional theory.

title: Selenium nanoparticles combined with calycosin treated sepsis through synergistic anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects
abstract: BSC is a new strategy for the treatment of sepsis, which can exert the activity of GPX enzyme to remove excess H 2 O 2 , inhibit the production of ROS, and suppress NF-κB pathway, thereby inhibiting the production of inflammatory factors.

title: Operando observation of gate defects in quantum dot-based field effect transistors
abstract: This article explores, using X-ray photoemission imaging, how the damage to the gate of a nanocrystal-based field-effect transistor affects the device energy landscape.

title: Two-dimensional BiTeX crystals with persistent luminescence induced by photochemical reactions
abstract: BiTeX (X = Cl, Br, I) nanosheets with high crystallinity through an electrochemical exfoliation method display a persistent luminescence effect, which can be ascribed to reversible bond cleavage and recovery under photoexcitation.

title: Bandgap engineering of lead-free double perovskites for efficient photocatalysis
abstract: Sb doping extends the absorption band edge of lead-free Cs 2 AgBiCl 6 double perovskite to 1450 nm, exhibiting excellent photocatalytic hydrogen performance of 4835.9 μmol g −1 h −1 while maintaining catalytic activity in the near-infrared region.

title: Carbon dots derived from organic drug molecules with improved therapeutic effects and new functions
abstract: This article reviews the functions of drug-derived CDs compared with their precursors and demonstrates their broad application prospects in biomedicine.

title: Coupled 2D quantum dot films for next generation solar cells: electronic structure and anomalous light absorption behaviour
abstract: Coupling between neighboring CQD layers in a stack is found to be detrimental to light absorption, yielding A ( n ) < nA (1), whereas in uncoupled stacks the absorbance A is proportional to the number of layers n , i.e. , A ( n ) = nA (1).

title: Nanocellulose hydrogels from agricultural wastes: methods, properties, and application prospects
abstract: This review highlights the preparation, properties, and versatile applications of nanocellulose-based hydrogels from agricultural wastes, focusing on sustainability, biomedical use, packaging, water purification, and future development trends.

title: Investigation of the oxygen ligand hole of Ni-rich layered cathodes: a new organic coating for enhancing battery performance
abstract: An organic coverage (OC) designed to enhance the stability of the oxygen ligand hole, enabling greater capacity through enhanced Li + interaction.

title: Smart wearable fibers and textiles: status and prospects
abstract: Smart wearable fibers and textiles: status and prospects.

title: 3D-printable liquid metal-based hydrogel for use as a multifunctional epidermal sensor
abstract: The liquid metal-based hydrogels coated with polyacrylic acid have the combination of conductivity, plasticity, printability, self-healing and skin-like mechanical properties, are therefore used as an epidermal sensor for human health monitoring.

title: Colloidally synthesized and bandgap-engineered luminescent titanium nitride quantum dots
abstract: We demonstrate the colloidal synthesis of luminescent titanium nitride (TiN) quantum dots with multicolor emission and tunable band gaps, providing safer alternatives to metal chalcogenides and halide perovskites for solar cells and LEDs.

title: Modulation excitation pair distribution function experiments in catalysis to increase phase sensitivity
abstract: Phase-sensitive detection analysis of PDF data taken under modulation excitation conditions of catalytic cycling considerably improves the PDF data quality of Ni@Al 2 O 3 catalysts, revealing subtle changes occurring under operating conditions.

title: Acoustic phonon-restricted four-phonon interactions: impact on thermal and thermoelectric transport in monolayer h-NbN
abstract: First-principles and Boltzmann transport analyses of h-NbN monolayer reveal strong four-phonon scattering despite mirror symmetry, governing heat transport. Strain further enhances conductivity and improves the thermoelectric figure of merit.

title: Co 2+ -mediated adsorption facilitates atomic force microscopy of DNA molecules at double-helix resolution
abstract: Aided by cobalt-mediated adsorption on mica, atomic force microscopy (AFM) has demonstrated the ability to resolve single DNA molecules in liquid at a spatial resolution that is sufficient to visualize the double helix structure.

title: Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene augments osmo-adaptive repression of the inflammatory stress response for improved wound repair
abstract: A bioactive Ti 3 C 2 T x dressing offers a tissue engineering approach to repress inflammation and oxidative stress, improving wound closure rates for chronic wound repair.

title: Synthesis control of rhombic dodecahedral PtNiFe nanostructures for enhanced oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: As the precursor reaction time increased, the catalytic performance improved. A 5.5-minute reaction time gave the best result due to Ni and Fe trapping and compressive strain that modulated oxygen intermediate adsorption.

title: Nanophotonics of mid-infrared plasmon-polaritons at interfaces between metals and two-dimensional crystals
abstract: In hBN/Au systems, where both surface plasmons and hyperbolic phonon polaritons (HPhPs) coexist, we characterize strong coupling between the SPP and the in-plane transverse optical phonon of hBN.

title: Topical application of insulin encapsulated by chitosan-modified PLGA nanoparticles to alleviate alkali burn-induced corneal neovascularization
abstract: Topical administration of CPI NPs alleviates alkali burn-induced CRNV in rats, accompanied by downregulation of FTO expression and increased RNA m6A modification, with reductions in IL-6, TNF-α, VEGF, and CD31 levels. Created by Figdraw.

title: Structure prediction for nanoscale magic-size CdSe clusters from a new efficient structure-searching strategy
abstract: An efficient geometry searching workflow was developed for (CdSe) n magic-size clusters. A novel adamantane-type core@cage structure was identified for (CdSe) 34 for the first time.

title: Construction of hydrophilic-and-cationic metalloporphyrin-based polymers for electrocatalytic small molecule activation
abstract: The cationic viologen-based metalloporphyrin polymers exhibit excellent hydrophilicity. With varied metal centers, three M-MV polymers specifically catalyze HER, OER, and NO 2 − RR in aqueous solutions.

title: Nano-Fe 2 O 3 guided synthesis of an F,N-coordinated FeCo bimetallic catalyst via H 2 -mediated reductive pyrolysis for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Hierarchical FeCo catalyst (FN/FeCoNC-H 2 -700) via ZIF-67 pyrolysis with H 2 defects, F/N doping, and 700 °C reconstruction addresses insufficient active sites, poor stability, and mass transport issues, achieving superior ORR performance to Pt/C.

title: Fabrication and characterization of ConA-conjugated curcumin-loaded solid lipid nanoparticles for theranostic applications in lung cancer treatment
abstract: ConA-conjugated curcumin-loaded solid lipid nanoparticles, their characterization (UV-vis, DSC, PSA, ATR-FTIR, XRD, SEM), and therapeutic applications in lung cancer, including drug release, anti-proliferation assays, and lung model studies.

title: Preparation of novel lithiated high-entropy spinel-type oxyhalides and their electrochemical performance in Li-ion batteries
abstract: Compositionally complex doping of spinel oxides toward high-entropy oxides and oxyhalides enhances their electrochemical performance substantially.

title: Bio-inspired nickel–iron-based organogel: an efficient and stable bifunctional electrocatalyst for overall water splitting at high current density
abstract: A bio-inspired nickel–iron-based organogel was developed as an efficient and stable electrocatalyst for overall water splitting at high current density, and it is suitable for large-scale AEM electrolyser application.

title: Charge noise in low Schottky barrier multilayer tellurium field-effect transistors
abstract: Multilayer tellurium FETs exhibit high-mobility p-type transport with a low Schottky barrier and low electrical noise governed by Hooge's mobility fluctuation model, positioning tellurium as a key material for low-noise, high performance devices.

title: Tuning polariton hybridization in hyperbolic hetero-bicrystals by twist angle engineering
abstract: Unlock new possibilities in polariton engineering with hetero-bicrystals, offering spectral gap manipulation, on–off switching for advanced nanophotonics.

title: Gold nanorods coated with self-assembled silk fibroin for improving their biocompatibility and facilitating targeted photothermal–photodynamic cancer therapy
abstract: Gold nanorods modified with silk fibroin and a peptide with drugs for reducing their biotoxicity and facilitating targeted photothermal–photodynamic cancer therapy.

title: Secondary electron dynamics in core–shell–satellite nanoparticles: a computational strategy for targeted cancer treatment
abstract: Influence of Fe : Au ratio on the dynamics of secondary electrons with varying configuration of Fe 3 O 4 –SiO 2 –Au nanoparticles for cancer X-ray therapy was investigated.

title: Tailoring electron redistribution and hydrogen adsorption in an epitaxial MnTe/Cr 2 Te 3 semiconductor/metal heterojunction for highly efficient hydrogen evolution catalysis
abstract: Solution-based Epitaxial MnTe/Cr 2 Te 3 semiconductor/metal heterojunctions enable directional electron transfer and optimized hydrogen adsorption, delivering enhanced HER performances.

title: Electrically and magnetically readable memory with a graphene/1T-CrTe 2 heterostructure: anomalous Hall transistor
abstract: The graphene/1T-CrTe 2 heterostructure shows electron transfer, exchange, and crystal field effects that shift and split graphene Dirac points, enabling an anomalous Hall transistor, spin-charge storage via spin-polarized anomalous Hall conductivity.

title: Ambipolar conduction in gated tungsten disulphide nanotube
abstract: High drain voltage bias enables ambipolar conduction in tungsten disulphide (WS 2 ) nanotube field-effect (photo-)transistors.

title: Understanding the interfacial interaction characteristics of asphalt nanocomposites reinforced with diamond nanothreads and carbon nanotubes
abstract: CNT-modified asphalt nanocomposites exhibit superior pullout performance compared to those reinforced with DNTs, primarily due to π–π stacking interactions that facilitate the wrapping of asphalt molecules around the CNT surfaces.

title: Single atom alloys aggregation in the presence of ligands
abstract: First principles calculations combined with machine learning reveal the key factors affecting the thermodynamic stability of single atom alloys in the presence and absence of surface ligands.

title: Ultrasensitive SERS-LFA for the detection of neurofilament light chain and machine learning-assisted Alzheimer's disease classification
abstract: A dual-mode lateral flow assay integrated with surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) and colorimetry, for ultrasensitive quantification of NfL and machine learning assisted clinical classification, for early Alzheimer's disease screening.

title: Metal cluster-mediated photocatalysis: synthesis, characterization and application
abstract: The review of metal cluster materials in photocatalysis including synthetic methodologies, especially AI-assisted synthesis, characterization techniques, and modification strategies and applications.

title: Nanoscale structural dynamics of cell edges in breast tumour cells revealed by scanning ion conductance microscopy
abstract: Time-lapse scanning ion conductance microscopy visualizes subcellular nanoscale structural dynamics at the edges of breast cancer cells.

title: Aligned conjugated polymer nanowires for enhanced performance in organic transistors and neuromorphic devices
abstract: Aligned PBTTT-C 14 nanowires fabricated via PDMS-guided edge-accumulation show enhanced molecular order, improved electrolyte interaction, and markedly boosted OECT and synaptic device performance.

title: Determination of the rate equations for erbium nanoparticles at arbitrary concentrations and drastically enhanced non-radiative transitions
abstract: Our focus is to determine the parameters of rate equations for erbium nanoparticles at arbitrary erbium concentrations for the first time. Our results can be used more generally for understanding complex nonlinear processes.

title: Phase transformations and magnetism in patterned FePd thin films
abstract: Solid-state dewetting can be engineered on demand by patterning, which can be used to tailor phase transformations as well as structural and magnetic properties of thin alloy films.

title: Chiral co-assembly of quantum dots and organic emitters enables high-efficiency circularly polarized luminescence via FRET
abstract: A chiral co-assembly strategy to prepare circularly polarized luminescent quantum dots has been developed by integrating red-emitting CdSe/ZnSe quantum dots (RQDs) into a polymer helical structure.

title: Rationally engineered Pd–Os nanosheets eliminating reactive oxygen and nitrogen species for in situ nanocatalytic therapy of ear inflammation
abstract: Two-dimensional Pd–Os nanozymes with outstanding antioxidative properties were developed for cell protection and in situ nanocatalytic treatment of ear inflammation by eliminating reactive oxygen and nitrogen species.

title: Correction: DNA conductance modulation via aptamer binding
abstract: Correction for ‘DNA conductance modulation via aptamer binding’ by Hashem Mohammad et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4nr05139d.

title: A high-sensitivity and wide-range flexible pressure sensor based on micro-pyramid arrays and porous nanofiber networks
abstract: Flexible pressure sensors have attracted significant attention as key components of smart wearable devices.

title: Ecofriendly fabrication of organic–inorganic fibers as a template for hollow titanium oxide structures via electrospinning and magnetron sputtering
abstract: Organic–inorganic fibers, serving as template for hollow structures, were obtained by coupling electrospinning with Ti magnetron sputtering. TiO x hollow nanofibers were developed using a procedure based on water solubilization of polymeric template.

title: Nickel-oxide embedded laser-induced graphene for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: This study explores the fabrication of nickel-oxide-embedded laser-induced graphene and its application in high-performance supercapacitors.

title: In situ fabrication of self-filtered near-infrared Ti 3 C 2 T x /n-Si Schottky-barrier photodiodes for a continuous non-invasive photoplethysmographic system
abstract: In situ fabrication of a Ti 3 C 2 T x /n-Si Schottky-barrier photodiode was achieved by post-etching treatment of a PLD-Ti 3 AlC 2 film. The unique self-filtered NIR photoresponse behavior of the device enables accurate detection of physiological parameters.

title: Enhancing stability and iterative learning in neuromorphic memristor via TiN/SiO x /TiN interface engineering
abstract: A TiN/SiO x /TiN memristor with an engineered oxygen reservoir layer exhibits linear and repeatable synaptic dynamics, achieving 92.21% MNIST recognition accuracy with improved stability, endurance, and gradual switching behavior.

title: In situ hybrid crosslinked poly-DOL quasi-solid-state electrolytes for stable high-voltage lithium metal batteries
abstract: A novel in situ hybrid crosslinked PDOL quasi-solid-state electrolyte is developed. It forms a 3D network polymer to lock high-voltage-resistant electrolytes, boosting ionic conductivity and oxidation resistance while enhancing battery performance.

title: Topological defects in semiconducting carbon nanotubes as triplet exciton traps and single-photon emitters
abstract: First-principles calculations reveal how topological defects in semiconducting carbon nanotubes trap triplet excitons and enable single-photon emission at telecom wavelengths, offering new insights into their potential for photonic devices.

title: Interplay of geometry and magnetic coupling in ferromagnetic nanorings
abstract: We have systematically investigated, using magneto-optical Kerr effect, the angular dependence of magnetization reversal in circular and rounded rectangular Ni 81 Fe 19 nanorings under both isolated and strongly interacting conditions.

title: A high output power density triboelectric nanogenerator based on surface-patterned PI/UIO-66-NH 2 mixed matrix membranes
abstract: A hierarchical optimization strategy integrating a PI matrix, MOF doping and surface patterning dramatically increases the output power density of a triboelectric nanogenerator, achieving a maximum output power density of 78.27 ± 3.42 μW cm −2 .

title: Atomic force microscopy as a multimetrological platform for energy devices
abstract: Nanowires, such as the ones used in energy applications, pose unique challenges in characterization due to their complexity and fragility. AFM provides a multimetrological platform with its various feedback methods to tackle this issue with finesse.

title: Electrochemical analysis of flexible symmetric supercapacitors using WSe 2 @graphite thin film electrodes under different pH conditions
abstract: A key focus of this research is to investigate the influence of electrolyte pH on the supercapacitive performance of a flexible symmetric supercapacitor (SS) based on WSe 2 @graphite composite electrodes.

title: Mechanism for spiral growth of β-antimonene on a pitted substrate: vacancy line aggregation triggered by nanoscale step-loops
abstract: Compressive strain regulates the growth modes of 2D β-Sb: twist spiral would form in the step-loops, since the space confinement reduces vacancy formation energy, inducing linear vacancy alignment and screw dislocation nucleation.

title: Dye-sensitized upconversion nanoparticles with enhanced aqueous luminescence for neuronal imaging
abstract: A novel near-infrared dye was synthesized to enhance the sensitization of upconversion nanoparticle (UCNP) system. Significant sensitivity improvements were observed in both organic and aqueous solutions.

title: How many electrons to stabilize the icosahedral Cu 55 core in ligated nanoclusters? The example of [Cu 55 (NHC) 6 ]
abstract: The Cu@Cu 12 @Cu 42 = Cu 52 Mackay superatom can be stabilized by the non-“magic” electron counts of 48, 50, 52 or 56.

title: Sub-5 nm monolayer SnNX (X = Cl, Br)-based homogeneous CMOS devices
abstract: Sub-5 nm homogeneous CMOS devices with ultra-high ON-state current of 4533 μA μm −1 can be achieved by SnNX (X = Cl, Br) monolayers.

title: Bi 2 O 2 Se nanosheets for dual-mode electrochemical/fluorescence turn-off sensing of ferric ions
abstract: Schematic of a dual electrochemical and fluorescence turn-off sensor based on exfoliated Bi 2 O 2 Se nanosheets, enabling highly sensitive, selective, and real-time detection of Fe 3+ ions.

title: Formation of electron-deficient Ni in a Nb/NiFe-layered double hydroxide nanoarray via electrochemical activation for efficient water oxidation
abstract: The Nb/NiFe-LDH nanoarray material was successfully synthesized, which has high-efficiency alkaline oxygen evolution activity.

title: In situ transmission electron microscopy insights into nanoscale deformation mechanisms of body-centered cubic metals
abstract: A schematic summary of deformation mechanisms and their interrelationships in BCC metals by in situ TEM is provided.

title: Ultrasensitive electroanalytical sensing platform using aptamer-conjugated V 2 CT x MXene for the detection of the HER-2 biomarker
abstract: A cost-effective, ultrasensitive electrochemical aptasensor using V 2 CT x MXene is developed for real-time, point-of-care HER-2 breast cancer detection, offering high sensitivity, selectivity, and suitability for resource-limited settings.

title: Regulation of the coordination number of Zn single atoms to boost electrochemical sensing of H 2 O 2
abstract: The inert element Zn was turned into an active electrocatalyst via atomization and coordination number regulation for boosting electrochemical sensing.

title: Low temperature inkjet-printed metal oxide sensors for sensitive and selective NO 2 detection
abstract: This study presents inkjet-printed metal oxide gas sensors, SnO 2 and In 2 O 3 , for detecting nitrogen dioxide (NO 2 ), demonstrating high sensitivity down to 10 ppb, with SnO 2 operating effectively at room temperature and In 2 O 3 at 100 °C.

title: A self-powered ice growth sensing system for transmission lines based on a triboelectric nanogenerator and a micro thermoelectric generator
abstract: Transmission line icing is a major natural hazard affecting overhead power lines, especially under specific meteorological conditions such as low temperatures, high humidity, and strong winds.

title: Dual additives balance phase distribution in all-bromide quasi-2D perovskites for spectrally stable pure-blue light-emitting diodes
abstract: The dual additives strategy with strong and weak confinement effectively regulates the balanced distribution of the n -phase in the all-bromide q-2D perovskite film, enabling the fabrication of pure blue PeLEDs with excellent spectral stability.

title: Deciphering the multifaceted role of oleic acid in synthesizing multibranched cobalt ferrite nanoparticles with enhanced magnetic properties
abstract: We decipher the multifaceted role of oleic acid in forming cobalt ferrite octapods evolving from spherical and cubic forms and their reversion to polyhedral shape over time, revealing a surfactant driven growth of anisotropic nanoparticles.

title: A flexible MXene–carbon nanotube-based triboelectric nanogenerator for self-powered pH sensors
abstract: The fabricated flexible triboelectric nanogenerator (FTENG) with MXene and carbon acts as a self-powered pH sensor.

title: Optical anisotropy in van der Waals heterostructures integrated with a low symmetry layered compound Nb 4 P 2 S 21
abstract: The optical and optoelectronic properties of an isotropic MoS 2 flake can be tuned to exhibit anisotropic responses by stacking it with a low-symmetry Nb 4 P 2 S 21 flake to form a vdW heterostructure.

title: One-pot synthesis of ultra-bright water-soluble gold nanoclusters for LAT1-targeted cancer cell imaging
abstract: We synthesized ultra-bright, water-soluble gold nanoclusters via a one-pot approach, demonstrating their excellent performance as fluorescent probes for LAT1-targeted cancer cell imaging.

title: Unlocking the potential of potassium-ion batteries: anode material mechanisms, challenges, and future directions
abstract: Potassium-ion batteries (PIBs), leveraging their abundant potassium resources, low cost, and a working principle analogous to that of lithium-ion batteries, have emerged as promising candidates for next-generation large-scale energy storage systems.

title: Catalytic and biological properties of Ag–Pt bimetallic nanoparticles: composition-dependent activity and cytotoxicity
abstract: This study elucidates the cytotoxic mechanisms of Ag–Pt bimetallic nanoparticles (AP NPs), highlighting how their catalytic oxidase-like activity interacts with composition to influence the biological responses.

title: Interfacial engineering and electronic structure modulation of two-dimensional WSe 2 towards improved electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: WSe 2 nanosheets with enlarged interlayer spacing were developed through the incorporation of organic inclusion compounds. WSe 2 NSs prepared with ammonium oxalate incorporation during the synthesis exhibited remarkable electrocatalytic HER activity.

title: Ionizable lipid nanoparticles enhance lung delivery of gold nanoclusters for improving acute lung injury alleviation
abstract: Ionizable lipid nanoparticles (iLNPs) were used as lung-delivery carriers to improve the alleviating efficiency of anti-inflammatory gold-nanoclusters in acute lung injury.

title: Quantum-dots-based phase separation into a 3D/0D perovskite heterojunction for boosting X-ray detector performance
abstract: Direct conversion X-ray detectors based on metal halide perovskites (MHPs), notably CsPbBr 3 , promise high performance but suffer from detrimental dark currents and ion migration.

title: Development of phenylboronic acid functionalized poly (lactic- co -glycolic acid) nanoparticles for novel co-delivery of chemo-herbal combination towards lung cancer: an in silico and in vitro proof of concept
abstract: This study includes the development of phenylboronic acid functionalized poly (lactic-co-glycolic acid) drugs co-loaded nanoparticles (PBA-PLGA-BCA-LTB NPs) for targeted drug delivery in lung cancer.

title: van der Waals pressure assisted synthesis of solid state nanomercury from mercury salts under ambient conditions: a sustainable approach for effortless Hg( ii ) removal from wastewater and safe storage thereof
abstract: Metal ion induced self-assembly of GOQDs in the presence of mercury ions and van der Waals pressure mediated solid state mercury formation upon Hg( ii ) reduction.

title: Sulphur-decorated Ti 3 C 2 MXene structures as high-capacity electrode for Zn-ion batteries: a DFT study
abstract: MXene-based structures have gained tremendous attention in energy storage applications, especially in ion batteries due to their promising electrical conductivity and high energy storage properties.

title: Photoswitching of luminescence from red emitting CsPbI 3 quantum dots at the single-particle level using a zwitterionic diarylethene ligand
abstract: Luminescence photoswitching of perovskite quantum dots/zwitterionic diarylethene hybrids has been demonstrated at the single nanoparticle level. The non-luminescent OFF state is formed solely by energy transfer from the quantum dot to diarylethene.

title: Placental targeted drug delivery: a review of recent progress
abstract: Key strategies for placental-targeted drug delivery.

title: Emerging devices based on chiral nanomaterials
abstract: Chiral nanomaterials, as a class of advanced materials, have recently attracted extensive attention on account of their unique physical and chemical properties that stem from their special geometry.

title: Plasmon-enhanced broadband visible-light optical limiter with low threshold in silver nanowire filled electrospun nanofibers
abstract: Silver nanowires (AgNWs) effectively enhance the nonlinear absorption (NA) properties of polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) nanofibers and significantly improve their optical limiting performance in the visible region.

title: A flexible all-solid-state supercapacitor based on carbon-supported Ni-embedded boron nitride
abstract: This work reports a new combination of Ni-embedded boron nitride with carbon derived from cigarette filters as an efficient electrode for a flexible all-solid-state asymmetric supercapacitor.

title: Microstructure modulation improving the stability performance of a Bi anode for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Metallic Bi is a classic metal-type anode material characterized by its high volume-specific capacity (3785 mA h cm −3 ) and theoretical specific capacity (386 mA h g −1 ).

title: Functionalization with polymer ligands enhances the catalytic activity of surfactant-stabilized gold nanoparticles
abstract: Polymer grafting boosts the catalytic activity of surfactant-stabilized gold nanoparticles and enhances stability in colloidal solution under reaction conditions, enabling catalyst recovery.

title: Recent progress in two-dimensional Bi 2 O 2 Se and its heterostructures
abstract: Overview of the key aspects of 2D Bi 2 O 2 Se and representative schematic diagrams.

title: Surface-engineered core–shell upconversion nanoparticles for effective hypericin delivery and multimodal imaging
abstract: Poly( N , N -dimethylacrylamide- co -2-aminoethylacrylate)-alendronate-coated core–shell upconversion nanoparticles have considerable potential for effective delivery of hypericin to tumors. The particles enabled multimodal detection of cancer cells.

title: Synergistic enhancement of physicochemical and biomedical properties in microwave-synthesized copper-doped zinc oxide nanoparticles
abstract: The synthesis of Zn 1-x Cu x O NPs using microwave technique.

title: A broadband long-wavelength infrared all-dielectric metasurface absorber based on Mie resonance
abstract: An all-dielectric metasurface LWIR absorber achieves >96% average absorption (8–14 μm) with angle- and polarization insensitivity, enabling infrared camouflage and microbolometer applications.

title: Two-dimensional 1T-phase Mn x Ir 1− x O 2 for high-performance acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Two-dimensional 1T-phase Mn x Ir 1− x O 2 can achieve high electrochemical performance and endurance of PEMWE with only a small amount of Ir, providing a feasible approach to alleviate the problems of low Ir reserves and high prices.

title: Introduction to theranostic nanoplatforms for biomedicine
abstract: Nguyen Thi Kim Thanh, Sylvie Begin, Sophie Laurent and Teresa Pellegrino introduce this Nanoscale and Nanoscale Advances themed issue on theranostic nanoplatforms for biomedicine.

title: Bamboo-inspired anisotropic hydrogels with enhanced mechanical properties via cellulose nanocrystal-reinforced heterostructures
abstract: We report a biomimetic hydrogel reinforced with cellulose nanocrystals that mimics the anisotropic mechanical properties of bamboo, enhancing both strength and functionality.

title: Effect of kidney disease and vascular calcification on the circulation and distribution of tetracycline-functionalized polymer nanoparticles
abstract: The ability of tetracycline-decorated polymer nanoparticles to target vascular calcification in a rat model of chronic kidney disease is evaluated.

title: Plasma-induced nanogap narrowing and morphological transformation in gold nanoparticle assemblies
abstract: Plasmon coupling between gold nanoparticles evolves as plasma treatment narrows the nanogaps within the assemblies, eventually welding the nanoparticles.

title: Mathematically inspired structure design in nanoscale thermal transport
abstract: Mathematically inspired structure design has emerged as a powerful approach for tailoring material properties, especially in nanoscale thermal transport, with promising applications both within this field and beyond.

title: Optically/electrically controlled Ag + metallization in solution-processed oxide memtransistors for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Low-cost, solution-processed oxide memtransistor with stable resistive switching, ultra-low voltage and power, tunable synaptic behavior, and high neural network accuracy (98% optical and 95% electrical), enabling energy-efficient neuromorphic use. Head image by Tumisu via Pixabay.

title: A tunable ion relaxation process of a thermoelectric system for adjustable memory of thermal perception
abstract: Interfacial modification of ion diffusion channels interferes with the ion relaxation of an ionic thermoelectric system to achieve tunable memory.

title: Correction: Transformable nanodrugs for overcoming the biological barriers in the tumor environment during drug delivery
abstract: Correction for ‘Transformable nanodrugs for overcoming the biological barriers in the tumor environment during drug delivery’ by Xuejian Li et al. , Nanoscale , 2023, 15 , 8532–8547, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2NR06621A.

title: Tunable photoluminescence on 2D WS 2 quantum dots
abstract: Ultrasonic and laser exposure on solutions of WS 2 materials allows the synthesis of bright 2D quantum emitters.

title: Correlative characterization of molecular two-dimensional van der Waals material heterostructures on the nanometer scale
