abstract: The fabrication and characterisation of the 2D molecular heterostructure consisting of graphene, rhodamine 6G and MoS 2 is presented. We demonstrate that the molecular layer modifies the polariton frequencies of MoS 2 .

title: An operando photoconductivity platform reveals defect–adsorbate–carrier coupling in ultrathin MoS 2 for solar-driven CO 2 reduction
abstract: An operando photoconductivity platform uncovers defect–adsorbate–carrier coupling in ultrathin MoS 2 , offering mechanistic insights into persistent photoconductivity and advancing solar-driven CO 2 reduction catalysis.

title: Conduction band photonic trapping via band gap reversal of brookite quantum dots using controlled graphitization for tuning a multi-exciton photoswitchable high-performance semiconductor
abstract: Brookite exists as the metastable phase of titania and often mediates the transformation of anatase to rutile.

title: Trivalent ion-exchanged magadiite: structural and textural modifications accompanied by spontaneous nanoparticle formation
abstract: Cation exchange of magadiite with trivalent metal ions (M 3+ ) disrupts its layered structure, increases porosity, and leads to M 3+ grafting and nanoparticle formation.

title: Engineering ultra-small nanoceria with antioxidant and UV-shielding properties as functional nanomaterials in composite coatings for complex surface protection
abstract: The development of advanced catalytic shields against UV-induced and oxidative degradation phenomena is critical to address multifaceted deterioration processes.

title: Fabrication of AFM tips for tip-enhanced Raman spectroscopy using Au nanoparticle monolayer films and electrodeposition
abstract: Lab-fabricated AFM-TERS tips were achieved via a facile approach bansed on the assembly of nanoparticles and electrodeposition. An enhancement factor of 3.2 × 10 4 offered the promising practical application of TERS.

title: Self-limiting selective phase separation of graphene oxide and polymer composite solution
abstract: Self-limiting selective phase separation (SPS) behavior was reported in the mixture of graphene oxide and polymers solutions. We invented SPS spinning to fabricate hierarchically structured graphene-based fibers with extraordinary flexibility.

title: Machine learning recognition of hybrid lead halide perovskites and perovskite-related structures from X-ray diffraction patterns
abstract: We proposed a simple approach for quickly identifying the dimensionality of inorganic substructures, types of connections of lead halide polyhedra and structure types using common powder XRD data and a ML-decision tree classification model.

title: Colloidal semiconductor quantum shells for solution-processed laser applications
abstract: Laser diodes based on solution-processed semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) present an economical and color-tunable alternative to traditional epitaxial lasers.

title: Nickel single atoms anchored on a bipyridine-based covalent organic framework: boosting active sites for photodegradation of acetaminophen
abstract: Single Ni atom photocatalyst Ni-TpBpy with tetra-coordinated N 2 –Ni–Cl 2 environment showed boosted visible-light-driven photodegradation activity of ACT, attributed to efficient O 2 adsorption and activation, enhanced charge separation and migration.

title: Fabrication of nanoporous anodized aluminum oxide based photonic crystals with multi-band responses in the vis-NIR region
abstract: This work demonstrates the AAO-based photonic crystals (PC) with multi-band response in the visible-near-infrared (vis-NIR) region, which exhibits structural color and optical Tamm states (OTS) properties.

title: Systematic probing of protein adsorption on protein-based nanoparticles in dependence of the particle surface charge
abstract: Fluorescence correlation spectroscopy reveals how surface charge affects protein adsorption on nanocages.

title: Photocatalytic synthesis of hydrogen peroxide: recent advances, challenges, and future perspectives
abstract: General overview of the optimization strategies for high-efficiency photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production.

title: Ab initio investigation of layered TMGeTe 3 alloys for phase-change applications
abstract: We substituted Cr in CrGeTe 3 with other 3d transition metals and identified two promising candidates, ScGeTe 3 and ZnGeTe 3 , with electronic contrast between amorphous and crystalline states as PCM and MnGeTe 3 for magnetic switching.

title: Light-induced charge transfer from a fullerene to a zeolitic imidazolate framework enhances alkaline electrocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Light-induced charge transfer from a photo-responsive fullerene C 60 carbon cage to the Co metal sites of flake-like ZIF-67 enhances the OER activity under UV light irradiation.

title: Effect of the number ratio and size ratio on the formation of binary superlattices assembled from polymer-tethered spherical nanoparticles of two sizes
abstract: Binary superlattices can be prepared via the self-assembly of polystyrene tethered spherical gold nanoparticles of two kinds, and the unique configurations of the BNSLs are controllable by tuning the sizes and number ratios of the NPs.

title: Potential relationship of spin magnetic moment with thermal conductivity and catalytic performance in Fe–Co bimetallic catalysts: a machine-learning interatomic potential and density functional theory study
abstract: The phonon group velocity and phonon lifetime reveal the direct factors of the high thermal conductivity of G-FeCoN 6 -3. However, the magnetic moment is a potential factor affecting the thermal conductivity and catalytic performance.

title: Nanostructured films from poly(3-hexylthiophene)- graft -poly(ε-caprolactone) as light-responsive generators of reactive oxygen species
abstract: Photoactive films made from poly(3-hexylthiophene)- graft -poly(ε-caprolactone) copolymers with tunable nanostructured morphologies through thermo-oxidation, modulating reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation upon photostimulation.

title: N-/Si-co-doped nanocrystalline diamonds as highly sensitive luminescent thermometers
abstract: Highly thermal-sensitive luminescent nanothermometers, thanks to the luminescence characteristics of NV- and SiV- centres, were designed based on N-/Si-co-doped nanocrystalline diamonds, enabling its use in biological tissues.

title: Fabricating 3A optical films via transfer printing from a nickel nanocone array template
abstract: A nickel nanocone array template is fabricated by controlling screw-dislocation growth through electrodeposition and then is applied to transfer-print resin structures, resulting in a nanostructured 3A optical film.

title: Nano-bismuth vanadate supported on fibrous silica reduces the intrinsic charge impedance for superior photoelectrochemical water-splitting performance
abstract: An FSBVO photoanode was synthesized for PEC water-splitting, and it showed a photocurrent density of 19.8 mA cm −2 at 1.23 V RHE .

title: Electrospun freestanding anodes for metal-ion batteries: structural design and application
abstract: This review highlights the structural design of freestanding nanofibrous anodes for a thorough understanding of the synergistic effect between structural design and anode active materials in metal-ion batteries.

title: Atomic insights into the electrocatalytic properties of LaBO 3 perovskite oxides for lithium–sulfur battery performance
abstract: LaBO 3 electrocatalysts effectively anchor Li 2 S n , accelerate SRR kinetics, and reduce the Li 2 S decomposition energy barrier.

title: Single-step, conformal, and efficient assembly of ligand-exchanged quantum dots for optoelectronic devices via an electric field
abstract: In this work the authors combined in-solution ligand exchange with an electrically driven film deposition technique to produce conformal PbSe quantum dot films on non-flat substrates in a single step without the need for post deposition processing.

title: Two-dimensional [Co(btbH) 2 (dpe) 2 ]·DMF metal–organic framework-derived low-cost nanocomposites for electrochemical nitrate reduction for ammonia production
abstract: CoNPs/NC-600, derived from 2D [Co(btbH) 2 (dpe) 2 ]·DMF MOF, show excellent NO 3 RR performance with 72.25% FE and −66.03 mA cm −2 partial current density at −0.5 V vs . RHE.

title: Evidence of Au(111) topological states in a kagome analogue lattice and their robustness beyond ultra-low temperatures and defect-free conditions
abstract: Kagome analogue lattice formed by the self-assembled molecular structure on Au(111) gives rise to the robustness of the topological states across varying conditions, highlighting their stability and potential for practical applications. Graphical abstract image developed using CorelDRAW.

title: Effect of acid leaching on the denitrification efficiency of a MnCe@FA catalyst derived from fly ash: structure, crystal phase, active component interaction phase and active components
abstract: Acid modification of fly ash makes it a high quality denitrification catalyst carrier. It has shown significance for the resource utilization of low-temperature denitrification catalysts with fly ash.

title: Temperature-dependent sign reversal of tunneling magnetoresistance in van der Waals ferromagnetic heterojunctions
abstract: The temperature-dependent reversal of tunneling magnetoresistance (TMR) polarity, along with the coexistence of positive and negative TMR in van der Waals magnetic tunnel junctions, originates from the magnetization of the tunneling barriers.

title: Multivariate chemometric design of nitric oxide-releasing chitosan nanoparticles for skin-related biomedical applications
abstract: Chemometric optimization of nitric oxide-releasing chitosan nanoparticles enabled enhanced stability, sustained NO delivery, and increased intracellular S -nitrosylation for skin-related biomedical therapies.

title: Similar structure but different thermodynamic, dielectric, and frictional properties of confined water in twisted 2D materials: MoS 2 vs. graphene
abstract: Nanoconfined water in twisted 2D materials, such as molybdenum disulfide and graphene, could have a similar structure, yet exhibit different thermodynamic, dielectric, and frictional properties.

title: Enhancing hydrogen separation performance through ZIF-8-incorporated PMMA–PC blend-based mixed matrix membranes
abstract: A novel approach for the H 2 gas separation was developed using mixed matrix membranes based on a polymethyl methacrylate–polycarbonate (PMMA–PC) blend, embedded with ZIF-8 nanoparticles.

title: Dynamics of ultrafine silver implanted DNA nanowire formation
abstract: The process of silver (Ag) cluster growth on a DNA template is investigated using a single-molecule technique. How Ag modifies the mechanics of the DNA molecule is discussed by means of optical tweezers measurements.

title: FRET-driven hybrid polymer–perovskite matrices for efficient pure-red emission
abstract: Enhancing the pure-red perovskite emission via Förster Resonance Energy Transfer (FRET) in a polymer–perovskite blend system, leveraging optimal spectral overlap between polymer emission and perovskite absorption.

title: Surface defects in atom-precise copper nanoclusters and their different catalytic efficiencies
abstract: Atomic-level insights into surface defects of nanoclusters reveal that subtle differences lead to distinct catalytic behaviors, guiding future rational design of functional nanomaterials for catalysis.

title: Quantitative photoacoustic spectral transformations in theranostic solid lipid nanoparticles labelled with increasing concentrations of a photoacoustic NIR BODIPY
abstract: Transformations of photoacoustic and absorption spectra with increasing BODIPY dye content in solid lipid nanoparticles reveal optical and photothermal phenomena.

title: Aromatic ring compounds with different conjugation degrees in a boronic acid matrix to realize multicolor phosphorescence for time division colorful multiplexing
abstract: Schematic illustration of the synthesis of 2-Ph@BA, 9-Ph@BA and 1-Py@BA, and the actual pictures of RTP CDs after the UV light excitation is turned off.

title: Broad-spectrum light-responsive N-doped graphene quantum dots for efficient photocatalytic generation of hydroxyl radicals and antibacterial applications
abstract: Metal-free N-doped graphene quantum dots efficiently convert H 2 O 2 into hydroxyl radicals under visible light and exhibit light-switchable antibacterial activity, enabling broadband carbon photocatalysis.

title: Glucose intercalation-induced 1T-G-MoS 2 hybrids for high-performance rechargeable aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Stable 1T-rich MoS 2 hybrids (1T-G-MoS 2 ) prepared by glucose intercalation induction possess an enlarged interlayer spacing and increased conductivity, thereby accelerating the reaction kinetics and improving the electrochemical performance of ZAIBs.

title: Effect of titanium–copper co-substitution in O3-type layered oxide cathodes on the electrochemical performance of sodium-ion batteries
abstract: NNCMT cathode exhibits high capacity, excellent cycling stability and air stability.

title: MXenes in healthcare: transformative applications and challenges in medical diagnostics and therapeutics
abstract: Schematic representation of the multifaceted applications of MXenes in biomedical fields. This figure was Created with Biorender.com.

title: CeO 2 - and Mn 3 O 4 -based nanozymes exhibit scavenging of singlet oxygen species and hydroxyl radicals
abstract: Dex-CeO 2 and Dex-Mn 3 O 4 nanoparticles exhibit hydroxyl radical and singlet oxygen scavenging activities, with the potential to alleviate oxidative stress in mammalian systems.

title: Synergistic design of a graphene oxide-mediated polyaniline/α-Fe 2 O 3 ternary heterostructure: advancing photocatalytic degradation and adsorption efficiency
abstract: This research involved synthesizing a novel Z-scheme PANI/GO/α-Fe 2 O 3 ternary heterostructure and exploring its potential for degrading organic pollutants under visible light.

title: Origin and mitigation of the imprint effect in hafnia-based ferroelectrics
abstract: A new imprint mechanism in HfO 2 -based ferroelectrics—the oxygen vacancy segregation-charge trapping model—enables a novel method for its effective mitigation.

title: Emerging frontiers in chiral metal–organic framework membranes: diverse synthesis techniques and applications
abstract: In this review, the preparation and applications of chiral metal–organic framework (MOF) membranes for sensing and separation of enantiomers and circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) are summarized.

title: A new optical fiber sensor based on the CDs@UiO66 complex fluorescence probe for nitric oxide detection
abstract: A new optical fiber NO sensor based on the carbon quantum dots (CDs)@UiO66 complex is prepared, with a low NO detection limit of 5 nM.

title: Tuning structures and properties of self-healing silsesquioxane films using block copolymers
abstract: Lamellar organosiloxane films with crack-healing ability were prepared using PEO–PPO–PEO block copolymers, showing improved hardness and solvent resistance compared to those prepared with alkylammonium surfactants.

title: Two-photon pumped forward, backward and random lasing in a stilbazolium dye nanocomposite solution containing LAPONITE® as a scattering center
abstract: Multiphoton pumped upconversion regular and random lasing in a dye solution containing LAPONITE® nanoparticles as scattering centers.

title: Reservoir computing with networks of nanoscale memristors: optimisation of memristor responses for maximal computational performance
abstract: Self-assembled networks of memristors can successfully perform various computational tasks. Here we demonstate the effect of tuning memristor parameters on computational performance.

title: 3D and in situ electron microscopy studies of the nucleation and growth processes of cobalt-based nanoparticles synthesized via thermal decomposition on carbon nanotubes
abstract: The confinement effect of carbon nanotubes (CNTs) on the solvothermal synthesis of cobalt-based nanoparticles (NPs) was investigated using 3D TEM and in situ TEM environmental-cell analyses.

title: Fe/Fe 3 C nanoparticles encapsulated in ZIF-8-derived carbon nanotubes as a cathode oxygen reduction catalyst for microbial fuel cells
abstract: Fe/Fe 3 C@NiNC, derived from Fe, Ni co-doped ZIF-8, shows enhanced ORR activity and durability via CNT encapsulated Fe/Fe 3 C nanoparticles and fast electron transfer, with E 1/2 of 0.87 V vs. RHE and MFC peak power of 1600 mW m −2 .

title: Sub-5 nm monolayer KMgX (X = P, As, Sb)-based homogeneous CMOS devices for high-performance applications
abstract: Sub-5 nm monolayer KMgX (X = P, As, Sb) MOSFETs possess ultrahigh ON-state current and a high level of symmetry between n- and p-type devices, beneficial to the development of homogeneous CMOS.

title: Analogous copper nanoclusters (Cu 16/17 ) with two electron superatomic and mixed valence copper( ii )/copper( i ) and copper( i )/copper(0) characters
abstract: Three in one: Three Cu NCs of three different characters ( e.g. two electron superatomic and mixed-valent Cu II /Cu I and Cu I /Cu 0 ), stabilized in a common Cu 12 S 12 truncated octahedron, are reported.

title: Mn-based Li-rich layered oxides obtained by double-surface modification engineering as high performance cathode materials for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The performance of Mn-based Li-rich materials was improved by adopting the double-surface modification technology.

title: Recent advances in nanoporous NO x gas sensors: synergizing Raman spectroscopy, IoT, and machine learning for high-performance detection
abstract: NO x are toxic pollutants causing respiratory issues. Nanoporous materials like MOFs, COFs, and hybrids show promise in chemiresistive NO x sensing, with Raman spectroscopy, IoT, and machine learning boosting selectivity and real-time monitoring.

title: Nanoscale insights into vibration-induced heterogeneous ice nucleation
abstract: Acoustic waves at the left wall create negative pressure in supercooled water (blue) inside a nanopore, initiating ice formation (white) that merges into a growing ice front moving towards the right wall.

title: Hierarchical assembly of upconversion nanoparticle–grating hybrids on flexible nanofibers for multi-level physical unclonable functions
abstract: Interfacial co-assembly and microcontact printing place silver nanocubes with upconverting nanoparticles on electrospun membranes, yielding five-fold upconversion luminescence and angle-dependent rainbow holography with unclonable patterns.

title: Small upconversion-ruthenium nanohybrids for cancer theranostics
abstract: NIR-triggered drug release from upconversion nanoparticles functionalized with mesoporous silica shells destroys the nuclei of cancer cells.

title: Sensitive detection of miRNA-141 using an endogenous dual-switch activated bipedal DNAzyme walker biosensor
abstract: We developed an endogenous dual-gated fluorescence biosensor by leveraging miRNA-141 and APE1 overexpression in tumors, employing a cascade dual-amplification strategy via strand displacement and bipedal DNAzyme walking.

title: Structural and optical properties of in situ Eu-doped ZnCdO/ZnMgO superlattices grown by plasma-assisted molecular beam epitaxy
abstract: In situ Eu-doped {ZnCdO/ZnMgO} superlattices (SLs) with varying ZnCdO and ZnMgO sublayer thicknesses were deposited using plasma-assisted molecular beam epitaxy.

title: Potential-modulated SERS profiling via GLAD-fabricated Ag nanorod arrays for ultrasensitive and label-free spectroelectrochemical sensing
abstract: GLAD-fabricated AgNRs@SPE for an ultrasensitive and label-free EC-SERS detection platform, suitable for portable and onsite pharmaceutical, drug delivery, and analytical applications.

title: Fine-tuning the Ni/Co ratio to elucidate the coordination structure–activity relationship of MOF-derived bimetallic layered double hydroxides for highly sensitive enzyme-free lactate biosensors
abstract: A straightforward and controllable synthesis method for nickel–cobalt bimetallic layered double hydroxides derived from a metal–organic framework was designed for enzyme-free lactate biosensors.

title: Green synthesis of zinc oxide nanoparticles using plant extract for catalysis applications
abstract: The peel extract of Citrus sinensis was used as a reducing and capping agent for green ZnO NPs synthesis, characterized using XRD, SEM, FT-IR, TGA/DTA, UV-Vis, and TEM techniques. These materials efficiently catalyzed the alkylation of acetophenone.

title: Deep-learning-enabled high-throughput Screening of MXene photocatalysts for hydrogen production
abstract: Workflow of deep-learning-enabled high-throughput screening for photocatalytic materials.

title: Femtosecond laser processing of carbon nanotubes: synthesis, surface modification, and cutting
abstract: This paper focuses on the femtosecond laser processing technology for carbon nanotubes.

title: Fabrication of nanoparticles with precisely controllable plasmonic properties as tools for biomedical applications
abstract: Highly homogenous nanoparticles fabricated via nanoimprint lithography with minimal batch-to-batch size variation and tailor-made plasmonic properties. Polymer surface modification ensures functionality, good biocompatibility and minor cytotoxicity.

title: Enhanced stability and optical performance of CsPbBr 3 @FAPbBr 3 core–shell perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: A double-shelling strategy with FAPbBr 3 and SiO 2 shells is proposed to significantly improve the linear and nonlinear optical performance of CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals and ensure their stability under harsh environmental conditions.

title: Application of carbon quantum dots in smart polymer films for biomedical diagnostics
abstract: In the era of personalized medicine and wearable technologies, there is a growing demand for flexible, biocompatible, and highly sensitive sensors capable of continuous biomarker monitoring directly on the skin surface. The graphical abstract was generated using Copilot and BioRender.

title: Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 /HfO 2 /Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 laminated thin films and CF 4 plasma passivation for improved memory and synaptic characteristics of ferroelectric field-effect transistors
abstract: The simultaneous application of ferroelectric lamination and CF 4 plasma treatment can enhance the memory and synaptic properties of HZO-based FeFETs, serving as a potential engineering knob for future neuromorphic applications.

title: Impact of lignin structuration on its chemical and adhesive properties at the nanoscale: involvement with native and polymer matrices
abstract: This work highlights the use of the Langmuir–Blodgett technique rather than the more traditional chemical grafting method to control AFM tip functionalization with lignin increasing the resolution of adhesion force measurements at the nanoscale.

title: A new computational approach for evaluating bending rigidity of graphene sheets incorporating disclinations
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) materials exhibit remarkable flexibility and can be transformed into various shapes.

title: Laser-assisted thermoelectric-enhanced hydrogen peroxide biosensors based on Ag 2 Se nanofilms for sensitive detection of bacterial pathogens
abstract: This study presents a thermoelectric biosensor using Ag 2 Se nanofilms on 3D-printed resin for precise H 2 O 2 detection. It accurately quantifies S. sanguinis and MRSA , achieving a detection threshold of 10 4 CFU ml −1 , meeting clinical standards.

title: Atomic doping and light irradiation promote anodic hydrogen evolution through furfural oxidation on Cu 2+1 O/Cu nano-dendrites
abstract: Atomic Pd doped Cu 2+1 O/Cu nanodendrites serve as more active electrocatalysts for the anodic hydrogen evolution reaction via furfural oxidation. Notably, visible light irradiation could significantly enhance the reaction kinetics.

title: Thermal conductivity of individual single-crystalline F 16 CuPc nanoribbons
abstract: Thermal conductivity and the thermal transport mechanism of single-crystalline F 16 CuPc are studied.

title: Nanoscale imaging and atomic vibrations of eumelanin superstructures modulated by functionalized micronized graphene oxide
abstract: Bioactive affinity of natural eumelanin units, in response to external stimuli from functionalized graphene oxide, leads to the structural disassembly of irregular nanosheets and reassembles them into spherical and elongated nanostructures.

title: Correction: A green carbon dot@silver nanoparticle hybrid: as a turn-on fluorescent probe for the detection and quantification of cholesterol and glucose
abstract: Correction for ‘A green carbon dot@silver nanoparticle hybrid: as a turn-on fluorescent probe for the detection and quantification of cholesterol and glucose’ by Nasrin Rahmatian et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 10043–10056, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR00629E.

title: Water-soluble N-heterocyclic carbene stabilized gold nanoparticles by top-down synthesis: performance in catalysis and photoacoustic imaging
abstract: N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) stabilized gold nanoparticles synthesized by the 'top-down' approach show greater promise in catalysis and photoacoustic imaging compared to their 'bottom-up' counterparts.

title: Regulation of phenol oxidation into polymeric derivatives ready for flocculation using polyaluminum chloride
abstract: Phenol is firstly oxidized into polymeric derivatives with fewer H 2 O 2 andi Fe-TAML catalysts and then flocculated by polyaluminum chloride. The new strategy is more cost-effective than conventional advanced oxidation processes

title: Interface-engineered Bi 2 O 3 /N-doped carbon heterostructure enabling synergistic effects for advanced energy storage
abstract: Metal oxide/carbon nanocomposites have emerged as prospective electrodes for electrochemical energy storage.

title: Sequential self-assembly and release of a camptothecin prodrug for tumor-targeting therapy
abstract: A CPT prodrug (CPT-SS-FFEYp-Biotin) based on three strategies (tumor-targeting, prodrug design and drug delivery) for enhancing therapeutic efficacy of CPT.

title: Chiral nanomaterials as vaccine adjuvants: a new horizon in immunotherapy
abstract: Chiral nanomaterials are emerging as a promising class of vaccine adjuvants with the potential to significantly enhance vaccine efficacy, especially in the context of cancer immunotherapy.

title: Energy harvesting from NiCo 2 S 4 /Co x S y nanoflakes: a two-fold strategy by morphology control and using redox-additive electrolytes
abstract: A two-fold approach of choosing an appropriate morphology and a redox-additive electrolyte has been adopted to improve the supercapacitive performance of macroporous NiCo 2 S 4 /Co x S y nanoflakes.

title: A tale of two syntheses: a comparative study of the physical and photocatalytic properties of covalent triazine frameworks (CTFs)
abstract: Two synthesis methods followed by thermal- or mechanical treatment lead to CTF-1 variants with different catalytic-, photoelectrochemical- and structural properties.

title: Platelet backpacking nanoparticles based on bacterial outer membrane vesicles enhanced photothermal-immune anti-tumor therapy
abstract: IR780-SLN@O–P nanoparticles, delivered to the tumor site by platelet “backpacking”, subsequently act in concert with photothermal therapy and immunotherapy to effectively suppress tumor growth.

title: Role of the Pr/Ni loading sequence in boosting the photothermal catalytic activity of CeO 2 for methane dry reforming
abstract: This work demonstrates enhanced photothermal catalytic performance for dry reforming of methane through a strategic Pr/Ni loading sequence on CeO 2 , achieving 80.1% CH 4 and 83.1% CO 2 conversions with an ideal H 2 /CO ratio of 1.0.

title: Transparent perovskite-based nanoceramics elaborated from full glass crystallization
abstract: Transparent GdAlO 3 based perovskite nanocrystalline ceramics were prepared for the first time using a low-temperature (1000 °C) glass crystallization of 74 mol% Al 2 O 3 –26 mol% Gd 2 O 3 bulk glass.

title: Influence of zinc oxide nanoparticles on the carbon accumulation on silver exposed to carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction conditions
abstract: Ambient pressure X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy and scanning tunneling microscopy elucidate the effect of carbon deposits on a silver catalyst with and without zinc oxide nanoparticles.

title: Silver-functionalized carbon dots regulate amyloid aggregation and microbial infection
abstract: Silver-functionalized carbon dots efficiently inhibit amyloid aggregation, disaggregate amyloid aggregates and relieve microbial infections. Created by https://Figdraw.com.

title: Differentiating α-synuclein aggregates using charge-sensitive gold nanoclusters
abstract: Label-free gold nanoclusters (AuNCs) with tailored surface charges distinguish wild type and mutant α-syn monomers from amyloids with pM-level sensitivity.

title: RuN x clusters anchored on hierarchically porous N-doped carbon spheres for efficient alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: In situ anchoring of ultrafine Ru clusters on hierarchically porous N-doped carbon spheres (Ru/NCS) was achieved to optimize the Ru–H/Ru–OH bond strength and facilitate bubble transport in alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: Impacts of localized charge accumulation on photocurrent dynamics in metal−MoS 2 contacts
abstract: Monolayer molybdenum disulfide (1L-MoS 2 ) has attracted a lot of attention due to its excellent electrical and optoelectronic properties.

title: The effect of atomic vibration on thermal transport in diatomic semiconductors investigated via ab initio molecular dynamics
abstract: Based on AIMD methods, temperature and velocity statistics of diatomic semiconductors are classified by atomic species, facilitating the understanding of their electronic properties at finite temperatures.

title: Unravelling the origin of strong non-reciprocal chiroptical features in thin films of a chiral diketopyrrolo[3,4- c ]pyrrole dye
abstract: A chiral bis-anthracenyl-DPP dye was studied via ab initio calculations, microscopy and synchrotron radiation Mueller matrix polarimetry imaging to quantify the linear contributions responsible for the strong non-reciprocal features in thin films.

title: Biodegradable cellulose nanocrystal composites doped with carbon dots for packaging and anticounterfeiting applications
abstract: This work focuses on water-based carbon dots and CNCs from Vigna mungo , used in anti-counterfeit inks and sensor films. These CDs are UV-visible, pH-sensitive, antimicrobial, and biodegradable, offering multifunctional packaging solutions.

title: Advancing thermoelectric and sensing performance in constrained GeTe thin films
abstract: The TE performance of GeTe thin films was significantly enhanced by the application of tensile stress. Thermoelectric film devices based on the optimized GeTe thin films demonstrated superior self-power supply and sensing performance.

title: Design of engineered nanoparticles for biomedical applications by computational modeling
abstract: Engineered nanoparticles exhibit superior physicochemical, antibacterial, optical, and sensing properties, rendering them attractive for biomedical applications. Multiscale modeling aids in understanding and optimizing these properties.

title: One-pot synthesis of photonic microparticles doped with light-emitting quantum dots
abstract: One-pot synthesis of hybrid photonic microparticles embedded with quantum dots showing vivid structural colours and stable photoluminescence for advanced nanotechnologies.

title: Boron nitride memristors: from mechanism and device optimization to integrated applications
abstract: This review comprehensively introduces the preparation, performance, mechanism and application of BN memristors, making up for the lack of content in this field.

title: A multifunctional wearable hydrogel-based charge-trapping TENG enabling electrophysiological signal monitoring and high-efficiency energy harvesting with enhanced stability
abstract: A stretchable and biocompatible HCTEG utilizes EWCI to achieve enhanced triboelectric output (207.9 V, 88.8 nC), enabling efficient energy harvesting (23.2 μW cm −2 ) and high-fidelity (SNR > 23 dB) bio-signal monitoring with enhanced stability.

title: General synthesis of nanostructured phase-pure bimetallic carbides of molybdenum and tungsten
abstract: Nanostructured transition metal carbides are considered to be promising catalytic materials.

title: Cost-efficient folding of functionalized DNA origami nanostructures via staple recycling
abstract: Cost-efficient folding of DNA origami nanostructures is demonstrated by reusing unincorporated staple strands from previous folding reactions. This allows for a reduction in staple costs by up to 41% for large numbers of folding reactions.

title: Construction of bimetallic oxy-hydroxides based on Ni(OH) 2 nanosheets for sensitive non-enzymatic glucose detection via electrochemical oxidation and incorporation
abstract: Bimetallic oxy-hydroxides based on Ni(OH) 2 nanosheets ((CoNi)O x /Ni(OH) 2 @NF) were prepared by an electrochemical oxidation and incorporation method as high performance non-enzymatic sensors for glucose detection.

title: Mitochondria-targeting nanostructures from enzymatically degradable fluorescent amphiphilic polyesters
abstract: pH-responsive aqueous nanoassembly from a fluorescent neutral polyester selectively targets mitochondria in cancer cells over non-cancerous cells due to positive charge gain, while similar assemblies from cationic polyesters show no such selectivity.

title: Spectral and dynamical properties of multiexcitons in semiconductor nanorods
abstract: Applying an MCMC sampling approach for global target analysis enables us to determine the contributions of multiple exciton decay via Auger recombination and the contribution of surface exciton states to the observed decay dynamics.

title: Correlating photochemical H 2 production and excited state lifetimes of heterostructured and doped ZnCdS nanoparticles
authors: 'Mathew T. Fortunato', 'Joseph M. O'Shea', 'Jie Huang', 'Hashini Chandrasiri', 'Eun Byoel Kim', 'Abdelqader M. Jamhawi', 'A. Jean-Luc Ayitou', 'Preston T. Snee', 'Claudia Turro'
abstract: A variety of ZnCdS-based semiconductor nanoparticle heterostructures with extended exciton lifetimes were synthesized to enhance the efficacy of photocatalytic hydrogen production in water.

title: Electrochemically synthesized Cu–Mg mesh catalysts for methanol steam reforming
abstract: Cu–Mg-mesh catalyst exhibits excellent catalytic activity in methanol steam reforming (MSR).

title: 3D real-time single particle tracking using two-photon fluorescence from bright dye-based organic nanoparticles
abstract: Two-photon single particle tracking with unique precision using dye-based organic nanoparticles.

title: Understanding fatigue and recovery mechanisms in Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 capacitors for designing high endurance ferroelectric memory and neuromorphic hardware
abstract: Back-end-of-line compatible HZO devices with wake-up free Pr shows recovery from fatigue state up to 90% of pristine value through designed fast recovery pulsing.

title: All-in-one photothermal/catalytic flexible membrane for highly efficient desalination and organic pollutant degradation
abstract: A novel nitrogen-containing honeycomb carbon nanosheet (NHC) serves as a photothermal/catalytic agent in the multifunctional evaporator NHC@PEI/MCE, proving efficient vapor generation, stable desalination, and organic pollutant water purification.

title: Reactive deposition of Pt single-atoms on g-C 3 N 4 : effect of Pt-precursors
abstract: Reactive deposition of Pt SAs on g-C 3 N 4 optimized by precursor selection and solution tuning enables minimal Pt use with superior hydrogen evolution efficiency.

title: Impact of interface disorder on the crystallization and charge transport of conjugated polymer thin films in organic field-effect transistors
abstract: Interface disorder in isoindigo-BTBT films disrupts polymer crystallization and impairs charge transport in OFETs. X-ray studies reveal chain misalignment at interfaces, linking structural disorder to reduced field-effect mobility.

title: Unveiling the transformation of CoNi hydroxides to CoNiFe layered double hydroxides with implications for electron modulation in one-step coprecipitation for efficient oxygen evolution electrocatalysis
abstract: CoNiFeLDH exhibited unprecedented oxygen evolution reaction with an overpotential of 280 mV at 10 mA cm −2 . The facile electron movements in Fe–O–Ni and Fe–O–Co units and the enhanced surface area of CoNiFeLDH are reasons for the enhanced activity.

title: Self-assembly induced dual-wavelength active photothermal and photodynamic therapies using a near-infrared triimide dye nanoformulations
abstract: Biocompatible liposome-based nanoformulations of an NIR absorbing triimide dye are developed for combined photothermal therapy (PTT) and photodynamic therapy (PDT) with dual-wavelength activity.

title: g-C 3 N 5 -based photocatalysts for energy and environmental applications: insights into properties, structural modifications, challenges, and future directions
abstract: The growing demand for sustainable energy and environmental remediation has intensified the search for efficient, metal-free catalysts.

title: Nanoscale high-entropy alloys and oxides for supercapacitor electrodes: size effects, structure–property relationships, and energy storage potential
abstract: High-entropy alloys and oxides (HEAs and HEOs), composed of multiple principal elements in near-equiatomic ratios, have emerged as promising candidates for supercapacitor electrodes.

title: An organic solvent-free self-assembly strategy for scalable preparation of nanobiopesticides with enhanced insecticidal activity against houseflies
abstract: An organic solvent-free self-assembly strategy for nano-delivery systems using aspartic acid, spinosad, and alkyl polyglucoside demonstrated enhanced insecticidal activity against houseflies and improved mammalian cell safety.

title: Combined in- and out-of-plane chemical ordering in super-ordered MAX phases ( s -MAX)
abstract: Investigation of s-MAX phase stability reveals 26 fully ordered and 14 partially disordered stable compositions, providing insights into atomic size effects and guiding the design of advanced materials and MXene precursors.

title: Efficient and rapid recovery of scandium from acidic wastewater using a β-cyclodextrin-based phosphorus-functionalized covalent organic polymer
abstract: A phosphorus-functionalized covalent organic polymer (β-HCCD) was successfully synthesized and demonstrated high efficient for the selective recovery of scandium from acidic wastewater.

title: Emulsion-LARP method: Optimization of demulsifier for synthesis of methylammonium lead bromide nano-structures
abstract: Emulsion-based synthesis using various demulsifiers enables control over nanocrystal morphology and crystallisation.

title: Stepwise electrochemical reconstruction of a Bi-based anode for enhanced aqueous battery energy storage
abstract: BiO x @BiO/Bi nanoflakes are prepared by electrochemical reconstruction, showing a flower open and close structral evolution in charge and discharge process and excellent electrochemical performance.

title: Synthesis of hexagonal tungsten bronze nanoparticles at a significantly lower temperature and in a shorter time
abstract: This study provides a method for synthesizing h -M x WO 3 NPs (M = Cs, Rb, K, and Na) at a much lower temperature and in a shorter time while retaining the intrinsic optical-responsive properties of the as-synthesized products.

title: Multi-orbital hybridization in a one-dimensional monolayer of DPh-BTBT
abstract: The 1-D DPh-BTBT monolayer was studied to explore intermolecular multi-orbital mixing. UPS showed significant broadening of HOMO and HOMO−1 levels. DFT calculations indicate that electrostatic effects shift on-site energies, enabling hybridization.

title: In intercalation and an Si-containing protective layer enhance the electrochemical performance of NaNi 0.5 Mn 0.5 O 2 for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel In-intercalated sodium cathode material with an Si-containing protective layer for excellent electrochemical performance.

title: Probing defect formation in sulfur-annealed graphene for TMDC integration
abstract: The integration of graphene with other 2D materials has been extensively studied over the past decade to realize high-performance devices unattainable with single materials.

title: Reversible DNA translocation as a molecular caliper to probe the nanoscale asymmetry of glass nanopores
abstract: We use DNA as a molecular caliper and show that its reversible translocation can probe and quantify the effective sensing region of a conical nanopore. This provides a framework to characterize nanopore sensing behaviour.

title: A bifunctional nanozyme with dual oxidase- and catalase-mimicking activities for catalytic oxidation cycling of its target substrate
abstract: As a bifunctional nanozyme, it can convert O 2 to H 2 O 2 via its oxidase-mimicking activity, and then regenerate O 2 from H 2 O 2 via its own catalase-mimicking activity, enabling catalytic oxidation cycling of its target substrate.

title: Viscoelastic time responses of polymeric cell substrates measured continuously from 0.1–5000 Hz in liquid by photothermal AFM nanorheology
abstract: PT-AFM nDMA, a new nanorheology technique, is used for the first time in liquid to measure cell substrate viscoelasticity over a broad, continuous frequency range to enhance the study of how cell behavior relates to nanoscale polymer mechanics.

title: Surface engineering of Pt nanocatalysts with transition metal oleates for selective catalysis: a case study on the hydrogenation of α,β-unsaturated aldehydes
abstract: The adsorption of transition metal oleates on Pt nanoparticle surfaces creates a negative charge, promoting selective hydrogenation of CO bonds over CC bonds in nonpolar solvents, ensuring reactant solubility and improving catalytic efficiency.

title: The intriguing role of l -cysteine in the modulation of chiroplasmonic properties of chiral gold nano-arrows
abstract: Morphology control and chiroptical inversion depending on the cysteine concentration.

title: Chemically deposited Cu nanocubes on a PUA gyroid lattice for lightweight and flexible electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Macro-to-nanoscale synergistic customization: a versatile strategy to fabricate flexible and robust EMI shielding materials by chemically depositing nanostructured Cu coatings onto 3D-printed PUA lattice substrates.

title: The impact of ligand chain length on the HER performance of atomically precise Pt 6 (SR) 12 nanoclusters
abstract: This study investigates the impact of ligand chain length on the electrocatalytic HER performance of atomically precise Pt 6 (thiolate ligand, SR) 12 nanoclusters. Analysis reveals that a shorter ligand chain enhances the electrocatalytic performance.

title: Acid-stable oxygen-evolving catalysts: progress in non-precious material engineering and scalability barriers
abstract: This work breaks new ground by linking catalyst design with engineering needs for proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers, providing a roadmap for non-precious catalysts in acid conditions.

title: Small-signal capacitance in ferroelectric hafnium zirconium oxide: mechanisms and physical insights
abstract: Phase-field simulation study of the mechanisms governing small-signal capacitance in ferroelectric hafnium zirconium oxide and correlation to polarization domain configurations and switching mechanisms.

title: Constructing fecal-derived electrocatalysts for CO 2 upcycling: simultaneously tackling waste and carbon emissions
abstract: We developed a Cu-based electrocatalyst from pig manure-derived biochar for efficient CO 2 reduction, achieving 87.14% faradaic efficiency and 44.80% multicarbon product selectivity.

title: Rational design of stable carbon nitride monolayer membranes for highly controllable CO 2 capture and separation from CH 4 and C 2 H 2
abstract: CO 2 capture and separation from natural and fuel gas are important industrial issues that refer to the control of CO 2 emissions and the purification of target gases.

title: Manganese-based nanoenzymes: from catalytic chemistry to design principle and antitumor/antibacterial therapy
abstract: This review summarizes the catalytic principles and different structure-based insightful design strategies of Mn-based nanoenzymes, as well as their latest applications and challenges in antitumor and antibacterial applications.

title: Advances of nano-photofertilizers in plant research
abstract: The work summarized the light-responsive nanomaterials for plant research including their interactions with plants and recent advancements in their photon-mediated imaging, growth regulation, detection and antimicrobial applications. Images reproduced with permission; full details in paper.

title: Pushing the boundary of the stability and band gap Pareto front by going towards high-entropy perovskites
abstract: Medium/high-entropy vacancy-ordered double perovskites can push the boundary of the stability and band gap Pareto front, exhibiting emerging properties that are not present in their pure counterparts.

title: A facile approach for fabricating efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) with high power conversion efficiencies (PCEs) can be produced using a variety of methods, such as different fabrication methods, device layout modification, and component and interface engineering.

title: Festschrift issue of Nanoscale in honour of Santanu Bhattacharya
abstract: Asish Pal, Praveen Kumar Vemula and Shyni Varghese introduce the Nanoscale themed collection, Celebrating the 65th birthday of Professor Santanu Bhattacharya.

title: Electronic confinement induced quantum dot behavior in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene
abstract: Reducing the dimensions of magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene devices can amplify disorder potentials caused by doping inhomogeneity, leading to enhanced carrier confinement.

title: Layer-controlled continuous MoS 2 growth using a spin-coatable metal precursor buffer
abstract: Achieving continuous MoS 2 on SiO 2 via chemical vapor deposition faces challenges in ensuring reproducibility. Here, we present pH-optimized metal precursor enabling uniform spin-coating, leading to such growth.

title: Fluorescence tunable carbon dots for in vitro nuclear dynamics and gastrointestinal imaging in live zebrafish and their in vivo toxicity evaluation by cardio-craniofacial disfunction assessment
abstract: Synthesis of high-performance N-doped carbon dots (TNCDs) with an enhanced quantum yield for dual imaging and their in vivo toxicity evaluation in a zebrafish model.

title: Surface engineering of NIR-II luminescent gold nanoclusters for brain glioma-targeted imaging
abstract: Through rational surface engineering of ultrasmall NIR-II luminescent gold nanoclusters, brain glioblastoma-targeted NIR-II photoluminescence imaging has been achieved.

title: Enzyme-free optical detection of uric acid using corona phase molecular recognition in near-infrared fluorescent single-walled carbon nanotubes
abstract: DNA-functionalized single-walled carbon nanotubes enabled a point-of-care uric acid testing in human urine with a robust NIR fluorescence response without an enzyme through corona phase molecular recognition.

title: Synergistic effect of Mg and Ti ions by dual-site modulation strategy induces enhanced ordering and electrochemical performance of the layered cathode for sodium–ion batteries
abstract: Doping of inert Mg 2+ and Ti 4+ ions in different crystallographic sites tunes the local structure, inducing enhanced ordering in transition-metal layers and step-like charge/discharge profiles that improve electrochemical performance.

title: Organic electrochemical transistors based on a conjugated diketopyrrolopyrrole-dialkoxybithiazole copolymer
abstract: The performance of the donor–acceptor polymer PDPP-TEG-2Tz in organic electrochemical transistors was investigated using various anions.

title: Shape memory composite membrane with widely programmable electromagnetic shielding effectiveness
abstract: EVA/PDA/MXene-30-6 composite membrane achieves a programmable shielding effectiveness ranging from 26.2 to 74.7 dB under 0–30% tensile strain.

title: A polyoxotungstate/W 18 O 49 nanocomposite with a bird-nest-like network supports high-performance electrochromic supercapacitors
abstract: The W 18 O 49 /[PEI/P 2 W 18 ] 20 film with a unique bird-nest-like network structure exhibits excellent electrochromic energy storage capacity. The ECSC based on this film has a high area capacitance and retention rate, and the storage state is visualized.

title: Nanophotonic biosensors for COVID-19 detection: advances in mechanisms, methods, and design
abstract: The growing societal impact of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) has underscored the urgent need for innovative strategies to address the ongoing challenges posed by the pandemic.

title: Cellulose nanopaper with polymeric nanoparticle additives – what is the role of nanoparticle surface functionality?
abstract: Polymeric nanoparticles with tunable surface functionalities were synthesized via polymerization-induced self-assembly (PISA) to study their interactions with TEMPO-oxidized cellulose nanofibrils (TO-CNFs) in wet and dry states.

title: 2D carbon-based dual pioneers: graphene oxide and graphdiyne guiding solar evaporation through three-dimensional mastery
abstract: Graphene oxide and graphdiyne enable solar evaporation through 3D mastery: broadband light harvesting, anisotropic heat confinement, and ion-selective water transport, reshaping optimization strategies for next-gen solar water-energy systems.

title: Activation and electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide by transition metal atom-doped copper clusters
abstract: Doping transition metals in Cu 13 clusters enhance CO 2 reduction by altering electronic and magnetic properties, lowering overpotentials by ∼20%.

title: Emergent Dirac fermions in graphene and underlying Au monolayer with two-dimensional ferrimagnetism
abstract: We reveal Dirac fermions in graphene and an underlying Au monolayer with two-dimensional ferrimagnetism. The observed features of the electronic structure are intimately linked to the network of triangular loop dislocations at the Au/Co interface.

title: Microwave-assisted synthesis of mesoporous high-entropy alloy and core–shell nanoparticles
abstract: Mesoporous high-entropy alloy core-shell nanoparticles (Au-RhAgCuPdPt) can be synthesized via microwave-assisted heating. Their thermal conversion to high-entropy alloy nanoparticles (AuRhAgCuPdPt) were further invesitgated by in situ heating TEM.

title: Fluffy mesoporous Al 2 O 3 supported Ag–In 2 O 3 schottky junction catalysts for selective hydrogenation of CO of α,β-unsaturated aldehydes
abstract: Highly selective hydrogenation of CO of α,β-unsaturated aldehydes to unsaturated alcohols was achieved over a fluffy mesoporous Al 2 O 3 -supported Ag–In 2 O 3 Schottky junction catalyst.

title: In vivo synthesis of semiconductor nanoparticles in Azotobacter vinelandii for light-driven ammonia production
abstract: We have developed a novel biohybrid system that integrates the in vivo biosynthesis of CdS nanoparticles within the nitrogen fixing bacteria Azotobacter vinelandii for efficient, light-driven ammonia production under ambient conditions.

title: Deterministic fabrication of highly reproducible monochromatic quantum emitters in hexagonal boron nitride
abstract: Carbon-ion implantation through a carbon mask enables deterministic fabrication of highly reproducible, stable, monochromatic quantum emitters in hexagonal boron nitride.

title: Antimicrobial peptide-conjugated graphene coatings for prevention and treatment of bacterial infections
abstract: An antimicrobial peptide (AMP) was successfully conjugated on graphene coating and presented significant antimicrobial activity in vitro and ex vivo , demonstrating capability to prevent bacterial infections caused by biofilm.

title: 2D Janus SnSeS monolayers for solar energy conversion: insights from DFT and excitonic analysis
abstract: Schematic representation of SnSeS monolayer as a photoabsolver inside a solar device, showing it's electronic, optical and excitonic properties.

title: Insight into the structural reconstruction of alkaline water oxidation electrocatalysts
abstract: This review explores precatalysts’ structural reconstruction during alkaline OER, summarizes methods probing structural evolution and influencing factors, guides dynamic reconstruction regulation, and discusses reconstruction impacts on performance.

title: Designing nitride-derived fullerenes X 20 N 20 (X = B, Al, and Ga) for the effective delivery of the cardiovascular drug felodipine: a DFT study
abstract: Schematic illustration of the preparation steps for a felodipine-loaded nanocarrier.

title: Atomically precise large-sized Au n (SR) m nanoclusters and scaling relationships among size, bandgap and excited-state lifetime
abstract: Large-sized Au 100 (SR) 42 and Au 102 (SR) 44 nanoclusters are synthesized. Their ultrasmall energy gaps and ultrafast carrier dynamics are measured. Some general trends among the energy gap, excited-state lifetime, size ( n ) and shape are discussed.

title: What makes β-NaYF 4 :Er 3+ ,Yb 3+ such a successful luminescent thermometer?
abstract: The upconversion phosphor β-NaYF 4 :Er 3+ ,Yb 3+ is one of the primary working horse examples for ratiometric luminescent Boltzmann thermometry. But why? Theoretical arguments are used in this work to unravel that recorded success.

title: A rapid extended-gate field-effect transistor-type biosensor composed of a truncated DNA aptamer and UiO-66 metal–organic framework nanoparticles for HPIV detection in bronchoalveolar lavage fluid
abstract: A rapid EGFET biosensor employing UiO-66 MOF nanoparticles and HPIV aptamers enables sensitive detection of HPIV in BALF samples.

title: Fully compensated ferrimagnetism in a Cr 2 TiC 2 monolayer MXene
abstract: Fully compensated ferrimagnets have the combined advantages of the band spin splitting of ferromagnetism and the zero net magnetization of antiferromagnetism, which endows them with numerous advantages in fast and energy-efficient spintronic devices.

title: Advances in flexible ionic thermal sensors: present and perspectives
abstract: Review of ionic thermal sensors (ITSs) – flexible, biocompatible alternatives to electronic sensors – covering mechanisms, sensor architecture, multimodal sensors, applications, and future perspectives. Images reproduced with permission, see article.

title: Sulfur functionalized diamondoid phosphines enable building nanocomposites interfacing sp 3 -carbon and gold nanolayers
abstract: The mild chemical vapor deposition of the complex MeAuPMe 3 enables gold coating on a vapor phase-assembled sulfide-phosphine diamantanol, which serves as a suitable substrate for oxidation-resistant metal–organic nanocomposite formation.

title: Quantum transport simulations of monolayer β-AgI: a prospective nanochannel material in sub-1 nm gate-length MOSFETs
abstract: This study examines quantum transport in monolayer β-AgI MOSFETs (sub-1 nm gate) using DFT + NEGF. The stable β-AgI monolayer, with a 2.18 eV bandgap, reduces leakage. Simulations confirm its potential for low-power nanoelectronics, meeting ITRS-2013.

title: A hypoxia-responsive liquid metal-containing nanomaterial for cancer phototheranostics
abstract: This work reports a hypoxia-responsive liquid metal nanomaterial that integrates azobenzene's hypoxia response and photothermal effect of liquid metal nanoparticles under NIR irradiation to achieve tumor hypoxia imaging and cancer theranostics.

title: An artificial light-harvesting supramolecule with a Ru 4 Cu 2 core for efficient CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: A highly efficient multimetallic supramolecule, boasting robust light absorbing and catalytic properties, effectively facilitates the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 .

title: Tracking 2D-to-3D crystal growth of a layered material in situ with X-ray scattering
abstract: This study examines the 2D-to-3D crystal growth of the layered photocatalyst Bi 24 O 31 Br 10 upon calcination using in situ X-ray total scattering.

title: Correction: Nanoparticles modified by triple single chain antibodies for MRI examination and targeted therapy in pancreatic cancer
abstract: Correction for ‘Nanoparticles modified by triple single chain antibodies for MRI examination and targeted therapy in pancreatic cancer’ by Jinmao Zou et al. , Nanoscale , 2020, 12 , 4473–4490, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9NR04976B.

title: Correction: Copper nitroprusside analogue nanoparticles against melanoma: detailed in vitro and in vivo investigation
abstract: Correction for ‘Copper nitroprusside analogue nanoparticles against melanoma: detailed in vitro and in vivo investigation’ by Sanchita Tripathy et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 13580–13596, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR01857E.

title: In vitro recording and stimulation performance of multi-electrode arrays passivated with plasma-enhanced atomic layer-deposited metal oxides
abstract: An in vitro Au multi-electrode array was fabricated with a 50 nm-thick indium–tin oxide track layer and a passivation stack of plasma-enhanced atomic layer-deposited Al 2 O 3 (40 nm) and HfO 2 (20 nm), and was modified with a nanoporous Au structure.

title: Quantifying the polar skyrmion motion barrier in an oxide heterostructure
abstract: We have developed an NEB method to quantify the polar skyrmion motion barrier in oxide superlattices, and discovered that it can be significantly reduced with the reduction of the superlattice periodicity to 8 uc.

title: May the target be with you: polysaccharide-coated upconverting nanoparticles for macrophage targeting
abstract: A mannan-functionalized UCNP surface strategy improves stability and facilitates selective macrophage targeting for bioimaging and theranostic applications. Image created using BioRender, see article for details.

title: Study of defect-induced magnetic anisotropy in MWCNT and RGO dispersed SnTe using spin resonance and magnetic measurement
abstract: Anchoring SnTe NPs on MWCNT and RGO increases vacancy formation energy, reduces Sn vacancies, and tunes WAL, stress, and band gap, inducing magnetic anisotropy and RKKY interaction, making it ideal for catalytic, thermoelectric, and spintronic uses.

title: Interfacial chalcogen pair-mediated, biaxial strain- and interlayer distance-tuneable type-2 band alignment in an SnSSe homogeneous bilayer: a density functional theory-based analysis
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive density functional theory-based investigation of the electronic properties of homogeneous bilayer Janus tin sulphide selenide (SnSSe) with different interfacial chalcogen pair configurations.

title: The significance of lipid membrane curvature and lipid order for nano-C60 uptake
abstract: Interactions that invasive mesoscopic nanoparticles (NPs) experience with the variety of lipid membranes present in our body have the potential to trigger diverse biological responses and endocytic pathways.

title: Tailored photoactivity of 2D nanosheets synthesized by electron irradiation of metal–organic Ru( ii ) monolayers
abstract: This work presents the modular preparation of photoactive 2D nanosheets from self-assembled Ru( ii ) polypyridine complexes, exploring substituent effects on formation and stability, as well as the nanosheet’s electrochemical and optoelectrical behavior.

title: Reducing noble metal dependence: oxygen evolution reaction with a Ru-minimized Bi 2 Ru 2 O 7 @MOF-801 composite
abstract: A noble metal-minimized Bi 2 Ru 2 O 7 @MOF-801 composite delivers high catalytic OER activity and improved durability with the synergy of high conductive Bi 2 Ru 2 O 7 and high surface area MOF-801.

title: Asymmetric [Dy2] molecules deposited into micro-SQUID susceptometers: in situ characterization of their magnetic integrity
abstract: The controlled integration of magnetic molecules into superconducting circuits is key to developing hybrid quantum devices.

title: Planar supercapacitor devices with nanometer electrodes
abstract: MoS 2 /graphene nanodes in supercapacitors.

title: A study on the prediction of MoS 2 transistor electrode contact characteristics based on transfer learning methods
abstract: We propose a machine learning model combined with density functional theory calculations to predict the barrier heights of different precision functionals, while performing quantum transport calculations on ML MoS 2 FETs.

title: Hydrogel–polymersome composites as a sensing platform for monitoring food spoilage
abstract: Hydrogel–polymersome composites acting as sensors for food spoilage detection and as ‘sense-and-release’ devices. The graphical abstract was created in BioRender. Kraus, M. (2025) https://BioRender.com/n22p907.

title: Observation of the anomalous Hall effect in proximity coupled Cr 2 Ge 2 Te 6 /graphene heterostructures
abstract: In heterostructures of Cr 2 Ge 2 Te 6 (CGT)/graphene, the anomalous Hall effect is clearly observed through experiments, which is caused by the spin polarization in graphene induced by the magnetic proximity effect.

title: Unravelling the cell death mechanism triggered by catalytic copper–iron nanoparticles in vitro and in vivo
abstract: We present an in-depth study of the working mechanisms of CuFe@DMSA NPs with high capability of glutathione (GSH) depletion with simultaneous production of reactive oxygen species (ROS), showing a significant reduction of tumour growth in vivo .

title: Multilevel, solar-blind, and thermostable physical unclonable functions based on host-sensitized luminescence of β-Ga 2 O 3 :Dy 3+
abstract: The luminescent properties of β-Ga 2 O 3 :Dy 3+ enable the development of optical PUF tags with exceptional encoding capacity, solar-blind response, and thermal stability, making them highly effective for anti-counterfeiting and encryption applications.

title: 3D printing of superhydrophobic and multifunctional objects via simple and inexpensive vat photopolymerization
abstract: We fabricated superhydrophobic and multifunctional objects using a simple and inexpensive vat photopolymerization 3D printer with a 2-component ink and a 2-step process.

title: Core–shell-structured Z-scheme CdS@MIL-88(A)Fe heterojunctions for efficient visible light photocatalytic oxidative coupling of benzylamines to imines
abstract: The Z-scheme heterojunction of CdS@MIL-88(A)Fe enhances carrier separation and boosts photocatalytic performance for benzylamine oxidative coupling. This study offers key insights for green photocatalysis and advanced photocatalyst development.

title: Recent advances in biotin-based therapeutic agents for cancer therapy
abstract: Biotin binds to drugs in two main modes: SMBCs and NBCs. Tumor cells have more biotin receptors than normal cells, allowing for greater biotin uptake, which can enhance drug delivery to tumors.

title: High strength chitosan-based nanocomposites with aligned nanosheets and crosslinked networks
abstract: The prepared nanocomposite films have dense covalent crosslinked networks and highly aligned nanosheets, which endows them with high mechanical properties, which are much higher than those of all other chitosan-based composites films.

title: Synergistic Zn 2+ desolvation and diffusion acceleration for high-performance aqueous zinc batteries at low temperature
abstract: VO-PBA facilitates Zn 2+ diffusion both at the electrolyte–cathode interface and within the cathode bulk, significantly improving low-temperature performance.

title: Recent advances in nanoporous organic polymers (NPOPs) for hydrogen storage applications
abstract: Nanoporous organic polymers (NPOPs) as versatile materials with robust thermal stability, large surface area, and customizable porosity, making them ideal candidates for advanced hydrogen (H 2 ) storage applications.

title: Self-regulated photoresponsive heterogeneous PNIPAM hydrogel actuators
abstract: Self-regulated actuators harness material intelligence to enable complex deformations and dynamics, representing a significant advancement in automated soft robotics.

title: Glass-confined carbon dots: transparent afterglow materials with switchable TADF and RTP
abstract: The confined synthesis of carbon dots (CDs) in solid matrixes is a promising avenue for developing new afterglow materials.

title: Mechanistic insights into plasmon-driven hot electron transfer from silver nanoclusters to PFAS: interplay of size and electronic structure
authors: Sharma S. R. K. C. Yamijala
abstract: Visible light excites plasmons in silver nanoclusters (NCs) and generates hot carriers (HCs) that are transferred to the water pollutants (here, PFAS). The HC transfer from NC to PFAS is stable and unidirectional for larger cluster sizes. The graphical abstract was created in part via Canva.

title: Co-doped RuIr nanoparticles for enhanced activity and stability in alkaline overall water splitting
abstract: Co-doped RuIr bifunctional electrocatalysts exhibit outstanding HER and OER performance through a synergistic electronic modulation and defect engineering strategy.

title: Insights into the reaction process and mechanism of CNT growth from n -hexane on Fe-based catalysts
abstract: Due to their outstanding physicochemical properties, carbon nanotubes (CNTs) have been widely studied and applied in the nanoscience and nanotechnology fields.

title: High-performance optoelectronics enabled by synergistic integration of 2D heterostructures with perovskite quantum dots
abstract: In the era of IoE- and AI-driven intelligent optoelectronics, ultrafast, highly sensitive photoelectric systems are crucial for quantum information, autonomous driving, and biosensing.

title: DNA-amphiphilic nanostructures: synthesis, characterization and applications
abstract: DNA's extraordinary potential reaches far beyond its role as a carrier of genetic information.

title: Controlled synthesis of hierarchical Bi nanostructures by electrodeposition for selective electroreduction of CO 2 to formate
abstract: The morphology of Bi catalysts can be selectively tuned by optimizing the electrodepositing current density for highly selective CO 2 reduction to formate.

title: Insight into the role of tunable nitrogen vacancies in transition metal nitrides for ammonia synthesis
abstract: The work found that the generation of nitrogen-vacancies is the origin of promising activity site for N2 fixation and reduction on transition metal nitrides, and the LaN expresses the best ammonia synthesis performance.

title: Anchoring Ru single-atoms on MXene achieves dual-enzyme activities for mild photothermal augmented nanocatalytic therapy
abstract: In this system (Ru-Ti 3 C 2 T x -PEG), the Ru single-atom enhances light absorption and catalyzes the conversion of H 2 O 2 into ·OH. In addition, the nanozymes possess the ability to generate O 2 and deplete glutathione.

title: ZrN nucleation layer provides backside ohmic contact to MBE-grown GaN nanowires
abstract: ZrN layers provide high-quality backside ohmic contact to GaN nanowires, enabling nanoprobe-based SEM characterization of single nanowire p–n junctions.

title: Mesoporous SiO 2 based nanocomplex enzymes for enhanced chemodynamic therapy of pancreatic tumors
abstract: The core-shell Gen@mSiO 2 @MnO 2 -PEG nanozymes can serve as an effective platform for ROS-mediated tumor therapy, combining the strengths of CDT and drug therapy in the treatment of pancreatic cancer.

title: Self-patterning of liquid Field's metal for enhanced performance of two-dimensional semiconductors
abstract: Liquid Field’s metal self-propagates along nanoscale electrodes during vacuum annealing, forming semimetal contacts with 2D semiconductors that reduce contact resistance and boost device performance without modifying electrode architecture.

title: Controlled bioorthogonal catalyst self-assembly and activity using rationally designed polymer scaffolds
abstract: Polymer-based nanocatalysts can mediate the bioorthogonal uncaging of substrates to form therapeutic molecules in situ . The supramolecular interactions within the host scaffold allow for the generation of thermo-responsive nanocatalysts.

title: Recent progress in outermost surface engineering for solar panels
abstract: This paper briefly outlines the basic concepts and current developments in anti-reflection, anti-smudge, and spectrum regulation technologies, discussing the challenges and trends related to the applications on the surface of solar cells.

title: Recent advances in using severe plastic deformation for the processing of nanomaterials
abstract: Processing by severe plastic deformation (SPD) refers to imposing a high strain without producing any significant dimensional changes. This review summarizes the principles of SPD processing.

title: Challenges, opportunities, and roadmap for ionic liquid-based electrolytes in advancing energy storage devices
abstract: This review offers a thorough and authoritative overview of research advancements in the field of ionic liquid-based electrolytes with an emphasis on Li-ion batteries and supercapacitors.

title: Recent progress in non-noble metal nano-electrocatalysts for hybrid water splitting
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the recent progress in non-noble metal-based nanocatalysts for hybrid water splitting, including fundamental concepts, catalysts design, reaction mechanism and typical hybrid water splitting systems.

title: Quantum coherence and relaxation of single spins on surfaces probed by ESR-STM
authors: 'Dalong Xuan', 'Di'an Wu', 'Xiaobin Geng', 'Sihao Li', 'Xianglong Wu', 'Yu Wang', 'Xue Zhang'
abstract: The miniaturization of modern devices to the nanoscale has inspired the development of quantum information processing, in which quantum coherence is of vital importance for both fundamental research and applications.

title: Applications of machine learning in high-entropy alloys: a review of recent advances in design, discovery, and characterization
abstract: High-entropy alloys (HEAs) have attracted considerable attention due to their exceptional properties and outstanding performance across various applications.

title: Spin-multiplexed phase and amplitude manipulations of terahertz waves based on chiral metasurfaces
abstract: This article utilizes chiral phase to achieve spin-decoupled phase control of co-polarized circularly polarized terahertz waves, and further employs chiral phase and circular dichroism for the manipulation of amplitude and phase.

title: A low-cost, lithium-rich zirconium-based oxyhalide solid electrolyte featuring an efficient ion transport structure
abstract: This study investigated the doping of Li 2 O with Li 2 ZrCl 6 as a solid electrolyte and its impact on electrochemical and battery performance, offering new insights into sustainable energy solutions.

title: Photochromism in the periodic nanospace of zeolite LTA by the transfer of photoexcited electrons of adsorbed Na atoms
abstract: Cationic Na clusters and related species incorporated into the β-cages of Na-form LTA zeolite exhibit photochromism. Metastable Na clusters with C 3 v symmetry are formed in α-cages by electron transfer from the initial species in the β-cages.

title: Hydrophilicity modulation of surface acoustic wave resonators for high-performance humidity sensing
abstract: Polymer-blend SAW humidity sensors with tailored hydrophilicity exhibit ultra-fast response (2.2 s), short recovery (1.2 s), and high stability under 15–98% RH, outperforming commercial sensors in sensitivity and response speed. Image partly generated using Sora AI.

title: Triangular-shaped Cu–Zn–In–Se-based nanocrystals with narrow near infrared photoluminescence
abstract: Colloidal anisotropic Cu–Zn–In–Se-based nanocrystals synthesized in a one-pot seed-mediated approach exhibit an intensive narrow photoluminescence with maxima ranging from 1080 nm to 1220 nm and quantum yields reaching 40%.

title: High-performance aqueous copper-ion batteries based on iron hexacyanoferrate cathodes for enhanced energy storage
abstract: The intercalation/deintercalation mechanism of copper ions in iron hexacyanoferrate (FeHCF) was explored, and an aqueous battery with a voltage range of 1.6–2.5 V was prepared. These findings highlight the potential of FeHCF-based aqueous batteries.

title: Broadband and large surface enhancements of the local electric field enabled by cross-etched hyperbolic metamaterials
abstract: This work presents a novel cross-etched HMM (CeHMM), which can exhibit two resonant high- k modes under incident light from free space and provide large enhancements of surface electric fields over a broadband spectral range extending beyond 500 nm.

title: Phase separation-driven modulation of cell membrane interactions with benzo[ a ]pyrene-bearing graphene nanosheets: molecular insights into combined toxicity
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations reveal the pivotal role of phase separation in modulating cell membrane interactions with graphene nanosheets preloaded with benzo[ a ]pyrene, a common environmental contaminant.

title: Synthesis of MXene/carbon composites via controlled etching of Ti 3 SiC 2 in the gaseous etchant CCl 4
abstract: A new method is proposed for the rapid in situ synthesis of MXene (Ti 3 C 2 Cl 2 )/carbon composites and carbide-derived carbon (CDC) through the etching of Ti 3 SiC 2 in a sealed ampoule using the novel gaseous etchant CCl 4 .

title: Rational design of hierarchical CuO/Cu 2 O/SnO 2 branched supernanowires for highly sensitive non-enzymatic glucose sensors
abstract: Rationally designed CuO/Cu 2 O/SnO 2 barnyardgrass-like branched supernanowires, featuring large surface areas and strong synergistic interactions, achieved excellent glucose electrocatalytic performance with high sensitivity and a low detection limit.

title: Fabrication of polymersomes with liposome-extracted lipid membrane preserving original leaflet asymmetry
abstract: We report a novel method to fabricate polymersomes with liposome-extracted lipid membranes while preserving leaflet asymmetry, achieving stable hybrid vesicles with drug-loading potential for advanced DDS applications.

title: Constant voltage driving current oscillation in artificial graphene ribbons with deterministic chiral edges
abstract: QD-based artificial graphene with deterministic chiral edges is achieved on Si substrates. An abnormal current oscillation at constant voltages is observed due to the effects of edge states, depending on gate voltage, magnetic field and temperature.

title: Correction: Unveiling the potential of Cu–Pd/CdS catalysts to supply and rectify electron transfer for H 2 generation from water splitting
abstract: Correction for ‘Unveiling the potential of Cu–Pd/CdS catalysts to supply and rectify electron transfer for H 2 generation from water splitting’ by Ejaz Hussain et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 3436–3450, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4nr03381g.

title: Expression of concern: A hysteresis-free perovskite transistor with exceptional stability through molecular cross-linking and amine-based surface passivation
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘A hysteresis-free perovskite transistor with exceptional stability through molecular cross-linking and amine-based surface passivation’ by Hyeong Pil Kim et al. , Nanoscale , 2020, 12 , 7641–7650, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9NR10745B.

title: Patterning of layered semiconductor GeS 2 by laser photooxidation and water immersion
abstract: A lithography-free photopatterning method for layered GeS 2 is demonstrated, eliminating resist residue problems, enabling low-power laser patterning with minimal heat damage, and employing water itself as an environmentally friendly etchant.

title: Nucleation and crystallization of metal oxides from carbonates
abstract: The stepwise transformation of rare-earth carbonates into oxides via dehydrogenation, decarbonization, and crystallization is revealed by in situ TEM, providing atomic-scale insights into oxide formation.

title: An Rh–Au nanocluster protected by an N-heterocyclic carbene: synthesis, structure, and single-molecule conductance properties
abstract: This study presents the first NHC-protected Rh–Au alloy cluster. Single-cluster conductance measurements demonstrate a 338% enhancement in photoconductivity under 290 nm illumination, attributed to localized surface plasmon resonance.

title: Exploring interface tension as a tool to control the morphology of binary organic semiconductor nanoparticles prepared by nanoprecipitation
abstract: Morphological control of binary organic semiconductor nanoparticles prepared by nanoprecipitation.

title: Photosensitive resistive switching in parylene–PbTe nanocomposite memristors for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Reliable parylene–PbTe memristors controlled via electrical and optical stimuli replicate key synaptic functions and are applicable in neuromorphic computing systems.

title: Mechanical and electromechanical properties of 2D materials studied via in situ microscopy techniques
abstract: This review summarizes recent advancements in in situ microscopy techniques applied to the study of mechanical and electromechanical properties of 2D materials. The latest results, corresponding challenges and future opportunities are discussed.

title: Strategic design of emerging (K,Na)NbO 3 -based perovskites for high-performance piezocatalysis and photo-piezocatalysis
abstract: This comprehensive review of (K,Na)NbO 3 -based perovskites explores design strategies, mechanisms, and applications in piezocatalysis and photo-piezocatalysis for sustainable environmental and energy solutions.

title: Self-assembly processes of 2D Au( i )–S(CH 2 ) 2 COOH lamellae
abstract: Molecular level assembly processes of two-dimensional Au( i )-3-mercaptopropionic acid lamellae are proposed based on their selected area electron diffraction patterns.

title: Construction of an Sc-NiFe-LDH electrocatalyst for highly efficient electrooxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural at industrial current density
abstract: Sc-NiFe-LDH catalyzed electrooxidation of HMF to FDCA, achieving the FDCA faradic efficiency and selectivity of 96.5% and 99.5%, respectively, at a current density of >600 mA cm −2 .

title: Carbon-based nanocarriers for plant growth promotion: fuelling when needed
abstract: CB-NMs, have revolutionized the agricultural sector in various ways including the on-demand supply of essential nutrients, biomolecules, and growth factors to plants.

title: Exploring graphene's impact on graphite/PANI matrix composites: high-pressure fabrication and enhanced thermal-electrical properties
abstract: This study investigates the effects of the graphene content and applied pressure on the electrical and thermal conductivities of graphite/polyaniline (GP) and graphite/graphene/polyaniline (GGP) composites produced via direct mixing method.

title: Recent advances in multimodal mechanoluminescent sensors enabled by nanostructure design
