title: Size-dependent hydrogen evolution in organic photovoltaic catalysts: balancing exciton dissociation and charge transport
abstract: Organic photovoltaic nanoparticles, with their tunable light absorption and exceptional charge dynamics, have shown promising potential for solar-driven hydrogen evolution.

title: Heterogeneous and microporous structure evolution of heated/radiated polyacrylonitrile fibers revealed by X-ray micro-computed tomography and scattering
abstract: Heat treatment, a critical pre-oxidation process, plays an essential role in the preparation of carbon fibers.

title: Engineering oxygen vacancies in NiCoAl-LDH@NiP hybrid catalysts for an efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Synergistic engineering of NiCoAl-LDH@NiP for an enhanced HER: co-doping induces lamellar NiCoAl-LDH growth on NiP, enriching oxygen vacancies and surface-active sites to facilitate the Volmer–Heyrovsky mechanism.

title: Hydrogel sensing platforms for monitoring contractility in in vitro cardiac models
abstract: This review summarizes current methods for monitoring cardiomyocyte contractility and explores recent advances in hydrogel-based platforms for monitoring cell contraction forces. Copyright 2023, Springer Nature; 2019 and 2022, John Wiley and Sons.

title: Mesoporous anatase TiO 2 mesocrystal for high-performance photocatalysis and lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Mesoporous anatase TiO 2 mesocrystals with aligned nanostructures exhibit unprecedented photocatalytic and electrochemical performance for advanced applications.

title: Temperature dependence of excitonic Hubbard U interaction in WS 2 /WSe 2 moiré superlattices
abstract: WS 2 /WSe 2 moiré superlattices, formed with small twist angles, realize unique quantum phenomena such as the Hubbard effect due to periodic potential wells confining excitons.

title: Fullerene-driven photocarrier processes in perovskite solar cells: recent advances
abstract: Recent advances in understanding photocarrier dynamics of fullerenes in PSCs, focusing on charge transport, interfacial interactions, and the impact of functionalized derivatives on efficiency and stability.

title: Phage–nanomaterial platforms for precision antimicrobial therapy: from design to therapeutic application
abstract: This review summarizes the design, antimicrobial mechanisms, and infection-model applications of phage–nanomaterial platforms, highlighting strategies for synergistic antibacterial activity and perspectives for clinical translation.

title: NiFe layered double hydroxides as high-performance electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction: recent developments
abstract: Recent advancements in NiFe-LDH electrocatalysts in the past two years have been summarized, as well as the current challenges and future perspectives.

title: Improving the washability of conductive textiles by constructing a dually crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol network with silver nanowires
abstract: Performance of wearable electronic textiles relies heavily on washability. Fixing silver nanowires using a dually crosslinked polymer network on surface endows fabric with excellent washability and tunable conductivity.

title: Recent advances in poly(amino acids), polypeptides, and their derivatives in drug delivery
abstract: This review systematically describes the preparation of poly(amino acids) and peptides, and their use in drug loading for various diseases, offering insights for research and clinical applications of degradable biomedical polymers.

title: Comparative electromagnetic shielding performance of Ti 3 C 2 T x -PVA composites in various structural forms: compact films, hydrogels, and aerogels
abstract: The study investigates the structural design of Ti 3 C 2 T x PVA composites in hydrogel, aerogel, and film forms, showing that EMI shielding and mechanical properties are influenced by the composite's structural configuration and Ti 3 C 2 T x areal density.

title: Metal/TiO 2 composite photocatalysts with tunable activity for nitrogen fixation: the impact of metal nanoparticles
abstract: Metal-loaded TiO 2 (M-TiO 2 , M = Au, Pd, Cu) composites were constructed and applied in photocatalytic nitrogen fixation. It has been found that the status of Cu 2+ , as a generic interacial mediator, was conducive to the catalytic performances.

title: Structural distortions control scaling of exciton binding energies in two-dimensional Ag/Bi double perovskites
abstract: Structural distortions in Ag/Bi quasi-2D double perovskites are responsible for the unexpected trend in the exciton binding energies.

title: Engineered anti-HER2 drug delivery nanosystems for the treatment of breast cancer
abstract: Schematic representation of clinical and pre-clinical workflow settings for light controlled release of PLGA NPs for breast cancer treatment. Created in BioRender.com. Vanni, S. (2025) https://BioRender.com/r11b297.

title: Effectively enhancing ion diffusion in superconcentrated ionic liquid electrolytes using co-solvent additives
authors: 'Jhonatan Soto Puelles', 'Luke A. O'Dell', 'M. C. Dilusha Cooray', 'Maria Forsyth', 'Fangfang Chen'
abstract: The cosolvent's higher affinity for metal cations is key to enhancing ion diffusion in salt-concentrated ionic liquids by disrupting ion aggregation and reducing ion caging time, thus promoting the structural diffusion of metal ions.

title: Silver-decorated boron nitride nanocomposite-infused cotton fabric with durable antimicrobial and multifunctional properties
abstract: Modified fabric displays strong antibacterial activity, enhanced antioxidant capacity, improved thermal conductivity, and higher UPF.

title: Electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 to produce the C 2+ products: from selectivity to rational catalyst design
abstract: Electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 (eCO 2 RR) into valuable multi-carbon (C 2+ ) products is an effective strategy for combating climate change and mitigating energy crises.

title: A MPB-intensified tube microreactor system for continuous synthesis of Ag + doped CdS quantum dots
abstract: Recent advances in microreactor technology have established these systems as promising platforms for colloidal nanocrystal synthesis.

title: Sustainable additive manufacturing through recycled and reinforced thermoplastic composites: state of the art
abstract: The transformation of plastic waste into valuable products remains a significant challenge for sustainable manufacturing.

title: MXenes in microbiology and virology: from pathogen detection to antimicrobial applications
abstract: MXenes, a rapidly emerging class of two-dimensional materials, have demonstrated exceptional versatility and functionality across various domains, including microbiology and virology.

title: High-entropy alloy nanoparticles functionalized with reduced graphene oxide as a high-performance cathode for lithium–oxygen batteries
abstract: The rGO and HEA NPs synergistically optimized the reversible generation/decomposition kinetics of the discharge product Li 2 O 2 , resulting in excellent electrochemical performance of the lithium–oxygen battery based on PtFeCoNiCu@rGO.

title: Near-infrared circularly polarized luminescence enabled by chiral inorganic nanomaterials
abstract: We summarize the latest developments in NIR-CPL generated by chiral inorganic nanomaterials and provide new perspectives for constructing NIR-CPL materials and developing new applications for chiral nanomaterials.

title: Photoluminescence fluctuations in single perovskite nanocrystals: structural, environmental and ligand effect
abstract: This review discusses key factors influencing photoluminescence fluctuations of single perovskite nanocrystals and highlights surface passivation strategies to enhance their stability.

title: Nanoparticle-assembled interconnected PbO 1.44 hollow spheres enabled by PVP-driven transformation of β-PbO 2 and self-sacrificial templating for superior lithium storage
abstract: Nanoparticle-assembled interconnected PbO 1.44 hollow spheres (NAHSs) were crafted by a robust strategy of PVP-driven transformation of β-PbO 2 microspheres (MSs) combined with a self-sacrificial templating mechanism for superior lithium storage.

title: Cu 2 O-CuX NPs rich in oxygen vacancies modulated by halogen ions and their application in photocatalytic degradation
abstract: Cu 2 O-CuX NPs rich in oxygen vacancies modulated by halogen ions for photocatalytic degradation of MB.

title: Nanoscale profiles: contributors to the 2025 Emerging Investigators collection
abstract: Our Emerging Investigators collection gathers some of the best research being conducted by nanoscientists in the early stages of their independent career. Congratulations to all of the researchers featured.

title: Nanoscale size impact of nanoparticle interaction and activity studies with urease
abstract: Interaction of urease with four different sizes (5–100 nm) of silver nanoparticles and inhibition studies.

title: Rigid support–molecular regulation–interface reinforcement synergistic strategy enables PEO-based electrolytes for solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: A high-performance solid-state lithium battery was realized with the aid of rigid support-molecular regulation-interface reinforcement synergistic strategy.

title: Correction: Simulation of the resistance switching performance and synaptic behavior of TiO 2 -based RRAM devices with CoFe 2 O 4 insertion layers
abstract: Correction for ‘Simulation of the resistance switching performance and synaptic behavior of TiO 2 -based RRAM devices with CoFe 2 O 4 insertion layers’ by Fei Yang et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 6729–6738, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR05935A.

title: Integration of two-dimensional WS 2 in flexible textile triboelectric nanogenerators via electronic dyeing for self-powered sensing
abstract: We present flexible textile TENGs by electronically dyeing polyester with 2D WS 2 , tailoring its triboelectric properties via drop casting, immersion, and spray coating.

title: MINFLUX: μs and nm precision 3D tracking of dynamic lipid mobility on nanoparticles
abstract: MINFLUX microscopy opened unique opportunities to investigate complex molecular systems at a single molecule level with μs temporal and nm spatial resolutions in 3D.

title: Leaf-like hematite-decorated flexible carbon-textile for enhancing mass transfer at triphasic interfaces in photoanodes
abstract: In this work, oxygen-containing functional groups were introduced on the surface of carbon cloth to change the hydrophilicity of the PEC photoelectrode. The triphasic interfaces of the reaction were dynamically regulated by nano-confinement.

title: Multicolor and sign-invertible circularly polarized luminescence from nonchiral charge-transfer complexes assembled with N-terminal aromatic amino acids
abstract: Multicolor CPL materials with controllable handedness were constructed from nonchiral luminescent charge transfer complexes through co-assembly with chiral N-terminal aromatic amino acids.

title: Charge transfer between van der Waals coupled metallic 2D layers
abstract: Charge transfer in a K/graphene/Pb-monolayer heterostack on SiC: Approximately 44% of the charge donated from the K adlayer is transferred into the next-nearest neighbour Pb-layer, while the rest remains in the graphene.

title: Janus MoXYCl (X = S, Se, Te; Y = N, P, As) monolayers: a promising family of 2D materials for high-performance p–i–n photodetectors and spintronic applications
abstract: The development of two-dimensional p–i–n homojunctions offers promising potential for future electronic and optoelectronic devices.

title: A novel high endurance HZO FeFET with monolayer graphene inserted in the gate oxide
abstract: A high endurance HfO 2 -based FeFET with monolayer graphene (GR) inserted in the gate oxide.

title: A hybrid supervised and unsupervised machine learning approach for identifying nucleoside drugs using nanopore readouts
abstract: A hybrid supervised–unsupervised machine learning framework is developed for identifying anti-viral and anti-cancer drugs based on nanopore transmission signatures.

title: Electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction to ammonia by graphene-supported Au 4 Cu 2 and Au 2 Ag 2 nanoclusters
abstract: We synthesized Au 2 Ag 2 (PPh 3 ) 2 (PhCC) 4 and Au 4 Cu 2 (PPh 3 ) 4 (PhCC) 6 nanoclusters and found that graphene-supported Au 4 Cu 2 NCs exhibit superior ENRR performance compared to other supported or unsupported systems.

title: Prediction of UV-vis absorption spectra of thiolate-protected gold nanoclusters based on graph convolutional neural networks
abstract: A graph convolutional neural network (GCNN) model is developed to accurately characterize the structure of gold nanoclusters and predict their UV-vis absorption spectra.

title: Engineering mechanical strength and resistance to fatigue of a nanostructured protein material through genetic removal of electrostatic repulsions
abstract: The strength and fatigue resistance of a nanostructured protein array can be increased by removing charge-charge repulsions.

title: Enhanced directional transfer of charge carriers and optimized electronic structure in fluorine doped polymeric carbon nitride nanosheets for efficient photocatalytic water splitting
abstract: F doped PCN to enhance the directional transfer of charge carriers and optimize the electronic structure for efficient photocatalytic water splitting.

title: A nanocatalytic membrane with sono-responsive antibacterial therapy (SRAT) for rapid sterilization and enhanced chronic wound healing
abstract: A novel PN-bioHJ membrane combining black phosphorus and barium titanate on a PLGA platform achieves >99% antibacterial efficiency under ultrasound, with excellent biocompatibility for infected tissue regeneration.

title: Engineering sulfur vacancy defects in self-supporting Mo-doped NiS ultrathin nanosheets grown in situ on nickel foam for enhanced water splitting
abstract: With sulfur vacancy engineering, Mo-doped NiS ultrathin nanosheets were hydrothermally grown on nickel foam. As a dual electrode, Mo-NiS/NF required only 1.56 V for 10 mA cm −2 in 1.0 M KOH, showing excellent total water splitting activity.

title: An optical/magnetic imaging nanoplatform-mediated convection-enhanced delivery of oxaliplatin for glioma treatment
abstract: A nanoplatform (Gd/Cy5-Oxa@NPs) combines a pH-responsive copolymer, cyanine 5 dye, gadolinium chloride, and oxaliplatin for efficient drug delivery, bypassing the blood–brain barrier, and enabling pH-dependent dual-modal imaging for glioma treatment.

title: Field-induced demagnetisation of bisegmented cylindrical ferromagnetic nanowires mediated by skyrmion tubes
abstract: Cylindrical magnetic nanowires offer exciting possibilities for potential applications in spintronic devices that rely on manipulation of topologically non-trivial three-dimensional domain walls.

title: A fluorinated Janus-type dendrimer as an ionizable one-component nanocarrier for nucleic acids
abstract: Here we report a fluorinated Janus-type dendrimer (FJD2N) showing a robust, 19 F-MRI-active hydrophobic self-assembling moiety and a biocompatible hydrophilic scaffold as a low cytotoxic gene-delivery vector.

title: On the feasibility of quantum teleportation protocols implemented with silicon devices
abstract: We present a computational investigation of entanglement-based quantum information applications implemented on an electrically defined quantum dot structure in silicon.

title: Pairing Ru-doped NiCo-layered double hydroxides and selenide derivatives as self-supporting electrocatalysts for alkaline overall water splitting
abstract: The preparation procedures for Ru-NiCo LDH/NF-4 and Ru-NiSe 2 /CoSe/NF-4 are described. Therefore, Ru-NiCo LDH/NF-4 and Ru-NiSe 2 /CoSe/NF-4 were combined to construct a water-splitting device, utilizing their advantages.

title: Monodisperse Pt nanoparticle arrays via block copolymer nanopatterning and their reaction kinetics on CO oxidation
abstract: This study presents a well-defined 2D model catalytic system, bridging it with 3D catalysts, and showcasing BCP nanopatterning for obtaining a foundational structure for advancing catalytic research.

title: Regenerated and reformed gold and titanium dioxide quantum dots from waste for sustainable and efficient environmental monitoring
abstract: Regenerated Au–TiO 2 hybrid quantum dots demonstrate enhanced photocatalytic efficiency for environmental applications, promoting sustainable waste reforming and advanced material reuse.

title: Ternary metal oxynitride Y 2 Zr 2 O 7− x N x for selective NO 2 electrochemical sensing
abstract: A novel ternary metal oxynitride, Y 2 Zr 2 O 7− x N x (0.49 ≤ x ≤ 0.98), was synthesized through controlled nitridation, marking its first application in electrochemical gas sensors.

title: In situ growth of enzyme-inorganic hybrid nanoflowers on paper strips for the visual detection of saliva-level glucose
abstract: This study innovates an in situ growth method of multi-enzyme-inorganic hybrid nanoflowers (HNFs) on cellulose paper, boosting enzyme immobilization efficiency and advancing diabetes diagnostics.

title: Integrating piezoelectric dressings with botanicals as emerging smart dressings for diabetic wound healing
abstract: Three aspects that piezoelectric materials promote wound healing are as follows: (A) electrical stimulation promotes tissue repair, (B) piezoelectric catalytic antibacterial action, and (C) drug-loaded piezoelectric dressings.

title: The role of composition and diameter in the crystal purity of InAs x P 1− x nanowires
abstract: This work provides insights into the interplay between diameter, composition and crystal phase in III–V semiconductor ternary nanowires. It demonstrates a diameter-dependent critical composition required to achieve phase-pure wurtzite nanowires.

title: Lithium aluminum titanium phosphate (LATP) composite solid-state electrolytes: progress and prospects for all-solid-state batteries
abstract: This review summarizes the research progress of LATP-based polymer composite solid electrolytes (CSEs), providing a reference for future research directions.

title: Dynamic regulation of ion transport through a bis(1,3-propanediol)-based channel via allosteric azobenzene photoswitching
abstract: Dynamic photoswitching in azobenzene linked bis(1,3-propanediols) enables the formation of self-assembled barrel rosette anion channels in the trans -form, while the cis -form remains inactive due to its poor self-assembly.

title: Improvement of adsorption of sulfadiazine on tannic acid–chitosan biochar by nickel doping through a polyphenol metal network
abstract: In this paper, nickel was successfully loaded onto the surface of chitosan via polyphenol metal network and carbonized to form biochar material, which adsorbed 123.89 mg g −1 and 152.56 mg g −1 of SDZ at 298 K and 318 K, respectively.

title: Hydroxyapatite nanoparticle films with designed nanostructures for advanced cell culture
abstract: This is the first systematic review of the properties of cell culture substrates in tissue engineering, with a focus on the application of surface functionalization of hydroxyapatite nanoparticle films.

title: Efficient and color stable blue perovskite light-emitting diodes achieved via dual-additive phase modulation
abstract: EDACl 2 and NaBr additives enhanced blue PeLEDs (474 nm, 4.6% EQE) via phase and Cl control in quasi-2D perovskites, enabling stable emission.

title: Covalent organic framework encapsulated multi-walled carbon nanotubes for ultrasensitive electrochemical determination of lead ions in water and e-cigarette samples
abstract: A Tp-Bpy-COF@MWCNT-based electrochemical sensor has been developed for the ultrasensitive detection of lead ions, achieving an impressive low detection limit of 0.0749 μg L −1 . Graphical abstract created, in part, with 3D Max and Materials Studio.

title: Ligand-controlled Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles as reusable adsorbents for dye removal in the chemical recycling of coloured polyester textiles
abstract: Magnetically retrievable hydrophobic-ligand Fe 3 O 4 nanoadsorbents rapidly decolorize PET depolymerization products, enabling recovery of high-purity monomer.

title: Highly selective and sensitive uric acid sensor based on a self-assembled CdS/CdIn 2 S 4 heterojunction
abstract: Uric acid (UA) detection plays a crucial role in the early diagnosis and treatment of diseases linked with hypo-/hyper-uricemia.

title: Hydrogel–exosome complexes: a novel strategy for cardiovascular regeneration
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains one of the leading causes of high mortality and morbidity worldwide, posing a substantial threat to global health.

title: Lignosulfonate as a versatile regulator for the mediated synthesis of Ag@AgCl nanocubes
abstract: The remarkable catalytic activity, optical properties, and electrochemical behavior of nanomaterials based on noble metals (NM) are profoundly influenced by their physical characteristics, including particle size, morphology, and crystal structure.

title: Improving diacetylene photopolymerization in monolayers and ultrathin films
abstract: Simple, yet efficient: self-assembly annealing and inert atmosphere promote nano-localized photoinitiation and improve the quality of polydiacetylenes.

title: Activating discharge and inhibiting self-corrosion by adding indium to the anode of Mg–air battery
abstract: Herein, the mechanism by which In element improves the discharge voltage and efficiency of Mg anode was revealed. Mg–1In exhibited excellent discharge performance as an anode for Mg–air battery.

title: A rapid synthesis of magnetic-core mesoporous silica-shell nanostructures – as potential theranostic agents – by means of microwave irradiation and the atrane method
abstract: A unique scheme to design theranostic materials by using energy-efficient MW-heating, the atrane method, and efficient green chemical extraction routes.

title: A highly-ordered close-packed metallic nanotube array for surface-enhanced Raman scattering
abstract: This study reports on a semiconductor-based, highly-ordered close-packed rhombic metallic nanotube array for surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) applications. The structure is made of Ag, comprising over one hundred million nanotubes per 1 cm 2 .

title: Silicon nanowires grown inside nitrogen-doped hollow carbon spheres as anode materials for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: An interesting approach fabricates Si nanowires in N-doped hollow carbon spheres as lithium-ion battery anodes via the “supercritical fluid-liquid-solid (SFLS)” mechanism.

title: Two-dimensional anisotropic semiconductors: from structure and properties to device applications
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the electronic band structures, optical properties and device applications of 2D anisotropic semiconductors.

title: The efficacy of oleic acid treatment in passivating MAPbI 3 films
abstract: Reliability, scalability, and excellent film properties with large crystals and low grain boundaries are essential for successfully commercializing perovskites in optoelectronic applications.

title: Carbon quantum dots modified with hyperbranched polyglycerol for bioapplications: improved photostability and temperature selectivity
abstract: Surface modification of carbon quantum dots with hyperbranched polyglycerol improved their photostability and enhanced their selectivity to temperature as a nanothermometer.

title: Spectroelectrochemistry with hydrogen-doped indium oxide electrodes monitors electron and hole injection into PbS quantum dots
abstract: Determination of the absolute band edges and Fermi level positions by monitoring electrochemically induced IR absorption changes of EDT cross-linked PbS quantum dot films. IHO substrates provide optical transmittance and electrical conductivity.

title: Hole scavenger concentration dependent photoreduction pathway of nitrobenzene catalyzed by CdS quantum dots
abstract: The concentration of hole scavenger Na 2 SO 3 in nitrobenzene photoreduction catalyzed by CdS quantum dots determines reaction selectivity, highlighting how scavenger quantity can direct photocatalytic pathways.

title: A review on multi-enzyme activity of ZIF, PBA, PCN and MIL MOF-derived cascade nano-enzymes for catalytic applications
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) with multiple enzyme-like activities termed multienzyme mimetic catalysts have emerged as powerful candidates for diverse catalytic applications, particularly in biosensing and biomedicine.

title: Synthesis, characterization, genotoxicity assessment and antibacterial applications of Zanthoxylum armatum silver nanoparticles (ZASNPs) with antibiotic efficacy enhancement potential
abstract: A visual summary of our study, merging herbal knowledge with nanotechnology to combat antibiotic resistance. Silver nanoparticles from Zanthoxylum armatum DC. show effective bactericidal activity and enhance antibiotic efficacy.

title: Strain-tunable optoelectronic and photocatalytic properties of 2D GaN/MoSi 2 P 4 heterobilayers: potential optoelectronic/photocatalytic materials
abstract: Biaxial tensile strain can induce type-II band alignment with the Z-scheme in a GaN/MoSi 2 P 4 heterobilayer, indicating potential applications in optoelectronic and photocatalytic devices.

title: A data-driven machine learning approach for predictive modeling of transition metal dichalcogenide/carbon composite supercapacitor electrodes
abstract: A comprehensive machine learning framework was reported to predict the electrochemical performance of transition metal dichalcogenide/carbon composite supercapacitor electrodes.

title: Metal-free graphitic carbon nitride nanosheet for dual mode fluorescence and electrochemical detection of para -nitrophenol
abstract: A g-CNS-based novel, affordable, and portable dual-mode detection system for rapid and reliable p -NP analysis.

title: Tunable and robust optical and structural properties of a cooperative squaraine-dye aggregate-DNA DX-DAE tile system
abstract: Comprehensive study of a squaraine-dye-labeled DNA DX-tile aggregate system, including cooperative self-assembly of a double-tile, in solution and solid phase.

title: Optical properties of polymer-derived carbon dots
abstract: The optical properties of carbon dots produced from polymers are reviewed. The synthesis, structure and optical properties of polymer-derived carbon dots are highly varied and are explored for a wide range of applications.

title: Triphasic ATH@rGO-CoFe 2 O 4 hybrid nanofiller-integrated waterborne polyurethane composites for dual functionality: fire retardancy and EMI shielding
abstract: A novel bifunctional composite of waterborne polyurethane (wPU) integrating with the ATH@rGO-CoFe 2 O 4 core@shell hybrid nanofiller is synthesized for fire retardancy and EMI shielding.

title: Tunable bilayer WS 2 –WSe 2 and WS 2 –MoS 2 lateral heterostructures for self-powered photodetectors
abstract: Controlled CVD growth of bilayer lateral WS 2 –WSe 2 and WS 2 –MoS 2 heterostructures achieved via temperature gradient manipulation. The bilayer WS 2 –WSe 2 heterostructure exhibits high-performance self-powered photodetection.

title: Direct signatures of d-level hybridization and dimerization in magnetic adatom chains on a superconductor
abstract: Magnetic adatom chains on superconductors provide a platform to explore correlated spin states and emergent quantum phases.

title: Unravelling amino acid–graphene interactions: insights from polarizable force field simulations
abstract: Understanding the interactions between amino acids and graphene surfaces is crucial for advancing applications in biosensing, drug delivery, and nanobiotechnology.

title: Coordination tuning of nanoporous metal films by secondary electrodeposition to optimize methanol electrooxidation activity
abstract: The crystal structure of nanoporous metal films has been controlled by pulsed electrodeposition. The nanoporous structure was preserved, while the crystal structure was adjusted to increase the ECSA and improve the electrocatalytic performance.

title: Gate dielectric-induced lattice strain and band gap tuning in van der Waals 2D semiconducting channels
abstract: This study uses Raman spectroscopy to reveal that hBN gate dielectrics induce higher local lattice strain in van der Waals 2D semiconducting channels compared to air, optimizing gate–channel interfaces for improved transistor performance.

title: Oral delivery of MOMIPP lipid nanoparticles for methuosis-induced cancer chemotherapy
abstract: MOMIPP-SEDDS increased the relative bioavailability by 19.98-fold and achieved favorable tumor inhibition. Clear evidence of methuosis in the tumor tissue following MOMIPP-SEDDS treatment was revealed by TEM imaging.

title: A multilayer template stripping transfer printing method for engineered stretchable electronics
abstract: A multilayer template stripping fabrication method enables engineered microwire networks for stretchable electronics, bridging geometric and nanomaterial approaches while maintaining fabrication simplicity.

title: The influence of reducing agents on structure–activity relationships between oxygen vacancies and Au sites for CO preferential oxidation
abstract: Partially oxidized Au + , with a moderate Au–O coordination number induced by the concentrated distribution of oxygen vacancies, is beneficial for catalytic performance.

title: Radiative energy transfer enabling upconverted circularly polarized persistent luminescence for multilevel information encryption
abstract: Optically active persistent luminescent materials are highly promising for anticounterfeiting applications due to their distinct luminescent features and the ability to display unique optical polarization properties.

title: Kinetics of ion-mediated directed self-assembly of one-dimensional chains of metal nanoparticles in solution
abstract: Directed self-assembly of 10 nm gold particles to form a 1D chain was initiated by adding Cd 2+ salt and observed by UV-Vis spectrum in real time. Simulation-experiment analysis showed distinct “addition” and “condensation” regime confirmed by SEM.

title: Synthetic control over lattice strain in trimetallic AuCu-core Pt-shell nanoparticles
authors: Jessi E. S. van der Hoeven
abstract: Lattice strain in trimetallic Pt-shell Au 1− x Cu x -core nanoparticles was tuned from tensile strain to compressive strain by increasing the Cu fraction in the AuCu-core.

title: Kinetic analysis of silver nanowire synthesis: polyol batch and continuous millifluidic methods
abstract: Nucleation ( k 1 ) and growth ( k 2 ) rate constants for the polyol silver nanowire synthesis were determined for batch and millifluidic flow reactors (MFRs). The MFR rate constants are larger in magnitude and synthesize AgNWs faster than batch reactors.

title: Flexible and stable piezoelectric nanogenerators based on monoclinic phase CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: Piezoelectric nanogenerators based on a composite of monoclinic phase CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals and PDMS are demonstrated. A output voltage and current of up to 50 V and 5.5 μA are obtained under an applied force of 4.2 N with long-term stability.

title: Start-up flow of nanoscale particles and their periodic arrays: insights from fundamental solutions of the unsteady Stokes equations
abstract: Streamlines due to the start-up flow induced by nanoscale particles.

title: Local ferroelectric domain switching phenomenon in PbTiO 3 thin films affected by the imprint effect and external force
abstract: Adjusting the DPTO layer in DPTO/PTO multilayers controls imprint effects, reducing local ferroelectric switching energy. External forces induce flexoelectric phenomena, further lowering switching energy.

title: Simultaneously modulating the morphology and electronic structure of carbon-fiber: a strategy for constructing an efficient electrocatalyst for the in situ production of H 2 O 2 over a wide pH
abstract: F-doped hierarchical porous carbon fiber enhances 2e − ORR for efficient H 2 O 2 production over a wide pH range.

title: Investigation of Ti nanostructures via laboratory scanning-free GEXRF
abstract: Laboratory scanning-free GEXRF in the tender X-ray range is utilized to investigate a Ti nanograting. For the first time, a reconstruction of a periodic 2D nanosample is presented based on laboratory measurements.

title: Innovative integration of MXenes in ion-conductive polymer membranes: synthesis, properties, and applications
abstract: Water purification and a variety of electrochemical energy conversion and storage devices, such as fuel cells, electrolyzers, and redox flow batteries, depend heavily on ion-conductive polymer membranes.

title: Insight into charged drug release from metal–organic frameworks
abstract: Singly charged models were loaded into a range of MOFs to probe the influence of electrostatic interactions during loading and release to evaluate how drug release in the medical setting could be improved for a ubiquitous drug delivery approach.

title: Nano vacuum devices utilizing two-dimensional semiconductors and their potential
abstract: Monolayer TMDC-based vacuum transistors with edge emitters exhibit gate-tunable cold emission and sub-terahertz operation, enabled by strong field enhancement and effective Fermi level modulation.

title: Reconfigurable multiwavelength nanophotonic circuit based on a low-voltage, optically readable engineered resistive switch
abstract: A multiwavelength reconfigurable nanophotonic circuit based on engineered resistive switch with optical readout, featuring a high extinction ratio (27 dB), low-voltage operation (±2 V), and high retention suitable for programmable photonic circuits.

title: Nanocatalysis—facing a sustainable future
abstract: Zhiqun Lin, In Young Kim, and Michelle Personick introduce the Nanoscale themed collection on Nanocatalysis.

title: Graveyard effects of antimicrobial nanostructured titanium over prolonged exposure to drug resistant bacteria and fungi
abstract: The response of nanostructured titanium substrates to prolonged microbial exposure.

title: Correction: Relieving immunosuppression during long-term anti-angiogenesis therapy using photodynamic therapy and oxygen delivery
abstract: Correction for ‘Relieving immunosuppression during long-term anti-angiogenesis therapy using photodynamic therapy and oxygen delivery’ by Qianyuan He et al. , Nanoscale , 2020, 12 , 14788–14800, https://doi.org/10.1039/D0NR02750B.

title: White circularly polarized luminescence from a dual-component emitter induced by FRET between tetraphenylene and PDI derivatives
abstract: LHS with TPE-ASP as the chiral donor and PDI as the energy acceptor was constructed by electrostatic interaction, achieving 94.7% Φ ET and g lum up to |2.41 × 10 −2 |. Meanwhile, white CPL (0.32, 0.33) was modulated by these two components.

title: Three-roll milling exfoliated graphite nanoplatelets with semi-graphitized hard carbon by Ni-catalyzed pyrolysis of phenolic resin for high-performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Three-roll milling exfoliated graphite nanoplatelets (GNPs) with semi-graphitized hard carbon (SGHC) by Ni-catalyzed pyrolysis of phenolic resin were prepared as anode for sodium-ion batteries, which exhibited exceptional stability and ultra-high-rate endurance.

title: Europium intercalation as a route to modulate electronic and magnetic properties of h -BN/Ni(111)
abstract: Intercalation of Eu into the h -BN/Ni(111) interface restores the dispersion of the h -BN π-band and causes doping effects, while the energy shift of the π-band at the Γ point is very sensitive to the interface structure.

title: Atomic engineering of trace endogenous Fe within natural clays into a self-supported Fe 1 –P single-atom cocatalyst for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The trace endogenous Fe species within natural halloysite nanotubes have been engineered into a single-atom Fe 1 –P cocatalyst with high H 2 evolution activity and stability in both dye-sensitized and semiconductor-based systems.

title: Multifunctional Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene/boron nitride nanosheet/polydimethylsiloxane nanocomposites with integrated dielectric and electromagnetic interference shielding performance
abstract: By rational assembly of 2D conductive Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene and insulating boron nitride nanosheets (BNNSs) within the polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) matrix, a multifunctional dielectric nanocomposite with integrated EMI shielding ability is prepared.

title: Broadband photoresponse based on a Te/CuInP 2 S 6 ferroelectric field-effect transistor
abstract: A Te ferroelectric FET photodetector (637 nm–10.6 μm) achieves uncooled mid-IR detection ( R = 10.2 A W −1 , NEP=2.3 pWHz −1/2 ), enabling high-speed broadband photodetection.

title: Heteroatom number-dependent cluster frameworks in structurally comparable Pd–Au nanoclusters
abstract: This work achieved the manipulation of Pd dopant numbers in the cluster framework based on two structurally comparable Pd x Au 12 ( x = 1, 2) nanoclusters.

title: Mapping of multiple plexcitons in disk supershape hybrid nanoparticles
abstract: Here, we reported the excitation of multiple plexcitons in disk supershape hybrid nanoparticles, both as core–shell and bilayer structures, using the finite difference time domain method.

title: Activation of lattice oxygen in a medium-entropy oxide to facilitate an enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: CoFeMnMoO x with abundant oxygen vacancies can provide more active sites for the OER, and the lattice oxygen plays a dominant role in the OER pathway.

title: Synthesis and electronic structure of atomically thin 2H-MoTe 2
abstract: A few millimeter-sized monolayer and bilayer MoTe 2 single crystals are successfully fabricated by a modified mechanical exfoliation technique. Their electronic band structure is studied for the first time by angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy.

title: Plasmonic MXene/Au@Ag core–shell heterostructures: a unified platform for electromagnetic stealth, antimicrobial action, and environmental remediation
abstract: Plasmonic MXene/Au@Ag heterostructures integrate broadband EM absorption (7.6 GHz), >95% antibacterial efficiency, and >90% dye removal, enabling a multifunctional platform for stealth, sterilization, and environmental remediation.

title: Facet-controlled electrosynthesis of nanoparticles by combinatorial screening in scanning electrochemical cell microscopy
abstract: SECCM allows screening of facet-controlled electrodeposition of nanoparticles.

title: Built-in electric field in the Mn/C 60 heterojunction promotes electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction to ammonia
abstract: The built-in electric field in the Mn/C 60 heterostructure optimized the adsorption energy of the key intermediate *NH–*NH 2 and reduced the free energy barrier for the third hydrogenation step, which enhanced the intrinsic activity of the NRR.

title: Enhanced mid-visible light absorption and long-lived charge carriers in an electronically and structurally integrated BiVO 4 –TiO 2 photoanode for efficient artificial photosynthesis applications
abstract: BiVO 4 –TiO 2 films with abundant heterojunctions were produced using the SILAR process. The synergy between BiVO 4 QDs and TiO 2 leads to long-lived charge carriers, significantly enhancing photocatalytic efficiency.

title: Particle surface engineering at the nano-micro scale interfaces of metal-nonmetal bonded polymeric coatings: experimental and in silico evaluations
abstract: Polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) is a well-known and cost-effective synthetic polymer that offers a variety of applications, including medical, food, aerospace, automotive, and material industries, for the construction of structures.

title: Verifying the efficient functional N species of metal-free N-doped carbons for CO 2 -to-CO electrochemical conversion using zeolite-templated carbons with N species tuned by a recarbonization treatment
abstract: In this work, we have controlled the local chemical state of N species of zeolite-templated N-doped carbon materials by a recarbonization treatment to investigate the effective functional N species for CO 2 -to-CO conversion.

title: High throughput screening of Ohmic contacts in 2D metal–semiconductor van der Waals heterojunctions
abstract: High-throughput DFT calculations have been employed to investigate the contact formation of 1297 semiconducting two-dimensional (2D) monolayers from the C2DB as channel materials with three 2D metal monolayers: PdTe 2 , NbSe 2 , and ScS 2 .

title: Efficient construction of core/double-shelled structured AP@nano-graphite@F 2603 energetic microcapsules with low sensitivity and hygroscopicity
abstract: In this work, AP-based energetic microcapsules with low sensitivity and hygroscopicity were prepared using a two-step continuous coating method, which demonstrated significant potential for applications in the field of propellants.

title: Regulating a Co 3 O 4 /ZnO heterojunction aerogel with a built-in electric field for enhanced CO 2 photoreduction to solar fuels from DFT insights
abstract: A novel P-N Co 3 O 4 /ZnO heterojunction aerogel photocatalyst boasts a nano-sized. Under simulated sunlight, it achieves methane and carbon monoxide yields of 18 μmol g −1 h −1 and 14.4 μmol g −1 h −1 , respectively.

title: Insights into the formation of free radicals using metal ferrite nanocatalysts (MFe 2 O 4 , M = Fe, Mn, Zn, Co) prepared by a highly reproducible microwave-assisted polyol method
abstract: A microwave-assisted polyol method yielded metal ferrite nanocatalysts with >95% reproducibility. EPR analysis revealed that Mn promotes ˙OOH formation, Zn suppresses ROS formation via passivation, and acetate buffer masks radical detection.

title: Synthesis of a hollow MoSe 2 @MXene anode material for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: MXene and MoSe 2 as a novel two-dimensional transition metal carbide and selenide, respectively, are widely used in battery electrodes.

title: Single electron transistor based charge sensors: fabrication challenges and opportunities
abstract: The Single Electron Transistor is crucial in various fields including semiconductor device fabrication, material science, medical imaging, Single Electron Memory, Spintronics, SET Microscopy and Bio Sensor.

title: Dynamically in situ tunable, highly robust and sensitive ionically conductive hydrogels enabled by the Hofmeister effect for potential sensor arrays
abstract: For soft electronic applications, regulated mechanical properties and adequate electrical conductivity often help fulfill the specific requirements of flexible materials across diverse applications.

title: Nanoscale sharpening of Ag tip apexes using high-frequency pulsed electrochemical machining
abstract: The fabrication of sharper tips is achieved using high-frequency pulsed electrochemical machining, owing to a reduction in electrical double-layer capacitance at elevated frequencies.

title: Aqueous phase near-infrared emitters: water transfer of colloidal 2D PbS, PbSe and PbTe nanoplatelets
abstract: We report on the aqueous phase transfer of NIR-emissive colloidal 2D PbS, PbSe and PbTe NPLs, using X- and L-type binding mercaptoundecanoic acid. The water-soluble 2D PbX NPLs exhibit efficient telecommunication-band PL relevant for fiber optics.

title: Multimodal imaging approach to track theranostic nanoparticle accumulation in glioblastoma with magnetic resonance imaging and intravital microscopy
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging and two-photon intravital microscopy metrics were correlated to track Ferumoxytol-based theranostic nanoparticles targeting glioblastoma multiforme in vivo at both macroscopic and microscopic levels.

title: Tuning mucoadhesion and mucopenetration in self-assembled poly(lactic acid)-block-poly(oligoethylene glycol methacrylate) block copolymer nanoparticles by controlling side-chain lengths
abstract: Poly(lactic acid)- co -poly(oligoethylene glycol methacrylate) nanoparticles enable precise control of mucoadhesion and mucopenetration at mucosal interfaces by varying the PEG side chain length, representing a versatile strategy for optimizing nanoparticle-based drug delivery systems.

title: Dynamic nanostructures based on DNA self-assembly: from molecular switches to intelligent biomedical applications
abstract: Dynamic nanostructures constructed through DNA self-assembly technology, which integrate chemical, physical, and biological response mechanisms, are widely applied in key areas such as biosensing, imaging, therapy, and molecular computing.

title: Europium doping effects on the properties of CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals: in situ vs. ex situ synthetic path analysis
abstract: This work explores the effect of Eu doping techniques on CsPbBr 3 NCs. In-situ doping allows Eu 3+ incorporation into the lattice and enhances optocatalytic properties, while ex situ sonochemical doping results in surface-bound Eu with limited effects.

title: Patterning technologies of quantum dots for color-conversion micro-LED display applications
abstract: A comprehensive summary of patterning technologies of quantum dots for construction of color-conversion layers in full-color micro-LED displays is provided.

title: Research advances of acoustic particle manipulation techniques in field-assisted manufacturing
abstract: Acoustic particle manipulation and typical acoustic tweezers are explained. Preparation of microdroplets, microstructured functional surfaces and anisotropic composites via acoustic field-assisted manufacturing (FAM) and applications in biomedical fields are discussed, plus challenges and prospects.

title: Unveiling the potential of Cu–Pd/CdS catalysts to supply and rectify electron transfer for H 2 generation from water splitting
abstract: As a future fuel, obtaining hydrogen from water could be a game changer for the renewable energy sector, because it has the potential to be used as an alternative to fossil fuels. Graphical abstract designed using image provided by Freepik.

title: Latest developments in the synthesis of metal–organic frameworks and their hybrids for hydrogen storage
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) are promising materials for hydrogen (H 2 ) storage due to their versatile structures, high surface areas and substantial pore volumes.

title: Excitation wavelength-dependent quantum yield in water-soluble CdTe quantum dots
abstract: The reduced quantum yield in CdTe quantum dots at longer wavelength is described by the presence of a large number of permanent dark particles.

title: Characterisation of polymeric nanoparticles for drug delivery
abstract: Polymeric nanoparticles represent an innovative approach to drug delivery, particularly for addressing complex diseases like cancer.

title: Recent progress in polyaniline-based catalysts for electrochemical energy conversion
abstract: This review clearly summarizes recent synthesis and applications of polyaniline (PANI)-based composites in electrocatalysis field, enhancing understanding of PANI-based electrocatalysts and inspiring future electrocatalyst design.

title: Recent advances in designable nanomaterial-based electrochemical sensors for environmental heavy-metal detection
abstract: Schematic of functional materials for electrochemical sensors, showcasing MWCNTs, CNFs, GO, Pd, Pt, Ni, Fe, Co, COFs, MOFs for detecting heavy metal ions (Pb, Cd, Hg, Ni, Cu, Cr, As) in complex environments with high sensitivity and reliability.

title: Smart self-assembly of a multifunctional theranostic nanozyme for self-enhanced precise chemo/chemodynamic therapy
abstract: A multifunctional TME responsive nano-carrier was developed by Mn 2+ -driven self-assembly of amino acid derivatives to realize effective chemo/chemodynamic combined therapy despite the inconsistent oxygen levels in various tumor regions.

title: A straightforward process manipulates the dramatic morphological changes of DNA rolling circle amplification products
abstract: We introduce a method to modify the morphology of RCA products from nanoparticles to macro-hydrogels. This is accomplished by implementing an additional cold treatment step on a circular DNA template before its extension with phi29 DNA polymerase.

title: Resonant chiroptical effects in an all-dielectric metasurface with monoclinic lattices for terahertz sensing applications
abstract: This work presents an all-dielectric chiral metasurface using circular air holes in an asymmetric monoclinic lattice to achieve strong circular dichroism, which enables label-free biosensing of BSA with high sensitivity and wide detection range.

title: Unveiling superior NH 3 sensing performance: ultrafast response and enhanced recovery kinetics in Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnO nano-hybrid sensors with UV-induced Schottky junctions
abstract: Under UV, Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnO sensors showed a response of 88 at 50 ppm NH 3 , with 10 s response and 13 s recovery at 10 ppm, and a 0.1 ppm LOD, arises from Schottky barriers, UV-induced carriers, and stable adsorption–desorption dynamics.

title: Thienothiophene-based quantum dots: calibration of photophysical properties via carbon dot and biomolecular interactions
abstract: Thienothiophenes are proved to be suitable for designing quantum dots for highly sensitive and customizable optical probes for biosensing, imaging and optoelectronic applications.

title: Mn 2+ -doped CsPbBr 3 perovskite supercrystals: enhancing morphology and substrate variation
abstract: Self-assembly of Mn 2+ -doped CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals on silicon, glass slides, and indium tin oxide-coated glass leads to the formation of cubic or chain supercrystals, where the morphology is dictated by the molar ratio of Mn 2+ added during synthesis.

title: Atomistic observation of defect evolution during solidification in semiconductor InAs
abstract: Defects appearing during the solidification of semiconductor InAs as the contact angle decreases.

title: Robust electro-mechanical actuation in hydrogenated Xenes leading to reversible topological transition
abstract: We report, from first principles, the possibility of reversible onset of the topological insulator (TI) phase in heavier Xanes exclusively through in-plane electro-mechanical actuation.

title: Preparing an MnS@CN composite with lasagna-like nanostructure through pyrolysis of an organic hybrid manganese sulfide for electrochemical lithium storage
abstract: An MnS@CN composite was synthesized by pyrolyzing Mn(phen) 2 S 6 , and its outstanding lithium storage performance originated from its lasagna-like nanostructure.

title: Influence of ethylene thermal decomposition on carbon nanotube growth: insights from a two-zone reactor study
abstract: Gas-phase decomposition of the carbon precursor is a critical yet not fully understood step in growth of carbon nanotubes (CNTs). We present a systematic investigation of how the thermal decomposition of C 2 H 4 influences CNT growth.

title: Electrode informatics accelerated the optimization of key catalyst layer parameters in direct methanol fuel cells
abstract: Finite element simulation combined with machine learning surrogate model was constructed to accelerate power density prediction and evaluate the influence of catalyst layer parameters on the maximum power density of direct methanol fuel cells.

title: DNA origami-templated individual gold nanoclusters: probing their photophysical dynamics using single-molecule fluorescence spectroscopy
abstract: Understanding the photophysical properties of single Au NCs on DNA origami by single-molecule confocal fluorescence microscopy.

title: Defect engineering synthesis of oxygen-doped carbon nitride microtubules for enhanced visible-light-driven photocatalysis
abstract: A 2D O-doped g-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst was synthesized via a H 2 O 2 -assisted hydrothermal method. The O-g-C 3 N 4 exhibited 87.7% degradation of tetracycline within 60 minutes, along with an excellent hydrogen production rate of 613.67 μmol h −1 g −1 .

title: Recent progress in ultraviolet photodetectors based on low-dimensional materials
abstract: UV photodetectors utilizing 0D, 1D, and 2D materials and heterostructures, enable high responsivity and fast response times. Their integration enhances charge transport, minimizes dark current, and improves UV sensing for advanced optoelectronics.

title: Progress in computational methods and mechanistic insights on the growth of carbon nanotubes
abstract: Advancements in modern computational tools reveal key CNT growth mechanisms and pave the way for targeted synthesis and scalable production.

title: Ionic conductivity mechanisms in PEO–NaPF 6 electrolytes
abstract: Overview of ion transport in PEO-NaPF6 electrolytes where salt concentration controls ion solvation, clustering, viscosity, and conductivity leading to nonmonotonic trend with peak transport near 1 M.

title: Advances in semiconductor quantum dot-based physical unclonable functions for enhanced security applications
abstract: Quantum dots (QDs) have emerged as promising platforms for physical unclonable functions (PUFs), where their nanoscale heterogeneity, emission variability, and spectral tunability enable robust, unique, and secure authentication.

title: UV-ozone surface pretreatment for high quality ALD-grown ultrathin coatings on bismuth oxyhalide photocatalysts
abstract: Illustration of the proposed layer-by-layer growth of alumina on BiOX substrates in the absence of (top) and following (bottom) UVOC pretreatment.

title: Correction: Nanophotonics of mid-infrared plasmon-polaritons at interfaces between metals and two-dimensional crystals
abstract: Correction for ‘Nanophotonics of mid-infrared plasmon-polaritons at interfaces between metals and two-dimensional crystals’ by Flávio H. Feres, et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 13229–13237, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR04543B.

title: Facile integration of single-crystalline phthalocyanine nanowires and nanotrees as photo-enhanced conductometric sensors
abstract: This article presents a reproducible and affordable methodology for fabricating organic nanowires (ONWs) and nanotrees (ONTs) as light-enhanced conductometric O 2 sensors.

title: Investigation of the Mo 2 Ti 2 C 3 T x MXene in the electrochemical immunosensing of the respiratory syncytial virus (RSV)
abstract: Schematic illustration of (A) the fabrication steps of the Mo 2 Ti 2 C 3 T x MXene-based immunosensor and (B) its application in the electrochemical detection of the RSV antigen.

title: Lead-free halide double perovskite nanoflakes as high-performance SERS substrates for detection of trace organic pollutants: chemical enhancement versus electromagnetic enhancement
abstract: Lead-free halide double perovskites nanoflakes acting as high-performance SERS substrates for detection of trace organic pollutants: relative contributions of chemical enhancement and electromagnetic enhancement.

title: Magnetic hyperthermia in focus: emerging non-cancer applications of magnetic nanoparticles
abstract: Magnetic hyperthermia extends beyond oncology, harnessing nanoscale heat generation for advanced bioapplications, catalytic processes, and environmental technologies.

title: Enhancing the photocatalytic water electrolysis performance of Zn 2 SnO 4 nanostructures via post-synthesis nitrogen doping
abstract: Nitrogen-doped Zn 2 SnO 4 nanostructures were synthesized via hydrothermal treatment exhibiting band gap narrowing, enhanced charge transfer properties, and improved photoelectrochemical water splitting performance.

title: SiO x /C composite spheres as an anode material for high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Sol–gel synthesized spherical SiO x /C composites with uniform SiOx distribution achieved high capacity (658.7 mA h g −1 at 2 A g −1 ) and stable cycling (500 cycles), effectively mitigating volume expansion for energy storage applications.

title: Smart sarcosinate-based catanionic vesicles for efficient doxorubicin delivery in tumor microenvironments
abstract: Stable, biocompatible SLSar-based vesicles enable efficient DOX loading, pH-triggered release, and strong anticancer activity at nanomolar levels.Graphical abstract created, in part, with BioRender. Machado, R. (2025) https://BioRender.com/xherjw3.

title: Hydrogel-based sensors for multimodal health monitoring: from material design to intelligent sensing
abstract: Hydrogels, due to their biocompatibility, tunability, and stimulus responsiveness, are promising materials for flexible health monitoring.

title: Atomically dispersed metal sites on covalent organic frameworks for photocatalysis
abstract: The effect of atomically dispersed metal-site content on catalysts.

title: CVD-grown NiO-carbon nanofiber decorated rGO hybrids for high-performance electrochemical hydrogen storage: role of hierarchical porous structures
abstract: The development of sustainable materials for green energy storage systems has accelerated due to the growing demand for energy worldwide and environmental concerns.

title: Self-reconstruction of FeCoNiMoW high entropy alloy to boost OER activity with robust stability for anion exchange membrane water electrolyzer
abstract: After self-reconstruction, the synthesised FeCoNiMoW@FeCoNiOOH catalysts with core-shell structure showed excellent activity and durability for OER.

title: Heat-treatment-induced enhancement of activity and stability in Rh/Mo-doped PtNi octahedra for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Rh/Mo doping stabilises octahedral PtNi nanoparticles during reductive annealing, leading to improved ORR activity and durability through shape retention and Pt-skin formation.

title: Upholding hyaluronic acid's multi-functionality for nucleic acid drug delivery to target EMT in breast cancer
abstract: Optimized HA-coated polyplexes selectively target EMT-phenotypic breast cancer cells via CD44v/s-mediated endocytosis while avoiding epithelial cells, in contrast to uncoated polyplexes (Px), which are internalized by all cells.

title: A nanoscale chemical oscillator: reversible formation of palladium nanoparticles in ionic liquid
abstract: During transmission electron microscopy imaging, nanoparticles display cyclic behaviour resembling that of living organisms: birth, growth, and decay, followed by the next generation of nanoparticles repeating the cycle.

title: Bright yellow fluorescent N-doped Ti 3 C 2 MXene quantum dots as an “on/off/on” nanoprobe for selective As 3+ ion detection
abstract: Bright yellow fluorescent nitrogen-doped MXene quantum dots were synthesized and employed to detect As 3+ and MBTZ via fluorescence “on/off/on” phenomenon.

title: 2D material-based infrared photodetectors: recent progress, challenges, and perspectives
abstract: 2D materials-based infrared (IR) photodetectors are essential for a wide range of applications, including optical communication, night vision, and environmental monitoring; graphical images reproduced with permission; full details in paper.

title: Recent progress on low-temperature selective catalytic reduction of NO x by NH 3 based on MOF-derived materials: a review
abstract: Selective catalytic reduction of NO by NH 3 using MOF derived materials are reviewed.

title: Evidence of the bottom stiffness effect on atomic force microscopy-based cell mechanobiology
abstract: AFM mechanical property measurements might be affected by stiffness of the solid support. A bottom effect correction model should be used to get the intrinsic mechanical properties of a cell.

title: Uncovering the electrocatalytic potential of two-dimensional Pt–Ni bimetallic aerogels
abstract: Schematic representation of 2D Pt-Ni aerogels for the methanol oxidation reaction (MOR).

title: Platinum single atoms on titania aid dye photodegradation whereas platinum nanoparticles do not
abstract: The study revealed that Pt single atoms on TiO 2 enhance AO7 photodegradation through ˙O 2 − production but have no impact on RhB degradation. The distinct behavior is due to different reaction pathways, with Pt nanoparticles exhibiting minimal effect.

title: Free standing epitaxial oxides through remote epitaxy: the role of the evolving graphene microstructure
abstract: Epitaxial BaTiO 3 (BTO) was grown via remote epitaxy using CVD graphene, and freestanding membranes were transferred to Si. It correlates graphene microstructure, defect formation during pulsed laser deposition, and BTO crystallinity.

title: Probing the influence of morphological transformation on the electrochemical properties of hydrated tungsten oxide (WO 3− x ·H 2 O) for a high-rate aqueous asymmetric supercapacitor
abstract: The present study aims to probe morphological tuning of hydrated tungsten oxide (WO 3− x ·H 2 O) nanostructures and investigate their electrochemical performance for energy storage in supercapacitors.

title: Ferroelectricity enhances ion migration in hard carbon anodes for high-performance potassium ion batteries
abstract: Ferroelectric BTO particle embedded hard carbon anodes accelerate potassium ion migration and result in better electrochemical performance.

title: Optimizing active sites via chemical bonding of 2D metal–organic frameworks and MXenes for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction activity
abstract: A conductive MXene (Ti 3 C 2 T x ) integrated with 2D Ni 3 (HITP) 2 -MOF via in situ synthesis enhances active site exposure, boosting electrocatalytic HER performance for efficient, sustainable hydrogen production.

title: Nonlinear memristor model with exact solution allows for ex situ reservoir computing training and in situ inference
abstract: A generalized logistic differential equation model of biomolecular memristors improves the tuning of hyperparameters of parallel-memristor physical reservoir computing systems by enabling ex situ training.

title: Multipurpose triadic MXene/garlic/gellan gum-based architecture in the horizon of bone tissue regeneration
abstract: Introducing Ti 3 C 2 T x nanosheets and garlic extract into a porous gellan gum scaffold could promote the hydrophilicity rate, mechanical strength, and antibacterial activity, providing a favorable structure for the growth and attachment of MG63 cells.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic H 2 generation and Cr( vi ) reduction by a sheet-on-sheet Cd(OH) 2 /CdS nanocomposite
abstract: Herein, we present a novel Cd(OH) 2 /CdS sheet-on-sheet nanocomposite, which demonstrates significantly enhanced photocatalytic performance for both H 2 generation and Cr( vi ) reduction under visible light.

title: Flexible porous carbon fiber composite electrodes for hydrogen and chlorine production with antibacterial applications
abstract: Flexible porous carbon fiber composite electrodes were successfully employed in hydrogen/chlorine production and antibacterial applications.

title: A smart nanocomposite system for controlled insulin release and glucose sensing in diabetes management
abstract: Schematic illustration of a smart nanocomposite system for integrated glucose sensing and insulin release in diabetes management.

title: Pressure effects on the surface enhanced Raman spectroscopy of a ZIF-67/R6G system
abstract: High-pressure SERS chemistry of a nano-ZIF-67 MOF host substrate and guest R6G molecule correlated with structural stability by HP-XRD.

title: Exploring the intrinsic dielectric behavior of SrTiO 3 -based heterostructures: the impact of layer architecture on soft mode dynamics
abstract: THz spectroscopy reveals ferroelectric soft mode behavior in SrTiO 3 /AlFeO 3 heterostructure compared to SrTiO 3 films. Intermediate AlFeO 3 layers prevent degradation of the intrinsic dielectric response of SrTiO 3 layers in the heterostructure.

title: Water oxidation on a sustainable polymeric proton relay: the role of post-phosphating of an oxide sub-layer on PCET
abstract: Proton transfer is greatly enhanced by combining the phosphate underlayer with the interfacial amine groups of the poly-lysine.

title: Reticular construction of a two-dimensional Ag 12 cluster-assembled material for rapid and selective luminescent sensing of antibiotics in aqueous media
abstract: A chemically robust Ag 12 -cluster material (TUS 8) exhibits exceptional aqueous photoluminescence and functions as a recyclable sensor for selective antibiotic detection in water.

title: A polarization-sensitive photodetector based on a b-AsP/In 2 Se 3 heterostructure
abstract: The b-AsP/In 2 Se 3 heterostructure phototransistor device exhibits excellent performance in solar-blind ultraviolet detection with a high photoresponsivity of 4129.3 A W −1 and a polarization dichroic ratio of ∼2.12.

title: Sensing relative humidity with a fluorescent seed-like biodegradable flier
abstract: Biodegradable plant-inspired artificial fliers change the intensity of their fluorescence as a function of relative humidity.

title: One-step sintering synthesis of a robust self-supported MoSe 2 /Ni 3 Se 2 heterostructure electrode for efficient hydrogen evolution at a high current density
abstract: This work presents a robust MoSe 2 /Ni 3 Se 2 heterostructure electrode fabricated via one-step sintering. The electrode delivers low overpotenial of 341 mV at 1000 mA cm −2 and maintains stable operation for 200 h at 800 mA cm −2 .

title: Reducing deformation of single-walled defective silicon carbide nanotubes under charge injection: a first principles study
abstract: Charge injection in large-diameter defective silicon carbide nanotubes effectively reduces their radial deformation and they exhibit excellent electromechanical response, highlighting them as promising candidates for nanoactuator applications.

title: l -Ergothioneine promotes the formation of long gold nanowires and their 2D assembly
abstract: l -Ergothioneine demonstrates potential as a directing agent for the growth and assembly of 1D gold nanowires.

title: Realizing zero-threshold population inversion via plasmonic doping
abstract: Achieving near-zero threshold population inversion in quantum dots via hot electron doping from plasmonic resonators.

title: Tunable functional coverage of biocompatible magnesium silicate nanotubes by microwave-assisted silanization
authors: 'Valeria Secchi', 'Daniela D'Alessio', 'Andrea Erroi', 'Silvia Mostoni', 'Giancarlo Capitani', 'Alberto Calloni', 'Gianlorenzo Bussetti', 'Massimiliano D'Arienzo', 'Roberto Lorenzi', 'Keren Keinan-Adamsky', 'Shai Rahimipour', 'Angelo Monguzzi', 'Marcello Campione'
abstract: A microwave-assisted method enables tunable functionalization of synthetic mineral nanotubes, providing precise surface control. Insights into the mineral–silane interface support rational design of multifunctional nanomedicine platforms.

title: Cerium-doped iron oxide nanostructures used as a voltammetric sensor for the electrochemical detection of thymol in the presence of bentazone
abstract: Cerium-doped Fe 2 O 3 (Ce–Fe 2 O 3 ) nanoparticles (NPs) were used to develop a modified electrochemical sensor based on a carbon paste electrode (CPE).

title: An assembly modules deformation strategy improved the chemical stability and self-assembly stability of docetaxel prodrugs nanoassemblies
abstract: The structural deformation enhanced the self-assembly and stability of prodrug nanoassemblies, balancing their effectiveness and safety.

title: Polymer nanocomposites reinforced with Al 2 O 3 nanoplates for high-temperature capacitive energy storage application
abstract: Schematic diagram demonstrating the design of polymer nanocomposites reinforced with Al 2 O 3 nanoplates for realizing high energy-storage performance under high-temperature conditions.

title: A Ni 2 P/NiMoO x nanocone electrocatalyst for efficient hydrogen evolution: tip-enhanced local electric field effect
abstract: Ni 2 P/NiMoO x nanocones can effectively enhance the electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction through a cross-scale synergistic effect of the mesoscale local electric field and the atomic scale electron structure.

title: Interfacial engineering of CuSe 2 /FeSe 2 heterojunctions for water splitting: a pathway to high-performance hydrogen and oxygen evolution reactions
abstract: Enhanced bifunctional electrocatalysis via CuSe 2 /FeSe 2 heterojunctions for efficient water splitting was achieved.

title: Strong magnetic exchange coupling of a dibenzo-fused rhomboidal nanographene and its homocoupling with tunable periodicities on a metal surface
abstract: STM/STS measurements reveal strong singlet–triplet excitations of dibenzo[5,4]rhombene on Au(111). Chains with variable periodicities exhibit the absence of magnetic coupling, preserving the individual magnetic properties of each unit.

title: Dynamic FeO x /FeWO x nanocomposite memristor for neuromorphic and reservoir computing
abstract: Memristors are crucial in computing due to their potential for miniaturization, energy efficiency, and rapid switching, making them particularly suited for advanced applications such as neuromorphic computing and in-memory operations.

title: Correction: Targeted imaging of the lysosome and endoplasmic reticulum and their pH monitoring with surface regulated carbon dots
abstract: Correction for ‘Targeted imaging of the lysosome and endoplasmic reticulum and their pH monitoring with surface regulated carbon dots’ by Shuang E et al. , Nanoscale , 2018, 10 , 12788–12796, https://doi.org/10.1039/C8NR03453B.

title: Heterostructured assembly of ternary SnS/Mo 2 S 3 /g-C 3 N 4 nanocomposites for high-performance hybrid supercapacitors, photodegradation of organic dyes and Cr( vi ) reduction
abstract: This research engineered ternary SnS/Mo 2 S 3 /g-C 3 N 4 heterojunction nanocomposites, which exhibited advantageous physicochemical properties, thereby enhancing energy storage and photocatalytic performance.

title: Stability of multifunctional Pd–Rh electrocatalysts supported on Co 3 O 4 (111) in alkaline environment: impact of the electronic metal–support interaction
abstract: EMSI stabilizes noble metals against dissolution, but results in strong oxidation controlled by the nature of the metal/support interface.

title: Biocatalytic 3D binary crystals formed through the self-assembly of enzyme-embedded ferritin
abstract: Enzyme-embedded protein cage crystals are formed via electrostatic co-crystallization, enabling reusable biocatalytic assemblies with spatial control.

title: Energy-conversion nano/micromaterial based retinal prostheses for vision restoration
abstract: This review focuses on retinal prostheses using energy-conversion nano/micromaterials (photovoltaic, piezoelectric, upconversion, and photothermal), which enable wireless/self-powered operation with advantages like high sensitivity and resolution.

title: Eco-friendly nitrogen-doped carbon dots from Sauropus androgynus leaf extract: a sustainable tool for mung bean germination, bioimaging and tissue culture enhancement
abstract: This work reports the impact of carbon dots (CDs) on plant growth and their application in tissue culture media.

title: Conductance modulation in graphene/antiferromagnet van der Waals heterostructures induced by magnetic order
abstract: Graphene-antiferromagnet (AFM) van der Waals heterostructures can be used to modify graphene conductance upon changes in the AFM magnetic state.

title: Device-level modelling for predicting the total density of states of single-walled CNTs with increasing chirality: a fusion of ab initio modeling and a machine learning framework
abstract: A DFT-ML hybrid model predicts the TDOS of zigzag SWCNTs ( n = 4–10), revealing chirality-dependent electronic behavior with a metallic or semiconducting character, enabling efficient nanoscale material design.

title: Chiral co-assembly of a polyoxometalate complex with an achiral pyrene derivative enables redox-modulated circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: We report the fabrication of helical structures with responsive circularly polarized luminescence via the chiral co-assembly of a cholesterol-modified Lindqvist type polyoxometalate complex and an achiral pyrenyl derivative.

title: Asymmetric yolk@shell structured Fe-doped polydopamine or carbon@mesoporous silica composite particles
abstract: Post- and pre-doping strategies are employed to introduce Fe 3+ ions into polydopamine (PDA) nanospheres for the construction of asymmetric yolk@shell structured Fe-doped PDA or carbon@mesoporous silica composite particles.

title: Surface palladium nanoparticles in ionic liquids modified with phosphorus ligands for enhanced catalytic semi-hydrogenation
abstract: Thermodynamic stability of nanoparticles necessitates the use of stabilizing agents to provide steric and electronic protection.

title: Advances in carbon nanomembranes for separation: from free-standing films to composite structures
abstract: This work reviews carbon nanomembranes (CNMs) from self-assembled monolayers to composite membranes, focusing on their development and potential in separation applications.

title: Lattice stability and elastic evolution of CdTe membranes fabrication using III–V heterostructures as a substrate
abstract: CdTe is a key compound for II–VI semiconductor systems providing a general roadmap for telluride compounds, ranging from optically active layers to diluted magnetic semiconductors and topological insulators.

title: Crystallization control of Cu( i )-halide via thermal evaporation for improving resistive switching memory performance
abstract: Enhanced resistive switching performance in CsCu 2 I 3 thin films through crystallinity improvement via substrate deposition and post-annealing temperature control in thermal evaporation.

title: Unravelling the potential of a hybrid borocarbonitride biphenylene 2D network for thermoelectric applications: a first principles study
abstract: The crystal structure and electronic band spectrum of the biphenylene 2D network of hybrid borocarbonitride, along with its thermoelectric efficiency in terms of power factor and figure of merit.

title: Electronic structures and charge transport mobilities in hybrid organic–inorganic mixed Sn–Pb alloyed perovskites
abstract: An all-perovskite tandem cell based on narrow-bandgap mixed tin–lead (Sn–Pb) alloyed perovskites is a potential photovoltaic device whose power conversion efficiency can exceed the Shockley–Queisser limit of a single-junction solar cell, 33%.

title: What is the potential of walnut shell-derived carbon in battery applications?
abstract: The use of walnut shells for energy storage offers a sustainable, innovative approach, enhancing energy density and cost efficiency. While it presents environmental benefits, challenges remain in scalability and optimization.

title: Unveiling the doping effect of mixed-halide CsPb(Br 1− n X n ) 3 (X = I, Cl) single crystals toward high-sensitivity radiation detection
abstract: This study demonstrates that light halide doping in CsPb(Br 1− n X n ) 3 single crystals enhances radiation detection performance by improving resistivity, suppressing ion migration, and optimizing carrier transport.

title: Physical determinants of nanoparticle-mediated lipid membrane fusion
abstract: The interplay between nanoparticle size and membrane curvature plays a key role in regulating synthetic nanoparticle-mediated membrane fusion.

title: Boosting nanoparticle yield: enhanced atom-to-nanoparticle conversion in gas aggregation
abstract: Pulsed magnetron sputtering can boost nanoparticle yield by factor of 10 without increasing material or energy input.

title: Tailored nanoscale structure of flame-made antimony doped tin oxides and their near-infrared shielding properties
abstract: Systematic investigation of flame-made ATO nanoparticle size effects on NIR-shielding, achieving optimized performance for energy-efficient applications.

title: High-efficiency photoacoustic transducers based on plasmonic EGaIn liquid metal nanoparticles
abstract: Plasmonic LMNPs enhance light-acoustic conversion. Self-assembled nanogaps boost coupling. 5.1e-3 efficiency achieved (300 nm/120 μm), outperforming noble metals by 10–100×.

title: A molecularly engineered MOF photocatalyst for CO production from visible light-driven CO 2 reduction
abstract: Installation of bis-terpyridine Ru( ii ) complex as a catalyst center into a Zr( iv )-based NDI MOFs through SALE to develop a highly efficient photocatalyst for selective CO 2 to CO conversion.

title: Control of Cu morphology on TaN barrier and combined Ru-TaN barrier/liner substrates for nanoscale interconnects from atomistic kinetic Monte Carlo simulations
abstract: Atomistic kMC investigation of the effect of Ru-modified TaN combined barrier/liner materials on Cu interconnect deposition to promote the desired 2D growth.

title: Covalent functionalization of transition metal dichalcogenides with perylene for light harvesting devices
abstract: Perylene molecules on single-layer TMDs tune optical and electronic properties via covalent functionalization. Trap states in hybrids influence behavior, enabling high-performance nanotechnology devices.

title: Formation of hydrocarbons and carbon oxides in MXene reactions with water under varying oxidative conditions
abstract: This study explores the environmental stability of MXenes, revealing the formation of higher hydrocarbons and carbon oxides. It highlights methane dominance under reducing conditions and carbon oxide production under oxidative environments.

title: Multifunctional ZnO-based optical memristors for synapse–neuron integration and neuromorphic vision systems
abstract: The optical memristor based on ITO/ZnO/ITO integrates synapse-neuron functions under light. It shows PPF, reservoir computing, nociceptor-like responses under high light intensity and probabilistic spiking, enabling RBM based recognition under low light intensity.

title: Machine learning assisted preparation of highly crystalline cellulose nanocrystals: lessons from cellulose sources and reaction conditions
abstract: We successfully developed a machine-learning model for predicting the crystalline nature (highly crystalline, semi-crystalline, or low crystalline) using a KNN classifier with an accuracy of 95%.

title: Strategies for industrial-grade seawater electrolysis: from electrocatalysts and device design to techno-economic analysis
abstract: Electrocatalysts for industrial-grade seawater electrolysis.

title: Halloysite nanotubes as a vector for hydrophobic perfluorinated porphyrin-based photosensitizers for singlet oxygen generation
abstract: The combination of perfluorinated porphyrins with halloysite nanotubes allows PDT-mediated tumor cell death under light irradiation.

title: Nanomaterial-enabled anti-biofilm strategies: new opportunities for treatment of bacterial infections
abstract: Scheme of nano-delivery systems in anti-biofilm application. Created with BioRender.com.

title: Donor–acceptor conjugated polymers as high-mobility semiconductors: prospects for organic thermoelectrics
abstract: Donor–acceptor conjugated polymers combine electron- and hole-transport units, enabling both p- and n-type doping. Their high intrinsic mobility and tunable energetics make them promising for efficient organic thermoelectrics.

title: Next-generation lipid nanocarriers for Parkinson's therapy: nose-to-brain innovations and clinical prospects
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) remains one of the most formidable challenges in central nervous system (CNS) drug delivery due to the restrictive blood–brain barrier (BBB) and limited efficacy of current dopaminergic therapies. Created in BioRender. Fahmy, S. (2025) https://BioRender.com/vsusgrv.

title: Conformal chemical vapor deposition of B 4 C thin films onto carbon nanotubes
