abstract: We use a newly developed conformal CVD process to uniformly coat CNTs in a membrane structure with boron carbide. We show that the CVD process preserves the structure of the CNTs and merely adds a layer of B 4 C to their outer surface.

title: Composition-controlled synthesis of Pt–Pd–Cu ternary metal nanoparticles for an enhanced electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Synthesis of controllable ternary Pt–Pd–Cu nanoparticles and their application in the hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: Pulsed laser-induced dewetting for the production of noble-metal high-entropy-alloy nanoparticles
abstract: Pulsed laser-induced dewetting was used as a facile method to form AgCuPdPtAu high-entropy-alloy nanoparticles with controllable atomic composition. The electronic structure and charge redistribution in the nanoparticle were studied by XPS and XANES.

title: Rapid mapping of spinal and supraspinal connectome via self-targeting glucose-based carbon dots
abstract: Ultrasmall glucose carbon dots rapidly cross the blood-spinal cord barrier and target neurons at the spinal and supraspinal network. The graphical abstract was created in BioRender.

title: Electrochemical restructuring of H 2 O 2 activated copper selenide for CO 2 reduction
authors: 'Wenjian Hu', 'Deema Balalta', 'Zhiyuan Chen', 'Imran Abbas', 'Jia Song', 'Balázs Barhács', 'Márton Guba', 'Tibor Höltzl', 'Francesco D'Acapito', 'Thomas Altantzis', 'Jan Vaes', 'Sara Bals', 'Didier Grandjean', 'Deepak Pant', 'Ewald Janssens'
abstract: Sequential chemical and electrochemical activation induces controlled restructuring of Cu 2− x Se nanowires, yielding phase-separated architectures that modulate CO 2 RR selectivity.

title: Highly sensitive and spectrally tunable UV photodetectors via interface barrier engineering in floating-gate transistors
abstract: The floating-gate transistor demonstrates a highly sensitive response to UV light. Its exceptional spectral selectivity is controlled via the interface barrier height.

title: A flexible and energy independent fluorescence radiation fiber film dosimeter fabricated by electrostatic spinning
abstract: This article proposes a flexible and energy independent fluorescence radiation fiber film dosimeter fabricated by electrostatic spinning, providing a candidate material for wearable visual dosimeters for X-ray detection in the range of 0–350 Gy.

title: Rational design of NiCo 2 O 4 @carbon hollow spheres as a high-performance electrode material for flexible supercapacitors
abstract: The unique hollow morphology of NiCo 2 O 4 , combined with a uniform carbon coating, provides exceptional ion accessibility and electron transport.

title: Facile design of MOF-derived porous CeO 2 /MWCNT nanocomposites for the hydrogen evolution reaction and machine learning-assisted stability forecasting
abstract: This study shows that a MOF-derived CeO 2 /MWCNT nanocomposite boosts the hydrogen evolution reaction by 1.3 × compared to bare CeO 2 , due to improved conductivity, stability, and the electrode–electrolyte interface.

title: Precise estimation of surface-layer monomer counts in nanoparticles
abstract: A universal expression accurately determines surface monomer counts in nanoclusters, overcoming conventional geometric limitations and revealing critical insights into scaling laws and nucleation theory.

title: Synthesis of intrinsically sodium intercalated ultra-thin layered MnO 2 and its ionic charge transport
abstract: Synthesis of large-area single-crystalline layered Na-MnO 2 has been demonstrated via molten-salt-assisted chemical vapor deposition. The interlamellar ionic transport facilitates neuromorphic applications.

title: Thin films based on nanocomposites of crumpled graphene fully decorated with Prussian blue: a new material for aqueous battery systems
abstract: This study involves synthesizing thin films through an interfacial method, which relies on composites of Prussian blue nanoparticles and nanostructures derived from graphene, known as crumpled graphene.

title: High-temperature annealing enables the dielectric modulation of MXene for enhanced electromagnetic wave absorption and shielding
abstract: Schematic of the mechanism of electromagnetic-waves-shielding action of MXene samples annealed at 350 °C and 500 °C.

title: High-throughput nanoscale metrology of ultrathin inert and functionalized dielectric substrates using plasmonic nanoparticles
abstract: Rapid and cost-effective approach for thin film metrology based on spectral shifts of plasmonic nanoparticles combined with a dark-field-based platform.

title: One-step construction of mesh-like CoSe 2 microflowers for magnesium–lithium hybrid batteries with enhanced rate performance
abstract: The mesh-like CoSe 2 microflowers architecture shortens ion-diffusion pathways, enlarges reaction area, and accommodates volume change, thus achieving high rate performance in Mg–Li hybrid batteries.

title: Innovative formulation of a functional nano-copolymer derived from glycidyl methacrylate and acrylonitrile as an exceptionally sensitive and selective electrochemical sensor for folic acid detection in pharmaceutical and food samples
abstract: The detection of folic acid in food samples or pharmaceutical drugs is crucial since folic acid affects the development and growth of a fetus and the biological processes of the human body.

title: The role of trap states in MoS 2 -based photodetectors
abstract: In this study, we revealed trap states' impact on MoS 2 photodetector photocurrent changes during optical ON/OFF cycles. Results show trap states significantly affect performance, providing a basis to optimize 2D material-based photodetectors.

title: Electrodeposition of Cu nanowires with ultrahigh-density twin boundaries: an electrochemical perspective on nanotwinning
abstract: Ultrahigh-density coherent twin boundaries are introduced in copper nanowires through the process of template-assisted electrodeposition.

title: Photonic crystal enhanced fluorescence using a hybrid hexagonal boron nitride spacer and plasmonic gold cryosoret cavity
abstract: Electric and magnetic field hotspots enhanced using a hBN spacer with Au cryosoret nanocavities. The radiating dipole at the cryosoret–hBN–photonic crystal interface drives unprecedented photonic crystal enhanced fluorescence (PCEF).

title: Recent advances in metal–organic frameworks and derived multifunctional materials for photocatalytic applications
abstract: Recent advances and challenges in MOFs-based photocatalysts are reviewed in classification, structural properties, and modifications. Photocatalytic applications of renewable energy and environmental sustainability were systematically summarized.

title: Recent progress of carbon dots in the theranostics of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases
abstract: We summarize recent advances in the theranostics of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases using carbon dots (CDs), and introduce the specific application performance of CDs.

title: High-performance all-inorganic CdSe/CdS nanorod-based light emitting diodes enabled by controlled electrophoretic deposition
authors: 'Nandita Biswas', 'Anthony Mullen', 'Lin Lyu', 'Yongliang Zhang', 'Charlie O'Mahony', 'Matthew Snelgrove', 'Sergey Beloshapkin', 'Devika Laishram', 'Jinlei Wu', 'Syed A. M. Tofail', 'Christophe Silien', 'Kevin M. Ryan', 'Ning Liu'
abstract: Electrophoretic deposition is used to achieve vertically aligned CdSe/CdS nanorod film in all-inorganic NR-LEDs. Passivating the nickel oxide hole transport layer with Pentaflurothiophenol molecules, the devices exhibit a maximum EQE of 10.8%.

title: Abundant active-site engineering enables porous Co–N–C electrocatalysts towards superior oxygen reduction reaction activity
abstract: Riboflavin was used to modify ZIF-8 and ZIF-67, leveraging the high electronegativity of nitrogen atoms in the isoalloxazine ring to enhance the anchoring effect on metal ions, thereby enhancing the electrochemical performance of Co–N–C catalysts.

title: Strain-induced Ni–Re alloy electrodeposits as efficient electrocatalysts for water-splitting reactions
abstract: Strain-induced Ni–Re alloy electrodeposits as electrocatalysts for water-splitting reactions.

title: Simultaneous pore confinement and sidewall modification of an N-rich COF with Pd( ii ): an efficient and sustainable heterogeneous catalyst for cross-coupling reactions
abstract: A Palladium-supported covalent organic framework (COF) designed for efficient C–C cross-coupling reactions with good stability and recyclability.

title: Data driven approaches in nanophotonics: a review of AI-enabled metadevices
abstract: AI surrogates are displacing brute-force simulations in meta-optics; we review strategies from CNNs to LLMs that speed design, capture coupling, and yield fabrication-friendly, multifunctional devices.

title: Morphology-tuned MnO x /TiO 2 nanocatalysts for recycling PET plastic waste using biomass-derived ethylene glycol
abstract: This work elucidated the effect of TiO 2 morphology on the structure–activity properties of MnO x /TiO 2 nanocatalysts toward developing practically feasible catalytic routes for the plastic recycling industry.

title: Atomic level surface roughness of epoxy resin induced by novel chemical mechanical polishing with high material removal rate and its mechanisms elucidated using molecular dynamics simulations and density functional theory
abstract: Epoxy resins are widely used in the coating, adhesive and electronics industries.

title: Unique hierarchical NiFe-LDH/Ni/NiCo 2 S 4 heterostructure arrays on nickel foam for the improvement of overall water splitting activity
abstract: A hierarchical heterostructure of NiFe-LDH/Ni/NiCo 2 S 4 nanowire grown on NF exhibits a superiority in the water-splitting reaction with a low cell voltage of 1.53 eV, which evidently exceeds the standard Pt–C/NF//RuO 2 /NF cell of 1.61 V.

title: Biomimetic red blood cell membrane-coated FePt metal–organic framework nanoparticles: a multifunctional theranostic system for enhanced MRI and targeted therapy
abstract: Iron-platinum nanoparticles encapsulated in metal–organic frameworks and coated with red blood cell membranes enhance MRI stability and tumor targeting, enabling improved visualization and real-time diagnosis of hepatocellular carcinoma.

title: Temperature-dependent resistive switching statistics and mechanisms in nanoscale graphene–SiO 2 –graphene memristors
abstract: Stochastic switching nature in nanoscale graphene–SiO 2 –graphene memristor, we statistically investigate the switching mechanisms.

title: A dual-switch fluorescence biosensor with an entropy-driven and DNA walker cascade amplification circuit for sensitive microRNA detection
abstract: We developed a dual-switch fluorescence biosensor that integrates a two-step cascade signal amplification circuit of EDC and DNA walker on AuNP for sensitive miRNA detection, enabling the effective differentiation of cancer cells from normal cells.

title: Loading Pt clusters is more conducive to photocatalytic hydrogen evolution compared to single atoms and nanoparticles
abstract: TiO 2 -supported Pt nanoclusters demonstrate significantly superior photocatalytic hydrogen production performance via water splitting compared to those loaded with Pt single atoms or nanoparticles.

title: Quantitative comparison of local field enhancement from tip-apex and plasmonic nanofocusing excitation via plasmon-assisted field emission resonances
abstract: A novel method for comparing near-field intensities of the localized surface plasmon excited by different schemes in a scanning-tunneling-microscope junction.

title: Hierarchical chirality observed from chiral supramolecular assembling of racemic and enantiopure helicene derivatives on silica nanohelix surfaces
abstract: The combination of silica nanohelices and helicenes reveals different orientations of helicenes in helical supramolecular assemblies formed on silica surfaces, and the chiroptical signals were induced by helicene adsorption and helical organization.

title: NiCr-LDH/V 4 C 3 MXene nanocomposites as an efficient electrocatalyst for urea oxidation
abstract: NiCr-LDH/MXene nanocomposites are developed as electrocatalysts to improve the performance of direct urea fuel cells (DUFCs), offering a sustainable solution by converting urea-rich waste into clean energy.

title: ∼5-Fold enhancement in the thermoelectric figure of merit of sustainable 3D-CuNi interconnected nanonetworks due to ultralow lattice thermal conductivity
abstract: The figure of merit ( zT ) exhibited an impressive increase of more than four times (4.8) for free-standing 3D-CuNi nanonetworks (NNs) when compared to bulk.

title: Machine-learning-empowered FDTD/FEM simulations for predictive solar energy absorption in plasmonic metamaterial nanocavity arrays
abstract: FDTD/FEM simulations, strengthened by machine learning, achieve 95.44% optical absorption efficiency in TiO 2 /TiN plasmonic nanocavities for solar energy harvesting.

title: Nickel-doped δ-MnO 2 abundant in oxygen vacancies as a cathode material for aqueous Zn-ion batteries with superior performance
abstract: Ni doping modified the crystallinity of δ-MnO 2 and introduced extra oxygen vacancies, enhancing conductivity, specific surface area, and total pore volume. Thus endowing the cathode with a high specific capacity of 401.6 mA h g −1 at 0.1 A g −1 .

title: Ultrafast electron transfer at the ZnIn 2 S 4 /MoS 2 S-scheme interface for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: An S-scheme ZnIn 2 S 4 /MoS 2 heterostructure is designed. Transient absorption spectroscopy reveals the interfacial electron transfer from ZnIn 2 S 4 to MoS 2 , which facilitates the photocatalytic hydrogen production rate for the heterostructure.

title: 3D-printed kirigami-inspired asymmetric dressings: custom elasticity and self-pumping for enhanced wound healing
abstract: A customizable self-pumping dressing via a 3D-printed kirigami and electrospun hydrophobic nanofibers was developed, enabling unidirectional fluid transport, antibacterial activity, and tailored elasticity (26–244%) for dynamic wound management.

title: Ultralight flexible 3D nickel micromesh decorated with NiCoP for high stability alkaline zinc batteries
abstract: The stability of the positive and negative electrodes in alkaline zinc-ion batteries is improved by the incorporation of highly oriented metal micromesh electrode structures and interfacial modifications.

title: Interfacial polarization-induced tribological behavior in MoS 2 /β-Te and G/β-Te heterostructures
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) heterostructures have opened up new avenues for controlling nanoscale friction; however, the relationship between their interfacial characteristics and frictional behavior remains to be thoroughly explored.

title: Graphene quantum dots induced performance enhancement in memristors
abstract: This review summarizes the recent advances of GQD-based memristors mainly in terms of GQD features, memristive mechanisms, and application fields, thereby providing insights for the development and design of GQD-based memristors.

title: Interface engineering of 2D dielectric nanosheets for boosting energy storage performance of polyvinylidene fluoride-based nanocomposites with high charge–discharge efficiency
abstract: The discharged energy density of PVDF film was enhanced through the synergistic effect of 2D interfacial barrier layer and a cross-linking layer.

title: Fabrication and assessment of n -eicosane/poly(stearylmethacrylate- co -butylacrylate) nanocapsules: impact of long alkyl polymer chains on thermal performance and stability
abstract: Nanocapsules were synthesized via mini-emulsion polymerization of poly(stearylmethacrylate- co -butylacrylate) around the n -eicosane core, showing high E ES (93.6%), E R (87.2%), and excellent C es (93.1%).

title: Unlocking enhanced properties: surface-engineered carbon fibers via microwave-grown ZnO nanoflakes for advanced thermoplastic composites
abstract: Controlled microwave-assisted growth of ZnO nanoflakes on carbon fibers enhances the mechanical, Joule heating, and EMI shielding performance of carbon fiber-reinforced polyetherimide laminates.

title: Tuning the nanoscale tribological characteristics of thermally evaporated transparent polyaniline–graphene nanocomposite thin films
abstract: Tuning the frictional properties of transparent polyaniline (PANI)-graphene nanocomposite thin film by varying the amount of graphene in the PANI matrix.

title: Mechanistic insights and rational catalyst design in NO x electroreduction
abstract: This review integrates theoretical and computational advances to identify active sites and key intermediates in NO x electroreduction, offering mechanistic insights and guiding principles for selective ammonia synthesis.

title: Cactus-inspired dual-sided micro-cilia arrays with swelling-induced CNT networks for high-performance wearable pressure sensing
abstract: Cactus-inspired dual-sided micro-cilia arrays with swelling-induced CNT networks exhibit ultrahigh sensitivity and linearity across a broad pressure range, enabling robust wearable sensing and human–machine interfacing.

title: Intense, self-induced sustainable microwave plasma using carbon nanotubes made from CO 2
abstract: An intense new energy source is triggered by carbon captured carbon nanotubes.

title: Gel polymer electrolytes for room-temperature sodium sulfur batteries
abstract: Functional PVDF-HFP-based gel polymer electrolyte with high ionic conductivity and sodium ion transference number for steady cycling performance of room temperature sodium-sulfur energy storage system.

title: A close-space sublimation approach to tungsten oxide and sulfide nanostructure formation
abstract: A highly resource-efficient Close-Space Sublimation (CSS) approach is presented, along with versatile one-step and two-step process designs, for the controlled synthesis of a wide range of tungsten (sub)oxide and tungsten disulfide nanostructures.

title: Direct thermal atomic layer deposition of high- κ dielectrics on monolayer MoS 2 : nucleation and growth
abstract: Investigation of the nucleation and growth of Al 2 O 3 and HfO 2 films grown directly on chemical vapour-synthesised monolayer MoS 2 via atomic layer deposition.

title: Dislocated output coupling within a selected band in an OLED based on an in-plane DBR microcavity array
abstract: Periodic array of DBR microcavities is introduced into an OLED. Combined Bragg- and grating-diffraction produce narrow-band electroluminescence in a separated location from the direct output, where optical gain is observed with band-narrowing effect.

title: Intrinsic setting of the exciton state in MoS 2 monolayers via tailoring the Moiré correlation with a sapphire substrate
abstract: This study aims to elucidate the specific Moiré correlation and associated exciton properties within MoS 2 monolayers grown randomly oriented on a c-cut single-crystalline sapphire (Al 2 O 3 ) substrate, which facilitates a distinct Moiré correlation.

title: Atomic-scale investigation of the role of supports in nanocrystal growth
abstract: This work conducted an in situ TEM study on how the metal–support interactions can direct the anisotropic growth of metals, resulting in tailored surface structures and electronic properties that enhance the electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Micromagnetic structure of oxidized magnetite nanoparticles: sharp structural versus diffuse magnetic interface
abstract: The oxidation of magnetite to maghemite is a naturally occurring process that leads to the degradation of the magnetic properties of magnetite nanoparticles.

title: Face-controlled chirality induction in octahedral thiacalixarene-based porous coordination cages
abstract: Two pairs of enantiomers were obtained via self-assembly of sulfonylcalix[4]arene tetranuclear M( ii ) clusters (M = Co or Ni) with enantiomerically enriched linkers based on tris(dipyrrinato)cobalt( iii ) complexes, leading to nanosized porous cages.

title: Probing the interactions in graphene oxide/MoS 2 and reduced graphene oxide/MoS 2 nanoarchitectures using multimodal scanning probe microscopy
authors: Camilla K. B. Q. M. de Oliveira
abstract: A set of SPM techniques helped in identifying different interactions between 2D/2D nanoarchitected thin films (GO/MoS 2 and rGO/MoS 2 ). Notably, the incorporation of MoS 2 led to several alterations in the properties of neat GO or rGO films.

title: Stacking engineering in two-dimensional multiferroic CuInP 2 S 6 /CrI 3 heterostructures
abstract: Stacking engineering offers a powerful technique to achieve the desired properties of two-dimensional (2D) van der Waals materials via interlayer coupling, thereby enabling multifunctional applications.

title: Engineering plasmonic charge kinetics and broadband photoelectrochemical spectral responses using a multi-resonant Au–TiO 2 plasmonic particle grating-based optical resonator
abstract: The synergy of plasmonic and photonic resonating effects for broadband spectral photoelectrochemical reactions and controlled plasmonic charge dynamics have been investigated.

title: Effects of structural parameters on the desalination performance of a multilayer stacked graphene oxide membrane: insights from molecular dynamics simulation
abstract: With the rapid development of nanotechnology, functionalized graphene membranes have shown great advantages, attracting increasing attention in recent years.

title: Self-powered triboelectric sensor using GaN nanowires and stress concentration structure
abstract: A self-powered triboelectric sensor with GaN nanowires using a nanoridge structure inspired by the structure of the human epidermis and dermis.

title: A high-quality and -throughput colloidal lithography by mechanical assembly and ice-based transfer
abstract: We present a new defect-free and scalable colloidal lithography using particle rubbing and ice-mediated transfer. This low-cost process allows precise nanostructuring for numerous applications, including optics and immunotherapy.

title: In-plane anisotropy-driven directional charge transport in van der Waals p–n heterojunctions
abstract: Anisotropic band dispersion in GeS governs directional transport in p-GeS/n-MoS 2 heterodiodes, enabling orientation-dependent rectification and polarization-sensitive optoelectronics.

title: A one-dimensional edge state induced by strain in a copper nitride monolayer on Cu(001)
abstract: A lattice strain-induced one-dimensional electronic state is found at the step edge of a Cu 2 N monolayer on Cu(001) by observing standing waves confined on rectangular terraces using scanning tunneling microscopy.

title: Voltage-driven flexible skyrmioniums for high-speed transport and reversible logic in discrete electrode nanowires
abstract: Voltage-controlled skyrmionium motion via discrete electrodes reaches 101 m s −1 without Joule heating. A theoretical framework and kinetic analysis are presented, with logic gates (Boolean, INV/COPY, Fredkin) designed, showing reliable dynamics.

title: Robust large-area molecular junctions of self-assembled monolayers of a model helical paddlewheel complex
abstract: We report the preparation of robust molecular junctions embedding helical paddlewheel complexes presenting electrically robust behavior.

title: Enhanced spin lifetime in colloidal quantum dots by growth from singly Mn-doped molecular cluster seeds
abstract: Seed clusters enable the growth of colloidal quantum dots singly doped with Mn.

title: Novel microscopy based on simultaneous utilization of third harmonic generation and photoluminescence microscopy for thin film analysis and molecular detection
abstract: We present a high-speed, non-destructive method combining THG and PL microscopy for rapid 3D profiling and compositional analysis of perovskite films and structured glass, achieving micrometer resolution over millimeter-scale areas.

title: Dual field magnetic separation for improved size fractionation of magnetic nanoparticles
abstract: A dual-field magnetic separation method that significantly enhances size fractionation of magnetic nanoparticles, compared to conventional gradient-based methods.

title: Shape effects on the 2D self-assembly of lithographically fabricated nanoparticles
abstract: Lithographically-defined polygonal Au nanoplates assemble at liquid-air interface. Shape governs 2D order: hexagonal>pentagonal>square, correlated with coordination/tiling. Binary mixtures with compatible six-fold coordination show enhanced ordering.

title: Magnetic nanoparticle-based hydrogels as reliable platforms to investigate magnetic interactions
abstract: Systematic methodology to control dipolar interactions within magnetic nanoparticle-based hydrogels through an amphiphilic polymer coating with variable alkyl chain lengths, as robust platforms to investigate magnetic interactions.

title: First-principles study of anisotropic planar 2D BC 2 N for sub-5 nm high-performance p-type transistors
abstract: Two-dimensional BC 2 N with anisotropic electronic properties shows great potential as p-type channel for next generation transistors.

title: Correction: Distinct autophagy-inducing abilities of similar-sized nanoparticles in cell culture and live C. elegans
abstract: Correction for ‘Distinct autophagy-inducing abilities of similar-sized nanoparticles in cell culture and live C. elegans ’ by Qin Wang et al. , Nanoscale , 2018, 10 , 23059–23069, https://doi.org/10.1039/C8NR05851B.

title: Correction: Carbon dots with tunable dual emissions: from the mechanism to the specific imaging of endoplasmic reticulum polarity
abstract: Correction for ‘Carbon dots with tunable dual emissions: from the mechanism to the specific imaging of endoplasmic reticulum polarity’ by E. Shuang et al. , Nanoscale , 2020, 12 , 6852–6860, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9NR10982J.

title: Single -crystal sodium nickel phosphate nanoparticles as supercapacitor cathodes with ultra-high capacitance and rate-performance
abstract: Single-crystalline maricite phase NaNiPO 4 rod-like nanoparticles with high phase purity have been prepared for the first time. The obtained NaNiPO 4 @C composite cathode displays a record discharge specific capacitance and excellent rate capacitance.

title: Upconversion nanohybrids for NIR-induced photorepair of DNA etheno adducts
abstract: Near-infrared (NIR) light triggers the repair of mutagenic etheno-DNA adducts back to their original nucleosides using upconversion nanohybrid-based nanozymes. DNA helix generated with AI.

title: Enhanced dielectric performance of AFRP via bidirectional modification: a fluorinated SiO 2 honeycomb-like architecture on fiber surfaces and Al 2 O 3 nanoparticle doping in a resin matrix
abstract: Through the bidirectional structural design of AFRP employing FSiO 2 and Al 2 O 3 , the optimized AFRP exhibited 64.45% enhancement in breakdown field strength, 123.02% improvement in interlaminar shear strength, and 129.13% increase in tensile strength.

title: Electrochemical energy storage enhanced by intermediate layer stacking of heteroatom-enriched covalent organic polymers in exfoliated graphene
abstract: Rational design and successful implementation of a heteroatom-enriched covalent organic polymer combined with exfoliated graphene has been applied towards the development of supercapacitors.

title: Sea urchin-like ZnO nanoflowers decorated on 3D porous graphene for efficient photocatalytic degradation
abstract: ZnO nanostructures with tunable morphologies were hydrothermally synthesized on a laser-induced 3D porous graphene framework (3DGF). The resulting composites demonstrated outstanding photocatalytic degradation efficiency under UV LED irradiation.

title: Polymeric nanoparticles for efficient nose-to-brain delivery
abstract: Brain disorders affect over one billion people globally, presenting significant challenges for effective treatment due to the limited drug bioavailability in the brain. The graphical abstract was created with BioRender.

title: A rapid route to perovskites: barium titanate nanoparticles via microwave-assisted solvothermal synthesis
abstract: Barium titanate (BaTiO 3 ) is a perovskite material with remarkable dielectric, ferroelectric, and piezoelectric properties, making it valuable in biomedical and functional devices.

title: Altermagnetism in two-dimensional Ca 2 RuO 4 perovskite
abstract: We propose a novel two-dimensional altermagnetic material, Ca 2 RuO 4 . DFT calculations reveal an enhanced altermagnetism via symmetry breaking and dimensionality reduction, offering a new platform for spintronics and topological phenomena.

title: Single photon generation from quantum dots: recent advances, challenges and future directions
abstract: Single photon sources (SPS) enable precise quantum communication, secure encryption and quantum computing. Recent research highlights quantum dots as ideal candidates for SPS due to their tunable emission, high stability, brightness and scalability.

title: Advances in microneedle technology for skin wound healing: integrating nanomaterials and intelligent drug delivery systems
abstract: Schematic overview of nanomaterial-integrated microneedle patches designed to modulate the wound microenvironment and promote efficient, responsive healing.

title: Nanoparticle-based biosensors for virus detection in food systems: from farm to fork
abstract: This review assesses nanobiosensors for detecting viral threats. It covers technologies for crop, livestock, and foodborne viruses to enhance the resilience and safety of the global food system. Graphical abstract image via Canva.

title: Thermodynamics and kinetics of early stages of carbon dot formation: a case of citric acid and ethylenediamine reaction
abstract: Owing to their extraordinary photophysical properties, carbon dots (CDs) have found applications across various fields, including bioimaging, sensing, and environmental research.

title: Exploring interfacial magnetism in all-spinel Fe 3 O 4 /MgCr 2 O 4 /Fe 3 O 4 epitaxial heterostructures
abstract: This study highlights the structural, chemical, and magnetic properties of Fe 3 O 4 /MgCr 2 O 4 /Fe 3 O 4 spinel oxide trilayers and the conditions for preserving the attributes of Fe 3 O 4 thin films for potential device applications.

title: Versatile methodology for the synthesis of stable magnetic SERS-encoded clusters for sensing applications
abstract: A versatile method for the synthesis of stable SERS-encoded clusters of silver nanoparticles on magnetic microparticles within a titania-coated shell, tunable for functionalization, and identifiable via automated SOM-based analysis.

title: Scalable and bright: unlocking functional silicon quantum dots with near-unity internal quantum yield through universal plasma-driven engineering
abstract: We present a plasma-based synthesis–modification method for silicon quantum dots featuring size control, simplicity, and good optical performance (near-unity internal quantum yield, a photoluminescence quantum yield of up to 20%).

title: Quantum and critical Casimir effects: bridging fluctuation physics and nanotechnology
abstract: This review sheds light on the primary representation of fluctuation-induced forces, the Casimir effect, both in its classical (critical) and quantum expressions, highighting their role on the development of nanoscale devices.

title: Amplification-free detection of Mycobacterium tuberculosis using CRISPR-Cas12a and graphene field-effect transistors
abstract: In this work, we present a graphene field-effect transistor integrated with the CRISPR/Cas12a system for the ultrasensitive detection of Mycobacterium tuberculosis, achieving a detection limit of 2.42 × 10 −18 M within 5 minutes.

title: An all-vanadium-based lithium-ion full battery with hierarchical yolk–shell structure electrodes
abstract: An all-vanadium-based lithium-ion full battery is assembled with hierarchical micro–nano yolk–shell structures V 2 O 5 and V 2 O 3 as electrodes, which are obtained through a facile solvothermal method with heat treatment under different atmospheres.

title: Electric field-induced breaking behavior of silver nanowire-based transparent and conductive films and their protection
abstract: Cracks are seen on AgNW-based TCFs during electric-stress processes, which correlate closely with the distribution of the electric field.

title: Abnormal homodirectional responses of magneto-phosphorescence and magneto-fluorescence in organic phosphorescent crystals under third-component doping
abstract: Tunable phosphorescence in organic ternary crystals is triggered by third-component doping, where abnormal homodirectional responses of magneto-phosphorescence and magneto-fluorescence are observed.

title: Tuning the optical properties of gold nanoparticles via photoactive liquid crystalline azo ligands
abstract: Gold nanoparticles capped with photoactive azo ligands exhibit tunable optical properties through SPR modulation. The ligands, synthesized without reducing agents, induce a chiral lamellar superstructure in the nanoparticles.

title: Introduction to the Nanoscale and Nanoscale Advances joint themed collection: Synthesis, physical properties and applications of advanced nanocrystalline materials
abstract: An editorial introducing the themed collection on advances in nanocrystalline materials, published in Nanoscale and Nanoscale Advances .

title: Microscopic morphology modulation and microwave absorption properties of nano-ZnO
abstract: Electromagnetic waves can affect the human body and equipment, and the development of unique nanostructured materials with excellent reflection loss (RL), thin thickness, wide frequency band and light weight highly valued.

title: Graphene oxide functionalized with a conjugated polyelectrolyte for a nonvolatile digital-type memristor and encryption applications
abstract: A novel conjugated polyelectrolyte, PFTPE-NMI + Br − , is non-covalently bonded to the GO surface to give a PFTPE-NMI + Br − :GO composite material. The as-fabricated electronic device, with a configuration of Al/PFTPE-NMI + Br − :GO/ITO, demonstrates a non-volatile ternary rewritable memory effect.

title: Enhanced magnetic and optical properties of Y 3 Fe 5 O 12 (YIG) films with Au nanoinclusions
abstract: Y 3 Fe 5 O 12 (YIG)–Au nanocomposites showed improved magnetic damping over pure YIG films while also introducing new hyperbolic optical properties.

title: Correction: Optical response of magnetically actuated biocompatible membranes
abstract: Correction for ‘Optical response of magnetically actuated biocompatible membranes’ by H. Joisten et al. , Nanoscale , 2019, 11 , 10667–10683, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9NR00585D.

title: Heterostructure nanodisks for multicolor polariton lasers with controllable whispering-gallery modes
abstract: Tunable multicolor polariton nanolasers are achieved in disk-shaped axial heterostructures by the optical confinement effect and active waveguiding of whispering-gallery modes.

title: Field-effect transistor based sensors for heavy metal ion detection in water systems
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in FET-based sensors for detecting HMIs in water, focusing on sensing mechanisms, channel materials, and functionalization strategies. It also highlights the development of integrated sensor arrays aided by AI.

title: Constructing a nickel complex/crystalline carbon nitride hybrid with a built-in electric field for boosting CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: A well-designed CCN/Ni hybrid was constructed with a built-in electric field, accelerating photogenerated charge separation and migration as well as exhibiting improved CO 2 photoreduction performance.

title: Modulating peptide co-assembly via macromolecular crowding: Recipes for co-assembled structures
abstract: Investigation of peptide co-assembly under crowded conditions using coarse-grained discontinuous molecular dynamics simulations. Peptides form fibril structures in the presence of hydrophobic crowders.

title: The interaction of glycogen nanoparticles with human blood
abstract: We investigate the interactions of glycogen nanoparticles (emerging biomaterials for theranostic applications) from different sources with human blood, finding that all are hemocompatible nanomaterials. Image via Blender Foundation (www.blender.org).

title: Steering electron flow by constructing an integrated structure in a metal–organic framework (MOF) via iminopyridine units for efficient CO 2 photoreduction to C 2 H 4 and C 2 H 6
abstract: Constructing integrated photocatalysts by immobilizing photosensitizers and active metal sites in metal–organic frameworks steers electron flow to accelerate the selective CO 2 photoreduction to C 2 H 4 and C 2 H 6 .

title: Copper nanoclusters with aggregation-induced emission: an effective photodynamic antibacterial agent for treating bacteria-infected wounds
abstract: Highly luminescent Cu NCs with AIE as photodynamic antimicrobial agents were synthesized and they exhibited exceptional photodynamic bacterial inactivation in vitro , alongside effective treatment of bacteria-infected wounds in vivo .

title: In search of the smoothest nanoparticle surface: diffusion and mobility on Ag clusters
abstract: By computer simulations, we show that the shape of a nanoparticle influences adatom mobility on its surface. The sharpest and most rounded geometries enable the fastest diffusion, revealing a non-intuitive link between form and atomic mobility.

title: Spin effects in metal halide perovskite semiconductors
abstract: This review explores the spin effects in both achiral and chiral metal halide perovskites and their opto-spintronic applications.

title: Thermally induced correlation effects studied by Raman spectroscopy in PtSe 2 /Al 2 O 3 systems
abstract: Thermal-dependent Raman spectral data were analysed to discuss dominant vibrational mode correlations in thin-layered (1L–10L) PtSe 2 -based systems.

title: Self-supporting poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) and Fe 3 C Co-decorated electrospun carbon nanofibers as Li 2 S supporters for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: The PEDOT and Fe 3 C co-decorated carbon nanofibers provide a fast electron transfer pathway and high catalytic activity for sulfur reduction reactions, which can significantly improve the cyclic stability of lithium sulfur batteries.

title: Unraveling packing-dependent surface potential contrast in a single-walled carbon nanotube bundle network
abstract: Kelvin probe force microscopy reveals that surface potential variations in SWCNT bundles are dominated by bundle thickness and configuration, offering new insights for optimizing nanoelectronic and sensing devices.

title: An all-solid-state Z-scheme mechanism in the enhanced photocatalytic performance of NiO–Au–ZnO triple-layer thin films
abstract: This study demonstrates the fabrication of a triple-layer NiO–Au–ZnO heterostructure with significantly enhanced photocatalytic activity.

title: Tailoring the electronic configurations of YPc 2 on Cu(111): decoupling strategies for molecular spin qubit platforms
abstract: YPc 2 -based molecular heterostructures with diamagnetic ZnPc spacers prevent spin quenching even in close proximity to Cu(111), enabling the development of individually addressable architectures for molecular spin qubits.

title: Rayleigh streaming phenomena at the physical origin of cellulose nanocrystals orientations during combined ultrasound and ultrafiltration processes
abstract: Rayleigh acoustic streaming, a phenomenon resulting from the interaction of ultrasound (US) with a fluid, was revealed for the first time during simultaneous frontal filtration and US processes on a cellulose nanocrystal (CNC) suspension.

title: Controllable synthesis and biomedical applications of bismuth-based nanospheres: enhanced photothermal therapy and CT imaging efficiency
abstract: We report the controllable synthesis of monodisperse and uniform bismuth-based (Bi 2 S 3 , Bi, and Bi 2 O 3 ) nanoparticles, and evaluate their potential for CT imaging and photothermal therapy applications.

title: Hydrothermal microwave synthesis of water soluble NIR-II emitting Ag 2 S quantum dots
abstract: Microwave-assisted synthesis using l -glutathione as the sulphur source and ligand yields Ag 2 S nanocrystals in a size range of 4–17 nm. Their evolution from the amorphous to the crystalline state leads to tuneable emission from 930 to 1220 nm.

title: PEGylated lipid screening, composition optimization, and structure–activity relationship determination for lipid nanoparticle-mediated mRNA delivery
abstract: Screening PEG-lipids and optimizing lipid nanoparticle (LNP) composition for intramuscular mRNA delivery. Advanced characterization of LNPs elucidates the structure–activity relationship and identifies key LNP attributes for in vivo transfection.

title: Nitrogen-rich nanoporous iron phosphonate as an acid–base bifunctional catalyst for efficient and selective CO 2 conversion without co-catalyst and solvent
abstract: Development of efficient co-catalyst- and solvent-free CO 2 cycloaddition using bifunctional iron-based metal phosphonates with tunable acidic–basic sites.

title: Nanomaterials for spin-based quantum information
abstract: 0D, 1D, and 2D nanomaterials for quantum information application, showcasing their dimensional advantages in coherence, scalability, and efficient qubit manipulation.

title: Mixed transition metal phosphides: recent progress and frontiers in secondary batteries and supercapacitors
abstract: Metal phosphides, which have been extensively explored for energy storage applications such as batteries and supercapacitors, possess unique physicochemical, morphological, and electrochemical properties.

title: Design strategies and performance enhancements of PVDF-based flexible electrolytes for high-performance all-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: Lithium metal is considered one of the most promising anode materials for lithium batteries due to its high theoretical specific capacity (3860 mA h g −1 ) and low redox potential (−3.04 V).

title: A novel water-soluble polymer nanocomposite containing ultra-small Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles with strong antibacterial and antibiofilm activity
authors: 'Galina Prozorova', 'Artem Emel'yanov', 'Anastasiya Ivanova', 'Tatyana Semenova', 'Tat'yana Fadeeva', 'Anna Nevezhina', 'Svetlana Korzhova', 'Alexander Pozdnyakov'
abstract: A novel water-soluble polymer nanocomposite with ultra-small iron oxide nanoparticles intercalated into a biocompatible matrix of 1-vinyl-1,2,4-triazole and N -vinylpyrrolidone copolymer exhibits pronounced antibacterial and antibiofilm activity.

title: Grazing-incidence X-ray diffraction elucidates structural correlations in fluid monolayers of lipids and surfactants
abstract: GIXD measurements on fluid lipid monolayers at the air/water interface reveal regions of spatiotemporally localized, structurally correlated hydrocarbon chain ordering. The abundance of these regions correlates to the lateral packing density.

title: Patternable chiral Au nanocrystal-doped composite films for information encryption: the role of optical rotation
abstract: Au nanocrystals of opposite chirality can rotate linearly polarized light in opposite directions, and are utilized for information encryption.

title: Tailoring MXenes for energy storage: insights into element doping
abstract: Element doping can endow MXenes with enhanced electrochemical properties, catalytic performance, and structural characteristics. This review focuses on the doping mechanism, doping methods and applications of MXenes in the field of energy storage.

title: Influence of substituted aromatics on the formation and stability of β-sheet-based peptide hydrogels
abstract: Peptide hydrogels with altered β-sheet assembly, fibril morphology and gel properties were created upon substitution of the aromatic side chains. Characterization of the gels revealed new correlations between fibril morphology and gel properties.

title: Metal oxide plating for maximizing the performance of ruthenium( iv ) oxide-catalyzed electrochemical oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: TiO 2 nanowire array anode coated by a uniform single-crystalline RuO 2 film with a thickness of ∼2.5 nm produced O 2 from water with an efficiency of ∼100% and a mass specific activity of 341 A g cat −1 at 1.50 V ( vs. RHE).

title: Preparation of sulfur-doped porous carbon from polyphenylene sulfide waste for photothermal conversion materials to achieve solar-driven water evaporation
abstract: This study provides a new use for waste polyphenylene sulphide and the KCP-wood evaporator has good photothermal evaporation performance and cyclic durability.

title: Superconducting diode effect in the Weyl semimetal T d -MoTe 2 that has a surface modulated by Al nanoparticles
abstract: We studied the superconducting diode effect in the type-II Weyl semimetal T d -MoTe 2 that is covered with Al nanoparticles.

title: Universal scaling laws on the rotational energy landscape for twisted van der Waals bilayers
abstract: We reveal the universal scaling laws on the interface rotational energy landscape as a function of rotational angle, geometric size, and mismatch strain for twisted van der Waals bilayers.

title: Innovative non-toxic exfoliation of bulk g-C 3 N 4 into ultra-thin 1D/2D nanosheets and nanotubes for accelerated sunlight-driven photocatalysis
abstract: A green, one-step in situ H 2 O 2 -mediated solvothermal method enables simultaneous exfoliation and heteroatom doping in g-C 3 N 4 , yielding CN-48 with enhanced photocatalytic activity via quantum confinement.

title: Charge transfer-mediated fluorescence kinetics of yellow AIE carbon-dots for solid-state optoelectronic applications
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) are promising luminescent materials but often suffer from aggregation-caused quenching (ACQ) in the solid-state.

title: High-performance hybrid supercapacitors enabled by CoTe@CoFeTe double-shelled nanocubes
abstract: CoTe@CoFeTe double-shelled nanocubes are fabricated for hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Bioconjugates of photon-upconversion nanoparticles with antibodies for the detection of prostate-specific antigen and p53 in heterogeneous and homogeneous immunoassays
abstract: Strategies for the surface modification of upconversion nanoparticles were explored, and the prepared conjugates were employed as labels in immunoassays for cancer biomarker detection.

title: A review on n-type chemical doping of graphene films: preparation, characterization and applications
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of n-type chemical doping strategies for graphene films, emphasizing doping mechanisms, structure–property relationships, and emerging device applications.

title: Polymeric nanoparticle synthesis for biomedical applications: advancing from wet chemistry methods to dry plasma technologies
abstract: This review critically compares wet chemical and plasma polymerization methods for polymeric nanoparticle synthesis, highlighting plasma polymerization as a dry, scalable platform with insights into clinical translation and commercialization.

title: A metallosupramolecular-based ring-in-ring complex showing reversible host–guest dynamics and switchable electrochemical properties
abstract: We report a pyrene-NDI heterocyclophane that forms a high-affinity complex in an iridium metallobox, altering its redox behavior. Guest uptake/release is reversible and tunable via Cl − or Ag + , as shown by variable temp 1 H NMR and electrochemistry.

title: Graphene quantum dots covalently functionalized with zinc porphyrin for digital-analog dual-mode memristors
abstract: We report a digital–analog dual-mode memristor based on a donor–acceptor structured nanomaterial, ZnTPP-g-GQDs, which is synthesized through the covalent functionalization of graphene quantum dots with zinc porphyrin.

title: van der Waals devices for surface-sensitive experiments
abstract: We present a fabrication process that combines contact patterning with a stencil and gold-assisted exfoliation of van der Waals materials, producing pristine surfaces and micron-scale device geometries on thin flakes down to the monolayer limit.

title: A DMSO-modified porous organogel with breathability and degradability for wearable electronics
abstract: An agar organogel with porous microstructures exhibits excellent mechanical properties, excellent moisture permeability and degradation which can be used as a breathable and degradable substrate for wearable electronics.

title: Nanostructure engineering for ferroelectric photovoltaics
abstract: Nanostructure design has been demonstrated to be an effective strategy for enhancing bulk photovoltaic performance.

title: Hot carrier harvesting at the interface of CsPbI 3 nanocrystals
abstract: Hot Carrier harvesting at the CsPbI 3 NC–NMA interface occurs on a sub-picosecond timescale. FCS shows NMA forms a weak complex with NCs, but charge transfer happens much faster, before dissociation.

title: Catalytic synergism in heterostructural Ta-doped Mo–Ni–S nanospheres: an efficient bifunctional catalyst for water splitting
abstract: This work presents a multifunctional catalystwhich exhibits outstanding electrocatalytic performance due to the spontaneous electron transfer at the interface between the two phases and a strong link between components enhanced by Ta doping.

title: Green synthesis of zinc oxide nanoparticles for copper-ion detection and energy storage in aqueous media: an electrochemical and DFT study
abstract: Green ZnO@graphite electrode enables simultaneous copper detection and energy storage in aqueous media.

title: In situ growth of layered 1T-MoS 2 onto carbon nanofibers as a strategy towards advanced hybrid materials for electrochemical energy storage and catalysis
abstract: We designed 1T-MoS 2 /CNFs with different thicknesses of MoS 2 layer, which affect conductivity, charge storage and transfer. Optimized 1T-MoS 2 /CNFs enhance supercapacitance and hydrogen evolution performance, offering a strategy for energy devices.

title: Manganese-doped nickel–iron layered double hydroxides for enhanced peroxidase, oxidase, and catalase activities
abstract: NiFeMn-20%-C demonstrated superior enzymatic activities compared to NiFeMn-20%, primarily due to differences in the doping sites of Mn. In NiFeMn-20%-C, Mn replaces both Ni and Fe sites, whereas in NiFeMn-20%, it substitutes only the Fe sites.

title: Recent advances in heterostructure-based organic field-effect transistor memory
abstract: We reviewed the applications of different heterostructures in OFETM devices and their use in the field of artificial intelligence.

title: Nanoscale chemical characterization of functionalized graphene by heterodyne AFM-IR and chemical force microscopy
abstract: The nanoscale heterogeneity of chemical functionalization on graphene surfaces is successfully revealed by a combination of heterodyne AFM-infrared microscopy (AFM-IR) and chemical force microscopy (CFM).

title: Overcoming the tumor microenvironment in pancreatic cancer via barrier-specific nanoparticle drug delivery
abstract: Major barriers in the pancreatic tumor microenvironment (TME), including metabolic stress, physical penetration limits, and immune suppression, and the nanoparticle-based drug delivery strategies designed to overcome these challenges.

title: Recent advances in MXene nanozyme-based optical and electrochemical biosensors for food safety analysis
abstract: Utilization of MXene nanozymes in electrochemical and optical biosensors for food safety.

title: Hot carrier dynamics in the BA 2 PbBr 4 /MoS 2 heterostructure
abstract: The hot phonon bottleneck in BA 2 PbBr 4 perovskite and MoS 2 heterostructure extends electron relaxation, offering insights into interfacial coupling and dynamics for efficient optoelectronics.

title: Coupling alloying and doping strategies to improve and stabilize Pd-based catalysts for acidic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The activity and stability of the acidic hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) in palladium (Pd) and Pd-based materials can be significantly enhanced through the synergistic effects of alloying and doping.

title: Deciphering the emission dynamics of colloidal PbS quantum dots: a spectroscopic exploration under diverse environmental and experimental conditions
abstract: This study employs a combination of spectroscopic techniques, including absorption, transient absorption, photoluminescence (PL) and time-resolved PL kinetics, to elucidate the dynamics of excited states of colloidal PbS quantum dots in toluene.

title: Fully atomistic molecular dynamics modeling of photoswitchable azo-PC lipid bilayers: structure, mechanical properties, and drug permeation
abstract: A phenol molecule is shown at its free energy minimum in a trans azo-PC lipid bilayer, where it interacts with the azobenzene groups that are incorporated into one of the two phosphatidylcholine lipid tails.

title: Recyclable luminescent metal–organic frameworks with self-formed diketone-like sites for cascade ratiometric detection
abstract: A novel luminescent metal–organic framework with abundant self-formed diketone-like sites can work as a recyclable ratiometric sensor for the cascade detection of Tb 3+ and cysteine (Cys) with high selectivity.

title: Ink formulation and electrolytes affect electrochemical oxygen reduction into H 2 O 2 : a kinetic study
abstract: A protocol is proposed for preparing carbon inks and evaluation conditions towards more efficient 2-electron oxygen reduction into H 2 O 2 , and a linear correlation between the electrolyte concentration and selectivity is revealed.

title: ZnO quantum dots as an electron-transport layer for highly efficient and stable organic solar cells
abstract: Colloidal ZnO quantum dots with a size of ca . 4.5 nm deliver a superior performance as an electron-transport layer in organic bulk-heterojunction solar cells as compared to commercially available ZnO inks with larger nanocrystal sizes.

title: Joule-heating synthesis of Pd/C catalysts with excellent electrocatalytic performance
abstract: A facile solid-phase synthesis strategy that couples ball milling with rapid Joule-heating to fabricate Pd/C catalysts for high-performance electrocatalysis.

title: Substitutional Mo doping in a Ta 3 N 5 photoanode: mitigating native defects through engineering and enhancing water-splitting performance
abstract: Mo doping in Ta 3 N 5 supresses the formation of Ta reduced species, improves optical properties and bulk/surface charge transfer, thereby, enhancing its PEC water splitting.

title: A flame-retardant local high-concentration electrolyte for high-voltage lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A non-flammable carbonate electrolyte (LiTFSI/FEC/TTE) forms LiF-rich SEI/CEI, enabling stable cycling of NCM811∥Gr batteries at 4.5 V.

title: Correction: Investigation of the phase transition to the Ruddlesden–Popper phase in La- or Nb-doped Sr 2 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ double perovskites and the impact of lanthanum or niobium doping
abstract: Correction for ‘Investigation of the phase transition to the Ruddlesden–Popper phase in La- or Nb-doped Sr 2 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ double perovskites and the impact of lanthanum or niobium doping’ by Agata Ducka et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 12371–12384, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5NR00596E.

title: Investigation on thyroglobulin and myoglobin using graphene-enhanced Raman spectroscopy as a tool
abstract: Illustration of an experimental setup for recording the Raman spectra of Tg and Mb proteins dispersed over an MGF-based substrate.

title: Sliding ferroelectricity in bilayer phosphorus analogue compounds: mechanisms and applications
abstract: This article explores the sliding ferroelectric mechanism in bilayer and multilayer phosphorus analogue compounds.

title: Ln 3+ -ion-mediated enhancement in UV/X-ray-induced optical emission from Mn 2+ -doped ZnSe nanocrystals
abstract: Enhancement of UV/X-ray-excited luminescence in ZnSe:Mn 2+ ,Ln 3+ QDs was revealed via XEOL, XAFS, and PL spectroscopy, attributed to charge transfer, e–h pair localization near Mn 2+ , and energy transfer from Ln 3+ 4f to Mn 2+ 4 T 1 .

title: Quantitative correlation between carbon nanotube tip morphology and field emission properties at cryogenic temperature
abstract: We present a thorough analysis of the field emission properties of three varieties of vertically aligned carbon nanotubes (VA-CNTs), characterized by different morphologies as a consequence of different post-growth plasma etching treatments.

title: Computational exploration of M-sites with chemical order and disorder in M′ 2 M′′B 2 and M′ 4 M′′B 3 compounds
abstract: High-throughput computational screening identifies novel stable ternary boron-based materials using detailed stability and bonding analyses.

title: From molecular to nanoplastic SERS detection: insights into the role of analytes in plasmonic substrate design
abstract: SERS substrates exhibiting excellent enhancement, as measured with a small molecular analyte, are not necessarily the most efficient ones when hundreds of nm sized analytes, such as nanoplastics, are to be detected.

title: Constructing protein-functionalized DNA origami nanodevices for biological applications
abstract: In this review, Zhao et al. have summarized the construction of protein-functionalized DNA origami nanodevices and their biological applications. The challenges and potential directions in cell biology and structural biology have also been discussed.

title: Polarisation-dependent Raman enhancement in hexagonal boron nitride membranes
abstract: This work presents a polarisation and position-dependent Raman enhancement employing flat hexagonal boron nitride membranes as part of a microcavity.

title: Design of multicore–shell structured Co 3 O 4 –NiO nanocomposites as high-performance cathodes for zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: A multicore–shell structured Co 3 O 4 –NiO nanocomposite with an optimized 1 : 0.2 ratio exhibits superior cathodic performance in zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors, achieving a specific capacitance of 355 F g −1 via synergistic enhancement effects.

title: Catalyst-derived hierarchy in 2D imine-based covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Dual role of scandium triflate as catalyst and growth modifier enables hierarchical growth of 2D imine-based covalent organic framework sorbents.

title: Holey graphyne anode for metal-ion batteries: suitability for potassium-ion batteries
abstract: This study provides a theoretical assessment of the performance of holey graphene as an anode for Li-, Na-, K-, Mg-, Ca-, Zn-, and Al-ion batteries in terms of capacity, volume change, and diffusivity.

title: Bimetallic PdPt nanoparticle-incorporated PEDOT:PSS/guar gum-blended membranes for enhanced CO 2 separation
abstract: Novel membranes have been prepared utilizing natural polymer guar gum (GG), conjugate polymer (poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene)-poly(styrenesulfonate)) PEDOT:PSS, and bimetallic PdPt nanoparticles for efficient CO 2 separation.

title: Nanowires in precision diagnostics: bridging preventive health care with nanoscale innovation
abstract: Schematic of nanowires, and their synthetic routes, properties, and applications in the sensing of clinically and environmentally important molecules. The graphical abstract artworks were obtained via Canva.

title: Modulating hot carrier relaxation and trapping dynamics in lead halide perovskite nanoplatelets by surface passivation
abstract: Hot carriers generated in two-layer (2L) CsPbBr 3 nanoplatelets (NPLs) can be directly trapped by surface defects before they relax to the band edge, which accounts for the absence of the hot-phonon bottleneck effect in 2L CsPbBr 3 NPLs.

title: Contactless manufacturing of TERS-active AFM tips by bipolar electrodeposition
abstract: Wireless electrochemistry is used for the parallel fabrication of gold-coated AFM tips with high reproducibility to enable nanoscale TERS imaging.

title: Progress in understanding triple ionic–electronic conduction in perovskite oxides for protonic ceramic fuel cell applications
abstract: Optimizing ORR in PCFC cathodes by balancing proton and oxygen–ion transport.

title: Co-reduction coupling of bicarbonate and nitrate toward efficient urea synthesis
abstract: The spatial charge region is rationally designed by constructing a Mott–Schottky heterostructure in the Cu-WN catalyst, achieving accurate activation of NO 3 − and HCO 3 − to accelerate the targeted electrochemical C–N coupling reaction.

title: Machine learning-guided fabrication of carbon dot–pepsin nano-conjugates for enhanced bioimaging, synergistic drug delivery, and visible light-induced photosensitization
abstract: AI-guided synthesis of CD–pepsin nanoparticles enables enhanced imaging, visible light induced ROS generation, and sustained drug delivery, offering a multifunctional platform for advanced chemo-photodynamic therapy and bioimaging.

title: Interface-engineered CsPbBr 3 –TiO 2 nanocomposites for efficient and selective photocatalytic conversion of HMF to DFF
abstract: In situ TiO 2 -coated CsPbBr 3 quantum dots act as robust photocatalysts, enabling solar-driven and selective oxidation of HMF to DFF with enhanced stability and efficiency.

title: Polymer additives in liquid electrolytes for advanced lithium batteries
abstract: This review focuses on the recent application progress of polymer additives in liquid electrolytes for lithium batteries.

title: Hot carrier transfer from CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals to Au 25 clusters: the pivotal role of ligand-controlled diffusion
abstract: CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals transfer hot carriers to Au 25 clusters efficiently, governed by ligand-controlled diffusion. Trioctylphosphine ligands double transfer rate vs. pristine, highlighting ligand optimization's key role in enhancing carrier extraction.

title: Size-dependent antiferromagnetism and direct observation of Néel axes in NiO nanoparticles
abstract: The ToC figure shows a magnetization curve at room temperature and Néel axis distribution in single 34 nm NiO NP.

title: DetectNano: deep learning detection in TEM images for high-throughput nanostructure characterization
abstract: DetectNano enables AI-powered detection and real-time size estimation of polymer nanostructures in TEM images, accelerating high-throughput analysis in soft matter research.

title: Microfluidic-assisted sol–gel preparation of monodisperse mesoporous silica microspheres with controlled size, surface morphology, porosity and stiffness
abstract: A facile microfluidic-assisted sol–gel method is proposed for the preparation of silica microspheres with precisely controlled properties such as size, surface morphology, porosity and stiffness.

title: Cloudberry-derived nanovesicles as stable oral drug delivery systems: gastrointestinal stability and age-related biodistribution in mice
abstract: Cloudberry nanovesicles (CNVs) remain stable under gastrointestinal conditions, efficiently cross intestinal barriers, and exhibit a favorable safety profile in both age groups, supporting their potential as oral drug delivery systems for all ages.

title: Recent advances in carbon-based quantum dots for sensing applications
abstract: Carbon quantum dots have attracted great attention due to their unique properties. This review outlines their synthesis and characterization, highlights their applications in sensing, and discusses current challenges as well as future perspectives.

title: Photoactivated defect engineering and nanostructure functionalization of MoS 2 via a photochemical Fenton process
abstract: This work reports the defect engineering and nanostructure functionalization of MoS 2 using a photochemical Fenton process to etch, create defects, and deposit functional nanoparticles.

title: The design of a novel Pd-nanoparticle-encapsulating covalent organic framework-based catalyst for solvent-free CO 2 conversion at ambient pressure
abstract: Novel Pd nanoparticle encapsulated covalent organic framework-based catalyst for solvent-free conversion of epoxides and CO 2 to cyclic carbonates at ambient pressure.

title: Symmetry breaking enhances the catalytic and electrocatalytic performance of core/shell tetrametallic porous nanoparticles
abstract: Spherical and symmetry-broken Au core particles are used to synthesize tetrametallic (Au@mPdPtIr) porous nanocatalysts. Symmetry breaking of the core significantly improves the catalytic activity in various catalytic and electrocatalytic reactions.

title: Stable antiparallel domains in 3D corrugated magnetic thin films
abstract: Corrugated Permalloy Films – Shape Anisotropy – Stable Antiparallel Magnetic Domains – Magnetooptical Enhanced Properties.

title: Mixed metal halide perovskite CsPb 1− x Sn x Br 3 quantum dots: insight into photophysics from photoblinking studies
abstract: Hot electrons from Sn populates Pb trap states in CsPb 1− x Sn x Br 3 QDs, promoting exciton radiative recombination as shown by the suppression of GRAY levels in Photoblinking.

title: Modulating contact resistance of organic electrochemical transistors via Cu x O interlayer
abstract: A Cu x O interlayer using a sol–gel process followed by thermal annealing is introduced to reduce the contact resistance of OECTs, enhancing the overall electrochemical transistor performance.

title: Revisiting protein protected gold and silver nanoclusters: Excited state dynamics and long-lived emission
abstract: Time-resolved luminescence spectroscopy unveiled the distinct excited state dynamics of protein-conjugated gold and silver nanoclusters.

title: Vanadium incorporation in ferrite nanoparticles serves as an electron buffer and anisotropy tuner in catalytic and hyperthermia applications
abstract: This study yields ferrite magnetic nanoparticles with a lower effective anisotropy constant, key for magnetic hyperthermia, and a vanadium-rich shell, important for catalysis.

title: DC field-biased multibit/analog artificial synapse featuring an additional degree of freedom for performance tuning
abstract: Tuning technical features and multibit operation of poly-TiO 2 memristor are achieved by DC-field biasing.

title: High entropy rare-earth Prussian blue analogues for boosting oxygen evolution catalysis
abstract: High-entropy PBAs containing Ni, Fe, La, Mo, and Co were prepared via a co-precipitation method. This specific elemental combination facilitates the adsorption and reaction of OH − ions, thereby reducing the overpotential of OER.

title: Revealing nanoscale slip within Taylor–Aris dispersion
abstract: Hydrodynamic slip at fluid–solid interfaces plays an important role in a range of transport phenomena, including Taylor–Aris dispersion.

title: Physicochemical characterization of a new porous 2D semiconductor carbon allotrope, C 16 : an investigation via density functional theory and machine learning-based molecular dynamics
abstract: This study comprehensively characterizes, with suggested applications, a novel two-dimensional carbon allotrope, C 16 , using density functional theory and machine learning-based molecular dynamics.

title: Cr 2 TiC 2 T x MXene as an adsorbent material in ultrasonic-assisted d-μ-solid phase extraction for trace detection of heavy metals
abstract: Cr 2 TiC 2 T x MXene was used as an adsorbent for the trace determination of heavy metals in food samples, with LOD values of 0.09 and 1.9 ng mL −1 , and dynamic ranges of 0.3–90 and 6–120 μg L −1 for cadmium and lead ions, respectively.

title: P(TT-TPA) featuring a conjugated extended structure: enabling high-performance flexible electrochromic-supercapacitors
abstract: Electrochromic-supercapacitors (EC-SCs) based on conducting polymers hold broad application prospects in fields such as smart electronic devices, wearable devices, and the Internet of Things.

title: “In Rust we Shine”: an all-in-one photo-electrocatalytic device for low-cost infrared-induced water splitting with a hematite–polymeric EVA film containing rare-earth up-conversion particles
abstract: Sustainable hydrogen production via photocatalytic water splitting with spectral conversion luminescent materials.

title: Electronic and surface engineering of Mn active sites by femtosecond lasers: enhancing catalytic performance for seawater electrolysis through Mn 4+ –OH − layers
abstract: Laser-induced modifications of La 0.51 Sr 0.49 MnO 3 (LSMO) perovskite electrocatalysts are explored for enhanced seawater oxidation under alkaline conditions.

title: Device area invariant conductance linearity in scalable silver nanostructure based neuromorphic devices with threshold activation following nociceptive behavior
abstract: Self-assembled Ag nanostructures exhibit area-invariant conductance linearity, integrate-and-fire neuronal dynamics, and nociceptive behaviour like allodynia and hyperalgesia, advancing scalable neuromorphic computing.

title: Humidity-stable submicron magnesium oxide particles for high-performance thermally conductive composites
abstract: A novel strategy for enhancing the humidity stability of submicron magnesium oxide particles for high performance thermally conductive composites is reported.

title: Nanoparticle shape is the game-changer for blood–brain barrier crossing and delivery through tunneling nanotubes among glioblastoma cells
authors: 'Giulia Sierri', 'Ines Saenz-de-Santa-Maria', 'Antonio Renda', 'Marcus Koch', 'Patrizia Sommi', 'Umberto Anselmi-Tamburini', 'Mario Mauri', 'Alessia D'Aloia', 'Michela Ceriani', 'Domenico Salerno', 'Francesco Mantegazza', 'Chiara Zurzolo', 'Francesca Re'
abstract: Discoidal nanoparticles showed highest blood-brain barrier permeability and transfer efficiency via tunneling nanotubes in glioblastoma cells, offering a promising strategy for nanoparticles delivery and intercellular distribution within tumors.

title: NIR light activates upconverting nanoparticles/Zn x Mn 1− x S core–shell nanoparticles for improved breast cancer treatment
abstract: TUC@ZMS targets cancer cells, depletes GSH, and integrates PDT, CDT, and H 2 S gas therapy to eliminate tumors and induce ICD, while H 2 S and Mn 2+ activate cGAS-STING, eliciting systemic anti-tumor immunity, curbing tumor growth and lung metastasis.

title: Comparative evaluation of polymer coated versus antibody modified SPIONs for tumor detection using magnetic particle imaging
abstract: This study reveals how surface modifications of SPIONs impact their properties, enhancing MPI's potential for tumor imaging.

title: Thermodynamics and kinetics in van der Waals epitaxial growth of Te
abstract: Chemical vapour deposition (CVD) in a tube furnace and molecular beam epitaxy (MBE) in a vacuum chamber represent the most effective methods for the production of low-dimensional nanomaterials.

title: Insights into the sequential oxidation mechanisms on the surface of carbon nanotubes by photoinduced force microscopy
abstract: Nanoscale chemical analysis using photoinduced force microscopy (PiFM) revealed a heterogeneous distribution of oxidized functional groups on CNT bundles.

title: Lattice dynamics of layered kagome lattice material Nb 3 Br 8 investigated via Raman spectroscopy and DFT
abstract: The phonon dynamics of breathing kagome material Nb 3 Br 8 are investigated via linearly polarized Raman spectroscopy and density-functional theory.

title: Copper–cobalt peroxide nanoparticles: a biomimetic cascade reaction for enhanced Fenton-like therapy at physiologically relevant pH
abstract: Traditional Fenton-like reactions used in chemodynamic therapy (CDT) for cancer treatment, face limitations due to mildly acidic tumor microenvironment and scarce H 2 O 2 . CCp offers a synergistic catalytic approach to address these challenges.

title: Feature mining for thermoelectric materials based on interpretable machine learning
abstract: We use interpretable machine learning methods to analyze the key molecular features in the characterized high-flux data and uses feature engineering methods to construct and optimize the machine learning model for the selected key features.

title: In situ synthesis of bimetallic chalcogenides with highly conductive carbon nanotubes for efficient symmetric hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: High energy density and long cycle stability have been achieved in the proposed energy storage device with the utilization of in situ synthesized bimetallic chalcogenides with highly conductive carbon nanotubes as symmetric electrodes.

title: Bifunctional interface engineering of fluorinated cosmetic cotton separators: synergistic Zn 2+ kinetics and hydrophobic shielding for ultra-stable Zn anodes
abstract: The PVDF–CP separator enables long-life AZIBs (2810 h Zn∥Zn, 99.0% CE) by combining Zn 2+ channels and a hydrophobic shield at the anode interface.

title: Smart polymer prodrugs via responsive prodrug-initiated ring-opening polymerization of lactide for improved drug delivery
abstract: Smart polymer prodrugs, created via responsive prodrug-initiated controlled polymerization of lactide, demonstrated extremely high drug loading, tuneable stimuli-triggered drug release, and significant tumor growth inhibition with minimal toxicity.

title: Modulation of polaritons in chemically doped graphene ribbon/α-MoO 3 heterostructures
abstract: A graphene/α-MoO 3 heterostructure enables precise 0-π phase shifts of hybrid plasmon-phonon polaritons via chemical doping of graphene. This work facilitates the development of ultra-compact optical modulators.

title: Magnetic nanoparticles as transducers for quick and direct virus detection in clinical samples
abstract: Innovative strategies for the rapid diagnosis of viral infections at primary medical assistance are actually of significant relevance. Images supplied by Servier Medical Art, licensed under CCBY4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).

title: Printing rare-earth-free (REF) magnetic inks: synthesis, formulation, and device applications
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in additive manufacturing of rare-earth-free (REF) magnetic materials, emphasizing innovations in material synthesis, ink formulation, printing techniques, and device applications.

title: Nitrogen and sulfur co-doped carbon quantum dot-decorated Ti 3 C 2 T x -MXenes as electrode materials for high-performance symmetric supercapacitors
abstract: This work demonstrates a smartly engineered 0D/2D NS-CQD/MXene hybrid that unfolds rich redox activity, improved ion transport and pseudocapacitance by obstructing MXene restacking- paving the way for next generation supercapacitor applications.

title: Understanding the construction of nano-structured LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 by a co-precipitation strategy and Mg 2+ doping towards high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Nano-structured LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4− x Mg x PO 4 /C microspheres are prepared by using NH 4 Mn 0.6 Fe 0.4− x Mg x PO 4 ·H 2 O obtained via co-precipitation as precursor for the first time. Mg 2+ doping improves the performance, kinetics and structural stability of the cathode.

title: Enhanced catalytic oxidative desulfurization performance via facile synthesis of a MoO 2 /BCN nanocomposite under solventless and mild reaction conditions
abstract: Efficient removal of refractory sulfides using a facilely constructed MoO 2 /BCN catalyst.

title: Multi-emissive graphene oxide quantum dots with remarkable pH-responsive long-wavelength emission
abstract: Different supramolecular interactions led to GOQD aggregates at different pH values, governing the emission characteristics of dilute aqueous GOQD solutions.

title: Self-cleaning, transparent, and anti-icing properties of polylactic acid films with stiffness-toughness balance via a stretching-nonsolvent-induced phase separation-liquid infusion process
abstract: A facile, green NIPS-liquid infusion strategy fabricates polylactic acid (PLA) films with self-cleaning, transparent, anti-icing, and balanced stiffness-toughness properties, showing great promise for sustainable packaging.

title: Self-assembled monolayer functionalized metal oxides: a path toward highly selective and low-power consuming gas sensors
abstract: The emerging functionalization strategy of self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) offers transformative potential for enhancing the performance of nanostructured metal oxides (MOXs)-based gas sensors.

title: Zn–Sn interface layer design strategy towards high-stability Zn powder anode
abstract: An electrodeposited Zn–Sn alloy interface layer suppresses dendrite formation and side reactions, enabling ultra-stable Zn powder anodes for aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Computational roadmap of stable Mo–S nanoclusters: atomic structures and magic compositions
abstract: Computational screening reveals 22 novel stable Mo–S nanoclusters and identifies the planar Mo 10 S 20 ring as a stable building block for extended nanostructures like nanotubes and thin films.

title: Colloidal gold-palladium-platinum alloy nanospheres with tunable compositions and defined numbers of atoms
abstract: In this study, we demonstrate that the combination of seed-mediated growth routes with pulsed laser irradiation produces high-quality colloidal AuPdPt alloy nanocrystals featuring spherical shapes, tunable compositions, and defined numbers of atoms.

title: Understanding structure–property relationships in coordination polymers: a comparative study of the copper( ii ) and zinc( ii ) coordination mechanism
abstract: In metal–organic coordination polymers, metal ions can adopt square-planar or tetrahedral geometries, affecting electronic properties. These configurations are studied via AFM, XPS, and DFT modelling to compare structural and electronic differences.

title: Sustainable catalysts in a short time: harnessing bacteria for swift palladium nanoparticle production
abstract: Adapting biological systems for nanoparticle synthesis opens an orthogonal Green direction in nanoscience by reducing the reliance on harsh chemicals and energy-intensive procedures.

title: Photocatalytic detoxification of a sulfur mustard simulant using donor-enhanced porphyrin-based covalent-organic frameworks
abstract: Porphyrin-based COF-OMe with a strong electron-donating group exhibited the highest photocatalytic activity among the three COFs for the detoxification of CEES in the methanol solvent.

title: In situ synthesis of a UIO-66-NH 2 @Ti 3 C 2 composite for advanced electrochemical detection of acetaminophen
abstract: This research involved synthesizing a novel UIO-66-NH 2 @Ti 3 C 2 composite through a one-step hydrothermal process, followed by calcination at 300 °C under argon and exploring its potential for the electrochemical detection of acetaminophen.

title: Dual-enhancements of stability and wettability in O3-Na 0.95 Ni 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathodes by converting surface residual alkali into ultrathin Na 2 Ti 3 O 7 coatings
abstract: Residual-alkali-derived Na 2 Ti 3 O 7 coatings boost electrolyte wettability and electrochemical performance of a layered oxide cathode for sodium-ion batteries.

title: Tuning lipid nanocarrier mechanical properties to improve glioblastoma targeting and blood brain barrier penetration
abstract: Five different nanocarrier lipid systems (NLSs) were developed and tested for mechanical properties, glioblastoma cell uptake, and blood-brain barrier transport, showing potential for brain drug and gene delivery.

title: Facile synthesis of in situ carbon-coated CoS 2 micro/nano-spheres as high-performance anode materials for sodium-ion batteries
