abstract: In situ carbon-coated CoS 2 micro/nano-spheres were successfully prepared by sulfuric calcination using the solvothermal method with glycerol as the carbon source without introducing extraneous carbon.

title: Nonspecific metal-coordination-driven control over higher-order DNA self-assembly
abstract: A simple, versatile metal-coordination-driven regulation strategy in DNA nanotechnology is reported. Switchable self-assembly of higher-order DNA nanostructures is achieved by utilizing nonspecific Ag + –DNA coordination.

title: Photocurable cellulose nanofibers and their copolymers with polyacrylamide as microgels to support 3D cell cultivation
abstract: Sustainable microgels derived from photocurable cellulose nanofibers and their polyacrylamide hybrids efficiently support 3D cell culture as assembled granular hydrogels.

title: Eco-friendly synthesis of a porous reduced graphene oxide–polypyrrole–gold nanoparticle hybrid nanocomposite for electrochemical detection of methotrexate using a strip sensor
abstract: Chemotherapy is a crucial cancer treatment, but its effectiveness requires precise monitoring of drug concentrations in patients.

title: Towards 1D supramolecular chiral assemblies based on porphyrin–calixarene complexes
authors: 'Massimiliano Gaeta', 'Chiara M. A. Gangemi', 'Matteo Barcellona', 'Gabriele Travagliante', 'Marco Milone', 'Anna Notti', 'Maria E. Fragalà', 'Ilenia Pisagatti', 'Melchiorre F. Parisi', 'Roberto Purrello', 'Alessandro D'Urso'
abstract: This study reveals precise control of stoichiometry, dimensionality, and chirality by UV-vis, CD and SEM on the hierarchical self-assembly of chiral linear noncovalent porphyrins-biscalix complexes in aqueos solution.

title: Effect of reversible dislocation-based deformation on nanoparticle strain at failure
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations are used to isolate and quantify the reversible and irreversible mechanisms that contribute to deformation in platinum nanoparticles under compression.

title: Oleic acid rearrangement enables facile transfer of red-emitting quantum dots from hexane into water with enhanced fluorescence
abstract: Oleic acid (OA)-coated quantum dots (QDs) were very efficiently transferred (80% transfer) from hexane into water and became brighter at basic pH. No new ligands are needed since the transfer relies on rearrangement of OA at the surface of the QDs.

title: Unveiling magnetic transition-driven lattice thermal conductivity switching in monolayer VS 2
abstract: Effective thermal management is essential for maintaining the operational stability and data security of magnetic devices across diverse fields, including thermoelectric, sensing, data storage, and spintronics.

title: Small-rotation-angle moiré structures of 2H TaSe 2 monolayers on Au(111)
abstract: We demonstrate the growth of small-rotation-angle TaSe 2 moiré structures on a Au(111) substrate, providing important insights on the direct synthesis of small-angle twisted two-dimensional layers for future twistronics.

title: Unraveling a ligand-initiated oxidative etching pathway in gold nanoclusters
abstract: Thiol concentration governs the oxidative etching pathways of Au nanoclusters, switching between ligand-initiated and oxygen-initiated mechanisms, both leading to Au( i )-thiolate complexes via distinct intermediate steps.

title: Doping Gd 16 nanoclusters for expanded optical properties and thermometry applications
abstract: We achieved green–red color-tunable luminescence by controlling the doping of Tb 3+ and Eu 3+ ions in Gd 16 clusters and attained a high relative sensitivity of up to 4.6% K −1 in the as-doped Gd15Tb1−xEux ( x = 0.8).

title: Erasable laser 3D printing of stable perovskite CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals in fluorophosphate glass
abstract: Three-dimensional patterning in glass : formation of perovskite nanocrystals in fluorophosphates glass matrix for studying crystallization process under ultrashort laser pulses and achieving reversible optical data recording.

title: Fast and accurate characterization of bioconjugated particles and solvent properties by a general nonlinear analytical relationship for the AC magnetic hysteresis area
abstract: A nonlinear relationship for the magnetic hysteresis area of Brownian nanoparticles permits fast and accurate characterization of bioconjugated particles and solvent properties by an automated method.

title: 2 eV band gap tuning and optical properties of AgIn 5 S 8 quantum dots
abstract: A wide 2 eV tuning of the band gap energy, along with modulation of particle size and photoluminescence, was achieved in colloidally synthesized AgIn 5 S 8 quantum dots by adjusting the reactor temperature, sonication, and annealing conditions.

title: Quantum dots as multifunctional therapeutic agents: synergistic antitumor and antimicrobial mechanisms
abstract: Quantum dots (QDs) have emerged as versatile nanomaterials with significant potential for cancer therapy due to their unique optical properties, biocompatibility and multifunctionality. Image reproduced with permission, see article for details.

title: Hollow gold–platinum nanoshells as a delivery platform for Ce6: cascading catalysis for enhanced multimodal therapy in tumor ablation and antitumor immunity
abstract: Gold–platinum nanoparticles with triple nanozyme activity combined with photodynamic and photothermal effects for anti-tumor therapy.

title: Flexible electrodes for high-performance energy storage: materials, conductivity optimization, and scalable fabrication
abstract: This review highlights recent strategies in materials design and scalable fabrication for flexible electrodes, focusing on carbon-based and cellulose-derived systems for high-performance supercapacitors and Li-ion batteries.

title: White-light-driven plasmonic nanoparticle printing for opto-thermal manipulation and SERS application
abstract: We demonstrate a white-light-assisted optical printing method for plasmonic nanoparticles, enabling parallel optothermal tweezing of microscale objects and ultrasensitive SERS detection of an analyte molecule (62 pM for crystal violet).

title: The coexistence of Dirac cones and Fermi arcs in a graphene/WTe 2 heterostructure
abstract: We report on the electronic properties of a van der Waals heterostructure formed by graphene and a type-II Weyl semimetal ( T d -WTe 2 ), hosting features of both Dirac and Weyl semimetals.

title: Design of an RSM-optimized magnetic–fluorescent nanoprobe utilizing geothermal silica for enhanced bacterial sensing
abstract: RSM-optimized geothermal silica nanocomposites synergize magnetic and fluorescent functionalities for ultra-sensitive, rapid detection of Staphylococcus aureus , paving a sustainable path in biosensor engineering.

title: Unveiling the synergistic interface effects of Ag-deposited Fe 2 O 3 /biochar catalysts to enhance wastewater degradation
abstract: This study uses biochar from rape pollen as a template to synthesize spiky Fe 2 O 3 , followed by Ag nanoparticle deposition. The catalyst shows high dye selectivity, E. coli antibacterial activity, and effective aquaculture wastewater treatment.

title: Protein structure and bioactivity upon adsorption and desorption from nanosilicate sustained release delivery devices
abstract: Proteins form reversible complexes with two-dimensional nanosilicate particles, making the nanosilicates suitable for therapeutic delivery.

title: Radical initiated polymerization of p -styrenesulfonate on graphitic carbon nitride for interconnected water networks in short-side-chain PFSA membranes for low-humidity hydrogen fuel cells
abstract: Radical-initiated polymerization of p- styrenesulfonate on graphitic carbon nitride and its incorporation into a short-side chain polymer matrix reduce interfacial ionic transport resistance and improve low-humidity PEMFC performace.

title: Structural diversity dependent cation incorporation into magnetic Cr–Se nanocrystals
abstract: We report the colloidal synthesis of Cr–Se nanocrystals with tunable morphologies for cation exchange reactions to produce metal selenide nanocrystals.

title: Correction: Starvation induces diffusion hindrance at the nanoscale in mammalian cells
abstract: Correction for ‘Starvation induces diffusion hindrance at the nanoscale in mammalian cells’ by Sakshi Sareen et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 378–389, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR03620D.

title: Correction: Recent advances in nanoporous NO x gas sensors: synergizing Raman spectroscopy, IoT, and machine learning for high-performance detection
abstract: Correction for ‘Recent advances in nanoporous NO x gas sensors: synergizing Raman spectroscopy, IoT, and machine learning for high-performance detection’ by Vikas Yadav, et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5nr01757b.

title: Low-threshold colloidal quantum dot polariton lasing via a strong coupling microcavity at room temperature
abstract: Schematic of the CQD-microcavity device. The angle-resolved emission spectrum of polariton lasing formation at RT, showing the exciton state (red line), bare cavity mode (blue line), and coupled LP mode (green line), is indicated.

title: Magnetic field-assisted nanochain formation of intermixed catalytic Co–Pd nanoparticles
abstract: Mixed Co–Pd nanoparticles assemble into nanochains under a magnetic field, exhibiting catalytic properties that are useful for CO oxidation.

title: Enhancing both the long-term stability and methylene blue adsorption performance of TiVCT x via a facile antioxidation treatment
abstract: OA, SC, and TA were used to treat TiVCT x for stability improvement. The maximum adsorption capacity of TA-TiVCT x reaches 8061.03 mg g −1 . After being stored for 14 days, TA-TiVCT x still maintains an adsorption capacity of 3887.28 mg g −1 .

title: Revealing the chemical compatibility of common solvents and electrolytes with Mo 2 TiC 2 -based MXenes and their interfaces in aluminum-ion batteries (AIBs) through first-principles molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: A comprehensive investigation on the chemical compatibility of various solvents and electrolytes with Mo 2 TiC 2 -based MXenes is conducted, evaluating their possible use as solvents, additives and electrolytes in aluminum-ion batteries (AIBs).

title: Phase control in polymorphic transformation-based nonvolatile memory devices for reliable data reading in X-point structures
abstract: The phase control of MnTe, which exhibits reversible and nonvolatile polymorphic transformations, is crucial for reliable memory operations in X-point structure.

title: Efficient photochemical production of H 2 O 2 on carbon nitride photocatalysts with the optimized multi-synergistic effect of enhanced visible light absorption, charge separation, and surface kinetics
abstract: Photochemical production of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) using visible light response photocatalysts offers a sustainable green strategy. GA image: Background via Pixabay.com

title: Formation of covalent Ga–C bonds on liquid metal nanoparticles with enhanced stability and anti-oxidation
abstract: Spontaneous reaction of aryldiazonium salts enables covalent Ga–C bond formation on liquid metal nanoparticles, enhancing their stability, oxidation resistance, and functionalization for advanced electronic and biomedical applications.

title: Role of polycrystalline F–SnO 2 substrate topography in formation mechanism and morphology of Pt nanoparticles by solid-state-dewetting
abstract: SSD of thin films is increasingly utilized to fabricate nanoparticles for catalysis. RTA is combined with plan-view sample preparation for in situ TEM to provide insights into the initial stages of the SSD mechanism and formation of Pt NPs on FTO.

title: Close atomic surface on aluminum alloy achieved by a near-neutral novel green chemical mechanical polishing method with high material removal rate
abstract: A novel near-neutral green CMP slurry was developed, achieving a close atomic surface on aluminum alloy. The surface roughness (Sa) was 0.231 nm, and the material removal rate was 12.56 μm h −1 .

title: Enhanced superhydrophobic robustness of black silicon employing nanojungle structures
abstract: Bosch etching and photoresist micromasks were used to create nanojungle black silicon (b-Si) with heights over 10 μm. This nanojungle b-Si shows superior superhydrophobic robustness compared to traditional nanograss b-Si.

title: CT imaging of and therapy for inflammatory bowel disease via low molecular weight dextran coated ceria nanoparticles
abstract: This study investigates the synthesis and evaluation of a novel contrast agent, dextran-coated cerium nanoparticles (Dex-CeNP), designed to both image and treat IBD simultaneously.

title: Charge transfer dynamics and tuning of reorganization energy in graphene-encapsulated co-based alloy catalysts for fuel cells
abstract: Metal alloy nanocatalysts encapsulated by graphene shell demonstrate multifunctional catalytic activity and improved chemical stability.

title: Effect of pressure on the thermoelectric performance of monolayer Janus MoSSe materials with different native vacancy defects
abstract: The influences of pressure on the ZT of the monolayer Janus MoSSe materials at room temperature with the two types of vacancy defects are different.

title: Recent advances in nanozymes based on mesoporous materials for enhancing the performance of colorimetric biosensors: Applications and challenges
abstract: Nanozymes derived from mesoporous materials and their application in colorimetric sensing.

title: Innovative InAg–carbon nanocomposites: mesoporous design for OER enhancement
abstract: In this study, we developed bimetallic InAg–carbon nanocomposite catalysts for the OER, demonstrating accelerated kinetics, improved stability, and increased density of active sites.

title: Super-stretchable, freezing-resistant and self-powered organohydrogels for extreme environment-adaptable high-performance strain sensors
abstract: The rapid development of wearable sensors has indeed driven the demand for multifunctional conductive soft materials to new heights.

title: Harnessing chirality in plasmonics: from synthesis to cutting-edge applications
abstract: Chiral plasmonic nanostructures have been increasingly explored for asymmetric catalysis, high-sensitivity sensors with selectivity and advanced optical components. This review summarizes their synthesis methods and recent progress in the field.

title: Tunable heteroassembly of 2D CoNi LDH and Ti 3 C 2 nanosheets with enhanced electrocatalytic activity for oxygen evolution
abstract: CoNi LDH/Ti 3 C 2 heterostructures are obtained by electrostatic attraction of positively charged LDH and negatively charged Ti 3 C 2 nanosheets, which serve as effective catalysts for oxygen evolution reaction with enhanced electrocatalytic activity.

title: A fluorescent nonconjugated zwitterionic polymer dot: hydrothermal synthesis and application in the nano-molar sensing of 2,4,6-trinitrophenol
abstract: This study reports the synthesis of a poly(aminoamide)-based fluorescent zwitterionic polymer dot and its application in the nano-molar sensing of 2,4,6-trinitrophenol.

title: Thermoelectrochemical formation of a solid electrolyte interphase on a silicon negative electrode to enhance the durability of silicon-enriched lithium-ion batteries by compositional modification
abstract: The presented work focuses on reinforcing the interface of a silicon electrode, that is thermoelectrochemical activation of solid electrolyte interphase formation, which improves the cycleability of the cutting-edge lithium-ion battery chemistry.

title: Drug delivery systems for mitochondrial targeting
abstract: Diverse therapeutics comprising nucleic acids and small molecules are directed to mitochondria through nanomedicines. Several vectors were developed for treating mitochondrial diseases, encompassing PMDs, SMDs, and mitochondrial-related disorders.

title: Layered intercalation ferroelectricity induced by asymmetric ion coordination: a mini-review
abstract: The emerging intercalation ferroelectricity may provide significant scientific and technological opportunities.

title: Alternate InP synthesis with aminophosphines: solution–liquid–solid nanowire growth
abstract: Colloidal synthesis of InP nanowires is reported using aminophosphine and weakly-bound indium-ligand complexes, producing nanoribbons and nanowires. The aminophosphine is proposed to reduce indium for self-seeded solution–liquid–solid growth.

title: Magnetoelastic anisotropy drives localized magnetization reversal in 3D nanowire networks
abstract: Magnetization reversal in three-dimensional nanowire networks is driven by highly localized magnetic states, arising from the interplay of exchange, dipolar interactions and magnetoelastic anisotropy, thereby challenging the prevailing understanding.

title: Self-template impregnated silver nanoparticles in coordination polymer gel: photocatalytic CO 2 reduction, CO 2 fixation, and antibacterial activity
abstract: CO 2 fixation and light-assisted conversion of CO 2 in the presence of water into fuels and feedstocks are clean and sustainable techniques to alleviate the energy crisis and global climate change.

title: Effects of surface functionalization and size of MXene-based quantum dots on their optical properties: the exciton confinement matters
abstract: Quantum confinement in MXene quantum dots enhances excitonic effects, causing a blue shift of the first optical transition. Surface functionalization allows for fine tuning of exciton character and spectral behavior into desirable features.

title: Hydroxyapatite nanoparticle mediated delivery of full length dystrophin gene as a potential therapeutic for the treatment of Duchenne muscular dystrophy
abstract: Transfection of the mCherry tagged full length dystrophin plasmid (18.8 kb) using in-house synthesized R-HAp nanoparticles in mouse C2C12 cells, human skeletal muscle cells (hSKM) cells and DMD patient derived cells was accomplished.

title: Efficient synthesis of Au 36 (SR) 24 nanoclusters via the cluster-from-cluster approach
abstract: Ten examples of Au36 nanoclusters with different thiolate ligands have been prepared in high yield (up to 64%) via the cluster-from-cluster approach.

title: Enhanced two-photon absorption in hydrothermally synthesized La 2 O 6 Te nanorods for visible light photodetection
abstract: LOT nanorods are successfully prepared via a hydrothermal method. These LOT materials show good photodetection and NLO properties, which make them a strong contender in the field of optoelectronic devices and applications.

title: Exfoliated MoS 2 nanosheets immobilized in porous microbeads as recoverable photocatalysts
abstract: Immobilization of exfoliated MoS 2 nanosheets on polymer beads ensures high dispersion stability, resulting in excellent photocatalytic performance.

title: Theoretical understanding of the in-plane tensile strain effects on enhancing the ferroelectric performance of Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 and ZrO 2 thin films
abstract: The strain effects on the phase fractions and polarization in Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 and ZrO 2 were investigated. Under tensile strain at low temperatures, the ferroelectric phase was stabilized, while the formation of other phases was kinetically inhibited.

title: A temperature-sensitive and fluorescent Tr-CD/AuNP-based catalyst for efficient, monitorable, and recyclable catalytic reactions
abstract: A temperature-sensitive and fluorescent catalyst was prepared. It can not only monitor the catalytic reaction process using its own fluorescence change but also accomplish the recycling of the catalyst through simple temperature control.

title: Catalytic effects of iron adatoms in poly( para -phenylene) synthesis on rutile TiO 2 (110)
abstract: We studied light and iron adatoms’ influence on debromination of 4,4′′-dibromo- p -terphenyl precursors forming poly( para -phenylene) wires via Ullmann coupling on TiO 2 (110), using scanning tunneling microscopy and X-ray photoemission spectroscopy.

title: A generative diffusion model enables multi-objective on-demand inverse design of piezoelectric metamaterials
abstract: A multi-objective design strategy based on latent diffusion models was proposed to enable inverse design of piezoelectric metamaterials, achieving a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of only 1.06% relative to target performance criteria.

title: Room-temperature generation and transformation of graphitic CQDs into mixed sp 2 –sp 3 hybridized carbon allotropes from liquid oxygenates
abstract: Liquid oxygenates are converted into graphitic carbon quantum dots and instantly transformed into nanocrystalline sp 2 –sp 3 hybridized carbon allotropes under ambient conditions on in situ generated negatively charged Ag nanoparticles.

title: A visual language model enabling intelligent nanomaterial scanning electron micrograph annotation
abstract: SEM-VLM, a domain-specific vision-language model trained via contrastive learning on SEM image–text pairs mined from scientific literature, demonstrates superior performance in recognizing SEM images of nanomaterials.

title: Cu-based bimetallic catalysts for electrochemical CO 2 reduction: before and beyond the tandem effect
abstract: Cu-based bimetallic catalysts show enhanced electrochemical CO 2 reduction performance via the tandem effect. This review traces their progress, highlighting design advances, mechanisms, and challenges to guide efficient CO 2 conversion.

title: Hydrogenation effects on the structural stability and superconducting properties of calcium-intercalated bilayer graphene C 2 CaC 2
abstract: Hydrogenation of Ca-intercalated bilayer graphene (C 2 CaC 2 ) forms a stable, metallic HC 2 CaC 2 phase with a reduced superconducting T c from 18.9 K to 6.1 K, demonstrating tunable superconductivity via functionalization.

title: Forming N-heterocyclic carbene monolayers: not all deposition methods are the same
abstract: The graphic illustrates that the binding of N-heterocyclic carbenes to gold surfaces is influenced by both temperature and choice of precursor.

title: Synergistic role of Cl − and Br − ions in growth control and mechanistic insights of high aspect ratio silver nanowires for flexible transparent conductive films
abstract: Silver nanowires (AgNWs) with high aspect ratios are pivotal for the production of flexible transparent conductive films (TCFs).

title: A step towards efficient water splitting: a high-performance CuCo(OH) 2 /CNT/MoS 2 electrocatalyst
abstract: Visualizing the research workflow: synthesis, electrochemical analysis, and energy conversion application of the CuCo(OH) 2 /CNT/MoS 2 composite electrocatalyst. Images via Pixabay.com

title: Mechanics of granular distribution of aligned carbon nanotube bundles
abstract: We present the dynamic and mechanical behaviors of aligned nanotubes with varying curvatures during twisting.

title: Ti 3 C 2 O 2 MXene as a dual-action modulator of inflammatory and tuberculosis signaling: structural and in vitro insights
abstract: Ti 3 C 2 O 2 MXene nanosheets show dual anti-inflammatory and anti-tuberculosis effects by modulating host–pathogen pathways, supported by molecular docking, dynamics simulations, and in vitro studies.

title: Electrode reaction mechanisms in aqueous batteries: a comparative study and perspective on solid–solid versus solid–liquid–solid conversions
abstract: This work systematically compares solid–solid and solid–liquid–solid conversion mechanisms in aqueous batteries and provides new insights into designing high-performance aqueous energy storage systems.

title: π–π electron conjugation-assisted synthesis of a robust heterostructured CoO–MoO 2 catalyst: accelerated ammonia borane hydrolysis for hydrogen evolution
abstract: Heterostructured multicomponent catalysts can be developed to exploit their unique interfacial effects for enhancing their catalytic activities by optimizing their electronic structures.

title: Ultrafine intermetallic platinum-cobalt with a contracted Pt–Pt pair for efficient acidic oxygen reduction reactions
abstract: Ultrafine Pt 3 Co nanocatalysts were synthesized via a ‘metal-defect confinement’ strategy, exhibiting small size and high ECSA. After annealing and ADTs, they showed size increase activity loss (2%), demonstrating excellent ORR stability.

title: Investigating the visible range photoresponse of an organic single-crystal analogue of the green fluorescent protein
abstract: The photoresponse of a novel organic single-crystal analogue of the GFP in the visible region shows significant performance in terms of high responsivity, detectivity, and fast response, making it viable for next-generation broadband photodetectors.

title: Multifunctional applications of 2D anti-ambipolar transistors: frequency doubling and multi-valued inverter design
abstract: The rapid growth of information drives the need for highly integrated, low-power devices, making novel materials and architectures essential.

title: Core/shell BCN@Cu heterostructures via electroless deposition for interface-tailored electroactive materials
abstract: Copper thin layer was deposited onto boron carbon nitride (BCN) by electroless plating and the fabricated composite was utilized in electrochemical nitrate reduction into ammonia.

title: Design of colloidal vectors for active targeting via complexation of biotinylated copolymers with gadolinium ions
abstract: Biotin-functionalized hybrid polyionic complexes (HPICs) using RAFT polymers for targeted MRI applications. The synthesized HPICs with gadolinium ions, demonstrate high stability, relaxivity, and biotin accessibility for active targeting.

title: Mg-diamane-modified polypropylene separators achieving dendrite-free sodium metal batteries with a long cycle lifespan
abstract: A Mg-diamane/PP separator is designed and employed to ensure uniform Na + flux and suppress dendrites, delivering a long lifespan of 1850 h at 2 mA cm −2 with 1 mAh cm −2 .

title: Enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction performance of alloyed MoS 1.34 Se 0.78 with dominant 1T/T′ phases
abstract: Transition metal chalcogenides with metallic octahedral and semi-metallic distorted octahedral phases have demonstrated superior electrocatalytic activity for the HER compared to their semiconducting trigonal prismatic counterparts.

title: Expression of concern: Near-infrared light activatable hydrogels for metformin delivery
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Near-infrared light activatable hydrogels for metformin delivery’ by Li Chengnan et al. , Nanoscale , 2019, 11 , 15810–15820, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9NR02707F.

title: Correction: “Turning the dials”: controlling synthesis, structure, composition, and surface chemistry to tailor silicon nanoparticle properties
authors: 'Sarah Milliken', 'Alyxandra N. Thiessen', 'I. Teng Cheong', 'Kevin M. O'Connor', 'Ziqi Li', 'Riley W. Hooper', 'Christopher Jay T. Robidillo', 'Jonathan G. C. Veinot'
abstract: Correction for ‘“Turning the dials”: controlling synthesis, structure, composition, and surface chemistry to tailor silicon nanoparticle properties’ by Sarah Milliken et al. , Nanoscale , 2021, 13 , 16379–16404, https://doi.org/10.1039/D1NR04701A.

title: Tailored charge storage mechanism of a nickel metavanadate/polyaniline composite for high-performance hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Hybrid materials that integrate battery-type and supercapacitive properties hold significant promise for next-generation energy storage systems.

title: Toward scalable manufacturing of doped silicon nanopillars for thermoelectrics via metal-assisted chemical etching
abstract: Metal-Assisted Chemical Etching (MACE) using Ag enables the fabrication of vertically aligned crystalline silicon nanopillars (SiNPs) with high aspect ratios over a wide doping range, a system highly promising for thermoelectric applications.

title: Epitaxial growth of antiferromagnetic MnBi 2 Te 4 /CdTe heterostructures on GaAs(001) using molecular beam epitaxy: structure and electronic properties
abstract: MnBi 2 Te 4 is one of the most recent materials that integrates the class of topological quantum materials exhibiting topological insulating properties and magnetic ordering to investigate topological quantum states and design novel spintronic devices.

title: Terahertz shielding effectiveness of graphene oxide silicone rubber foam composite media
abstract: We fabricated a 10 mm thick GO@SiRF medium by combining graphene oxide (GO) with silicone rubber foam (SiRF) and investigated the influence of GO content on its terahertz shielding effectiveness.

title: Unraveling the infrared detection properties of Bi 2 Te 3 depending on thickness under the semiconductor and metal surface states
abstract: As the thickness of the Bi 2 Te 3 film increases, its photoelectric response mechanism changes from a photoconductive effect to a photobolometric effect.

title: Single-layer CrI 2 as a magnetic semiconductor: a detailed first-principles study
abstract: Combination of low-dispersive valence bands, semiconducting behavior, robust antiferromagnetism and rich vibrational characteristics positions monolayer CrI 2 as a promising material for advanced spintronic and optoelectronic applications.

title: Optimizing InAsPSb/InAsP cladding structures to control carrier overflow and enhance emission in multiple quantum well LEDs
abstract: Optimizing the interfacial barrier height between the cladding layer and the quantum barrier in multiple quantum well LEDs is crucial for controlling carrier overflow and enhancing carrier injection.

title: Calcium citrate nanoparticles as a new frontier in metabolic acidosis treatment
abstract: Metabolic acidosis often coexists with systemic diseases, contributing to cellular dysfunction through mechanisms such as protein degradation, bone loss, renal impairment, inflammation, oxidative stress, and apoptosis.

title: Lateral heterophase electric polar topological superstructures of monolayer SnS: a first-principles computational study
abstract: Three-phase coexistence based hierarchical nested flux-closure polarization patterns.

title: Tuning the peroxidase mimetic activity of cerium oxide nanozymes using a microwave-assisted rapid fabrication approach
abstract: Microwave-assited synthesis of CeO 2 nanoparticles for augmented peroxidase (POD)-like activity.

title: High-performance perovskite quantum dot synthesis investigated through exploratory data analysis
abstract: ML-guided exploratory data analysis, reinforced by domain knowledge, ranks synthesis factors and pinpoints the ligand pair OA/OLA at a 0.9 ratio, yielding ∼90% PLQY and stable metal–halide perovskite quantum dots.

title: Effect of grain boundaries on metal atom migration and electronic transport in 2D TMD-based resistive switches
abstract: This study examines metal atom migration across MoS 2 grain boundaries in vertical and lateral 2DM memristors. DFT and transport simulations reveal how GBs and defects affect filament formation and resistive switching performance.

title: Enhanced electromechanical response in Dy 3+ -doped PNN–PZT relaxor ferroelectrics
abstract: A high piezoelectricity and excellent temperature stability were achieved in PNN–PZT relaxor ferroelectrics, outperforming those of other typical piezoceramics.

title: Nanoparticles alter locust development and behaviour
abstract: The image illustrates the effect of nanoparticle-treated lettuce on locusts, showing their initial avoidance, eventual feeding, and subsequent death, suggesting a potential pest control strategy.

title: Isomer-driven supramolecular polymorphism of 4-hydroxytamoxifen modulating nanostructure-dependent cellular uptake and cytotoxicity
abstract: The efficacy of 4-OHT is influenced by its supramolecular structure, not just receptor affinity. Isomer-specific assemblies create amorphous nanoflakes ( Z -form) with high uptake and crystalline nanoribbons ( E -form) with limited bioactivity.

title: Disparity-amplified chiral SERS using a PSP–LSP-coupled substrate
abstract: Integrating chiral nanoparticles with gold-coated laser-induced periodic surface structures (LIPSS) creates a SERS substrate that significantly amplifies enantiomeric disparity in asymmetrically enhanced Raman signals.

title: Strategically engineering 2D MXene-based advanced adsorbents for sustainable wastewater remediation of dyes
abstract: Mxene nanosorbents engineered via surface, defect, bandgap, and doping modifications offer high adsorption, tunable bandgap, flexibility and sustainability, enabling efficient wastewater remediation and advancing One Health management.

title: Innovative applications of MXenes in dialysis: enhancing filtration efficiency
abstract: The purpose of this review is to comprehensively explore the multifaceted applications of MXenes and their composites in dialysis, highlighting challenges and future directions.

title: Structural and functional characterization of self-assembling fragments identified from the transthyretin amyloid-like structure
abstract: An integrated computational–experimental pipeline identifies peptide fragments from the PDB that self-assemble into cross-β nanostructures with tunable functional properties, enabling bioinspired material design.

title: Thermal conversion of metal hydroxide acrylate nanoparticles immobilized on TiO 2 toward noble-metal-free photocatalytic H 2 production
abstract: Thermal conversion of nickel hydroxide acrylate nanoparticles on TiO 2 produces Ni/NiO x /carbon nanostructures that boost photocatalytic hydrogen evolution, offering a noble-metal-free strategy for efficient water splitting.

title: Templated self-assembly of gold nanoparticles in smectic liquid crystals confined at 3D printed curved surfaces
authors: 'Mackenzie O'Keefe', 'Jane Bernadette Denise M. Garcia', 'Abeco J. Rwakabuba', 'Timothy M. Otchy', 'Daniel A. Beller', 'Mohamed Amine Gharbi'
abstract: Advancing 3D nanoprinting for curved surface fabrication, liquid crystal confinement, topological defect engineering, and nanomaterial assembly.

title: Enhancing redox activity and surface reactivity via hierarchical α-MoO 3 nanobelt incorporation into layered WS 2 nanosheets for high performance symmetric and asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Illustrating the charge storage mechanism in the WS 2 /α-MoO 3 symmetric cell and the fabrication and operation of the WS 2 /MoO 3 //AC asymmetric coin cell.

title: Bulk photovoltaic effect in a two-dimensional ferroelectric semiconductor α-In 2 Se 3
abstract: We present a demonstration of enhanced bulk photovoltaic response in α-In 2 Se 3 under the excitation of visible light, with photovoltaic current density nearly two orders of magnitude higher than that of conventional bulk ferroelectric materials.

title: Density functional theory-based surrogate kinetic models for heterogeneous reactions of hydrocarbon intermediates on silicon carbide
abstract: We used Density Functional Theory transition-state calculations to resolve hydrocarbon intermediates on silicon carbide and calculated reaction rates, which were then used to develop macroscopic surrogate kinetic models.

title: Surface-engineered PVDF-HFP/BNNS micro-nano fibers enable high-performance radiative cooling through synergistic photon scattering
abstract: PHB-4% is developed as a radiative cooling fabric via electrospinning, integrating BNNS and hierarchical micro-nanostructures to achieve efficient cooling, enhanced durability, and improved stain resistance for personal thermal management.

title: Cell-penetrating peptides as facilitators of cargo-specific nanocarrier-based drug delivery
abstract: Cell penetrating peptides have emerged as a prime tool for enhancing nanocarrier barrier penetration and targeted drug delivery outcomes. Illustration made using BioRender. The graphical abstract was created in BioRender. Maisel, K. (2025) https://BioRender.com/iouxsm2.

title: Chiral induction at the nanoscale and spin selectivity in electron transmission in chiral methylated BEDT-TTF derivatives
abstract: Tetramethyl-bis(ethylenedithio)-tetrathiafulvalene (TM-BEDT-TTF) enantiomers’ spin filtering and chiroptical properties are studied. Spin-filtering properties are assessed by recording spin-polarized I – V curves (mc-AFM) of TM-BEDT-TTF fibrils.

title: Moiré metasurfaces with tunable near-infrared-I chiroptical responses for biomolecular chirality discrimination
abstract: A moiré metasurface, consisting of two sheets of suspended bilayer metagratings, is demonstrated with active near-infrared-I chiroptical responses in both its sign and magnitude, which is exploited for label-free chirality detection.

title: Exciton–phonon coupling in quasi-two-dimensional Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites: impact of a mixed-phase structure
abstract: The exciton–phonon interaction strength increases in two-dimensional Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites with the number of layers. Moreover, it varies across different mixed-phase perovskite samples, likely due to changes in the local environment.

title: Deep reconstruction of crystalline–amorphous heterojunction electrocatalysts for efficient and stable water and methanol electrolysis
abstract: A self-reconstruction process consisting of cation doping with morphology control and interface engineering is used to prepare highly active and stable bifunctional electrocatalysts.

title: Advances in nanomaterial thin-film temperature sensors: materials and applications
abstract: This review provides a comparative overview of thin-film temperature sensors based on five major material groups, highlighting their mechanisms, strengths, limitations, and integration into wearable, e-skin, and robotic applications.

title: Morphology-driven multifunctionality: tailoring ZnO for enhanced EMI shielding and energy harvesting in PVDF/MWCNT nanocomposites
abstract: This study demonstrates the influence of morphologies of zinc oxide on the electrical, EMI shielding, piezoelectric, and triboelectric properties of PVDF-based hybrid nanocomposites.

title: Flow synthesis of Pt and Pt–Ag nanostructures towards hydrogen peroxide electroreduction
abstract: Anisotropic and isotropic Pt and Pt–Ag nanostructures synthesized via a flow-chemical methodology using a 3D-printed reactor for catalytic applications.

title: Neutral inorganic salt additives universally regulate multicolor perovskites for efficient electroluminescence
abstract: This paper reveals that neutral phosphate additives were used to achieve universal adjustment of phase distribution and device performance of multicolor perovskite films

title: Surface-engineered gold nanorods for targeted delivery of PD-L1 siRNA and cancer chemo-phototherapy
abstract: Surface-engineered gold nanorods enable targeted PD-L1 siRNA delivery and multimodal chemo-phototherapy, achieving enhanced tumor cell killing with combined therapeutic strategies.

title: The role of molecular ambient adsorption on light–matter interactions of 2D few-layered ReSe 2 photodiodes
authors: 'Nitzan Cohen', 'Yarden Mazal Jahn', 'Nathan Samuel Shultzman', 'Assaf Ya'akobovitz'
abstract: ReSe 2 photodiodes present outstanding performances, that rely on their interactions with ambient oxygen. Indeed, they show high responsivity and external quantum efficiency (EQE).

title: Correction: Fabrication of nanoporous anodized aluminum oxide based photonic crystals with multi-band responses in the vis-NIR region
abstract: Correction for ‘Fabrication of nanoporous anodized aluminum oxide based photonic crystals with multi-band responses in the vis-NIR region’ by Mengqi Li et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, 17 , 4099–4110, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR04744C.

title: Correction: Cost-effective carbon fiber precursor selections of polyacrylonitrile-derived blend polymers: carbonization chemistry and structural characterizations
abstract: Correction for ‘Cost-effective carbon fiber precursor selections of polyacrylonitrile-derived blend polymers: carbonization chemistry and structural characterizations’ by Qian Mao et al. , Nanoscale , 2022, 14 , 6357–6372, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2NR00203E.

title: Correction: A size-shrinkable nanoparticle-based combined anti-tumor and anti-inflammatory strategy for enhanced cancer therapy
abstract: Correction for ‘A size-shrinkable nanoparticle-based combined anti-tumor and anti-inflammatory strategy for enhanced cancer therapy’ by Zhengze Lu et al. , Nanoscale , 2018, 10 , 9957–9970, https://doi.org/10.1039/C8NR01184B.

title: Vacancy-induced modulation of the interfacial properties of Au 25 (SCH 3 ) 18 nanoclusters supported on defective graphene
abstract: The vacancy edge of defective graphene can effectively modulate the interfacial behavior of Au 25 (SR) 18 nanoclusters, leading to the removal of the staple Au–SCH 3 unit and facilitating the electroreduction of CO 2 to CO.

title: Solution synthesis of antiferromagnetic manganese nanoparticles
abstract: Highly electrophilic Mn(0) nanoparticles were synthesized via an organometallic approach. Their magnetic properties indicate antiferromagnetic behaviour, while solution NMR studies unveiled the dynamic nature of weakly coordinated surface ligands.

title: A novel 3DOM Pt/MeO x –SiO 2 catalyst for the catalytic oxidation of VOCs
abstract: In this work, the cooperative effect of different non-noble metal oxides in 3DOM SiO 2 on the catalytic performance of Pt-based catalysts was investigated, where the 3DOM Pt/CeO x –SiO 2 catalyst exhibited superior activity and strong sulfur resistance.

title: Selenium nanoparticles: influence of reducing agents on particle stability and antibacterial activity at biogenic concentrations
abstract: The stability of selenium nanoparticles (SeNPs) is influenced by the type of stabilizing agent when using different reducing agents. Several destabilizing processes occurres. The SeNPs demonstrate strong antibacterial activity.

title: Immobilization and accelerated conversion of polysulfides using vanadium-regulated metal sulfide catalysts for Li–S batteries
abstract: Lithium–sulfur batteries possess significant potential due to their high energy density and cost-effectiveness.

title: Machine learning-guided discovery of gas evolving electrode bubble inactivation
abstract: Experimental interrogation unveils that as much as 75% of the area underneath bubbles is electrochemically active. A simple method for estimating the degree of electrode inactivation due to bubbles is demonstrated.

title: Engineered molybdenum disulfide nanosheets as scavengers against oxidative stress inhibit ferroptosis to alleviate acute kidney injury
abstract: PVP-MoS 2 NSs with broad-spectrum ROS scavenging could be accumulated in injured kidneys, providing antioxidative protection, reducing inflammation, and maintaining kidney function. Created in BioRender. Wu, Z. (2025) https://BioRender.com/v46b503

title: Constructing macroporous nanofibers with enriched Co–N–C sites via a cascade chamber confinement strategy for an efficient oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: A cascade chamber confinement method was proposed for the preparation of Co–N–C nanofibers with a continuous macroporous carbon nanofiber network and dense Co–N–C sites.

title: In situ investigation of 1T′/1H phase transition in colloidal WS 2 monolayers
abstract: In situ study of colloidal WS 2 monolayers shows reducing-ligand stabilization of 1T′ up to 300 °C. At higher temperature, transition to 1H occurs with kinetics controlled by nanosheet size and surface chemistry.

title: Efficient solar-to-thermal management on femtosecond laser composite fabrication of hierarchical Janus plates
abstract: We fabricated a femtosecond laser-based hierarchically superhydrophobic Janus photothermal stainless-steel plate.

title: Non-invasive modulation of carrier injection efficiency via electronic phase separation at organic/inorganic interfaces
abstract: Magnetic-field-controlled phase separation in LPCMO dynamically shifts the interfacial barrier, offering a non-invasive route for amplifying carrier injection in hybrid organic/inorganic devices.

title: Nanostructured self-humidifying membrane electrode assemblies: toward efficient water management in air-cooled proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: By integrating material selection with structural design, tailored approaches can be applied to specific components to realize internal self-humidification functionality.

title: Supramolecular assembly of unstructured peptides into rigid bundlemer polymers
abstract: Designed 15-residue peptides that are unstructured in solution fold into coiled-coil, rod-like polymers 2 nm wide and over 5 μm long upon N-to-N conjugation through thiol–maleimide click chemistry.

title: Oral pH- and inflammation-targeted delivery system with biodegradable multi-layer core–shell nanocapsules for the treatment of ulcerative colitis
abstract: Oral pH and inflammatory targeted system MBSH@CA could deliver the biologics to macrophages and alleviate UC symptoms by regulating macrophage polarization, decreasing the permeability of the intestinal barrier and suppressing oxidative stress.

title: ORR, OER, and HER activity promotion in hierarchical yolk–shell structures based on Co-glycerate@cobalt carbonate hydroxide by dual doping with manganese and boron
abstract: Nowadays, exploring efficient electrocatalysts is crucial for the tri-functional oxygen reduction reaction (ORR), oxygen evolution reaction (OER) and hydrogen evolution reaction (HER).

title: Efficient regulation of surface hydroxyl groups on a Pt/Na/AC catalyst using two-step nonthermal plasma for high formaldehyde oxidation performance
abstract: A novel catalyst (Pt/Na/AC (P&P)) with 0.1 wt% Pt loading was synthesized via a two-step nonthermal plasma method, achieving excellent HCHO oxidation performance at ambient temperature by tailoring surface hydroxyl groups on Na/activated carbon (AC).

title: Driving catalytic carbyne formation within endohedral DWCNTs: the role of Ni vs. Pt
abstract: MD simulations show Ni catalysts are more effective than Pt for synthesizing long carbyne chains within carbon nanotubes. Ni promotes stable, self-propagating growth, while Pt leads to fragmented structures, aiding the design of novel nanocarbons.

title: Durable and efficient BTC-assisted 2D/0D Al–Ni-MOF nanostructures for modern electrochemical energy systems
abstract: Hybrid supercapacitor based on Al–Ni-MOF positive and AC negative electrodes with KOH gel electrolyte, delivering moderate capacitance, energy density, and stable cycling performance.

title: Synergistic effect of ligand–cluster structure and support in gold nanocluster catalysts for selective hydrogenation of alkynes
abstract: Disclosing the effect of ligand binding motif types on the catalytic activity of gold nanoclusters in selective hydrogenation.

title: Novel two-dimensional molybdenene as a promising electrocatalyst for the nitrogen reduction reaction: a first-principles prediction
abstract: Predicted novel and stable molybdenene shows a phonon spectrum without imaginary frequencies and is expected to be an excellent electrocatalyst for the nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR) due to its low overpotential and superior selectivity.

title: Integration of Bi 2 Te 3 nanosheets with laser desorption/ionization mass spectrometry for sensitive glucose detection in soft drinks
abstract: Accurate and sensitive detection of small biomolecules such as saccharides in foods and beverages remains difficult due to poor ionization and spectral interference in conventional organic matrix-based LDI-MS.

title: Tuning infrared emissivity of multilayer graphene using ionic liquid gel electrolytes
abstract: Ionic liquid gel electrolyte is used as a doping source to dynamically control the emissivity of multilayer graphene through electrostatic gating. The device lifetime is dramatically improved, compared to the modulator using pure ionic liquid.

title: Simulation of laser-assisted machining of the GH2135 alloy using molecular dynamics: effects of machining parameters on cutting performance
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulation revealed that laser-assisted machining improved cutting machinability of GH2135 alloy. Higher laser power and cutting speed promoted amorphization, material removal and cutting efficiency, but accelerated tool wear.

title: Surface reconstruction of La 0.95 Pr 1.05 CuO 4 as an efficient bifunctional electrocatalyst for water splitting
abstract: The design of a cost-effective, stable, and efficient electrocatalyst for electrochemical water splitting is crucial for hydrogen generation.

title: Electronic structure tunability of carbon-based 1D-polymers combining cross-conjugation and nitrogen doping
abstract: We produce atomically precise cross-conjugated one-dimensional polymeric chains designed in their heteroatom content. The pyridine location and number in the chains effectively tune both the gap size and the frontier orbital energy positions.

title: Impact of electron–phonon coupling, Stokes shift and quantum confinement in boron nitride quantum dots for hydrogen production via water splitting
abstract: The electron–phonon coupling (EPC) in hydrothermally synthesized BNQDs is controlled, and the strength of the EPC is quantified in terms of Huang–Rhys factors. The role of Stokes shift, EPC, and quantum confinement in H 2 evolution is studied.

title: Non-invasive imaging-guided preclinical assessments reveal high performance photothermal effect of an Au-solid lipid nanomaterial
abstract: Multimodal imaging-guided evaluation of novel Au-SLN-mediated photothermal application showed precisive elimination of primary tumour mass in an orthotopic breast murine model, significantly enhancing life expectancy.

title: Nature of local disorder in β-NaYF 4 -based, near-infrared upconverting core nanocrystals due to deliberate incorporation of a symmetry perturbing agent
abstract: The work examines the relationship between atomic-level disorder and the distortion of local crystal field symmetry within a single-phase Upconversion nanocrystal through high-resolution synchrotron and neutron diffraction analysis.

title: How octopod Mn–Fe oxide nanoparticle tracers minimize relaxation time and enhance MPI resolution
abstract: 25 nm Octopod Mn–Fe oxide nanoparticles reduce crystal anisotropy, leading to faster relaxation times, a narrower MPI point spread function (PSF), and consequently improved spatial resolution.

title: Introduction to MXene chemistries in biology, medicine and sensing
abstract: Maksym Pogorielov, Açelya Yilmazer, Lucia Gemma Delogu and Yury Gogotsi introduce the cross-journal themed issue collection on MXene chemistries in biology, medicine and sensing, published in Nanoscale and Nanoscale Advances .

title: Evolution of the formation of AgBiS 2 colloidal nanocrystals for optoelectronic devices
abstract: Precise synthesis control and structural determination of AgBiS 2 NC formation allow the demonstration of high-performance photodetector devices.

title: Liquid-crystal-imprinted synthesis of chiral polypyrroles without a chiral centre using a two-step method of spark-discharge oligomerisation-electrochemical polymerisation
abstract: Pyrrole in a cholesteric liquid crystal was discharged using a Tesla coil to generate pyrrole radicals, affording linear-shaped nano-ordered pyrrole oligomers.

title: Ultrasensitive multiplex detection of lung cancer EV-associated immune checkpoints using a mesoporous gold enhanced SERS biosensor
abstract: A mesoporous gold–integrated SERS biosensing platform enables multiplexed, ultra-sensitive, and minimally-invasive profiling of immune checkpoint proteins (PD-L1, B7H4, CD276, and CD80) on lung cancer-derived extracellular vesicles.

title: An advanced plasmonic bimetallic nanostar composite for ultra-sensitive SERS detection of crystal violet
abstract: This study presents the synthesis and characterization of Ag/Au nanostar alloys. A 75:25 Ag/Au nanostar alloy achieved the highest SERS sensitivity for rhodamine 6G detection, emphasizing its potential for sensing applications.

title: General synthesis of core–shell FeAlO x nanosphere-based nanoreactors with enhanced catalytic performance
abstract: We report a general solvothermal/calcination strategy for preparing novel core–shell iron–aluminum oxide nanosphere-based nanoreactors. The resulting nanoreactors exhibit superior catalytic efficiency and durability in the organic reactions.

title: Constructing bimetallization and hydroxylation in metal–organic framework for efficient Cr( vi ) photoreduction
abstract: The bimetallization and hydroxylation of Zr-based UiO-66 (Al/Zr-UiO-66-(OH) 2 ) synergistically enhanced the photocatalytic reduction of high concentrations of Cr( vi ) under simulated visible light.

title: Interfacial excitons across dimensional boundaries: a mixed-dimensional SnS/BNNT heterostructure
abstract: Anisotropic behavior of interfacial excitons in mixed-dimensional SnS/BNNT heterostructure.

title: Recent progress on transition metal dichalcogenide-based composites for cancer therapy
abstract: In this review, we present the recent progress on transition metal dichalcogenide-based composites, including their typical categories, key fabrication strategies, and transformative roles in the field of cancer therapy.

title: An amino acid-fortified in situ encapsulation strategy for constructing highly active enzyme@HOF biocomposites
abstract: The water solubility challenge of hydrophobic carboxylate HOF linkers is addressed by a His-promoted dissolution strategy, enabling the construction of high-performance enzyme@HOF biocomposites under entirely aqueous and biocompatible conditions.

title: Multifunctional iron–cobalt heterostructure (FeCoHS) electrocatalysts: accelerating sustainable hydrogen generation through efficient water electrolysis and urea oxidation
abstract: Using an ideal synthetic strategy, we constructed an outstanding multifunctional electrocatalyst for UOR, OER, HER, and seawater splitting.

title: A high sensitivity prostate-specific antigen SERS detection platform based on laser resonance nanoparticles
abstract: An immunoassay based on SERS was established using antibody magnetic beads and SERS nanotags. This method has the advantages of simple operation, high sensitivity, fast (10 minutes) detection, low cost and portability.

title: Inclusion of gold ions in tiara-like nickel hexanuclear nanoclusters
abstract: The inclusion properties of various metal ions in Ni 6 (PET) 12 were investigated and it was found that it selectively includes gold ions.

title: A comparative study of bulk and surface W-doped high-Ni cathode materials for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This study examines tungsten (W) doping in NCM811 cathodes via bulk (co-precipitation) and surface (solid-state) methods. W doping enhances charge transfer and stabilizes structure, reducing capacity fade (retain 92% capacity after 500 cycles).

title: Enhanced solar harvesting efficiency in nanostructured MXene monolayers based on scandium and yttrium
abstract: A high-throughput screening of 22 Sc- and Y-based M 2 CT 2 MXenes using a FAIR-compliant automated workflow reveals eight semiconductors with strong excitonic effects and solar energy conversion efficiency of up to 33%.

title: Preparation of sandwich-structured V 6 O 13 via direct current magnetron sputtering for high-capacity zinc-ion batteries
abstract: In this study, sandwich-structured vanadium oxide (V 6 O 13 ) was prepared and employed as the cathode for zinc-ion batteries (ZIBs). It achieved a specific capacity of 550 mA h g −1 at 100 mA g −1 , with a coulombic efficiency maintained at 100%.

title: NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) and its functional nanomaterials – a versatile platform in the photocatalytic arena
abstract: Versatile photocatalytic applications of NH 2 -MIL-125(Ti) and its functional nanomaterials.

title: Challenges and future prospects of the 2D material-based composites for microwave absorption
abstract: 2D materials, including graphene, boron nitride (BN), and MXene, have attracted wide attention in microwave absorption due to their unique lamellar structure, large specific surface area, low density, and good thermal and chemical stability.

title: Advanced metal oxide catalysts for propane dehydrogenation: from design strategy to dehydrogenation performance
abstract: This review summarizes the design strategy of metal oxide catalysts for PDH, including the suitable supports and the modulated active sites of catalysts. The roles of reaction conditions and advanced characterizations are highlighted.

title: Hydrogen bubble evolution and its induced mass transfer on zinc electrodes in alkaline and neutral media
abstract: Hydrogen bubble evolution presents both challenges and opportunities in Zn-based batteries. While bubble evolution induces electrode instability and dendrite formation, it can also optimize mass transfer through controlled fluid dynamics.

title: Carbon dots: translating versatile physicochemistry into multidisciplinary application frameworks
abstract: This article systematically reviews the physical and chemical properties of carbon dots, especially optical, thermal, electrical, and interfacial features, and their applications in the field of biomedical engineering, energy and environment, etc .

title: Optical anisotropy of Bi 2 SeO 5 and near-field characterization of its waveguide modes
abstract: Characterization of optical anisotropic van der Waals material Bi 2 SeO 5 using photoemission microscopy.

title: Photogating-enhanced photodetection in substrate-assisted borophene–graphene hybrids
abstract: We demonstrate substrate-dependent borophene–graphene photodetectors, where borophene acts as an absorber and graphene as a transporter, revealing tunable photogating and photoconduction effects for broadband detection.

title: Biodegradable PBAT@CoFe 2 O 4 foils as magnetically active photothermal materials for smart surface heating
abstract: Our study is devoted to the development, physicochemical characterization, and NIR energy conversion to heat of biodegradable PBAT@CoFe 2 O 4 composite foil fabricated via the solvent evaporation casting technique.

title: Computational design of donor–acceptor stacked polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons as photocatalytic sites in carbon dots
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) are complex carbon-based nanomaterials with exceptional photoluminescence characteristics and great promise for sustainable metal-free photocatalysis.

title: Impact of chirality on nanotube fracture strain: comprehensive machine learning potential calculations
abstract: We present a comprehensive study of fracture strains in carbon nanotubes (CNTs) as a function of their chirality using the direct integration of the exchange potential (DIEP) machine learning potential model. Generated with AI.

title: Precursor concentration-driven structural evolution and phosphate distribution in electrospun zinc phosphate–carbon nanofibers for lithium-ion storage
abstract: Precursor concentration directs phase distribution and structural evolution in zinc phosphate–carbon nanofibers, enabling tunable electrochemical properties for lithium storage.

title: Modulation of photoacoustic NIR BODIPY self-assembly in theranostic solid lipid nanoparticles via alpha-group variation: influence on spectral and nanostructural properties
abstract: Links between spectral transformation and nanostructure of solid lipid nanoparticles enable informed choice of photoacoustic label for these theranostic particles.

title: Ru doping induces phase transition and in-plane S-site electronic modulation in ternary MoS 2 heterostructures to enhance hydrogen evolution in water/seawater
abstract: Optimizing the performance of in-plane S atoms of MoS 2 is crucial to extend the breadth of application of MoS 2 .

title: Changing the amyloid nucleation process using small molecules and substrates: a way to build two-dimensional materials
abstract: The assembly of two-dimensional (2D) peptide materials on substrates with the control of FG small molecules

title: Spectral accessibility tuning through perovskite quantum dots for up-/down-converting photovoltaic applications: a review
abstract: Schematic of tuned spectral accessibility through perovskite quantum dots (PQDs) for up-conversion (UC) and down-conversion (DC) in perovskite solar cells. The graphical abstract was created, in part, with VESTA.

title: Electronic and geometric effects in an Au@NiO core–shell nanocatalyst on the oxidative esterification of aldehydes
abstract: Density functional theory and microkinetic modeling show that an Au@NiO core–shell nanostructure enhances the oxidative esterification of aldehydes by electron transfer from NiO to Au, stabilizing intermediates and lowering activation energies.

title: Portfolio of colloidally stable gold–gold sulfide nanoparticles and their use in broad-band photoacoustic imaging
abstract: Growing interest in non-invasive photoacoustic imaging is driving the demand for non-toxic, stable, optical absorbers, particularly those operating in the visible and near-infrared regions to enable deep tissue penetration

title: Tunable magnetic and electronic properties of CrS 2 /VS 2 lateral superlattices
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) lateral heterostructures and superlattices, especially those based on transition metal dichalcogenides, boast exceptional properties for electronics, optoelectronics, and photovoltaics.

title: Nano-biohybrid systems for the targeted delivery of chemotherapeutics
abstract: We propose a biohybrid drug delivery system, through the fusion of cellular membranes and synthetic lipids. This doxorubicin-loaded biohybrid displays superior in vivo anticancer effects compared to drug-loaded liposomes and the free drug.

title: Investigation on temperature-dependent optoelectronic properties of MoSe 2 nanosheet/Si heterojunction photodetectors
abstract: MoSe 2 nanosheet/Si heterojunction photodetectors were fabricated by a mechanical exfoliation method, and their electrical and optical properties at different temperatures were investigated.

title: DFT insights into the role of A elements on the phase stability, crystal structure and properties of recently discovered M 3 AC 2 and Sc 2 AC
abstract: DFT reveals how A-element valence electrons tune M–A bonds in M 3 AC 2 /Sc 2 AC (M = Zr, Hf; A = In, Pb, Cd, Sb/Al, Ga, In), governing elasticity, exfoliation energy, and tensile strength to guide nanoMAX phase design for multifunctional applications.

title: Polyoxazoline functionalized magnetic spinel iron oxide nanoparticles for efficient removal of pharmaceuticals and heavy metal ions from water
abstract: Novel hybrid Fe 3 O 4 superparamagnetic nanoparticles with polyoxazoline coatings enable the selective adsorption of pollutants and heavy metals, offering a refined and versatile solution for cutting-edge water purification. Graphical abstract background via Canva.com.

title: Broadband and polarization-independent complex amplitude modulation using a single layer dielectric metasurface
abstract: Full space complex amplitude modulation using dual meta-atom interference, with a broadband response and polarization-independent features, enable near-field and far-field mixed displays under white light illumination.

title: NANO 2024 – nanostructured materials for energy, bio, photonics, and electronics applications
abstract: Introducing the themed collection connected with NANO 2024, which was held in November 2024 at Khalifa University, Abu Dhabi, UAE.

title: Cytochrome c adsorption on carboxylated surfaces: charge regulation and protein orientation modulated by surface curvature
abstract: Free-energy calculations reveal how curvature and charge regulation control cytochrome c adsorption on carboxylated surfaces, modulating distance and orientation with implications for electron transfer at nanoparticle interfaces.

title: Structure, properties and applications of HfO 2 -based piezoelectric films
abstract: The Hf 1− x A x O 2 films demonstrate strong piezoelectricity, exhibiting either positive or negative d 33 values. Consequently, they are utilized in novel MEMS/NEMS devices, including actuators, resonators, and sensors.

title: Engineering hydrophobic and electrostatic interactions for selective inactivation of bacteriophages by mixed-ligand nanoparticles
abstract: Bacteriophage contamination poses significant challenges in bacteria-based industries, disrupting processes that rely on bacterial metabolism, such as insulin production using Escherichia coli . The graphical abstract was created in BioRender. Raza, S. (2025) https://BioRender.com/n70n617.

title: Stimulus-responsive nanomaterials for ocular antimicrobial therapy
abstract: This review highlights recent stimulus-responsive antimicrobial nanotherapeutics for ocular infections, focusing on their design, progress, and advantages/limitations.

title: Covalently bonded graphene oxide–carbon nanotube hybrid nanofillers for achieving high-performance polyamide 6 composites with superior mechanical properties and thermal conductivity
abstract: High-performance PA6 composites are achieved through strong nanofiller–nanofiller and nanofiller–matrix interfaces.

title: Facile synthesis of magnetic core–shell structures for tunable microwave absorption
abstract: SnCo/C@MoS 2 with core-shell structure had the RL min of −64.27 dB, the EAB of 5.20 GHz and the RCS value below −20 dBm 2 , exhibiting remarkable microwave absorption properties.

title: Aptamer-based biotherapeutic conjugate for shear responsive release of Von Willebrand factor A1 domain
abstract: (A). Optical tweezers characterized the mechanical properties of the Single-MOlecule-based material REsponsive to Shear (SMORES). (B) Flow experiments demonstrated the release of therapeutic cargo at a threshold flow rate.

title: Expression of concern: Heat-based transdermal delivery of a ramipril loaded cream for treating hypertension
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Heat-based transdermal delivery of a ramipril loaded cream for treating hypertension’ by Anna Voronova et al. , Nanoscale , 2022, 14 , 12247–12256, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2NR02295H.

title: Engineering the morphology of Mn( ii )-chelated hybrid polyion nanocomplexes via tryptophan-directed assembly of double-hydrophilic block-copolymers for enhanced magnetic relaxation
abstract: Self-assembled nanostructures from Tryptophan-modified double-hydrophilic block-copolymers (DHBCs) offer versatile platforms for engineering nanomaterials with tunable morphologies and enhanced magentic resonance (MR) relaxivities.

title: Multi-stimuli responsive nanomaterials assembled from spiropyran-containing peptoids
abstract: Amphiphilic peptoids with a spiropyran group form stimuli-responsive nanosheets and nanotubes via SP-to-MC isomerization, with tunable crystallinity and catalytic activity that mimics carbonic anhydrase enzymes.

title: Laser-assisted electrohydrodynamic printing of sub-microscale 3D conductive features on low-melting-point polymeric substrates
abstract: The laser-assisted EHD printing technique enables damage-free fabrication of highly conductive sub-microscale 3D structures on polymeric substrates by selectively sintering the as-printed gold nanoparticles, boasting diverse applications.

title: Engineering Pb-free relaxor ferroelectric thin films for low voltage energy storage applications
abstract: Pulsed power technologies demand dielectric capacitors that possess a high energy storage density and efficiency at low applied electric fields/voltages.

title: Crystal structure modulation enabling fast charging and stable layered sodium oxide cathodes
abstract: In this work, a fast-charging and highly stable O3-type cathode is designed by adjusting the crystal structure. The contracted transition metal layer and expanded alkali metal layer can stabilize crystal structure and accelerate the Na + diffusion.

title: Oxidation and recharge of reactive structural Fe( ii ) in titanomagnetite (Fe 3− x Ti x O 4 ) nanoparticles
abstract: Mixed-valent iron oxide minerals, e.g. titanomagnetite (Fe 3− x Ti x O 4 ), are an important source of solid-state ferrous iron that affect the speciation and transport of electron accepting contaminants in the subsurface, such as radioactive pertechnetate.

title: A portable and versatile rGO-Co 3 O 4 -Pt nanocomposite-based electrochemical sensor for ex vivo and in vivo cardiac oxidative stress monitoring
abstract: The excessive production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) disrupts redox homeostasis, contributing to the development of cardiovascular diseases.

title: How tailor-made copolymers can control the structure and properties of hybrid nanomaterials: the case of polyionic complexes
abstract: The ion accessibility and environment in colloidal structures with a charged metal-rich core can be easily tuned by adjusting ion coordination. This adjustment enables the fine-tuning of their synthetic or catalytic properties.

title: Super-compressible aramid fiber-reinforced carbon nanotube/polydimethylsiloxane foams with high frequency tunable microwave attenuation and adjustable mechanical properties
abstract: Lightweight yet mechanically robust aramid fiber (AF) reinforced carbon nanotubes (CNT)/polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) foams were developed via an eco-friendly salt template method, showing highly tunable mechanical properties and outstanding microwave attenuation performance.

title: Spacer engineering in nanoparticle–peptide conjugates boosts targeting specificity for tumor-associated antigens
abstract: Orientation and steric hindrance decrease the efficiency for an antibody to attach to its specific receptor. Giving flexibility to a dendronized peptide through a small PEG fragment increases the peptides affinity to cellular receptors.

title: Tuning polysulfide adsorption and catalytic activity via surface functionalization of Nb 2 TiN 2 MXene in Na–S batteries
abstract: In the Na–S system, O-functionalized Nb 2 TiN 2 -based DTM-MXene outperforms S-functionalized counterparts as evidenced by faster reaction kinetics and stronger Na 2 S n binding.

title: Recent advances in 2D MXene-based heterostructures for gas sensing: mechanisms and applications in environmental and biomedical fields
abstract: Schematic representation of MXene based gas sensors: including synthesis, materials design, application and challenges.

title: Strong and reciprocal magneto-phonon effects in a 2D antiferromagnetic semiconductor FePSe 3
abstract: Magnon–phonon coupling and spin-phonon interaction are interplays between phonons and magnetism, offering new approaches to manipulate phonons related to magnetism.

title: Improved performances toward electrochemical carbon dioxide and oxygen reductions by iron-doped stannum nanoparticles
abstract: We have designed a catalyst that can efficiently convert CO 2 into CO through Zn–CO 2 batteries and the electrochemical CO 2 RR, addressing both energy conversion and environmental concerns simultaneously.

title: Blue CdSe/CdS core/crown nanoplatelet light-emitting diodes obtained via a design-of-experiments approach
abstract: The emissive material, consisting of blue-emitting CdSe colloidal nanoplatelets, and the organic charge injection layers were optimized using design of experiments, to produce a solution-processed blue light-emitting diode.

title: Fabrication of pitch-derived hard carbon via bromination-assisted pyrolysis strategy for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The pitch-derived hard carbon anode was fabricated via a bromination modification coupled with a pyrolysis strategy for sodium-ion batteries, exhibiting a high reversible capacity, excellent rate performance and long-term cycling stability.

title: Hydration shell water surrounding citrate-stabilised gold nanoparticles
abstract: The first implementation of Raman solvation shell spectroscopy on nanoparticle solvation shells: the water hydrogen-bonding network is ruptured in the hydration shells around citrate-stabilised gold nanoparticles dispersed in an aqueous medium.

title: Structural mechanisms of cellulose-based nanocomposites mimicking the structure of articular cartilage under uniaxial compression probed by in situ SAXS
abstract: A new method combining frontal ultrafiltration and ultrasound, followed by UV photocrosslinking enabled the fabrication of orthotropic CNC/PEGDA (poly(ethylene glycol) diacrylate) nanocomposites with the structure of articular cartilage.

title: Revolutionizing rheumatoid arthritis treatment with emerging cutaneous drug delivery systems: overcoming the challenges and paving the way forward
abstract: Overview of the ongoing trends in the targeting strategies of drugs via topical and transdermal delivery and the futuristic approaches in the world of drug delivery for the management of rheumatoid arthritis. Images are created with Biorender.com.

title: Joule-thermal synthesis of graphitic carbon-encapsulated Fe/Fe 3 N/FeN catalysts for efficient peroxymonosulfate activation toward quinoline degradation
abstract: A graphitic carbon-encapsulated Fe/Fe 3 N/FeN catalyst was synthesized using a carbon-assisted flash Joule heating method. The catalyst was specifically engineered to activate peroxymonosulfate (PMS) for effective quinoline degradation.

title: Effect of channel patterning precision on the performances of vertical OECTs
abstract: Channel patterning precision in vOECTs enables efficient modulation of key transistor parameters, including drain current, transconductance, transient times, etc .

title: Tuning the optical and charge transfer properties of tetrathiafulvalene-based D–A′–π–A dyes for DSSCs: a DFT and TD-DFT investigation
abstract: This study has explored a series of new tetrathiafulvalene-based dyes designed using the D–A′–π–A framework for dye-sensitized solar cells (DSSCs).

title: Enhancing perovskite solar cell efficiency and stability: a multimodal prediction approach integrating microstructure, composition, and processing technology
abstract: This work presents a comprehensive study on enhancing the efficiency and stability of perovskite solar cells through a multimodal machine learning approach that integrates microstructure, composition, and processing parameters.

title: Low-dimensional In 2 O 3 nanostructures on MgO cubes
abstract: Self-organized and selective stabilization of trigonal-In 2 O 3 on MgO nanocubes.

title: Flexible, wearable mechano-acoustic sensors for body sound monitoring applications
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in flexible, wearable mechano-acoustic sensors for body sound measurement, emphasizing their potential for ambulatory health monitoring. Created in BioRender. Dang, T. (2025) https://BioRender.com/l15d184.

title: BiAgOTe nanosheets with enhanced photoresponse ability: an experimental and computational study
abstract: BAOT nanosheets demonstrate excellent photonic response properties, positioning them as strong contenders for optoelectronic devices and related applications.

title: Unexpected green-light emission and correlating room-temperature diluted magnetism in pristine non-magnetic closed-shell 4d 0 yttria nanowires
abstract: We show unexpected green-light emission and the mechanism (a,b) and the correlating room-temperature diluted magnetism and the mechanism (c,d), in pristine non-magnetic closed-shell 4d 0 yttria nanowires.

title: Unraveling the role of PEGylation in the anti-aggregation stability of lipid nanoparticles
abstract: This study uses molecular dynamics simulations and experiments to show that PEGylation stabilizes lipid nanoparticles against aggregation through polymer-brush mechanisms governed by PEG chain length, grafting density, polarity, and temperature.

title: Ultra-confined plasmons reveal moiré patterns in a twisted bilayer graphene–talc heterostructure
abstract: This work reveals ultra-confined SP 3 modes and the transition from plasmonic waves to moiré patterns in TBG, highlighting solitonic boundaries as reflective channels for plasmonic waves. Image created via Blender Foundation (www.blender.org).

title: Ultrasensitive detection of 2,4-dichlorophenoxyacetic acid by inhibiting alkaline phosphatase immobilized onto a highly porous gold nanocoral electrode
abstract: Herein, we describe the design and implementation of an ultrasensitive enzyme inhibition-based biosensor for 2,4-dichlorophenoxyacetic acid (2,4-D) detection.

title: Crystalline polymer nanowires originating from solution-state aggregation for high-performance field-effect transistors
abstract: A straightforward and effective method for preparing crystalline conjugated polymer nanowires has been developed based on the solution-state aggregation behavior, yielding enhanced charge-carrier mobility in devices.

title: In situ QXAFS study of CO and H 2 adsorption on Pt in [PtAu 8 (PPh 3 ) 8 ]-H[PMo 12 O 40 ] solid
abstract: H 2 and CO adsorption/desorption properties of [PtAu 8 (PPh 3 ) 8 ]-H[PMo 12 O 40 ] were studied and H 2 was faster adsorbed on [PtAu 8 (PPh 3 ) 8 ] than through narrow channels than CO which involved [PtAu 8 (PPh 3 ) 8 ] structural isomerization.

title: Cell specific mitochondria targeted metabolic alteration for precision medicine
abstract: Mitochondria undergo significant alterations in cancer. Thus, in this study we demonstrate the utility of a dual-targeted polymeric nanoparticle to specifically target the mitochondria of cancer over normal cells for targeted metabolic alteration.

title: Engineering gold nanosheets into bugle-like assemblies for plasmon-enhanced near-infrared oncology treatment
abstract: Low-symmetry, bugle-like gold nanocrystals fabricated by rolling Au nanosheets exhibit strong near-infrared absorption and outstanding photothermal therapeutic efficacy under 808 nm laser irradiation.

title: Two-dimensional chromium telluride-coated 3D-printed architectures for energy harvesting
abstract: We demonstrate a flexible self-powered energy harvesting system using a 2D material-coated 3D-printed structure. This device efficiently harvests mechanical, thermal and magnetic energy to power microelectronics.

title: Study of the biomolecular corona of surface-modified polystyrene and silica nanoparticles: application to cancer
abstract: Surface-tailored nanoparticles capture distinct biomolecular corona patterns, enabling proof-of-concept identification of cancer-specific molecular signatures.

title: Direct and solvent-free patterning of flexible electrics through all-solid laser printing
abstract: All-solid-state laser printing (ASLP) deposits charged solid powders onto flexible substrates via solvent-free electrostatic attraction, enabling scalable fabrication of high-performance optoelectronic devices.

title: Heparanase-responsive nanomaterial for anaplastic thyroid cancer chemotherapy
abstract: A schematic illustration of a heparanase-responsive nanomaterial for anaplastic thyroid cancer chemotherapy.

title: Tunable DNA binding on graphene oxide via polyethyleneimine topology and density: mechanistic insights for nonviral gene delivery
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations reveal how PEI topology and grafting density on graphene oxide control DNA binding. Non-monotonic coverage effects and branched vs . linear PEI differences provide design principles for nonviral gene delivery.

title: Tunable self-assembly hierarchical superstructures of benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxamide-containing fullerene block molecules: from flower to urchin and rod-like morphologies
