abstract: Based on π–π interactions and threefold hydrogen bonds, fullerene-BTA block molecules can self-assemble into diverse hierarchical superstructures with flower, urchin, or rod-like morphologies.

title: Decoding in-plane orientation in cellulose nanopapers hybridized with tailored polymeric nanoparticles
abstract: Biobased cellulose nanofibrils (CNFs) constitute important building blocks for biomimetic, nanostructured materials, and considerable potential exists in their hybridization with tailorable polymeric nanoparticles.

title: Synergistic effects of charge transfer, energy transfer and cavity interference on the exciton emission in WS 2 /hBN/WS 2 heterostructures
abstract: In the WS 2 /hBN/WS 2 heterostructure, the excitonic emission is collectively governed by charge transfer, energy transfer and cavity interference effects.

title: Self-healing and highly adhesive conductive polydimethylsiloxane-based elastomers for chronic epilepsy monitoring
abstract: A self-healing and highly adhesive conductive electrode was fabricated using crosslinked block PDMS-based elastomer–doped CNTs for chronic epilepsy monitoring.

title: Inspiration of plant-related adhesion for plant wearable sensor interface design
abstract: This review presents a systematic framework outlining the construction strategies for adhesion layers in plant wearable devices, drawing inspiration from plant-based adhesion interfaces.

title: Low temperature multimode atomic force microscopy using an active MEMS cantilever
abstract: Low temperature atomic force microscopy is routinely performed using qPlus sensors. Here, we demonstrate that a piezoelectric MEMS cantilever can achieve higher contrast AFM frequency shift images at high imaging speeds compared to qPlus sensors.

title: Magnetically targeted delivery of probiotics for controlled residence and accumulation in the intestine
abstract: We developed a magnetically targeted delivery strategy to achieve controlled probiotic residence in the intestine based on electrostatic interactions between electropositive magnetic nanoparticles and electronegative probiotics.

title: Influence of excitation pulse duration on the efficiency of upconversion nanoparticle-based FRET
abstract: Laser power and pulse width strongly affect UCNP luminescence lifetimes by shaping the initial population distribution, thus biasing FRET quantification in UCNP-based assays.

title: Superconductivity in o-MAX phases
abstract: We performed a first-principles study to identify new superconductors within the o-MAX phase family M 2 M′AlC. Our investigation resulted in the discovery of 22 stable o-MAX phases, of which 16 are new superconducting variants.

title: Solution-processed negative gauge factor PtSe 2 strain sensors
abstract: We exfoliate PtSe 2 and study its piezoresistive response in a solution-processed network. High aspect ratio flakes (>300) yield conformal junctions and a negative gauge factor (−5.45) even after >1000 strain cycles, ideal for flexible electronics.

title: Efficient synthesis of kilogram-scale high-performance Na 3 V 2 O 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F as a cathode material for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Na 3 V 2 O 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F (NVOPF) has garnered significant attention in the field of sodium-ion battery cathode materials due to its high operating voltage and exceptional cycling stability.

title: In chemico categorization of magnetite-, hydroxyapatite-, and Ag-derived hybrid nanobiomaterials based on the surface oxidative reactivity: implications of doping and coating
abstract: Dopants and coatings regulate the redox profile of magnetite-, Ag-, and HA-hybrid NBMs. Created in BioRender. Alcolea-Rodriguez, V. (2025) https://BioRender.com/fm1uwvb.

title: Radiopaque poly(5-acrylamido-2,4,6-triiodoisophthalic acid)-based copolymers as theranostic carriers for image-guided drug delivery
abstract: Stimuli-responsive PNIPAM–PAATIPA copolymers integrate CT contrast and cationic drug delivery, offering a cost-effective platform for personalized theranostics. The graphical abstract was created in BioRender. Delaey, J. (2025) https://BioRender.com/lxkfyyj.

title: Correction: Passive nanorheological tool to characterise hydrogels
abstract: Correction for ‘Passive nanorheological tool to characterise hydrogels’ by Moira Lorenzo Lopez et al. , Nanoscale , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d5nr00875a.

title: Correction: Bridging colloidal and electrochemical syntheses of metal nanocrystals with seeded electrodeposition for tracking single nanocrystal growth
abstract: Correction for ‘Bridging colloidal and electrochemical syntheses of metal nanocrystals with seeded electrodeposition for tracking single nanocrystal growth’ by Ekta Verma et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 8002–8012, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR00202D.

title: Disclosing the contribution of vacancy defects to thermal transport at a liquid-Al/graphene adhesion interface
abstract: The intrinsic mechanism of adhesion force evolution and energy heat transport under different vacancy defects and temperature at the liquid-Al/graphene interface is revealed through interfacial thermal conductance and phonon participation rate.

title: A hydrophilic Ni 3 S 2 –MoN heterostructure on Ni foam (Ni 3 S 2 –MoN/NF) as an electrocatalyst for enhanced hydrogen evolution in alkaline media
abstract: A Ni 3 S 2 –MoN heterostructure is synthesized on Ni foam (Ni 3 S 2 –MoN/NF) via one-step annealing. The Ni 3 S 2 –MoN/NF shows excellent HER performance due to optimized HBE from Ni 3 S 2 bond elongation, a hydrophilic surface, and Ni(OH) 2 formation.

title: Recent advances in the synthesis and passivation of pure bromide-based perovskite nanoplatelets
abstract: The peculiar properties of low-dimensional inorganic lead halide perovskite materials have triggered the attention of researchers in the past decade. Variation of bandgap energy with the number of layers of the perovskite nanoplatelet.

title: SERS-based lateral flow immunoassay utilising plasmonic nanoparticle clusters for ultra-sensitive detection of salivary cortisol
abstract: We report the first SERS-based LFIA for salivary cortisol, achieving 0.014 pg mL −1 sensitivity—650× better than conventional LFIAs— via size-controlled Au clusters and dual-line Raman mapping, with strong correlation to UPLC-MS/MS.

title: Impact of different 2D materials on the efficacy of photothermal and photodynamic therapy in 3D-bioprinted breast cancer
abstract: The convergence of nanotechnology and tissue engineering has paved the way for innovative cancer treatments that leverage the unique light absorption properties of nanomaterials.

title: Co-doped perovskite nanocrystals for multiplexed anticounterfeiting applications
abstract: Transition and lanthanide metal ion doping in colloidal perovskite nanocrystals results in efficient visible to near-infrared emission. Created, in part, with BioRender (www.biorender.com).

title: Accelerated galvanic interaction for the fabrication of core–shell nanowires to boost the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The synthesis of core–shell Ru@Ag/AgCl catalysts via accelerating galvanostatic interactions in cathodes for dramatically boosting HER activity.

title: Magnetic alignment of carbon nanotubes in polymers for enhanced thermal conductivity
abstract: Preparation of thermally conductive materials with low filler contents using a magnetic field.

title: Recent advancements in the precise engineering of block copolymer microparticles via confined assembly in emulsion droplets
abstract: This review examines key parameters for preparing block copolymer microparticles via 3D emulsion-confined assembly, enabling the design of functional polymeric particles with integrated properties for diverse applications.

title: Atomic engineering of copper sites for efficient photocatalysis
abstract: This review summarizes advances in the design, synthesis, and application of Cu SACs in photocatalysis. Special emphasis is placed on structure–activity relationships and the underlying mechanisms governing charge dynamics and surface reactions.

title: Microneedles: multifunctional devices for drug delivery, body fluid extraction, and bio-sensing
abstract: Used in drug delivery, body-fluid extraction, and bio-sensing, microneedle-based devices provide a novel perspective to overcome the traditional limits of biomedical engineering.

title: Synthesis of high-entropy alloys for electrocatalysis
abstract: This review explores recent advances in high-entropy alloy (HEA) synthesis for electrochemical applications, covering methods, performance, properties, and the need for further fundamental research on HEA formation mechanisms.

title: Layered ion dynamics and enhanced energy storage: VS 2 /MXene heterostructure anodes revolutionizing Li-ion batteries
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) material-based anodes are pivotal for advancing next-generation ion batteries, showing remarkable ion loading capacity and mobility.

title: A review on polyimide-based structures for regulating the electromagnetic absorption and reflection
abstract: Summary of recent progress of polyimide-based composite materials for EMWs shielding and stealth. The graphical abstract was reproduced with permission (see main article for details).

title: Bioactive surface-functionalized MXenes for biomedicine
abstract: This work reviews the surface modification strategies and biomedical applications of MXene, discusses the challenges, trends and prospects of MXenes nanomaterials, which provides information for enhancing the clinical translation of nanomedicine.

title: Metal–organic frameworks for NH 3 adsorption and separation
abstract: MOFs are promising NH 3 adsorbent candidates due to their highly tunable structure and functionality, which can precisely match the characteristics required for NH 3 adsorbents in multiple application scenarios.

title: Environmental applications and performance engineering of COF membranes prepared by interfacial polymerization
abstract: Advancements in COF membranes via interfacial polymerization: a comprehensive review on synthesis, environmental applications, and future directions.

title: Versatile role of oleylamine in the controlled synthesis of copper nanoparticles with diverse morphologies
abstract: The versatile role of oleylamine (OLA) in stabilizing copper nanoparticles through its alkene or amine functional groups, depending on the crystalline facets, is highlighted by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy.

title: High-entropy oxide nanostructures for rapid and sustainable nitrophenol reduction
abstract: A simple combustion route yields low-cost HEO catalyst whose entropy-driven structure enables efficient nitrophenol reduction, offering a scalable platform for sustainable amine production.

title: Engineering the next generation of MXenes: challenges and strategies for scalable production and enhanced performance
abstract: Optimization strategy of MXene scale preparation.

title: A fluorenone-based conjugated polymer nanoparticle applied in drug delivery with real-time detection (color change) via the AILE mechanism
abstract: A novel pH-sensitive AILE luminescence mechanism nanoparticle.

title: Solid-state synthesis of Si 1− x Ge x nanoalloys with composition-tunable energy gaps and visible to near infrared optical properties
abstract: Solid-state synthesis of homogeneous Si1− x Ge x quantum dots with composition-tunable energy gaps and visible to near infrared absorption and emission properties.

title: One-pot synthesis of long-range aligned nanochannels for Li-ion transfer pathways
abstract: Long-range aligned nanochannels are synthesized and utilized as Li + ion pathways in solid polymer electrolytes. Cyano groups distributed within nanochannels accelerate Li + migration, leading to superior ionic conductivity.

title: Rationalizing high-entropy catalytic self-Fenton pollutant degradation from an effective porous piezoelectric composite film
abstract: Composite films with piezo-Fenton capabilities were developed by doping heterogeneous Fenton materials into the MgO@rGO/PVDF-HFP matrix. The impact factors of pollutant degradation efficiency were studied in application-mimetic scenarios.

title: Determination of the carrier temperature in weakly confined semiconductor nanocrystals using time-resolved optical spectroscopy
abstract: Using ultrafast PL is more suitable than TA for determining carrier temperatures of colloidal nanocrystals.

title: Fabrication of a symmetric supercapacitor device using MnO 2 /cellulose nanocrystals/graphite electrodes via sputtering for energy storage
abstract: This work focuses on MnO 2 /CNCs/graphite hybrid electrodes with high capacitance and excellent cyclic retention for energy storage. MnO 2 was deposited via RF magnetron sputtering to prepare the robust device.

title: Surface-enhanced electrochemiluminescence with mesoporous gold: understanding the electrochemical and optical effects
abstract: This work demonstrates mesoporous gold enabled surface-enhanced electrochemiluminescence. It is shown that an enhancement ratio of over 80 can be achieved, which is a combination of optical and electrochemical effects.

title: Nanohoop anchored plasmonic surfaces for polarity-dependent ultrasensitive sensing
abstract: A superhydrophobic thiophene-triazole nanohoop moiety is developed as a high-performance SERS substrate. The thiol-functionalized porous substrate anchors plasmonic Ag nanoparticles, enabling sensitive detection of di-butyl phthalate in edible oils.

title: Fabrication of an In 2 O 3 NP-based high-performance low-operating voltage phototransistor and tuning of its photosensitivity from UV to blue region
abstract: In this work, a visible-blind low-operating voltage phototransistor was fabricated using colloidal In 2 O 3 via a solution process technique, and its photosensitivity was tuned to the blue region by adding a PbI 2 layer to the channel.

title: Recent advances in pH-sensitive clay nanomaterials for enhanced immunotherapy of solid tumors
abstract: Restoring physicochemical homeostasis in tumor tissues with functional clay nanomaterials can convert the ‘cold’ immune microenvironment into a ‘hot’ one for enhanced immunotherapy of solid tumors.

title: Efficient adsorption of xylenol orange by reusable carbon quantum dots/cross-linked chitosan composites
abstract: The adsorption capacity of CQDs/CCS-2 for xylenol orange in aqueous solution is 173.8 mg g −1 . After four cycles of adsorption experiments, it still maintains a high removal effect, indicating that it is an efficient and renewable adsorption material.

title: Tuning photophysical properties of semiconducting polymer nanoparticles for improved photocatalytic activity
abstract: Fundamental energy decay processes following excitation by photons, favouring relevant charge transfer processes that are key for the creation of reactive oxygen species, and therefore, to the photocatalytic behaviour.

title: Nitrogen-doped nanocarbon as a metal-free catalyst for CO 2 hydroboration
abstract: The unprecedented catalytic performance of a metal-free N-doped nanocarbon catalyst in CO 2 hydroboration is reported with the catalytic mechanism uncovered.

title: Solvothermal aldol condensation of primary alcohols for the controlled formation of carbonized polymer dots with tunable optical properties and selective sensing
abstract: Solvothermal oxidation–aldol condensation of primary alcohols enables the controlled formation of carbonized polymer dots with chain-length-tunable optical properties and high sensitivity and selectivity toward Fe 3+ and p -nitrophenol.

title: Detection of post-translational modification in a peptide with single-amino acid resolution using a graphyne nanopore: findings from molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations reveal that the ionic current signal from a graphyne nanopore allows for the detection of post-translational modifications in polypeptide chains with single-amino acid resolution.

title: Stimulated Raman scattering imaging of atomically thin layers and a strained nanotent of hexagonal boron nitride
abstract: We demonstrate vibrational imaging with stimulated Raman scattering microscopy for atomically thin layers and a strained nanostructure in 2D hexagonal boron nitride.

title: Unveiling quantum phases in two-dimensional materials with optical quasiparticle probes
abstract: We establish a unified framework showing how optical spectroscopies reveal the intricate coupling between quasiparticles, dimensionality, and interations in two-dimensional materials.

title: Spectroscopic evidence of intra-unit-cell charge redistribution in a charge-neutral magnetic topological insulator
abstract: Intra-unit-cell charge imbalance in the charge-neutral Sb-doped MnBi 6 Te 10 crystal leads to a nanoscale p–n junction, resulting in the multiple surface photovoltage effects at the boundaries of crystal domains in time-resolved ARPES measurements.

title: Rigid organic molecule pillared Ti 3 C 2 towards high rate capability and fast sodium ion storage
abstract: 3,3′,4,4′-Biphenyltetracarboxylic acid molecules are pillared into Ti 3 C 2 interlayers through a dehydration condensation reaction, achieving high Na + diffusion kinetics and cycling stability.

title: A graphene oxide/niobium carbide MXene composite-based functional nanocomposite scaffold for artificial corneas
abstract: A bioactive GO/Nb 2 C nanocomposite scaffold for artificial corneas shows excellent mechanical stability, anti - inflammatory & antioxidant activities, validated in vitro and in vivo, offering a novel strategy for functional corneal tissue engineering.

title: Highly stable rare earth YS 2 and ScS 2 monolayers for potassium-ion batteries: first-principles calculations
abstract: The electrochemical K intercalation mechanism for the MS 2 monolayers was proposed via DFT calculations. Rare earth ScS 2 and YS 2 monolayers with high theoretical capacities were identified as the most promising intercalation candidates for KIB anodes.

title: Conformality limits of 2D WS 2 on 3D nanostructures
abstract: A critical radius of curvature of 4 nm for conformal deposition of WS 2 on 3D nanostructures was identified, offering design guidelines for integrating 2D transition metal dichalcogenides in advanced nanoelectronic and catalytic applications.

title: Targeted delivery of cytotoxic proteins via lipid-based nanoparticles to primary Langerhans cells
abstract: Using microfluidics, lipid nanoparticles encapsulating cytotoxic proteins were utilized for targeted delivery to Langerhans cells via human Langerin. Following endocytosis, proteins are released via endosomal escape, inducing cell apoptosis only in the targeted cells.

title: A high-efficiency and long-cycling aqueous indium metal battery enabled by synergistic In 3+ /K + interactions
abstract: The synergistic K + /In 3+ interactions lead to a high-performance aqueous indium metal battery with high efficiency and long cycling life.

title: Highly selective CO 2 electroreduction in an exsolution-induced flow cell using a hierarchical monolithic nano-Ag foam electrode
abstract: We developed a monolithic nano-Ag foam electrode with well-developed pores and a hierarchical nanostructure for the CO 2 RR in an FTDT cell, achieving a CO faradaic efficiency of 93% and an overall energy efficiency of 51.34% at 200 mA cm −2 .

title: Microwave chemistry and microwave-assisted synthesis of nanomaterials in biomedicine: a focused review
abstract: The review emphasizes on the microwave-assisted synthesis of nanomaterials, while concentrating on its biomedical applications, such as cancer therapy, drug delivery, and tissue engineering.

title: A-site cation substitution in CsPbI 3 perovskite nanoplatelets enables their tunable emission and efficient nonlinear absorption
abstract: A-site cation substitution in CsPbI 3 perovskite nanoplatelets enables tuning of the photoluminescence peak position and enhancement of nonlinear optical absorption.

title: From N-type doping to phase transition in large-area MoS 2 via controlled sulfur vacancy formation
abstract: A defect engineering strategy based on controlling sulfur vacancies enables precise tuning of the doping concentration, which systematically transforms the properties of MoS 2 from semiconducting to metallic.

title: Reductant-selected formation of copper nanoclusters with crystallization-induced emission enhancement performance
abstract: This work reported the reductant-selected formation of structurally comparable Cu nanoclusters with different photoluminescence behaviours in the crystalline state.

title: Improving the long-term stability of new-generation perovskite-based TCOs using binary and ternary oxides capping layers
abstract: Stability enhancement of indium-free transparent conducting oxide SrVO 3 using binary and ternary oxide capping layers.

title: Au–Ag controllable composition nanoalloying of hexagonal nanoplates: heterogeneous interfacial nanogaps enhance near-field focusing
abstract: In this study, we present a novel strategy for fabricating binary-array surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) substrates composed of gold (Au) and silver (Ag) plasmonic hexagonal nanoplates (h-NPLs), functioning as a “nanoalloy” system.

title: Self-assembled inorganic nanomaterials for biomedical applications
abstract: Self-assembly methods are presented for inorganic nanomaterials used in bioapplications. The most promising in vitro and in vivo results are reported. A discussion on safety and biocompatibility issues, future challenges and prospects is performed.

title: Mn( ii )-MOF nanoparticles conjugated with EOB-PEG as high-performance hepatobiliary-specific MRI contrast agents
abstract: Hepatobiliary magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a crucial diagnostic tool for early detection and staging of liver tumors.

title: A visible light enhanced Ti 3 C 2 T x /silicon nanowire array heterojunction broadband photodetector based on leaky mode resonance
abstract: A visible-light-enhanced Ti 3 C 2 T x /Si NW heterojunction broadband photodetector is constructed by employing a small diameter silicon nanowire array. This unique photoresponse is linked to leaky mode resonances in small-diameter Si NWs.

title: High-performance metal oxide nanoparticle materials synthesised using polar aprotic solvents for advanced supercapacitor applications
abstract: A surfactant free and scalable methodology for the synthesis of nickel hydroxide and nickel oxide nanoparticles is reported upon and evaluated for their potential for electrochemical battery-type supercapacitor applications.

title: Holey etching strategy of siloxene nanosheets to improve the rate performance of photo-assisted Li–O 2 batteries
abstract: P-siloxene nanosheets used as cathode catalysts for photo-assisted Li–O 2 batteries significantly improve the electrochemical performance of the devices.

title: Basic features of Pt/C catalysts synthesised via the non-isothermal decomposition of platinum acetylacetonate
abstract: Non-isothermal decomposition of platinum acetylacetonate as a powerful approach for determining the nanoparticle size effects in Pt/C catalysts.

title: Wafer-scale integration of monolayer MoS 2 via residue-free support layer etching and angular strain suppression
abstract: Using a novel PMMA/Bi-assisted debonding method, a crackless and residue-free transfer of 2-inch MoS 2 film with a 95% yield is achieved. This scalable transfer technique will facilitate the integration of MoS 2 into ultra-scaled nanoelectronics.

title: P-type doping in edge-enriched MoS 2− x nanostructures via RF-generated nitrogen plasma
abstract: Use of plasma-based magnetron sputtering for the synthesis of vertically aligned edge-enriched 2D MoS 2 nanostructures and the conversion of n-type to p-type 2D MoS 2 by the modulation of Fermi level using RF-generated N 2 plasma.

title: Advancements in DNA-PAINT: applications and challenges in biological imaging and nanoscale metrology
abstract: DNA-PAINT applications are guiding new advancements in a virtuous cycle that benefits bioimaging and nanometrology.

title: Nanoscale defect formation in fcc Ni and low-Fe content Ni x Fe 1− x single crystals induced by self-ion irradiation
abstract: Fcc Ni x Fe 1− x single crystal alloys serve as model systems to study defect evolution under self-ion irradiation at room temperature across fluences from 4 × 10 13 to 2 × 10 15 cm −2 .

title: Nano hollow carbon sphere buffering design to address volume expansion in micro-silicon anodes of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A low-expansion, integrated silicon anode was designed by nano hollow carbon spheres with certain elastic space as buffering the volume expansion of silicon particles, ensuring the structural integrity and structural stability of the electrode.

title: SnSb as a long cycle life anode material for sodium-ion batteries enabled by a high concentration electrolyte
authors: 'Stephen O'Sullivan', 'Temilade Esther Adegoke', 'Kevin M. Ryan', 'Hugh Geaney', 'Tadhg Kennedy'
abstract: The performance of a SnSb Na-ion battery anode composed of nanolayers of Sn and Sb is reported, wherein the cycle life was significantly enhanced by the use of a high concentration electrolyte.

title: Ligand-induced room-temperature synthesis of NiCoSe nanostructures: a highly efficient electrocatalyst for hydrazine-assisted hydrogen production
abstract: Innovative synthesis protocol for the ligand-induced room temperature synthesis of transition metal selenide nanostructures for electrocatalysis and energy applications. Canva software was used to make the image.

title: Selective and local flash-annealing for improvement in the contact characteristics of MoS 2 transistors
abstract: This work reports a groundbreaking and selective flash annealing process that leverages ultrafast photothermal effects induced by flash lamp irradiation to reduce contact resistance in two-dimensional (2D) transition-metal dichalcogenides.

title: Long-wavelength infrared metamaterial absorber with polarization and angle insensitivity using compact hybrid cylindrical structures
abstract: We demonstrate a compact broadband LWIR absorber with polarization and angle insensitivity. The A spe is 92.7% over 8–14 μm. The absorber is promising in IR signature suppression.

title: Anomalous ionic conduction in ferroelectric semiconductor junctions comprising multistate CuInP 2 S 6
abstract: Distinctive dual and quadruple polarization states of the ferroelectric semiconducting junctions give rise to distinct memristive and rectifying electronic behaviors, respectively.

title: The cellular response of the bronchial epithelium shapes the protein corona of inhaled nanoparticles
abstract: The stress response of the human bronchial epithelium reshuffles the protein corona of inhaled nanoparticles that forms in the lung mucus during exposure.

title: Controllable synthesis of environmentally stable vdW antiferromagnetic oxyhalide CrOCl
abstract: This work demonstrates APCVD synthesis of stable 2D CrOCl, a magnetic oxyhalide for spintronics and quantum devices. It reveals controllable gas-phase growth, with characterization confirming high-quality CrOCl free from Cr 2 O 3 and oxidation.

title: Fusing fluorescent proteins and ferritin for protein cage based lighting devices
abstract: A novel approach towards bio-hybrid light-emitting diodes (Bio-HLEDs) is developed, by encapsulating a green fluorescent protein within an apoferritin cage. This renders up to 30-fold higher device stability compared to the fluorescent protein alone.

title: Comparative analysis of synthesis techniques for citrate-capped gold nanoparticles: insights into optimized wet-chemical approaches for controlled morphology and stability
abstract: This study evaluates six methods for the synthesis of citratestabilized AuNPs, demonstrating how reagent order, reducing agent identity, and citrate concentration collectively govern particle size, dispersity, and colloidal stability.

title: Impact of high oxygen deficiency on the photoluminescence properties of black zinc oxide thin films
abstract: The presence of oxygen deficiency in ZnO thin films is a key factor for tailoring their optical and electrical properties.

title: Electrochromic thin films of tungsten oxide with high-exposure (002) crystal faces doped and modulated with cerium acetate
abstract: A cerium acetate-doped WO 3 film was prepared, forming Ce–O bonds that increase (002) face exposure, while acetate ions improve structural stability and electrochemical properties, enhancing the electrochromic effect.

title: Mechanistic insights into the enhanced photocatalytic efficiency of MoS 2 -tuned DyFeO 3 heterojunction nanocomposites for pollutant degradation
abstract: DyFeO 3 –MoS 2 heterojunction nanocomposites for efficient MB and LFX degradation under solar irradiation. MoS 2 enhances charge separation via a built-in electric field, reducing recombination and boosting photocatalytic performance.

title: More than a delivery system: the evolving role of lipid-based nanoparticles
abstract: Lipid-based nanoparticles are an important class of drug delivery vehicles. This review explores the use of lipid structures to make functional and targeted nanocarriers. highlighting use of synthetic and natural lipids. BioRender was used in part to generate the graphical abstract.

title: Flexible porous organic polymers with 1,2-diol subunits favoring the high loading of Pd nanoparticles
abstract: The flexible porous polymer with chelating 1,2-diol moiety facilitated the high loading and uniform dispersion of Pd nanoparticles to yield the catalyst Pd@BIT-POP-75-OH, which helped the catalytic reduction of 4-nitrophenol.

title: Advances and perspectives in nanoscale materials and optoelectronics
abstract: Associate Editor Professor Christian Klinke (University of Rostock) introduces this editors choice collection in Nanoscale on advances and perspectives in nanoscale materials and optoelectronics.

title: Revealing the influence of annealing on mid- and long-wave infrared dual-color HgCdTe p–n junctions at the nanometer scale
abstract: The spatial distribution of carriers across the cross-section of mid-long wave dual-band HgCdTe annealed at two different temperatures (410 °C and 380 °C) was obtained at the nanoscale level.

title: Correction: Mitigating substrate effects of van der Waals semiconductors using perfluoropolyether self-assembled monolayers
abstract: Correction for ‘Mitigating substrate effects of van der Waals semiconductors using perfluoropolyether self-assembled monolayers’ by Dae Young Park et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 10779–10788, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4NR00061G.

title: Double reactive oxygen species system photoinduced by Cu 8 NCs: synergistic catalysis of phenylacetylene self-coupling reaction
abstract: Three low-nuclear Cu 8 NCs were designed for photocatalytic phenylacetylene self-coupling. A feasible two-channel ROS participation mechanism has been proposed by free radical scavenging and electron paramagnetic resonance trapping experiments.

title: Upgrading commercial plastics for high-yield production of porous carbon electrode materials
abstract: We employed CuCl 2 as a chemical activator and typical commercial plastics to prepare porous carbon, which achieved high yields exceeding 45%.

title: Optically active chiral photonic crystals exhibiting enhanced fluorescence and circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: The successful creation of optically active photonic crystal films based on integrating system of “AIEgens + chirality” are devoted to enhance fluorescence and circularly polarized luminescence due to slow photon effect and valid chirality-transfer.

title: One stone, two birds: robust and self-absorption free flexible perovskite scintillators by metal–organic framework encapsulation
abstract: The energy transfer strategy in MOFs enables perovskite scintillators to achieve both stability and high efficiency.

title: Mechanism and catalytic activity of the water–gas shift reaction on a single-atom alloy Al 1 /Cu (111) surface
abstract: Al 1 /Cu (111) presents bifunctional active sites and promotes the whole WGSR in cooperativity.

title: Tunable formation of two-dimensional nanostructures and complex crystals via lead( ii ) bromide complexation and inclusion crystallization with polystyrene- block -poly(ethylene oxide) in 1,3,5-trimethylbenzene
abstract: Composition-dependent formation of irregular nanosheets and polygonal nanoplates via inclusion crystallization between polystyrene- block -poly(ethylene oxide) and lead( ii ) bromide in 1,3,5-trimethylbenzene.

title: Controlled synthesis of metal–insulator–metal nanoparticles for enhanced Raman spectroscopy
abstract: A metal–insulator–metal nanoparticle with a gold core, an ultrathin silica layer, and gold satellites delivers strong Raman enhancement without patterned substrates. Optimized probes enable ultrasensitive ciprofloxacin detection down to 1 pg mL −1 .

title: Direct synthesis of three-dimensional Ag nanocrystal superlattices and their superhydrophobic films for a potential surface-enhanced Raman scattering substrate
abstract: A one-step chemical synthesis yields three-dimensional superlattices of Ag nanocrystals. The superlattice films show superhydrophobicity and enhanced Raman signals via an analyte concentration effect.

title: Key factor in enhancing yield and stability in single-chirality carbon nanotubes extraction: solvent viscosity
abstract: This study shows that higher viscosity in low-polar solvents increases the yield of (6,5) SWCNTs. The use of a toluene–tetralin mixed solvent enhanced concentration, stability, deposition rate, and FET performance.

title: Impact of trivalent Sb 3+ -ion doping on charge carrier recombination dynamics of cesium lead bromide perovskite quantum dots
abstract: Sb 3+ -ion doping enhances CsPbBr 3 PQD PL properties by suppressing the formation of trap-inducing structural defects and increasing the exciton binding energy and the tolerance factor.

title: Phase-tailored CoCrFeNiAl nitride for enhanced electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution via cooling-mediated plasma strategy
abstract: Surface structure of the high entropy alloy nitride CoCrFeNiAl is precisely tailored by simply modulating the surface heating effect of plasma nitridation. Created with 3D Max and BIOVIA Materials Studio.

title: Semi-dry transfer of CVD graphene on Si: surface morphology and electronic properties
abstract: We develop a new process named semi-dry transfer, which combines wet etching and dry transfer to obtain graphene with a clean interface with the substrate.

title: Atomistic mechanisms of superlubricity in carbon nanotube heterostructures under linear elastic deformation
abstract: Heterostructures have been introduced to achieve superior performance by assembling low-dimensional van der Waals materials.

title: Adaptive states of carbon nitride semiconductor materials treated with hydrofluoric acid
abstract: Protonation of carbon nitride with HF enables adaptive tuning of its band gap. Three distinct electronic states can be accessed by using HF, heat and pressure as external stimuli driven by a remarkable topotactic behaviour.

title: H 2 O 2 self-supplying nanoparticles for chemodynamic and synergistic photodynamic therapy to augment cGAS/STING activation
abstract: Triple negative breast cancer (TNBC) characterized by easy metastasis and poor prognosis is one of the most intractable malignancies.

title: Time-lapsed nanoscale maps of the elastic modulus of collagen during cross-linking by bimodal AFM
abstract: Cross-linking processes increase the Young’s modulus of collagen nanoribbons by two orders of magnitude.

title: Hybrid approach to reconstruct nanoscale grating dimensions using scattering and fluorescence with soft X-rays
abstract: The hybrid approach of soft X-ray fluorescence scatterometry can reconstruct a nanoscale grating's actual line profile by utilizing a weighting parameter that accounts for the relative sensitivities of the combined complementary techniques.

title: Formation of prenucleation clusters and transformation to ZnSe quantum dots and magic-size clusters
abstract: Prenucleation clusters (PNCs, PC-299) form at the prenucleation stage of ZnSe QDs. PNCs isomerize to MSC-299 in the dispersion of CH–OTA, or fragment to Mos at 220 °C for the nucleation and growth of QDs.

title: Mechanism of polymer removal from semiconducting single-walled carbon nanotubes via rapid thermal processing: insights from in situ environmental transmission electron microscopy
abstract: A two-step (N 2 /O 2 ) rapid thermal process removes polymer residue from sorted carbon nanotubes, improving device performance. In-situ TEM reveals a side-chain-first degradation mechanism, guiding optimization for nanoelectronics.

title: A review on the insights into redox-based regeneration strategies for LiFePO 4 batteries
abstract: This review systematically compiles studies on redox reactions during SLFP regeneration, which are crucial for enhancing the understanding the regeneration of SLFP batteries.

title: Catalytic synergy in palladium-enriched tungsten oxide nanogranules: redefining electrochromic dynamics and energy storage capabilities
abstract: Amorphous palladium-enriched tungsten oxide (Pd-WO 3 ) (WPd) thin films were synthesized through a single-step electrodeposition process, showcasing exceptional electrochromic energy storage performance.

title: A CMOS-integrable ambipolar tellurene nanofilm-based negative differential transconductance transistor for multi-valued logic computing
abstract: A CMOS-compatible NDT transistor using ambipolar Te nanofilms achieves ternary logic with a simple process. Al 2 O 3 -encapsulated Te shows Fermi-level-modulated ambipolarity, enabling stable multi-valued logic for future high-density electronics.

title: Slippery liquid-like surfaces as a promising solution for sustainable drag reduction
abstract: Fluid slip and drag reduction induced by durable slippery liquid-like surfaces (SLLSs) made from flexible polymers.

title: Metal-organic chemical vapour deposition of van der Waals 2D chalcogenides and heterostructures: a review
abstract: This review highlights recent progress in metal-organic chemical vapour deposition of wafer-scale 2D chalcogenides and future guidelines. Key topics include precursor chemistry, growth mechanisms, epitaxy, heterostructures, and doping strategies.

title: Molybdenum in situ etching-treated ultrathin NiFeMo LDHs nanosheet arrays as a performance anodic catalyst for efficient industrial production of hydrogen
abstract: This work presents a novel in situ electrochemical etching strategy to transform nickel–iron foam (NFF) into a high-performance OER catalyst (NiFeMo/NFF) by reconstructing its surface with ultrathin Mo-doped nanosheets.

title: Gold nanoclusters Au 25 AcCys 18 normalize intracellular ROS without increasing cytoplasmic alarmin acHMGB1 abundance in human microglia and neurons
abstract: The gold nanocluster Au25AcCys18 modulates human microglia and neuronal cells at the level of oxidative stress and protein–protein interactions (HMGB1 and TFEB). Complementary computational and biological methods reveal these interactions.

title: Electrocatalytic biomass upgrading coupled with hydrogen evolution and CO 2 reduction
abstract: This review gives a detailed summary on the biomass upgrading coupled with electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution and CO 2 reduction, with critical challenges and perspectives provided to inspire more efforts in pushing forward the future development.

title: Carbon dot–phthalocyanine hybrids: synergistic effects that boost their multifaceted applications
authors: M. Graça P. M. S. Neves
abstract: This review covers recent advances in Pc@CD hybrids, emphasizing synthesis, conjugation strategies, and enhanced properties that drive applications in sensing, electrocatalysis, phototherapy, and photocatalysis.

title: Next-generation electrochemical etching for III-nitride semiconductors: Innovations, applications, and beyond
abstract: III-Nitride semiconductors offer wide bandgaps, thermal stability, and tunable properties, making them ideal for nanostructured devices. This review covers their synthesis, properties, and applications in photodetectors, gas sensors, and beyond.

title: A Pt/Nb 2 O 5 catalyst for oxidative conversion of 1,5-pentanediol into 5-hydroxypentanal and δ-valerolactone under ambient conditions
abstract: Valuable 5-hydroxypentanal and δ-valerolactone can be simultaneously obtained by 1,5-pentanediol oxidation over Pt-loaded Nb 2 O 5 under ambient conditions and this process can be promoted by Lewis acid sites and positively charged Pt in the catalyst.

title: Functionalization of rod-shaped plant viruses for biomedical applications
abstract: This review explores the development of biomedical nanomaterials based on rod-shaped plant viruses, providing insights into the design of innovative biomaterials for applications related to drug delivery, bioimaging and immune-therapy. Created in https://BioRender.com.

title: Research progress on functional solid polymer electrolytes for lithium batteries
abstract: Overview of polymer solid electrolytes based on different functionalities.

title: Ta-doped NiFe layered double hydroxide for efficient alkaline water oxidation at ampere-level current with 2000 h durability
abstract: Ta-doped NiFe LDH delivers outstanding OER performance with low overpotential and >2000 h durability, offering a promising catalyst design strategy for industrial-scale alkaline water electrolysis.

title: Remarkably enhanced luminol chemiluminescence with a manganese porphyrin-based metal–organic framework for the sensitive detection of dopamine
abstract: Manganese porphyrin-based metal–organic frameworks enhance luminol/H 2 O 2 chemiluminescence by over 1200-fold, enabling highly sensitive dopamine detection via their quenching effect.

title: Temperature-dependent structural and morphological engineering of nickel nitride via nitrogen plasma processing for efficient electrocatalysis
abstract: A facile cooling-mediated plasma strategy is applied to simply modulate the exposed facets and morphology of the achieved nickel nitride nano-frameworks for controllable HER and OER behaviors.

title: Recent advances in fundamental research on photon avalanches on the nanometre scale
abstract: Overview of photon avalanche upconversion: history, imaging applications, photonic applications, and challenges for frontier applications. Image created with BioRender.

title: Boosting magnetoresistance in manganese phosphide helimagnets through iron oxide interface strain engineering
authors: Nivarthana W. Y. A. Y. Mudiyanselage
abstract: Helimagnetic materials exhibit complex non-collinear spin structures, making them promising candidates for next-generation magnetoresistive devices.

title: Electrochemical performance and characterization of a supercapacitor device using carbon nanotube/graphene nanoplatelet composites with biomass-derived carbon dots
abstract: This work reports carbon dot-loaded CNT/graphene electrodes for symmetric supercapacitors, showing high capacitance, energy density, and excellent cycling stability in Na 2 SO 4 , making them promising for energy storage applications.

title: Concentration-dependent photophysics of InP/ZnS quantum dots: surface still matters despite thick shells
abstract: Core-shell InP/ZnS quantum dots exhibit the dilution-induced suppression of luminescence efficiency and lifetime. The results show that even in type-I heterostructures with thick shells excitonic wavefunctions remain susceptible to surface chemistry.

title: Active tumor targeting by core–shell PDMS–HA nanoparticles with sequential delivery of doxorubicin and quercetin to overcome P-glycoprotein efflux pump
abstract: Core-shell PDMS-HA nanoparticles for targeted tumor therapy, delivering doxorubicin and quercetin sequentially effectively bypasses P-glycoprotein efflux, improving drug retention and efficacy. Created in BioRender. Verma, M. (2025) https://BioRender.com/y29e681

title: Circular dichroism measurement of a single anisotropic chiral nanostructure using scanning circular dichroism microscopy
abstract: This study introduces scanning circular dichroism microscopy (SCDM) to accurately extract the “intrinsic” CD signal of a single anisotropic chiral nanostructure.

title: Theoretical insights into spacer molecule design to tune stability, dielectric, and exciton properties in 2D perovskites
abstract: We investigate how spacer molecules influence the properties of RP-type 2D perovskites. Modifying aromatic units (PMA, THMA, FUMA) reveals that small adjustments can significantly affect dielectric screening, exciton binding energy, and structure.

title: Enhancing the hydrothermal aging resistance of alumina-toughened zirconia via cerium oxide doping
abstract: The addition of a small amount of CeO 2 doping in alumina-toughened zirconia significantly enhances densification, suppresses hydrothermal degradation, and improves hardness by refining grain structure and stabilizing the tetragonal phase.

title: Research progress of transition metal catalysts for electrocatalytic EG oxidation
abstract: The synthesis method and properties of electrocatalysts for EGOR reaction are described. Images reproduced with permission; see Acknowledgements for details.

title: Hawthorn carbon dots: a novel therapeutic agent for modulating body weight and hepatic lipid profiles in high-fat diet-fed mice
abstract: Synthesis of HCD (A) and intervention on obesity in high-fat diet mice (B).

title: Optimization of the hole-injection layer for quantum dot light-emitting diodes
abstract: This review summarizes the optimization strategies for the HILs in QLEDs, focusing on the design of single HILs, dual HILs, and doped HILs. Additionally, treatment methods that can further optimize the interfacial properties are discussed.

title: Chirality generation on carbon nanosheets by chemical modification
abstract: Chirality is an intriguing property of molecules, and exciting area of study involves the generation of chirality in nanographenes (NGs), also known as graphene quantum dots.

title: Insights into the electroactive impact of magnetic nanostructures in PVDF composites via small-angle neutron scattering
abstract: Poly(vinylidene fluoride) (PVDF) is technologically relevant due to its thermal stability; chemical, mechanical and radiation resistance; transparency; biocompatibility; and ease of processing.

title: Boosting quantum efficiency and suppressing self-absorption in CdS quantum dots through interface engineering
abstract: Interface-engineered semiconductor quantum dots finally have it all – near-ideal photoluminescence efficiency and wide tunability without self-absorption.

title: Determination of absolute intramolecular distances in proteins using anomalous X-ray scattering interferometry
abstract: We establish high-resolution (<1 Å) intramolecular distance measurements in proteins using pairs of small (1.4 nm diameter) gold nanoparticle labels and anomalous small-angle X-ray scattering as a read out.

title: Corn silk-derived biomass carbon materials for low-frequency microwave absorption and energy storage
abstract: Corn silk-derived biomass carbon demonstrates enhanced low-frequency microwave absorption and outstanding energy storage performance attributed to its large specific surface area and abundant N and O heteroatoms.

title: Microfluidic-based nanocarriers for overcoming biological barriers in therapeutic delivery systems
abstract: Microfluidic technologies have revolutionized the synthesis of nanocarriers for drug and gene delivery, providing unparalleled precision and efficiency in the production of therapeutic nanoparticles.

title: A review: metal and metal oxide nanoparticles for environmental applications
abstract: Environmental applications of metal and metal oxide nanoparticles primarily comprise contaminant adsorbents, catalysts, and sensors for hazardous substances, etc .

title: Advanced disease therapeutics using engineered living drug delivery systems
abstract: This review highlights how smart nanomaterials overcome biological barriers by enhancing targeting, reducing off-target effects, and enabling controlled drug release. Created in BioRender. Orash, A. (2025) https://BioRender.com/g97k271

title: Carbon-based nanostructured materials incorporating carbon dots for supercapacitors: a review
abstract: This review summarizes the research progress on the combination of carbon dots and carbon materials in the field of supercapacitors and focuses on discussing the electrochemical properties of materials of different dimensions.
